It was shown that deep learning models performed better than classical in genomic-based cancer prediction with an accuracy of up to 96.1 and an F1-score of 95.1. Predictive treatment outcome modeling yielded higher treatment response prediction rates of more than 94 % on breast cancer datasets.

title: A Machine Learning-Assisted Approach for High-Precision PV Module Characterization
authors: Sanat Kumar Patro, Deepak Kumar Barik, Saumyaranjan Sahoo, Gunamani Jena
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: A machine learning-assisted approach for determining the parameters of a dual-diode photovoltaic module model is introduced, utilizing an enhanced differential evolution method with a two-phase mutation technique, referred to as Multimodal Multi-Objective Differential Evolution with Two-Stage Mutation and Modified Maximum Extension Distance (MMODE-TSM-MMED). This strategy is designed to enhance diversity and accelerate convergence. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is assessed by comparing the derived outcomes with those of alternative algorithms. Observations indicate that the optimal solution stabilizes at 6.682 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> across all iterations, with a maximum iteration count of fewer than 15.

title: A TVAE-Driven Data Augmentation and Explainable Ensemble Framework for Early Gallstone Disease Detection
authors: Visalaxi Sankaravadivel, Dinah Punnoose
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Gallstones represent a life-threatening condition prevalent in adult female population. The formation of gallstones in the gallbladder includes a large volume of cholesterol formation, more bilirubin in the system, and poor gallbladder emptying. The proposed approach identified 39 features for predicting the existence of gallstones. As the proposed approach comprises a small dataset, the state-of-the-art generative AI technique Tabular Variation Autoencoder (TVAE) was implemented to generate synthetic datasets. Three distinct sets of synthetic datasets were generated: 30%, 50%, and 70% of original data. The synthetic datasets were trained using an ensemble learning model incorporating random forest, gradient boosting, and extreme gradient boosting. The real dataset was tested using the three ensemble learning models. The performance of both the synthetic and real datasets was assessed using several metrics. Extreme gradient boosting demonstrated superior performance across both real and synthetic datasets. Among the three sets of augmented data, 50 % moderately augmented datasets achieved the higher accuracy (0.8594) and precision (0.8647). The global feature importance for gallstone prediction was determined using the cutting-edge explainable AI technique known as SHAP analysis. SHAP analysis was conducted across the three models to improve clinical decision- making. Based on the SHAP analysis C-reactive protein and Vitamin D were identified as the most influential features.

title: DualRateECGNet: Multi-Rate Latent State Estimation for Contactless ECG Reconstruction from mmWave Radar
authors: Meiyue Diao, Hengrong Cui
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) require continuous cardiac monitoring for early screening, risk assessment, and long-term management. Conventional contact-based electrocardiogram (ECG), however, is not ideal for prolonged use. Adhesive electrodes may cause discomfort, skin irritation, and restricted mobility. Millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar provides a promising non-contact alternative by sensing subtle chest vibrations. Nevertheless, reconstructing ECG from radar remains challenging due to electromechanical delay (EMD), motion interference, and the spectral bias of neural networks. This paper proposes DualRateECGNet, a multi-rate latent state estimation framework for radar-to-ECG reconstruction. The model combines a low-rate latent state estimation branch with a high-rate residual refinement branch. The data-driven Direct State Estimator is used to learn a compact latent representation associated with cardiac dynamics and thoracic motion, while the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) refines high-frequency waveform details to better preserve diagnostic transients such as the QRS complex. Experiments on the MMECG dataset under a 5-fold cross-validation protocol show that DualRateECGNet achieves a median Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.9905 and a median root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.02658 mV, indicating strong reconstruction fidelity within this evaluation setting.

title: AI-Enabled Cognitive Automata Framework for Self-Adaptive Orchestration in Hybrid Cloud–Edge IoMT Healthcare Systems
authors: Divya Warade, Mohammad Khan, Ankita Patil, Dhananjay Sushir, Sejal Lahare, Mritunjay Kr. Ranjan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The fast growth of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has transformed the healthcare system to a data-driven one, in which real-time smart sensing, analytics, and decision-making are conducted. Nevertheless, the current IoMT architectures are generally characterized by the lack of responsiveness, bandwidth congestion, and scalability because the number of medical data in distributed systems is high. This chapter suggests the Cognitive Automata Framework of Adaptive Orchestration of Hybrid Cloud-Edge IoMT Healthcare Systems to deal with such issues. This framework incorporates the automata theory with AI-driven coordination of the dynamic coordination of the computation between the cloud servers and edge devices. Changes in the states of the data flow, task scheduling and resource allocation are represented by the finite and hybrid automata allowing the realization of the self-adaptive and formally verifiable decision processes. In its turn, the cloud layer relies on federated AI models and performs global learning and long-term optimization. A cognitive controller tries to synchronize the movement of workloads with latency, energy, and privacy limitations with the help of automata states. Such a hybrid solution reduces the processing time thereof, increases the scalability, and guarantees the confidentiality of sensitive health data. Thus, the suggested framework does not only correspond to the theoretical rigor, but also gives the practical aspect, which can be strongly applied as the foundation of intelligent, transparent, and trustful healthcare automation in the intelligent hospitals and the telemedicine setting.

title: Dynamic Output Feedback RMPC for NCSs under Round-Robin Protocol subject to Hybrid Attacks
authors: Hongbin Cai, Fuchen Wang, Caiyun Sui, Ning Cui, Xiaoran Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper is concerned with the dynamic output feedback robust model predictive control problem for a class of networked control systems (NCSs) with round-robin (RR) protocol and subject to hybrid attacks. To begin with, two mutually independent Bernoulli random variables are used to characterize the DoS and deception attacks in the sensor-to-controller (S-C) channel. To avoid data collision, the RR protocol is employed to orchestrate the data transmission order in the controller-to-actuator (C-A) channel. Furthermore, the inequality analysis technique and slack matrices are subsequently used to resolve the complex non-convex optimization problem. Sufficient conditions are provided to guarantee the recursive feasibility of the proposed algorithm and the mean square (MS) security of the system. At last, a high-purity distillation column example demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: EduVault: A Blockchain-Based Framework for Secure Academic Credential Management and Verification
authors: Buddala Siddhartha, Dhanushya S, Adhithya S. R, Gopinathan D, Uma Devi Bitra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Academic credential verification is becoming increasingly important for educational institutions and organizations. The existing verification system is based on centralized databases and verification processes. This usually leads to issues such as fraud and verification delays. The purpose of this research paper is to introduce a new system called EduVault that will assist in the improvement of the management of academic credentials in a safe and transparent way. The new system is based on blockchain technology and uses AES-256 encryption and SHA-256 hashing with the implementation of a private blockchain in the MERN stack. The academic material is stored in a safe manner through Cloudinary for encrypted file management and MongoDB for immutable data and blockchain. The documents are hashed and encrypted to create a unique digital identifier, also known as a ‘fingerprint.’ This helps in maintaining documentary evidence. Moreover, it allows for quicker, safe, and transparent verification through QR code verification with Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), making it superior to other credential verification systems.

title: Fault-Tolerant Trajectory Tracking for Quadrotor UAVs via Sliding Mode Control
authors: Haowen Sun, Xichao Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a fault-tolerant control scheme based on Sliding Mode Control for quadrotor UAVs subject to actuator efficiency loss during flight. First, a mathematical model of the quadrotor considering nonlinear dynamics is established. Subsequently, a dual-loop control architecture is employed: the outer position loop calculates the desired attitude angles and total thrust based on the reference trajectory , while the inner attitude and altitude loops are designed using SMC algorithms to enhance robustness against parameter uncertainties and external disturbances via error switching functions. Furthermore, a fault estimation module is developed to evaluate the health status of actuators by monitoring the deviation between actual thrust and control commands in real-time. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control scheme effectively suppresses oscillations and achieves precise tracking of complex reference trajectories even under partial actuator failure, exhibiting superior disturbance rejection and fault-tolerant robustness.

title: Operational Scenario Generation for Microgrids using Generative Adversarial Networks and Improved Mean-Shift Clustering
authors: Zhiwei Wan, Kai Yuan, Chongbo Sun, Yudi Ding, Ming Nie
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The planning and operation scheduling of microgrids involve the modeling and analysis of a large number of source and load scenarios, which has a significant impact on the calculation efficiency and reliability of the planning and scheduling model. This paper proposes a typical scenarios generation method for microgrids, where the generative adversarial networks algorithm is utilized to generate a large number of PV output and load demand scenarios, and the performance of generated scenarios is evaluated using autocorrelation coefficient and cumulative probability distribution. The mass scenarios and the associated weight are obtained by an improved mean shift clustering algorithm, considering the total amount and temporal characteristics of the data. A simulation analysis is conducted based on actual data from the northwest region of China, and the results show that the proposed method can effectively simplify original scenarios while balancing the computational complexity and accuracy of the operation scenarios.

title: A Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization Approach for SVM Based Islanding Detection in Microgrids
authors: Ramswarup Kumar, U K Sinha, Jitendra Kumar, Pintu Kumar, Thakur Pranav Kumar Gautam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In this study, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) enhanced through Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization is employed for intelligent islanding detection in microgrids. The method is validated on the IEEE 13-bus microgrid system using real-time measurements of voltage, frequency, and power under various load and disturbance scenarios. Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization automatically selects optimal SVM parameters (kernel, C, gamma, and degree), significantly improving classification accuracy compared to conventional manual tuning. The optimized SVM achieves fast detection and clear separation between islanding and non-islanding events, making it suitable for reliable real-time operation. Additionally, the accuracy of training and testing for the microgrid islanding detection problem is evaluated between the Bayesian-optimized SVM and a decision tree-based method.

title: A Gated Multi-Modal Fusion Framework Via Mixture of Experts for Environment-Aware UAV Detection
authors: Yumou Wang, Jianxiao Zou, Ru An, Lei Jia
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: The rapid evolution of the low-altitude economy necessitates robust perception systems capable of operating in complex, open-world environments. While the detection of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) has seen significant progress, maintaining performance in complex low-altitude environments remains non-trivial, particularly when single-modality systems are subjected to environmental stressors like extreme lighting variations or elevated background noise. This paper explores an environment-aware multi-modal detection framework based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE) approach, referred to as AVLEnvMoE. By integrating audio, visual, and LiDAR modalities, this framework seeks to capitalize on the complementary strengths of heterogeneous sensors. Central to the proposed architecture is an Environmental Gating Network, which adaptively assigns fusion weights to the expert branches of audio, vision, and LiDAR by utilizing real-time physical metadata, including image brightness, contrast, and acoustic noise levels. This mechanism allows the system to prioritize more reliable modalities when the visual channel is compromised. Evaluation results on the MMAUD dataset suggest that, compared to traditional fusion methods, the proposed gated multi-modal scheme provides enhanced robustness and positioning accuracy across challenging environmental conditions.

title: Distillation-Driven Graph Recommendation by Unifying Local and Global Signals
authors: Hongjia Chen, Qunfeng Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Graph-based recommender systems can effectively exploit user - item interaction structures. However, under sparse feedback, many graph contrastive approaches fail to leverage reliable global cues, which may lead to unstable optimization and suboptimal generalization. In this paper, we develop an enhanced graph contrastive framework that integrates knowledge distillation with an exponential moving average (EMA) teacher. The EMA teacher produces temporally smoothed targets, and the student model learns from the teacher through distillation, resulting in more stable training dynamics and improved robustness to noise. Extensive experiments on multiple large-scale benchmarks demonstrate consistent gains over strong baselines, achieving notable improvements in ranking performance as well as training efficiency. These results suggest a practical way to strengthen global structural guidance in graph recommendation.

title: Intelligent Big Data Analytics Framework for Scalable and Real-Time Data Processing in Distributed Systems
authors: Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur, Hassandeep Singh, Ravi Shanker
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The sheer increase in the amount of data produced by distributed sources (IoT devices, cloud platforms, real-time applications) has led to a high demand for scalable and efficient big data analytics frameworks. Conventional data processing systems are limited with respect to their ability to process large volumes and high velocity and heterogeneous streams of data, especially in real-time. So as to mitigate such challenges, this paper will suggest an Intelligent Big Data Analytics Framework for scalable and real-time data processing in Distributed Systems that combines distributed computing with machine learning based analytics to effectively process and make decisions relating to data. The suggested framework comprises such main elements as data acquisition, distributed processing, an intelligent analytics engine, and a real-time decision module. The system uses parallel computing and adaptive learning to enhance scalability, minimize latency, and improve efficiency in processing data. Experimental testing shows that the suggested framework has a processing efficiency of 94.6, a latency decrease of 38, a scalability enhancement of 41, and an accuracy of 93.2 compared to the standard big data processing systems. The framework also uses resources most effectively and enables real-time analytics in making dynamic decisions. These findings demonstrate that the intelligent big data analytics model proposed has a strong, scalable, and efficient solution to real-time data processing in a distributed environment.

title: Improved ECC Algorithm Based on Dynamic Base Point and Dynamic Private Key and Its Application in Remote Capacity Verification Communication in Substations
authors: Yunchen Xu, Zonghe Ding, Ling Chen, Liming Chang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the advancement of smart grid construction, remote battery capacity verification for DC systems in substations has become a critical focus in digital transformation for operation and maintenance. However, multi-system interactions face security risks such as communication delays, data leaks, and device authentication vulnerabilities. This study proposes an improved ECC algorithm with public keyless transmission, dynamic base points, and dynamic private keys. Both parties derive these parameters via real-time synchronization, eliminating risks of public key interception, forgery, and static key accumulation. Security evaluation using the Dolev-Yao threat model demonstrates that the algorithm effectively resists various attacks through formal and informal analyses. Experimental results show that the proposed improved ECC scheme achieves only 60.1%,33.0%, and 17.2% of the computational, communication, and storage overheads compared to traditional ECC schemes, respectively, while maintaining 44.9%,44.8%, and 62.5% of the computational, communication, and storage overheads of conventional RSA public key encryption algorithms. The exponential increase in cryptographic complexity further highlights the scheme's high efficiency and robust security in edge computing scenarios.

title: PROMISE-Net: A Progressive Contextual Multi-Scale Integrated Network for Accurate Lung Nodule Segmentation and Classification
authors: V. Kavithamani, Jayashivani M, Asraf Ali A, Sowndharya. L, Suruthika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Accurate lung cancer prediction demands precise nodule segmentation and reliable malignancy classification. It is very critical to analyze when nodules show low contrast, irregular shapes and small sizes. The proposed PROMISE-Net is a unified deep learning framework that incorporates progressive multi-scale segmentation with maskguided contextual classification. The segmentation module, Contextual Residual Attention UNet (CR-AUNet) integrates the modules of Contextual Pyramid Attention and Progressive Boundary Refinement for enhancing structural detail and boundary accuracy. The classification module such as MaskConditioned Contrastive Transformer (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{M C}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>-Transformer) is proposed to create mask-guided ROI features together with global contextual embeddings. The Cross-Task Feature Bank(CTFB) enforces consistent representation learning through supervised contrastive loss. The proposed PROMISENet was tested on a lung CT dataset with benign, malignant and normal cases, achieving a Dice score of 0.95, segmentation accuracy of 97.8 %, and a classification accuracy of 98.6 %. These results showed that this model outperformed than existing baseline models in a lung cancer prediction.

title: Fusing Instance-Based and Density-Based Learning for Enhanced Accident Prediction
authors: Lanke Lakshmi Prasanna Kumar, Jayalakshmi Machiraju, I.V. Shashikala, T. Aditya Sai Srinivas, K. Dora Babu, Dhanunjay Sirigiri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Predicting the severity of traffic accidents is important in the development of intelligent transport systems that facilitate emergency response systems and the allocation of resources. The high dimensionality of the accident data, its complex nature and class imbalance means that applying a singular machine learning algorithm produces insufficient results. To this end, we develop and propose a novel hybrid ensemble framework for the K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN) and Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) technologies. The proposed methodology is built on a solid foundation of preprocessing steps that include label encoding, standardization, and addressing the class imbalance. KNN is a powerful instance-based classifier for the local pattern density and feature proximity, while DBSCAN helps in the recognition of structures and outlier elasticities in a particular data density, thereby assisting in the distribution of the data from a different perspective. The main novelty of this paper lies in the weighted ensemble mechanism that integrates KNN classification probabilities with DBSCAN's cluster-density-based confidence score. We provide a detailed analysis and comparisons of the hybrid model with standalone KNN, DBSCAN and advanced benchmarks such as Random Forest, XGBoost and KNN-OPTICS hybrid models. Using a public dataset of large-scale traffic accidents, the KNN-DBSCAN ensemble is shown to achieve state-of-the-art predictive accuracy of 92.4 % and F 1 -score of 0.91. This represents a statistically significant improvement over the baseline models. Using feature importance, the study also explains the major contributors to the severity of the accidents. This study provides a more sophisticated, explainable and reproducible accident analytics model that has direct real-world applications in improving the practices of road safety management and the development of data-based strategies and policy frameworks.

title:
Robust H
                    <sub>∞</sub>
                    Estimation for Nonlinear Complex Networks With Dynamic Links and Integral Measurements*
authors: Haixin Li, Yanshuo Wu, Yanqin Wang, Mengdi Chang, Liang Guo, Nan Hou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper addresses the H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> state estimation problem for a kind of nonlinear complex networks (CNs) with dynamic links, integral measurements, and stochastic noise. Vector difference equations describe the dynamic behavior of connection relationships, while stochastic nonlinearities are characterized based on their statistical properties. Since the network node states and dynamic link variables cannot be accurately measured, this study aims to design separate estimators for both the dynamic connections and the node states using the available output measurement signals. By leveraging Lyapunov stability theory, appropriate conditions are formulated to ensure the reliability that the estimation error system achieves the prescribed H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> performance index as well as the constraint of mean-square exponential stability. Subsequently, the estimator gain matrices are determined by solving linear matrix inequalities (LMIs). Ultimately, the performance of the proposed estimation algorithm is validated through a numerical simulation.

title: A Data-Driven Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics Model for Optimizing Fertilizer Use in Precision Agriculture
authors: Pradeep Kumar Mishra, Mritunjay Kr. Ranjan, Jitendra Sharma, Ambrish Singh, Rakesh Kumar Pandey, Rajashri Rikame
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The current research work puts forward a single framework that integrates Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and Business Analytics to improve fertilizer prediction for sustainable precision agriculture. The system, which addresses inefficiencies in input use, makes use of both historical and real-time data to accurately predict fertilizer needs. The technique combines soil moisture, evapotranspiration, and climatic parameters through a preprocessing and data integration pipeline, which is then feature engineering and label encoding. A Random Forest classifier is used to determine the need for fertilizer, and data streaming based on the API is used for real-time adaptability. The proposed framework through the use of predictive analytics and data-driven decision-making tools reduces the operational costs and the negative effects on the environment. In such a case, the application of this technology in the field of agribusiness would facilitate the stakeholders' access to reliable information, thus, enabling them to determine the optimal fertilizer distribution and to adopt eco-friendly agricultural practices. In essence, this AI-powered predictive tool is a great support for the efficient use of resources, the improvement of yields, and the consolidation of the basis for environmentally friendly precision agriculture.

title: Predicting Social Media Addiction Among University Students Via Stacking Ensemble Machine Learning Models: A Comparative Study
authors: Jayapriya R, Jeyachidra J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The accelerated use of social media by university students has added to the worriment about addiction behavioral patterns. Subjectivity and inconsistency of self-report survey data make it difficult to identify high-risk individuals at an early stage. Conventional forms of supervised learning make the assumption that there are trustworthy ground-truth labels and as a result fail to work well when trained on noisy psychometric answers. To solve this problem, this paper suggests a Confidence Guided Adaptive Supervised Learning (CGASL) model, which overcomes label uncertainty by a teacher student model. One of the main models computes scores on the prediction of confidence to filter problematic samples adaptively before the final training. The model boosts generalization and decreases overfitting to conflicting responses in that the high-confidence observations only are retained. Tests on a sample of 1,598 college students show that CGASL reaches 94.06% accuracy and AUC-ROC of 0.97, which is a major improvement compared to baseline models (75.62% accuracy). Sensitivity analysis establishes that a certain threshold (0.75) exists between the elimination of noise and the retention of data. The results show the efficiency of confidence-aware filtering in ethically and scalably deployable in academic contexts social media addiction screening.

title: Real-Time Fire Detection Using a Computer Vision-Based YOLOv11 Model on Raspberry Pi 5
authors: Kenny Hoang, David Mung, Pallav Kumar Bera
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: An optimized Raspberry Pi 5 deployment of a real-time fire and smoke detection system was developed by using the YOLO object detection framework. A custom dataset with three classes (fire, smoke, none) was used to train the model for 200 epochs. The system achieved a precision of 94.41 %, recall of 89.46 %, F1-Score of 91.87 %, mAP@50 of 93.73%, and mAP@50-95 of 85.44%. Results show that smoke detection outperforms fire detection, while most misclassifications occurred between fire and background. Despite these challenges, the proposed system demonstrates reliable performance and feasibility for low-power, edge-based fire monitoring applications. The system also demonstrates the potential of integrating lightweight deep learning models with embedded edge devices for continuous environmental monitoring. The optimized implementation on Raspberry Pi 5 enables low-cost deployment in smart buildings, industrial areas, and public safety applications while maintaining stable detection accuracy and efficient real-time processing performance.

title: Task-Driven Semantic Compression for Underwater Robotic Vision: A Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework
authors: Suresh Kumar Kanaparthi, Chandra Kowtarapu, Chanamolu Hima Varshini, G Sai Jayanth, Tiguti Sarupya, Avula Sri Sai Koushik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) rely heavily on visual data for exploration, environmental monitoring, and infrastructure inspection. However, the transmission of this visual data to surface operators is severely constrained by the physical limitations of under-water acoustic communication channels, which typically offer bandwidths lower than 10 kbps. Existing underwater image compression methods, such as the Efficient Under-water Image Compression Network (EUICN), predomi-nantly focus on preserving visual fidelity (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) for human interpretation. While effective for visualization, these methods often neglect the semantic needs of autonomous systems, transmitting redundant background information at the expense of mission-critical data. This paper proposes a novel Multi-Task Semantic Compression Network designed to bridge the gap be-tween image compression and robotic perception. The proposed Semantic Quantization Module utilizes pixel-level segmentation masks during the training phase to prioritize the encoding of high-value objects (e.g., fish, reefs, divers) while aggressively compressing nonessential background regions. A dual-decoder architecture is employed to simultaneously reconstruct the visual image and predict a semantic segmentation mask from a single, highly compressed latent representation. Experimental results on the SUIM dataset demonstrate a 97.91 % compression rate (0.5014 bpp), reducing file sizes by approximately 48 x while maintaining high semantic accuracy. This approach enables the real-time transmission of actionable intelli-gence alongside visual data, significantly enhancing AUV autonomy in bandwidthconstrained environments.

title: Development of Attribute-Based Cryptographic Mechanism for Secure Medical Data Retention in Cloud Systems
authors: P. Karthik, D. Latha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The rapid adoption of cloud computing in healthcare has highlighted the need for secure mechanisms to protect sensitive medical data. This paper proposes an Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) approach for securing medical data in cloud systems, ensuring data confidentiality and access control. ABE enables encryption based on user attributes, such as role or medical specialty, ensuring that only authorized personnel can decrypt the data. In a cloud environment, medical data can be stored securely, with access granted based on predefined policies linked to user attributes. This method minimizes the risk of unauthorized access while enabling flexible sharing of data among healthcare providers. The tool used in this approach is an advanced cryptographic library that implements ABE techniques, ensuring efficient encryption and decryption processes. The proposed system addresses the challenges of secure data retention and access control in cloudbased medical data management, offering a robust solution to privacy concerns in healthcare.

title: IoT Integrated Dual-Side Water Pumping System For Sustainable Agriculture
authors: E.B. Priyanka, S. Thangavel, P. Sivamathivanan, S Dharshana, P Jothika, P Kavyadharashini D, K P Vasanth Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The Dual-side water pumping water system is designed to achieve continuous and uninterrupted water flow for agricultural and industrial purposes. The system is powered by the scotch yoke mechanism to provide the pumping action. The system performance was very crucial to measure with the precise controller model. Implementing the IoT to monitor and control water flow rate, pressure level, vibration level, and pump status. The IoT-enabled PLC controller processes the data and push to the cloud data warehouse to ensure variation in the pressure level and flow. The system communication protocols like MQTT and HTTP to continuous data transfer to predict the variation for maintenance activities. In this system, water pumps will be operated bidirectionally to suck the high-velocity fluid with high pressure to achieve the continuous water flow. The microcontroller performs the decision-making algorithm to control the pump’s actuation and flow rate. The system proposed automatic system increases the system efficiency by $25 \%$ as compared to the initial phase to support the water delivery to ensure energy-efficient and seamless water flow.

title: Exploring the Human–AI Interface in Recruitment: A Qualitative Study of Chatbots in HRM
authors: Rashmi Kumari, Sutapa Mondal, Ritesh Kumar Upadhyay, Sushanta Mahanty, Subhash Adhikari, Kunal Sarkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly embedded in human resource management (HRM), with recruitment emerging as one of the earliest domains of adoption. Among AI-enabled tools, chatbots are widely deployed to automate high-volume and transactional recruitment activities such as candidate screening, scheduling, and communication. However, empirical evidence on their practical utility, limitations, and contextual fit in emerging and linguistically diverse markets remains limited. This study explores the human–AI interface in recruitment by examining how AI-powered chatbots are perceived, implemented, and constrained in Indian organizational contexts. Using a qualitative research design, semi-structured interviews were conducted with senior HR professionals possessing extensive experience in talent acquisition, HR analytics, and AI-enabled recruitment. Reflexive thematic analysis reveals that chatbots deliver substantial efficiency gains and improved candidate responsiveness when integrated with applicant tracking systems, particularly for entry-level and high-volume hiring. At the same time, significant limitations persist due to natural language processing constraints, contextual ambiguity, and challenges in assessing soft skills, leadership potential, and cultural fit. Findings further highlight clear task boundaries, with chatbots functioning effectively as process-support and triage tools, while human judgment remains indispensable for evaluative and strategic hiring decisions. The study contributes practitioner-centered insights to the AI-in-HRM literature by demonstrating that chatbot adoption in recruitment is best understood as augmentative rather than substitutive. It concludes by emphasizing the need for hybrid human–AI recruitment models, robust governance mechanisms, and context-aware chatbot design to ensure responsible and effective deployment in emerging markets.

title: Electromechanical Coupling Modeling of Piezoelectric-Electromagnetic Vibration Energy Harvesters Under Transmission Line Micro-Wind Vibration
authors: Shikai Zeng, Sheng Ye, Zhenyu Zi, Yeming Lai, Hongxiao Yang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To meet the self-powered demand of online monitoring equipment on transmission lines under micro-wind vibration conditions, an electromechanical coupling model of a piezoelectric–electromagnetic vibration energy harvester (PEVEH) is established. A mechanical vibration model combined with a mechanical-electrical equivalent circuit model is adopted to describe the vibration dynamics of the system. Based on this model, the electromagnetic conversion model and the piezoelectric conversion model are derived respectively. The electromagnetic part is analyzed in terms of induced voltage, load current, and output electrical power, while the piezoelectric part is described using an equivalent electromechanical circuit model to characterize the relationship between strain, voltage, and output power. The damping characteristics, frequency response, and power output mechanism of the coupled system are theoretically analyzed. Furthermore, the output power of four different configurations of piezoelectric-electromagnetic cantilever beams covering an energy harvesting bandwidth of 10 Hz to 50 Hz were verified through finite element analysis. The results demonstrated potential application prospects of the PEVEH in transmission line monitoring systems for recharging low-power wireless sensors.

title: Route Visualization and Smart Navigation Aid for Dense Urban Areas Using Google Maps and Street View Integration
authors: Bandi Bharath, Geetha Guttikonda, Choutha Akshay, Karasala Ratnakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: A high rate of urbanization has also complicated traffic in most cities, which makes navigation more difficult to daily commuters. Traditional digital maps just offer a poor image of the actual driving experience with the primary usage of traditional aerial perspective which is not dynamic. This paper proposes a visualization of routes and smart navigation system that creates a drive-through preview video in a smooth and realistic form based on the data provided by Google Maps and Street View. The proposed model combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) heading refinement, the ORB-based Visual Odometry (VO) motion estimation, and optical flow frame-toframe motion detection in order to provide constant visual changes. The user will then be able to choose start and destination points and adjust video settings to create a smooth preview of the route and a dynamic static map. The system minimizes the directional jumps and GPS noise to enhance route understanding and offer more intuitive nature of navigation to densely populated areas.

title: A High-Risk Prediction Method for Coal Mine Fires Incorporating Path-Coupled Features
authors: Wenjing Yu, Yong Qin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the dispersed nature of risk information and the insufficient representation of chain-like combinational relationships in coal mine fire accident investigation reports, a CatBoost-based high-risk prediction method incorporating structured risk information and path-coupled features was developed in this study. Risk prediction and the analysis of key influencing factors associated with coal mine fires were conducted through structured risk information encoding, core path screening, model comparison, threshold determination, and SHAP-based interpretation. The results indicate that, after the introduction of path-coupled features, CatBoost exhibited superior overall predictive performance and higher precision for the high-risk class, while enhancing the identification of chain-like combinational relationships underlying high-risk accidents. The findings further reveal that high-risk accidents are jointly shaped by multiple categories of factors. These results can provide support for high-risk screening, warning-threshold setting, and the prioritization of governance measures.

title: Scalable Floating-Point Satisfiability via Staged Optimization
authors: Yuanzhuo Zhang, Zhoulai Fu, Binoy Ravindran
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Solvable Tuple Patterns and Their Applications to Program Verification
authors: Naoki Kobayashi, Ryosuke Sato, Ayumi Shinohara, Ryo Yoshinaka
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Geometry Optimization of Non-Ideal Through-Glass Vias (TGVs) for Enhanced Electrical and Mechanical Performance
authors: Zhen Fang, Jinxu Liu, Borui Li, Wenlei Li, Jihua Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Through-glass vias (TGVs) have attracted considerable attention in advanced packaging because they offer low dielectric loss and good electrical insulation. However, the tapered sidewalls formed during fabrication can affect the reliability of glass-based packages. These sidewalls increase the parasitic inductance at high frequencies and cause nonuniform stress under thermal loading. Existing TGV inductance models still have limited ability to describe skin-current effects in nonvertical vias. This study presents an analytical model for tapered TGVs that includes skin current and defines the frequency range in which the model is applicable. The effect of taper angle on stress distribution at 200 °C is also examined. The results show that the taper angle redistributes stress in the copper column and reduces stress concentration at the Cu/glass interface. By further optimizing laser power, ultrasonic power, HF concentration, etching temperature, and ammonium fluoride concentration, precise control of the TGV taper is achieved. This work provides a design reference and an optimization strategy for high-performance glass interposers.

title: Parameter Efficient Emotional Transfer for Fine-Grained Emotional Audio-Driven Talking Portrait Generation_supp1-3699022.mp4
authors: Chi-Man Pun
abstract: Facial expression, especially emotion, plays a crucial role in building a realistic talking portrait video from audio. Previous emotional talking head methods train lip-synchronization with video-level annotated emotion labels jointly. However, the video-level annotated emotion labels make it hard to learn the accurate frame-wise emotions. Besides, since the emotional dataset is much smaller than the neutral faces, it becomes harder to generate lip-synchronized portrait videos with accurate expressions. To address these challenges, we propose a new method for efficient emotional transfer and detailed emotion capture. In detail, we first consider emotions as the residual of the neutral expression and train additional adapters (LoRAs in our case) for each emotion individually. Then, we use the frame-wise emotional annotations as input and design a novel method to jointly optimize the multiple adapters. After training, our method can produce high visual quality results by driving audio and fine-grained emotion guidance with negligible additional parameters. Extensive experiments also demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in synthesizing photo-realistic, emotionally expressive talking portraits that are well-synchronized with the input audio compared with other state-of-the-art methods.

title: Rate Optimization in Hybrid Multi-RIS Assisted NOMA Systems Based on WMMSE Algorithm
authors: Zhongrui Shu, Hong Wang, Youhong Feng, Peiyu Wang, Heyi Li
abstract: Both reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) and non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) are emerging technologies poised to revolutionize future wireless communications. The active RIS plays a crucial role in mitigating the severe path attenuation of multi-hop reflected links. This paper addresses the sum rate optimization problem in a hybrid cascaded RIS system, considering the constraints on power budget and phase shift of each reflection element. In order to tackle the formu lated nonconvex problem, a weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) algorithm is invoked to optimize the receiver, beamforming vector, and active load amplitude. Furthermore, the phase shifts of each RIS are calculated by using a phase rotation algorithm, followed by multiple rounds of alternating optimization. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme achieves the optimal data rate in various communication environments and outperforms the existing approaches significantly.

title: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
authors: N/A

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp2-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp4-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: Graph Processing with Contrastive Learning for Social Recommendation in IoT Interaction Networks
authors: Yan Wang, Yang Ma, Aijia Ouyang, Junyi Zhang, Weilong Peng
abstract: AI agents deployed in Internet of Things (IoT) environments increasingly rely on interaction data to perform adaptive decision-making and personalized service delivery. Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based recommendation models have demonstrated strong capabilities in modeling user preferences by leveraging relational information. However, IoT interaction networks often contain noisy or preference-irrelevant social connections that violate the homophily assumption, which may negatively affect information propagation and decision accuracy. Moreover, observed interaction data are typically sparse and may omit valuable implicit feedback, especially in dynamic IoT environments. To address these challenges, we propose GPCL, a graph processing framework with contrastive learning for adaptive AI agents in IoT interaction networks. GPCL progressively denoises the social graph by adaptively pruning low-confidence user–user links, while enriching the interaction graph by introducing high-confidence yet unobserved interactions. By jointly refining both relational structures, the framework improves structural quality and enhances representation reliability for downstream decision tasks. In addition, GPCL incorporates a lightweight embedding-level contrastive learning strategy that enhances representation robustness without relying on costly graph perturbations, enabling efficient representation learning. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets—Yelp, Ciao, and LastFM—demonstrate that GPCL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides an effective and efficient solution for adaptive decision modules of AI agents in IoT interaction networks, particularly under sparse and noisy relational environments.

title: Upper Limb Movement Assistance through Model-Based Path Planning and Control of Hybrid FES-Exoskeleton Systems_supp1-3700177.mp4
authors: Marcia O'Malley

title: A Heterogeneous System Architecture for Monocular Depth Estimation and Parallel Multi-Task Vision on Edge Devices
authors: Donghua Chen, Runtong Zhang, Dandan Li
abstract: To enhance the accuracy and resource utilization of monocular camera-based edge artificial intelligence (AI) applications, we introduce a heterogeneous system architecture for parallel multi-task vision tailored for resource-constrained edge devices. We developed an experimental self-powered vision system integrating a cutting-edge AI-enabled IMX500 vision sensor and a Hailo-8 AI accelerator. This architecture enables ultra-low power sensing and edge inferencing, successfully addressing the on-device challenges of multi-task sensor fusion in object detection, pose analysis, distance estimation, and post-processing decision support. The framework features a multi-objective and multi-stage intelligent visual perception architecture utilizing pretrained light-weight models. By incorporating state-of-the-art techniques such as YOLO, FastDepth, Depth Prediction Transformers and Lite-Mono, we achieve robust parallel sensing and inferencing within the constraints of monocular vision. Through comprehensive testing and evaluation on multiple datasets, our results demonstrate that the proposed architecture enhances complex parallel vision tasks while maintaining both high performance and a small form factor. Our work provides sustainable and scalable hardware-software co-design solutions for intelligent applications, including autonomous vehicles, smart robots, mobile assistants, and interactive devices.

title: Construction of Digital Terrain Maps from Multi-view Satellite Imagery using Neural Volume Rendering
authors: Josef X. Biberstein, Guilherme Cavalheiro, Juyeop Han, Sertac Karaman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Digital terrain maps (DTMs) are an important part of planetary exploration, enabling operations such as terrain relative navigation during entry, descent, and landing for spacecraft and aiding in navigation on the planetary surface. As robotic exploration missions become more ambitious, the need for high-quality DTMs will only increase. However, producing DTMs via multi-view stereo pipelines for satellite imagery, the current state-of-the-art, can be cumbersome and require significant manual image preprocessing to produce satisfactory results. In this work, these shortcomings are addressed by adapting neural volume rendering techniques to learn textured digital terrain maps directly from satellite imagery. The proposed method, neural terrain maps (NTM), only requires the locus for each image pixel and does not rely on depth or any other structural priors. NTM is demonstrated on both synthetic and real satellite data from Earth and Mars encompassing scenes on the order of 100km2. The accuracy and precision of the output terrain maps is evaluated by comparing with existing high-quality DTMs produced using traditional multi-view stereo pipelines. NTM shows promising results, with the precision of terrain prediction almost equal to the resolution of the satellite images even in the presence of imperfect camera intrinsics and extrinsics. As compared to high quality reference terrain maps, NTM results ranged from an error standard deviation of 498m for ASTER imagery with a grounds sample distance (GSD) of 15m to 19m for MRO CTX imagery with a GSD of 6m.

title: Edge Computing-based IoT System for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring and Decision Making
authors: Kanmani. K, D.Sathyanarayanan, P.N. Periyasamy, Samson Ebenezar Uthirapathy, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, S. Parthiban
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The careful observation of the environment is paramount for preserving ecological integrity, managing contamination, and fostering responsible growth in both urban and rural areas. Conventional environmental observation systems leveraging the Internet of Things (IoT) frequently depend on centralized cloud computing, which can lead to delays, limitations in data transmission capacity, and deferred decision-making processes, thereby diminishing their utility in situations demanding immediate action. This document introduces an IoT system based on edge computing designed for immediate environmental observation and informed decision-making. This system integrates dispersed sensors with localized edge devices, enabling on-site data manipulation and prompt analysis. The system gathers environmental metrics, encompassing air and water quality, soil hydration, temperature, and relative humidity. It employs streamlined machine learning algorithms at the edge to identify deviations, patterns, and possible

title: A Novel Deep Learning Framework with Feature Engineering for Stroke Risk Prediction
authors: Ahmad Al Athamneh, Mahmoud E. Farfoura, Abdel Fawaz Bagoudou, Saba Alshatali
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Stroke is the principal cause of mortality and long term disability all over the world with the known risk factors including old age, high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, and tobacco use. The proper identification of the people at risk is a key to successful prevention. In this work, a residual-gated deep neural network is suggested as the model to predict the stroke risk in the cases of structured healthcare data, using clinically-motivated feature engineering, class-imbalance management, and interpretability evaluation. In order to overcome the extreme class imbalance that can occur in model training, Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) is used. Interaction terms are added to the feature space, and the interaction-based composite clinical risk scores are utilized to increase the representational capacity. The suggested architecture uses gating mechanisms, residual connections, batch normalization and dropout so that it can be trained steadily. There is experimental analysis on a public healthcare dataset that has shown to perform well in terms of discriminative evaluation reaching a ROC-AUC of 0.976 with balanced performance in terms of precision and recall. The model interpretability can be provided based on permutation feature importance and probability distribution analysis, and calibration curves can also be used to evaluate the credibility of the risk score that has been predicted. These findings present the possibilities of integrating clinically informed feature engineering with state-of-the-art neural designs to predict the risk of stroke in controlled experimental contexts.

title: Adaptive Transformer-Based Neural Network for Robust Stereo Image Pair Rectification
authors: Wissam Alkhadour, Jawad Hasan AlKhateeb
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The rectification of stereo images is a basic preprocessing phase in depth estimation and 3D reconstruction processes. Conventional techniques use calibrated camera parameters that are vulnerable to the environment and mechanical errors. New deep learning methods have demonstrated potential but fail in large baseline settings and difficult conditions. In this paper, we are going to present AT-Rect, an Adaptive Transformer-based Rectification network, which is trained to directly estimate the homography transformations, using the unrectified stereo pairs of images. The new contribution is that we present a Multi-Scale Cross-Attention Module (MSCAM), which can capture both local geometric and global correspondence information at various levels of resolution. We also introduce an Adaptive Feature Enhancement Block (AFEB), which is a dynamic weighting of the feature significance in relation to the image contents and geometric consistency restrictions. Experiments on KITTI, Middlebury, and our newly acquired wide-baseline dataset have shown that AT-Rect can be used to achieve state-of-the-art performance, cutting the average epipolar error by 38.7% over the current methods, and can be run at 45 FPS in real-time on conventional hardware.

title: A High-Throughput Custom Accelerator Architecture for Transformer Models on Edge Devices
authors: Ramakrishna Penaganti
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Transformer models have become the most popular type of architecture in deep learning today. They can do the best work in natural language processing, computer vision, and multimodal tasks. But their use on edge devices is limited by high computational costs, high memory bandwidth needs, and high energy use. This paper introduces a high-throughput custom accelerator architecture specifically engineered to facilitate efficient Transformer inference in resource-constrained edge environments. The proposed architecture incorporates a systolic tensor processing array, a specialized multi-head attention engine, and a hierarchical on-chip memory subsystem to minimize data movement and optimize parallelism. Low-precision arithmetic (INT8/INT4) and quantization-aware execution make models even more energy-efficient without losing accuracy. By using a co-optimized hardware-software stack and a pipelined execution strategy, the accelerator makes big gains in latency, throughput, and power use. Tests on typical Transformer models, such as BERT-Tiny, MobileViT-Transformer, and DistilGPT, show that they can process data up to 18.4 times faster and use 7.2 times less energy than optimized CPU and commercial NPU baselines. These results show that specialized Transformer-centric accelerators could make real-time edge intelligence possible for applications like on-device NLP, smart sensing, aerial perception, and embedded vision systems.

title: Microbial Electrolysis Cells for Sustainable Biohydrogen Production and Wastewater Valorization
authors: Zakaria Ibrahimi, El Ayachi Chater, Najma Laaroussi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Microbial electrolysis cells represent a developing technology for the generation of hydrogen through microbial oxidation of organic matter coupled with electrochemicalassisted hydrogen generation. The present review is a focused, critical assessment of the development of microbial electrolysis cell technology, including operational methods, electrode materials, reactor configuration, and performance evaluation. The key performance metrics of hydrogen production rate, Coulombic Efficiency, Energy Efficiency, and Hydrogen Purity were reviewed and demonstrated significant relationships between material selection and system design. Although encouraging results have been achieved at the laboratory scale, the review also noted numerous challenges to the successful implementation of microbial electrolysis cells at larger scales. Challenges include hydrogen loss via methanogenic activity, electrode fouling and durability concerns, uncertainty regarding the energy balance, and financial constraints. Future directions will be explored, focusing on the design of modular reactors, integration of renewable energy sources into the system, and necessity of standardization in performance reporting and long-term stability assessments. Overall, microbial electrolysis cells provide a viable pathway for the generation of hydrogen from biomass through an integrated circular economy approach provided that the previously stated technical and operational barriers are addressed.

title: Comparative Study of Surrogate Models for Aeroelastic Wind Turbine Blade
authors: Harouna Illou Abdoulaye, Rabii El Maani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study compares four metamodeling techniques for approximating the aeroelastic response of a wind turbine blade subjected to one-way fluid-structure interaction (FSI). The evaluated methods include second-order polynomial response surfaces (RSM), Kriging, artificial neural networks (ANN), and genetic aggregation. An experimental design based on Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) was used to generate 27 training points for 5 input variables, supplemented by 5 independent points for validation. The results demonstrate that second-order polynomials offer the best generalization robustness for this problem, closely followed by Kriging. Genetic aggregation shows intermediate performance, while neural networks prove inadequate for capturing physical complexity in a low-sampling context. This study highlights the relevance of polynomial approaches and Kriging for aeroelastic optimization when computational cost limits the size of available data. These findings provide a practical framework for wind turbine designers to select cost-effective surrogate models, enabling rapid structural optimization and reliability assessment while significantly reducing the computational overhead of high-fidelity FSI simulations.

title: Microwave Hyperthermia Treatment Planning Via Combination of Focused Patterns and Low-Dimensional Global Optimization
authors: Sabrina Zumbo, Tommaso Isernia, Martina Teresa Bevacqua
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Electromagnetic field shaping plays a central role in microwave hyperthermia, where energy must be deposited selectively inside a tumor while limiting heating in healthy tissues. This paper introduces a reduced-order approach in which the field is synthesized as a linear combination of a small number of time-reversal focused patterns defined at some selected control points inside the target. The corresponding complex weights are optimized through a global optimization algorithm under field-intensity constraints. The method is preliminarily tested against a heterogeneous scenario mimicking a human breast at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1 5 ~ M H z}$</tex>.

title: High-Gain 24 GHz Microstrip Patch Antenna Array Using a Corporate Wilkinson Power Divider
authors: Mohamed Guermal, Jamal Zbitou, Fouad Aytouna, Stephane Ginestar, Mohammed El Gibari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a 32element microstrip patch antenna array operating at 24 GHz for millimeter-wave applications. The proposed array is designed on a Rogers RT5880 substrate. A compact Wilkinson power divider is employed to achieve uniform power distribution with excellent impedance matching and high port isolation. The simulated results demonstrate a reflection coefficient better than $\mathbf{- 3 0 ~ d B}$ for the single patch antenna, while the Wilkinson divider exhibits S11 below - 19 dB, equal power split of - 3 dB, and an isolation level of -20 dB between output ports. When integrated into the complete 32-element antenna array, the system maintains a strong impedance matching with a return loss around $-\mathbf{2 5 ~ d B}$ at $\mathbf{2 4 ~ G H z}$. The radiation characteristics confirm high directivity and improved gain, making the proposed antenna array suitable for automotive radar, millimeter-wave communications, and highfrequency wireless systems.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Multi-Head Self-Attention and Axial Attention in Transformer Architectures
authors: Amine Berquedich, Lahbib Ajallouda, Ahmed Zellou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Transformer architectures have become foundational models across natural language processing, computer vision, and multimodal learning, primarily due to the effectiveness of self-attention mechanisms. However, the quadratic computational complexity of standard self-attention limits scalability for high-dimensional inputs, particularly in vision tasks requiring high-resolution processing. Axial attention has emerged as a promising alternative that factorizes attention along individual axes to improve efficiency while maintaining global receptive fields. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of Multi-Head SelfAttention and Axial Attention, examining their theoretical foundations, computational properties, representational capacity, and practical trade-offs across domains. Through empirical evaluation on medical image segmentation using a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Transformer architecture, we demonstrate that while MHSA achieves marginally higher accuracy, Axial Attention provides substantial computational efficiency gains, enabling processing of higher resolution inputs with significantly reduced memory footprint. Our analysis provides practical guidelines for selecting appropriate attention mechanisms based on task requirements, computational constraints, and input characteristics.

title: An analysis study of the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to improve the management of colic in horses: Survival Prediction, Decision Support, and Automated Monitoring
authors: Karima Handani, Abdelhadi Assir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Horse colic remains a leading cause of mortality and economic loss in veterinary medicine, primarily due to its complex etiology and the inherent subjectivity of traditional diagnostic methods. This study offers a thorough review of the body of research on artificial intelligence’s (AI) revolutionary role in filling in these diagnostic gaps. Four main thematic axes found in the state of the art serve as the foundation for the study. It first analyzes documented methods for preparing clinical data, focusing on how imputation and class balancing affect model reliability. Second, the review compares the performance of various algorithmic architectures reported in the literature and synthesizes findings on survival prediction. Third, it explores at how AI can be used as a decision support tool, emphasizing how crucial Explainable AI (XAI) is to building clinical trust and making real-world bedside applications possible. Finally, the study investigates developments in wearable sensors and computer vision for automated pain assessment and early distress detection. This study shows that AI offers an unbiased support system to enhance clinical intuition, ultimately leading to better therapeutic outcomes and horse welfare, by combining data from recent studies.

title: Stability Study of Advance Mining Roadways in Fault Zones Under Mining Disturbance
authors: Hongyuan Tu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In addressing the instability of fault-affected advance haulage roadways in deep mining, we performed a thorough investigation that encompassed theoretical evaluations, numerical modeling, and field testing to analyze the evolution of stress and the deformation properties of the surrounding rock within the 402 haulage roadway. On this basis, differentiated control strategies incorporating 'anchoring-grouting-support' were subsequently developed based on a risk assessment of instability across different zones. Our results indicate that the risk of roof instability significantly increases under low fault dip angles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20^{\circ}-40^{\circ}$</tex>) or when the working face approaches within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 m}$</tex> of the fault. Furthermore, when the fault throw exceeds the coal seam thickness, both the peak abutment pressure and the deformation of the surrounding rock in the footwall advance roadway decrease as the throw increases. The roadway is categorized into three distinct risk levels: high, medium, and low. Further validation through field tests demonstrate that the proposed control scheme effectively reduces deformation and ensures roadway stability, thereby providing a theoretical foundation and practical guidance for similar conditions.

title: Robust Variational Bayesian Inference for Full-Duplex Computational Microwave Imaging
authors: Tianzhuo Yang, Qian Zhang, Ke Huang, Lixin Ran
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Metasurface-based computational microwave imaging offers a cost-effective and high-resolution solution for biomedical monitoring and security screening. While transitioning to full-duplex architectures enhances system compactness, it inevitably introduces signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) degradation due to self-interference arising from transmitted signal leakage. In this work, a full-duplex computational microwave imaging (FDCMI) system architecture employing a 1-bit phase reconfigurable transmitarray (PRTA) is presented, integrated with a robust reconstruction algorithm based on variational Bayesian inference (VBI). Unlike conventional compressive sensing methods yielding point estimates, the VBI framework incorporates statistical modeling and sparse priors to obtain Bayesian estimates of all unknown parameters, significantly enhancing robustness against noise and model mismatch while maintaining computational efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate the high-resolution capability of the FD-CMI system and the robustness of the proposed VBI, with an imaging resolution of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.27 \lambda$</tex> under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5} \mathbf{d B}$</tex> SNR, indicating its promise for practical imaging scenarios.

title: Performance Analysis and Optimization of Eulerian Video Magnification for Vital Signs Estimation in Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
authors: Aldo Cambronero-Ureña, Luis Chavarria-Zamora, Luis Alonso Barboza Artavia, Jason Leitón Jiménez
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive computational performance and accuracy analysis of an embedded system implementing Eulerian Video Magnification (EVM) for vital signs estimation. The system was evaluated on a Raspberry Pi 4 and PC, comparing five face detection models with MediaPipe selected for optimal efficiency. Results demonstrate that the implementation enables real-time heart rate (HR) estimation with an end-to-end processing rate of $44.69 \pm 0.91$ FPS. The system achieved perfect face detection (100% detection rate) with minimal positional jitter ($0.006 \pm 0.019$ pixels). HR estimation accuracy showed a mean absolute error of $12.39 \pm 11.28$ BPM, with 71.78% of measurements falling within $\mathbf{1 5 ~ B P M}$ of reference values and 46.21% within 5 BPM. The system maintained thermal stability without throttling at an average CPU frequency of 1.68 GHz, confirming the feasibility of EVMbased vital sign monitoring in resource-constrained embedded systems while providing realistic performance benchmarks.

title: Smart Mining Helmet for Health Monitoring and Safety Assistance
authors: Shaik Thasleem Bhanu, Aakash S, Bharatwajar Pssv, Don Becon M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Working conditions in the mines subject the workers to severe physical pressures and it is therefore important to identify the health conditions as quickly as possible in a bid to avoid the occurrence of fatal cases. In this paper, a Smart Mining Helmet is introduced that will have the ability to read the body temperature, respiration rate, and heart rate through integrated sensors via the Arduino Uno. The system diagnoses the first symptoms of heat stress, unconsciousness, breathing disorders, and uncharacteristic cardiac activity, so it is possible to provide first aid to oneself in the underground. In case of distress indicators, the helmet automatically switches on interventions, including cooling fog spray, wake-up buzers, and controlled nerve stimulation in order to revive or stabilize the miner. There is real-time vital knowledge that is shown on an LCD to achieve constant awareness. The given solution insurers and improves safety by offering immediate personal assistance prior to the arrival of external aid, which is a quality, efficient, and fast solution to the protection of miners.

title: An Ultra-High-Resolution Detection Framework for Micro-Scale PCB Surface Defects
authors: Salam Al-E’Mari, Yousef Sanjalawe, Fadi Fataftah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Printed circuit boards (PCBs) are essential for today’s electronic systems. Flecks caused by surface defects can appear during the Manufacturing process and can profoundly degrade the reliability and performance of the end-product. Automated Engineering and inspection are needed to identify signs of surface polishing defects remaining on boards, including micro-scale impacts, their high-density distribution, and their strong resemblance to the background. Even with the most recent deep learning approaches and object detection systems, the results are still not guaranteed. Most computer-based methods still rely on means and vector-based numerical methods, yielding poor results for identifying ultra-small defects. The ultra-high-resolution detection framework, tailored to the identification of micro-scale defects on PCBs, is the approach presented in this paper. The approach is built on the principle of prioritizing high resolution. One example of the approach is the reconstruction of stride-4 feature maps. Another example is the micro-scale detection head designed to preserve fine-grained spatial resolution. Additionally, the design incorporates multi-scale feature fusion to collate disparate feature sets, producing a more robust outcome while maintaining the desired low computational cost. Extensive experiments are conducted on the public HRIPCB dataset to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Quantitative results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a precision of 98.9%, a recall of 98.6%, an mAP@0.5 of 99.4%, and an mAP@0.5:0.95 of 63.8%, outperforming several state-of-the-art PCB defect detection approaches. Both quantitative and qualitative results confirm the superiority and practical applicability of the proposed framework for high-precision PCB surface inspection.

title: Analytical Modeling of Microstrip Antenna using Green House Analysis
authors: Rajesh Kumar Nema, Veena Upadhyay, Pramod Patel, Preeti Verma, Kavita Barahar, Amit Nema
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In this paper presents, green house analysis computational method for self inductance, positive and negative mutual inductance of the design antenna with presence and absent of the ground plane. Analyzed the effect of ground plane on geometrical inductance of the antenna. The computation has done in a with consideration of U type radiating patch. The proposed computation of geometry is done using negative mutual coupling approach. The geometrical structure has configured by ground plan, driven element and top layer U-Slot director. Optimization of the self inductance, positive and negative mutual inductance has been done by green house optimization technique After optimization, the optimum antenna design achieved and it has provided good operating impedance Bandwidth. The proposed parasitic array antenna has designed with the appropriate dimensions and can be used for different wireless communication. The green house analysis can be applied in an X Band, Ku Band and C-Band wireless communication.

title: Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Image Change Detection Based on DSAT and Decision-Level Fusion
authors: Sitong Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To address insufficient spatial-channel feature utilization, single-modal detection limitations, and cross-modal feature inconsistency in multi-modal remote sensing change detection, we propose DSAT-IODL, integrating improved Transformer feature extraction with intelligent decision-level fusion. DSAT employs dual spatial-channel attention to enhance small-scale change detection, while IODL adopts GAPSO hybrid optimization for adaptive multi-modal fusion via uncertainty quantification. On OSCD and WHU-OPT-SARCD datasets, DSAT achieves 89.5% F1 in unimodal detection, further improved to 92.8% after IODL fusion.
