Compared with ChangeFormer and STANet, the proposed method reduces false alarm rate by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 3}-\mathbf{4. 7 {\%}}$</tex> and improves overall accuracy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 1}-\mathbf{3. 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>, demonstrating strong potential for monitoring military strategic targets such as airports and ports.

title: EV-ProveBox: A Dynamic, Renewable, and Provenance-Aware S-Box Framework for Secure Healthcare IoT Encryption
authors: Rahma Berchi, Lemia Louail, Sarra Cherbal, Ashraf Mashaleh, Amjad Gawanmeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: In healthcare IoT systems, encryption relies on substitution boxes (S-boxes) to ensure confidentiality and resistance against linear and differential attacks; however, existing S-box approaches are static, repeatedly reused, and lack version control, making them increasingly vulnerable to cryptanalysis and unsuitable for long-term medical data protection. To overcome this limitation, we propose EV-ProveBox, a hybrid framework that combines chaotic Secretary Bird Optimization Algorithm (SBOA) for lightweight edge-level S-box generation, Genetic Algorithm (GA) for cloud-level fitness refinement, and blockchain–IPFS for immutable storage, provenance logging, version tracking, and automatic linkage between encrypted medical records and the specific S-box used in encryption. Each S-box is evaluated using nonlinearity, differential uniformity, avalanche, and BIC metrics before being assigned a unique IPFS hash and registered on a blockchain smart contract. Experimental results demonstrate that EV-ProveBox produces high-performing S-boxes with non-linearity values of 114–116 and differential uniformity between 8–10, while reducing cryptanalytic exposure through controlled S-box rotation. By transforming S-boxes from static components into traceable and adaptable security assets, EV-ProveBox enhances encryption resilience and supports forensic auditability through a blockchain-based lifecycle framework.

title: Saturated fault-tolerant control for uncertain switched systems with actuator faults*
authors: Kenza Telbissi, Abdoualaziz Benbraim, Abdelmounaim Khallouk
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper tackles the challenge of designing a robust fault-tolerant control strategy for switching systems with time-varying delays and uncertainties. Delay-dependent stability conditions are established by constructing an appropriate Lyapunov function for arbitrary switched systems. Leveraging these results, new criteria are developed to ensure the asymptotic stability of the closed-loop system, accounting for the effects of uncertainty and the presence of input saturation. To conclude, numerical simulation is proposed to prove the benefits of the given strategy. A comparison with passive fault-tolerant control shows that our strategy can detect, localize, and reconfigure actuator faults online, ensuring the convergence of performance requirements.

title: The Impact of AI Usage on Digital Leadership: Analyzing Direct and Indirect Effects through Technological Self-Efficacy
authors: Angle Lia, Kelly Falencia Irawan, Anasthasia Aurelia Dharma, Maria Grace Herlina
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The fast development of artificial intelligence (AI) has changed the way organizations work and leadership in the modern world, thus raising the question of how the use of AI impacts leadership effectiveness. This research aims to uncover not only the direct impacts of AI on digital leadership but also its indirect impacts, with belief in one’s own technological ability serving as a mediator. In line with the Technology Acceptance Model and Social Cognitive Theory, the research has tested four hypotheses using quantitative survey data from employees and leaders of organizations implementing AI in Indonesia. The data was analyzed using PLS-SEM. The findings reveal that AI is a powerful driver of digital leadership and, at the same time, increases technology-related self-efficacy, which further strengthens digital leadership capabilities. The belief in one’s own technological ability only partially mediates the relationship between AI and digital leadership. Therefore, it is the main factor in implementing AI as a tool for effective digital leadership when one is confident in their use of technology. These discoveries contribute to technology-enabled leadership literature while also indicating that leaders must be equipped with proper digital training, strategically implement AI, and develop their self-efficacy to handle the digital transformation era.

title: CAP-FL: A Context-Aware Personalized Federated Learning Framework for Next-Word Prediction
authors: S. Ramya, Swathi K, Thamil Vasanth G K, Tharani G
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In modern smart text interfaces, Next-Word Prediction (NWP) processes play an important part. However, the traditional approach to training such systems was done in a centralized location, which has created serious concerns related to user privacy and system scalability. Federated Learning system scalability. Federated Learning (FL) is a new technology that solves these challenges by allowing people to work together on training models to create new smart text interfaces without ever sharing raw data. While FL improves privacy preservation, conventional FL operations are not yet effective when working with Non-Independent and Identically Distributed (Non-IID) client datasets. The Context-Aware and Personalized Federated Learning (CAP-FL) process presented in this paper is an innovative concept for developing a personalized next-word prediction model based on GPT-2 inside a federated structure. It employs a two-tiered personalization process that includes: (1) Explicit Feature-Based Clustering, which groups clients with the same text style or genre, and (2) Context Token Injection (CTI), which prepends structured metadata tokens (e.g., play, genre, scene, and character) before the input sequences to enhance contextual conditioning. The experimental results obtained from testing with the Shakespeare Non-IID dataset demonstrate that CAP-FL is superior to existing standard FL algorithms such as FedAvg and FedProx, achieving lower cross-entropy loss, reduced perplexity, and higher prediction accuracy. In conclusion, CAP-FL is scalable, interpretable, and privacy-preserving, while supporting personalized text generation in federated environments. Future work will explore adaptive parameter tuning and parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods such as LoRA to further reduce communication overhead.

title: Transient Synchronization Stability Analysis of a Grid-connected VSC Based on Black-box Model
authors: Bixing Ren, Dajiang Wang, Jianhui Meng, Zheng Li, Dandan Zhu, Wenbo Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Transient synchronization instability is a critical issue affecting the secure grid integration of renewable energy. The control models and parameters of actual converters are often unknown, making it difficult to effectively assess transient synchronization stability. To address this, this paper investigates the transient synchronization stability assessment of converters under black-box model conditions. First, model of the grid-connected converter retaining the PLL dynamics is established, and the key factors influencing transient synchronization stability are identified from the perspective of the equal area criterion. Subsequently, a key parameter identification method based on disturbance testing is proposed, which, combined with the equal area criterion, enables the assessment of converter transient synchronization stability under black-box conditions. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through case studies.

title: Cost-Sensitive Machine Learning for Cloud Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Louay Karadsheh, Faten Hamad, Hussam Fakhouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Detecting malicious activity in cloud networks is a critical defensive task for security operations centres (SOCs). In practice, intrusion detection systems must achieve strong detection performance while keeping false alarms low enough to remain operationally actionable, since high alert volumes can overwhelm analysts and delay response. This paper presents a fully reproducible machine–learning pipeline for intrusion detection using the NSL-KDD benchmark dataset, comparing baseline classifiers—logistic regression, random forests, support vector machines, gradient boosting, and multi-layer perceptrons—under repeated stratified cross-validation with consistent preprocessing and hyperparameter handling. Evaluation goes beyond accuracy and includes metrics suited to imbalanced detection tasks, including precision, recall, F1-score, Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC), ROC-AUC, and PR-AUC. Beyond fixed decision thresholds, we introduce a threshold-optimised (and optionally cost-sensitive) decision rule for logistic regression that selects an operating point to explicitly trade detection against false alarms. Experiments show that a random forest achieves the strongest overall performance (highest F1-score and MCC), while threshold optimisation meaningfully improves the precision–recall balance of logistic regression, illustrating a practical mechanism for adapting simpler models to SOC alerting constraints. All code, figures, and LaTeX-ready tables are released to enable end-to-end reproducibility.

title: Securing Identities Without Cryptography: A Survey of Alternative Authentication Mechanisms
authors: Gajendra Singh Rajput, Harsh Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Authentication is now an essential part of contemporary. As the main protective measure against unauthorized, there is cyberspace security. access and identity fraud. Whereas conventional cryptography. authentication mechanisms have been the order of the day. challenges such as high computational have arisen over the decades. expenses, quantum computing threat vulnerability, and complex. key management requirements-have inspired researchers to find alternatives to cryptographic authentication. This survey thoroughly evaluates other authentication systems. that do not use classical cryptographic only. protocols. We analyze physical layer authentication. techniques, behavioral-based authentication methods, AI and authentication systems based on machine learning, and heuristic. rule-based methods. We offer a comparison analysis assessing effectiveness of security, computer efficiency, usability, and vulnerability to different forms of attack. Last but not least, we mention security and privacy problems that are inherent to non-cryptographic schemes and describe future research directions that are fundamental in enhancing identity. authentication in a more and more connected world.

title: Data Replication and Protection Mechanism for Secure Distributed Cloud Databases
authors: Divyaraj Singh Jatav, Chandra Shekar Reddy Avula
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Replication of secure data among distributed cloud databases is a significant obstacle because of the high latency and high bandwidth consumption as well as security vulnerability. This paper introduces a data replication and protection framework based on Qwen TextCNN to recognize data patterns, an improved Barnacles mating-based optimization (EBMO) to optimize the placement of replicas and double deep Q-network (DDQN) to make an adaptive decision-making in the changing cloud environment. The methodology uses classical and quantum simulation platforms, such as Qiskit Aer and Microsoft Quantum simulators, and AWS EC2 based classical network tests. The data has 100 runs in each configuration with controlled quantum noise and network delays and averaged results are provided with 95% confidence intervals. Findings demonstrate the decreasing replication latency and bandwidth usage with high data consistency and security of distributed nodes. The results reveal that, the suggested framework has the capability of enhancing the performance of data replication within cloud.

title: Interference-Induced Acoustic Transparency in Hybrid Resonator System for Selective and HighSensitivity Gas Detection
authors: Maroua Dahmani, Ali Khettabi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The detection of toxic and flammable gases is crucial for environmental monitoring, industrial safety, and public health. Beyond conventional mechanical and electrochemical sensors, wave-based approaches have gained increasing attention due to their high sensitivity and non-invasive operation. In this work, we propose a hybrid acoustic filtering and sensing platform based on a simple, non-periodic structure that integrates Helmholtz resonators and resonant cavities operating under open and closed boundary conditions. The sensing mechanism relies on interference-induced phenomena, namely Acoustic Induced Transparency (AIT), generating ultra-narrow transmission peaks with high quality factors. The acoustic response in pure air reveals an inherent frequencyfiltering capability, enabling highly selective spectral transmission. The introduction of different gases, carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), methane (CH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>), and Nitrogen gas (N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) induces distinct and measurable shifts in the resonance features, demonstrating strong sensitivity to changes in the surrounding medium. These results highlight the proposed architecture as a compact, high-performance acoustic biosensor for multi-gas detection.

title: Small Models, Big Insights: Compact Vision– Language Intelligence for Biomedical Imaging
authors: Himanshi Kirar, Vibha Tiwari, Sunil Shukla
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: An automatic summarization of biomedical images into clinically relevant text is critical in maintaining the efficiency of radiology workflow. Early template-based and CNN-RNN models dominated from 2015--2020, but suffered from limited reasoning and high hallucination rates. Since 2021, large vision-language models (7B-562B parameters) achieved major leaps using contrastive pre-training and instruction tuning. However, their size prevents real-world clinical deployment. This survey focuses on the rapidly emerging family of lightweight biomedical VLMs (less than 350M parameters) proposed between 2023-2025. We trace the technical evolution from classical to lightweight models, categorize current approaches into knowledge-distilled, parameter-efficient, and natively lightweight designs. We detail evolution, superiority, Bio NLP contributions, and future directions.

title: TGN-IDS: Detecting Lateral Movement with Temporal Graph Neural Networks
authors: Mohammad Emad Arafah, Dena Abu Laila, Israa Abuzaid, Ahmad Aladawi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Lateral movement attack is a serious threat in the modern cybersecurity world where cybercriminals intrude into compromised networks in search of high-value targets. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are not always able to extract the complexity in temporal and structural properties of such sophisticated attacks. This paper introduces a Temporal Graph Neural Network-based Intrusion Detection System (TGN-IDS) that uses dynamic temporal graphs to model network traffic in order to identify lateral movement and advanced persistent threats. Our approach creates time-windowed graphs by means of the network flow data, where the nodes represent hosts and the edges encode communication patterns which has a temporal feature. We use chronological attack signatures (temporal attention) by employing the GraphSAGE architecture. Extensive evaluation on a set of datasets from Cross-Site Encounter (CSE-CIC-IDS2018) and Indicators of Compromised Systems (CICIDS2017), TGN-IDS has a detection accuracy of 97.3% for infiltration attacks and 95.8% for botnet activities, providing detection accuracy 2.8% better than the best baseline with a false positive rate of 97%. The temporal graph representation allows successful identification of multi-stage attack patterns such as propagation of port scanning, command and control structures and stealthy lateral movement sequences, which evade conventional detection mechanisms.

title: Real-Time Vision-based Vehicle Speed Estimation using YOLOv8 and Enhanced Deep SORT
authors: P. Dhanalakshmi, S.Lakshmi Narayana Reddy, Shaik Baji, S.Mohammed Saleem, T.Chethak Sai Kumar, G. Sreevalli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: In this paper, a real-time vision-based vehicle speed estimation will be proposed to combine the effective use of YOLOv8 to detect vehicles and a superior Deep SORT tracking system to maintain the identity of vehicles. A single-stage detector called YOLOv8 can be used to perform the localization of objects with a more accurate result and with lower inference latency thereby being used in areas where real-time traffic monitoring is required. The Deep SORT algorithm is made more efficient to track vehicles in all the frames by attaching motion prediction and appearance-based embeddings to ensure that the same vehicle is tracked in different frames. A distance-time formulation computed vehicle speed based on tracked trajectories is used to identify overspeed and then vehicles are detected. Using experimental results, the proposed framework is proved to have high detection accuracy and much faster inference speed than traditional two-stage detectors.

title: Subgroup and Longitudinal Measurement Invariance of the Computational Thinking Test Short (CTt-S)
authors: Joseph C. Tise, Monica McGill
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Conference on Research on Equity and Sustained Participation in Engineering, Computing, and Technology
abstract: None

title: Multi-Sensor Information Fusion for Indoor Positioning Based on UWB and IMU with Deep Learning Optimization
authors: Enning Cao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Accurate indoor positioning is essential for applications such as smart buildings, industrial asset tracking, emergency rescue, and robot navigation. Ultra-Wideband (UWB) technology provides centimeter-level ranging accuracy but suffers from non-line-of-sight (NLOS) interference and signal occlusion in complex indoor environments. Inertial Measurement Units (IMU) offer continuous motion estimation but are prone to cumulative drift over time. To overcome the limitations of individual sensors, this paper proposes a multi-sensor information fusion method for indoor positioning that integrates UWB ranging data and IMU inertial measurements through a deep learning-optimized framework. First, a Transformer-based NLOS identification and mitigation module is designed to detect and correct UWB ranging errors caused by obstructions. Second, a Bidirectional Long ShortTerm Memory (BiLSTM) network is employed to model the temporal dynamics of IMU data and predict pedestrian displacement with reduced drift. Third, an Adaptive Attention Fusion Network (AAFN) is proposed to dynamically integrate the corrected UWB positions and IMU-predicted trajectories based on real-time reliability estimation. Experimental results conducted in a $1200 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$ multi-room indoor environment demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mean positioning error of 0.23 m, representing a $41.0 \%$ improvement over standalone UWB and a $67.1 \%$ improvement over IMU-only dead reckoning, while maintaining robust performance under severe NLOS conditions.

title: Sarcasm detection enhancement through a perspectivist features approach
authors: Ibtissam Touahri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Sarcasm that uses positive language to convey a negative sentiment, criticism or disapproval is among the most challenging NLP tasks. This mismatch between form and intent forces models to rely on contextual, speaker expectations, and often cultural cues rather than literal sentiment. In this paper, we aim to detect sarcastic samples among those of the ArSarcasm dataset. The dataset covers more than $\mathbf{1 0, 5 4 7}$ samples annotated at sarcasm, sentiment and dialect levels. The annotators used True and False tags to determine the presence and absence of sarcasm within a sample. For sentiment, they have employed positive, negative and neutral labels. The dialect label covers several varieties such as MSA, Egyptian, Levantine, Gulf, and Maghrebi dialects. We have performed several experiments using a combination of a transformer-based approach with sarcastic features, leveraging both linguistic cues and deep contextual representations to more effectively identify sarcastic expressions. Our system is based on AraBERT enhanced with GANs, then we have altered the pretrained model AraBERT vector by adding characteristics to it. GANs are used to improve the distinction between various classes based on an adversarial network. Whereas, the perspectivist sentiment labels help in detecting the presence of mixed sentiment which may be a sarcasm indicator. The dialect, in turn, may be an indicator of the dominant sentiment within a specific dialect. The obtained results were pertinent; However, since the studied corpus consists of highly underrepresented sarcastic samples, we investigated the effect of the combination of conflicting sentiment labels as well as dialect. The obtained results are promising since we have reached an accuracy of 88% and a sarcastic $\mathbf{F}$-measure of 0.64, which outperforms the state of the art on the same dataset by 11 and 2 points, respectively.

title: Integrating AI Technologies in Higher Education in the Arab Region: A Systematic Synthesis and Implementation Roadmap
authors: Mohammad A. Al-Shunnag, Ali Al Maqousi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI), especially large language models (LLMs), is rapidly reshaping higher education through scalable writing support, tutoring, and feedback generation. Yet integration in humanities and social sciences (HSS) remains uneven—particularly in Arabic-speaking contexts where language resources, governance capacity, and instructor AI literacy are still emerging. This paper is conceptual: it synthesizes the current state of the art in AI in higher education (AIEd), with emphasis on LLM-enabled assessment and feedback, learning analytics (LA) adoption, Arabic-language readiness, and educator competency frameworks. Based on this synthesis, we propose ALIGNED-HSS (Alignment–Localization–Integrity–Governance–Evidence), a multi-layer framework that links pedagogical intentions, assessment redesign, Arabic localization, data/analytics infrastructure, and responsible governance with capacity building and continuous evaluation. We translate state-of-the-art findings into an actionable roadmap for Arab-region HSS faculties and conclude with a research agenda for empirical validation via design-based and cross-institutional studies.

title: An Intelligent Vehicle Target Detection Method Based on LiDAR
authors: Yikai Ding, Shoubiao Tan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of autonomous driving technology, Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) has become an indispensable sensor for environmental perception due to its ability to provide high-precision 3D point cloud data independent of lighting conditions. This paper proposes an improved LiDARbased target detection method that integrates Adaptive Scale Pillars (ASP) and Correlative Attention (CA) mechanisms to address the challenges of low detection accuracy for small objects and poor robustness in complex weather. The ASP module dynamically adjusts pillar sizes based on target distances, while the CA module enhances feature associations within point cloud pillars. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a 5.8% improvement in mean Average Precision (mAP) compared to the baseline PointPillars algorithm, with a real-time inference speed of 22 frames per second (fps). The method also exhibits superior performance in adverse weather conditions, providing reliable environmental perception support for intelligent vehicles.

title: Beyond Three Doors: A Comprehensive Analysis of Generalized Monty Hall Problem
authors: ElMehdi Benmassoud, Salem Nafiri, Noureddine Semane, Abdelhak Elidrissi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper develops a unified probabilistic framework for the generalized Monty Hall problem with n doors and m prizes, explicitly incorporating the role of host information. The proposed ($n, m, k, r$) model describes scenarios in which the host opens k doors revealing r prizes, enabling a direct comparison between informed and uninformed strategies. Closed-form expressions for the winning probabilities of switching and staying are derived. In the informed-host case, switching is advantageous if and only if $n\lt \frac{m k}{r}$, while in the uninformed case, conditioning on the observed reveal preserves symmetry, making both strategies equivalent. These results unify several classical and extended formulations within a single framework. Monte Carlo simulations corroborate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate how optimal strategies vary with system parameters, providing further insight into decision-making under uncertainty.

title: Impregnation Process Parameters Tuning by Computational Fluid Dynamics Simulations
authors: Ali Sadeghianjahromi, Yaohui Gai, Juliette Soulard
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Efficient thermal management of traction motors is critical for enhancing the performance and reliability of hybrid and electric vehicles. One key factor is the quality of stator winding impregnation, which affects both heat dissipation and mechanical integrity. This study investigates the trickle impregnation process using a combined experimental and numerical approach. Three-dimensional multiphase Computational Fluid Dynamics simulations, using the Volume of Fluid model, were developed to model resin flow into the small gaps between stator windings and slot liners. Experiments were conducted to determine the heat transfer coefficient on the stator surface, which was found to be 3 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>·K and used as a boundary condition in the simulations. Parametric studies examined the effects of capillary force, stator material (laminated steel, solid steel, and aluminum), and temperature control. Results highlight the critical role of capillary action in resin distribution, showing improved process modelling accuracy. Trickle impregnation was influenced by stator material thermal properties and significantly improved when the stator temperature was kept constant. The findings confirm that numerical simulations can reliably predict the influence of the studied parameters on the trickle impregnation, offering a cost-effective alternative to extensive experimental during process parameter tuning.

title: A Two-Stage Modular Speech-to-Language Recognition Framework for Low-Resource Tibetan
authors: Zhenye Gan, Fengdi Li, Xiaolong Ren
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: To address the challenges faced by low-resource languages such as Tibetan in automatic speech recognition (ASR)—including a scarcity of labeled data, difficulties in aligning speech-to-text mappings, and the lack of deep semantic reasoning capabilities in traditional purely acoustic models—this paper proposes a two-stage modular speech-to-language recognition framework tailored for Tibetan. The framework employs a pre-trained Whisper model as the acoustic encoder and a deep incremental pre-trained large language model tailored for Tibetan as the language decoder, while introducing a query Transformer (Q-Former) as a cross-modal information bottleneck. To address the tendency of multimodal joint optimization to collapse under resource-constrained conditions, this paper adopts a two-stage incremental training strategy that combines "modal alignment pre-training" with "generative instruction fine-tuning." By integrating this approach with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), the large language model is enabled to precisely follow speech transcription instructions with minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, we extensively customized the SpecAugment data augmentation algorithm by introducing dynamic local mean masking and temporal scaling constraints, significantly enhancing the model’s robustness in resource-constrained environments. Experiments on a self-built 41,078-sentence mixed Ü-Tsang dialect dataset demonstrate that our model achieves a character error rate (CER) of 11.96%, representing a significant performance leap over the parameter-efficiently fine-tuned traditional Whisper baseline.

title: Improving Lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks Performance for Colorectal Cancer Diagnosis via Knowledge Distillation
authors: Sümeyye Karatekin, Enes Ayan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the most common and deadly types of cancer worldwide, and early diagnosis significantly improves treatment success. Computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems are gaining increasing importance in supporting physicians in endoscopic image analysis. However, large deep learning models require significant computational resources, which limit their use in computationally limited clinical environments. This study proposes a teacher-student architecture based on Knowledge Distillation to improve the classification performance of lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) models on endoscopic images. Twenty-one CNN models were trained using transfer learning and fine-tuning methods on the Kvasir v2 dataset. EfficientNetV2L, ResNet152V2, EfficientNetV2M, and EfficientNetB2 were selected as teacher models, while EfficientNetB1, MobileNetV2, and NASNetMobile were determined as student models. Knowledge distillation parameters were optimized to effectively transfer knowledge from teacher models to student models. The results showed that EfficientNetB1 achieved the highest F1-Score under all teacher models, reaching 0.9290 (1.42% improvement over baseline) when trained with EfficientNetV2L. MobileNetV2 and NASNetMobile also demonstrated notable F1-Score improvements of 4.46% and 19.34%, respectively, particularly with the EfficientNetV2M teacher model. These findings demonstrate that knowledge distillation enables lightweight models to achieve competitive performance while maintaining computational efficiency, making them suitable for use in clinical applications with limited resources.

title: Real-Time Food Products Nutrition Analysis Using Deep Learning
authors: G. Visalaxi, Yoganandhan M, Yogeshwaran S, Tharun Kaarthik G
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The growing burden of the lifestyle-related diseases like obesity, diabetes and cardiovascular disorders, highlight the growing necessity of the availability of tools that will help to promote nutrition awareness. The manual methods of assessing the nutrient content are usually inefficient, subjective and do not lend themselves to real-time decision making. The paper discusses a deep-learning-based system that can automatically detect food items and give real-time nutritional information of the food item through computer vision. The framework suggested incorporates the YOLO object detector algorithm in conjunction with a specific Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier to improve the level of recognition of various food genres. The system accepts the inputs of static images, pre-record video streams, and IP-based webcams which provides flexibility of application in any situation that is happening in the real world. When a food item is identified, the system looks up its relevant nutritional characteristics such as calories, carbohydrates, proteins and fats in a structured database using standardized dietary references. The values are then presented in an intuitive visual overlay that allows users to decipher nutritional values easy. Through experimental analysis, it was shown that the model can be highly detected and can perform in real-time even in a challenging background or lighting conditions. This flexibility of the system places it in the smart kitchens, dietary monitoring systems, restaurants, fitness areas, and healthcare systems. This work can showcase the potential of artificial intelligence in making the process of nutrition tracking easier and helping people choose healthier lifestyle options by integrating advanced object detection, effective classification, and user-friendly visualization interface. The results demonstrate that automated systems of food-recognition can greatly enhance their dietary consciousness and help address the inconvenience of health-related conditions.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization for Privacy-Preserving Federated Threat Intelligence: A Pareto-Based Approach
authors: Denzel Prince, Jeremy Blackstone, Appolo Tankeh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The deployment of federated learning in cybersecurity applications introduces an inherent tension between detection accuracy, privacy preservation, and communication efficiency. Traditional approaches treat these objectives independently, often sacrificing one for the benefit of another. This paper presents a multi-objective optimization framework for the Optimized Federated Privacy Framework (OPTIFED) system, employing Pareto front analysis to systematically characterize the trade-offs between detection performance, differential privacy guarantees, and bandwidth utilization. The study conducts 120 federated aggregation experiments across 12 privacy budget configurations ($\varepsilon \in[0.5,15.0]$) with $\delta=10^{-5}$, aggregating threat intelligence from 11 distributed client nodes. The results demonstrate that 75% of Pareto-optimal configurations achieve both high detection accuracy (F1 > 0.6) and strong privacy preservation (Privacy Score > 0.5), with communication efficiency remaining stable across all configurations ($\mu=0.845, \sigma=0.054$). The analysis reveals that privacy budgets in the range $\varepsilon \in[2.0,3.0]$ provide optimal balance, achieving F1 scores of approximately 0.82 while maintaining privacy scores exceeding 0.64-a configuration suitable for enterprise threat intelligence sharing where both detection capability and regulatory compliance are required.

title: Design of A FCRD9T SRAM cell for Low-Power Wireless Communications
authors: Ashish Sachdeva, Priyanka Bansal, Deepak Kumar, Deepika Bansal, Vikas Malhotra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: In this work, an innovative nine-transistor-based feedback-cutting read-decoupled (FCRD9T) SRAM bit-cell is analyzed. This design is targeted for the low-power cellular architectures. The analyzed design present 1.17× improvement in write static noise margin and 2.54× reduction in leakage power. Also, the presented design reduces the read power and write power dissipation by 1.32×, and 2.59×, respectively. Moreover, the design shows 1.99× improvement in read static noise margin comparing to the 6T SRAM bit-cell design. This design achieves a 1.18× reduction in read access time. However, the write access time is increased by 1.27×. Overall, the proposed design offers significant advancements in power efficiency and performance important for a promising solution for low-power cellular systems.

title: An Effectiveness Evaluation Method for Spaceborne Calibration Network Based on the Optimal Theory
authors: Jiaxin Zou, Weize Sun, Mingwei Xie, Guitong Chen, Lei Huang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To address the challenges in evaluating spaceborne calibration network performance, this paper proposes an optimality-driven assessment methodology. Initially, an evaluation framework is established, focusing on three core metrics: the maximum number of calibratable events, the average duration per calibration event, and the uniformity of network calibration. Subsequently, metric data for multiple constellation configurations are derived using an integrated STK/MATLAB simulation environment. The optimal solution set is identified through Pareto non-dominated sorting and crowding distance analysis. A quantitative assessment model is then developed, correlating individual constellation metrics with overall performance via linear fitting and dynamic weight adjustment strategies. Finally, simulation experiments utilizing the Gaofen-3 and Haiyang-2 satellite series as calibration targets validate the methodology's effectiveness, providing both theoretical foundations and data support for constellation design and optimization.

title: Artificial Immune System–Based Anomaly Detection for IoT Network Security
authors: Salam Al-E’mari, Yousef Sanjalawe, Fadi Fataftah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has significantly increased the attack surface of modern networks, making efficient and adaptive intrusion detection a critical requirement. Most existing IoT intrusion detection systems rely on supervised machine learning or deep learning models that require large volumes of labeled attack data and high computational resources, which limits their ability to detect zero-day attacks and operate in resource-constrained environments. In this article, an Artificial Immune System (AIS)-based anomaly detection scheme for protecting the security of IoT networks is introduced, using the practical dataset IoT-23. The novel scheme will define normal behavior of the IoT network using normal traffic data by employing Negative Selection, Immune Clone Selection, and the capabilities of immune memory to reflect anomalous behavior as an attack on the network’s self-profile. Flow-level statistical attributes will be extracted from Zeek log files after preprocessing, with variance-based features applied to improve efficiency. Performance evaluation on the IoT-23 dataset indicates that the novel scheme, which leverages AIS capabilities, achieves a competitive accuracy of 97.9% and an F1 Score of 97% without supervision. Additionally, the scheme places less emphasis on computationally intensive computations, offering greater adaptability to novel attacks without the use of supervision or deep learning techniques, making it most effective for real-world use.

title: Cost-Constrained Real-Time Optimization of Multi-Chip Interconnection Architecture for Power Load Forecasting
authors: Rongzi Cong, Yaoyang Guo, Hao Men, Junxiang Bu, Ming Li, Xuanzhi Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Power load forecasting is a critical function for intelligent power system dispatch, demand response, and stability control. With the growing complexity of data-driven forecasting models, single-chip platforms often cannot meet strict real-time execution requirements under power grid dispatching deadlines. Multi-chip parallel architectures offer scalable computing capability, yet they introduce additional inter-chip communication overhead and hardware deployment costs. To address this issue, this paper proposes a cost-constrained real-time optimization framework for multi-chip interconnection architectures dedicated to power load forecasting.First, a computation-communication coupled latency model is established to capture the total execution time of parallel forecasting tasks, including computational delay, synchronization latency, and data transmission overhead. A hardware cost model is further constructed to quantify chip deployment and interconnection expenses. On this basis, the hardware configuration problem is formulated as a constrained optimization issue, which aims to minimize the total system cost while satisfying the 1-second real-time deadline of power dispatching.Numerical simulation results show that under typical power edge parameters, the optimal chip scale is determined to be 26 chips with a minimum total cost of 5072.54. Comparative analysis verifies that 16 chips fail to meet the real-time constraint, while 32 chips cause 26.7% extra cost with negligible performance gain. In addition, increasing interconnection bandwidth from 10 GB/s to 200 GB/s reduces the minimum chip requirement by 78.7%, with diminishing returns beyond 100 GB/s.The proposed framework reveals the quantitative trade-off among workload scale, chip performance, interconnection bandwidth, and system cost, and provides clear engineering guidance for cost-efficient and real-time guaranteed hardware configuration in intelligent power load forecasting platforms.

title: An Optimized Deep Learning-based Lesion Segmentation Model for Early Gastric Cancer Diagnosis Using Pre-processed Gastroscopic Images
authors: Sourabh Budhiraja, Neera Batra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Gastric cancer (GC) is one of the most prevalent and fatal types of cancer worldwide, and the later it is detected, the lower chances of survival. Therefore, early diagnosis is crucial for improving clinical outcomes and enabling less invasive treatment plans. This paper presents an advanced deep learning (DL) architecture capable of identifying and localizing lesions in gastroscopic images for the diagnosis of early gastric cancer (EGC). The study integrates state-of-the-art feature extraction (FE) methods with a simplified feature selection process to enhance diagnostic precision and effectiveness. The publicly available Kvasir-SEG dataset was used, and the input images were preprocessed through resizing, filtering, and noise reduction to improve image quality. The annotated images were then detected and segmented using the trained and validated optimized DL model. The results of the comparative evaluation revealed that the proposed method outperformed existing methods in terms of key performance metrics, such as precision, accuracy, and the Dice coefficient. These results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively outline lesion boundaries and distinguish between pathological and normal gastric tissues. The findings indicate that the optimized DL model is a useful and reliable architecture for identifying early gastric cancer and can be successfully applied in real-time clinical practice and decision support in gastroenterology.

title: Ocular Disease Classification and Diabetic Retinopathy Detection using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Malti Rani, Amit Kumar Goyal, Sonali Dash
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A severe complication of diabetes that leads to permanently vision loss and early blindness give rise to the evolution of an ocular disease called as diabetic retinopathy. The major root cause of diabetic retinopathy is prolonged hyperglycemia that generally weakens the tiny retinal capillaries. The poor tiny retinal capillaries give rise to the development of microaneurysms and can completely block the flow of blood in the retinal vessels. This damaged retinal blood vessels induce the eruption of vascular endothelial growth factor VEGF which leads to the development of new fragile retinal blood vessels known as neovascularization. For this ocular diagnosing, fundus images play a crucial role. This paper represents a deep learning approach used for classification of diabetic retinopathy and normal fundus images from ODIR dataset integrating them with homomorphic filtering. Two convolutional neural network architecture, MobileNetV2 and VGG16 are hyper-tuned for image classification and disease detection. The final outcomes revealed better feature extraction and improved classification performance as the application of homomorphic filtering leads to enhanced contrast and illumination uniformly over the fundus images.

title: Constraint-Aware Hybrid Metaheuristic Optimization for Joint IDS Placement and Key-Refresh Scheduling in Resource-Constrained IoT Edge Networks
authors: Hasan Kanaker, Samer A. B. Awwad
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Security controls in Internet-of-Things (IoT) deployments must operate under tight energy, memory, and latency budgets, making how and where security is enforced an optimization problem rather than a fixed design choice. This paper formulates a joint IoT security optimization that selects (i) the placement of lightweight intrusion detection system (IDS) agents on edge nodes and (ii) per-device cryptographic key-refresh periods, while assigning devices to deployed IDS agents under capacity and distance constraints. The resulting mixed-integer nonlinear program is NP-hard due to combinatorial placement/assignment and nonlinear risk/overhead terms. To solve it, we propose a constraint-aware hybrid optimizer (CHPDE) that couples binary particle swarm optimization (BPSO) for placement with differential evolution (DE) operators for continuous key-refresh variables and a repair mechanism for feasibility. Using a synthetic IoT-edge benchmark generator, we provide theoretical performance tables and plots comparing genetic algorithm (GA), PSO, DE, and grey wolf optimizer (GWO) against CHPDE. Theoretical results indicate that CHPDE converges faster and achieves lower objective values with improved security-risk reduction under the same compute budget.

title: Optimizing Cloud Resource Allocation with Predictive Big Data Models
authors: Mohammad Al Khaldy, Amjad Aldweesh, Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cloud platforms increasingly host mixtures of latency-sensitive microservices and data-intensive analytics whose workloads vary at time scales comparable to autoscaler actuation delays. Reactive scaling policies therefore operate with inherent lag, leading to transient service-level objective (SLO) violations during demand surges and unnecessary overprovisioning when demand drops. Predictive autoscaling can reduce this lag, but many deployed and published approaches either (i) rely on point forecasts integrated through heuristic rules, or (ii) solve cost minimization without explicit treatment of forecast uncertainty and SLO risk. This paper presents Predictive Big-Data Model Resource Allocation (PBDMRA), a cloud-native control stack that couples large-scale telemetry processing with probabilistic forecasting, lightweight uncertainty calibration, and risk-bounded allocation. PBDMRA ingests metrics and traces (and can incorporate structured logs when available), constructs features using stream processing, produces multi-horizon predictive quantiles, calibrates empirical coverage via rolling residual adjustments, and computes scaling actions by solving a constrained optimization problem that trades cost against SLO risk and scaling churn. Trace-driven replay across microservice, streaming, and batch workloads indicates that PBDMRA reduces SLO violation minutes by 33-55relative to Kubernetes HPA and a point-forecast baseline, while also reducing scaling actions.

title: Design of Circularly Polarized Antenna Arrays on Different Substrates for Rectenna Integration
authors: Ayoub Elmai, Jamal Zbitou, Noha Chahboun, Stéphane Ginestar, Mohammed El Gibari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance evaluation of a circularly polarized antenna array intended for rectenna integration. A slot antenna element is first developed and optimized to achieve circular polarization with an axial ratio (AR) below 3 dB in the 5.8 GHz ISM band. To enhance the gain and improve power reception, a $2 \times 2$ antenna array configuration is then implemented. The effect of the substrate choice on antenna performance is investigated by considering three different materials: Rogers RO4003C ($\varepsilon_{r}=3.38, \tan \delta= \mathbf{0. 0 0 2 7}$, thickness $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1. 5 2 ~ m m}$), RT/duroid $\mathbf{5 8 8 0}$ (RT1) with 1.02 mm thickness $\left(\varepsilon_{r}=2.2, \tan \delta=0.0009\right)$, and RT/duroid 5880 (RT2) with 1.57 mm thickness $\left(\varepsilon_{r}=2.2, \tan \delta=0.0009\right)$. Full-wave simulations are carried out to analyze key parameters, including reflection coefficient (S11), axial ratio bandwidth, and peak gain. The results demonstrate that the substrate properties strongly affect impedance matching, circular polarization bandwidth, and radiation efficiency. Comparative analysis highlights the trade-offs between low dielectric loss and physical thickness, providing useful guidelines for substrate selection in rectenna applications. The proposed design shows promising characteristics for efficient wireless power transfer and energy harvesting systems.

title: Moroccan License Plate Detection for Intelligent Transportation Systems Using YOLOv8 and YOLOv11
authors: Doha El Kalef, Mobarak Zourhri, Abdeljalil El-Ibrahimi, Bachir Benhala, Cherradi Bouchaib, Hassan Silkan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) has become a critical component in intelligent transportation systems, urban surveillance, and access control. In this paper, we investigate the performance of two recent deep learning object detectors YOLOv8 and YOLOv11 for the task of realtime Moroccan license plate detection. The unique characteristics of Moroccan plates, which include multi-script content (Arabic and Western numerals), varied layouts, and inconsistent fonts, present specific challenges not commonly addressed in standard ALPR datasets. To evaluate model robustness under these regional constraints, we construct a custom dataset of annotated Moroccan vehicle images collected under diverse lighting and environmental conditions. Both YOLOv8 and YOLOv11 models are fine-tuned and benchmarked using standard object detection metrics such as mAP, precision, recall, and inference speed. Our experimental results show that YOLOv11 slightly outperforms YOLOv8 in detection accuracy while maintaining comparable real-time performance. This study highlights the potential of modern YOLO architectures for localized ALPR applications and provides a foundation for future research in multilingual and region-specific plate recognition systems.

title: Breast Cancer Classification in Ultrasound Images Using DenseNet169 and Advanced Transfer Learning
authors: Venkata Ramana S, Medi Pavani, Meesa Sreeja, Medhari Sharanya, MD Samreen, Lenkapally Sowmika
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Breast cancer is still a major health concern worldwide, and the development of reliable early detection methods is essential in order to improve the outcome of patients. We proposed a complete deep learning framework, which employs the state-of-the-art DenseNet169 architecture combined with an optimized transfer learning approach considering three scenarios for breast cancer classification on the Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) dataset. The proposed methodology employs a two-phase training system where in the first phase the last 50 layers are frozen and pre-trained weights act as feature extractors then, in the second phase, all layers are fine-tunned. Dense connectivity patterns and feature reuse capabilities of DenseNet169 for success on medical image analysis tasks collectively led our implementation to achieve 85.42% test accuracy on breast ultrasound images divided into normal, benign, and malignant classes. The model includes extensive data augmentation, adaptive learning rate scheduling using Reduce LR on Plateau, early stopping and batch normalization to improve performance and control for overfitting. Experimental results confirm that the densely connected convolutional networks have the potential to advance computer-aided diagnosis systems, presenting a robust breast cancer detection solution that could strongly supportclinical decision-making and improve mammography diagnostic accuracy in other ultrasound imaging settings.

title: Non-conventional hydrodynamic pad bearing bi-objective design optimization
authors: Ihssane Messoude, Samir Aghzer
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this study, the hydrodynamic behavior of a twodimensional non-conventional pad bearing, operating under steady-state, isothermal, and laminar conditions, and subjected to a slip condition on its stationary surface, is described using the modified Reynolds equation. The latter is discretized by means of the centered finite difference method and solved numerically using the iterative Gauss-Seidel scheme. A bi-objective optimization procedure, based on the modified Hooke and Jeeves algorithm, is then carried out to simultaneously maximize the load capacity and minimize the friction number. Three bi-objective optimization approaches are considered. The results show, on the one hand, that the modified Hooke and Jeeves algorithm is well suited to the optimization of non-conventional hydrodynamic pad bearings. On the other hand, they highlight the relevance of the utility function method when dealing with conflicting objectives, as the resulting trade-off curve allows the designer to select the compromise that best meets their design requirements.

title: Three-Vector MPCC-LADRC Cascade Control Strategy and Performance Analysis for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: Ye Gu, Chao Dong, Song Cai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are widely used in industrial servos, new energy vehicles and other fields due to high efficiency, high power density and fast dynamic response, and their control performance directly determines the stability and reliability of the drive system. To solve the problems of slow dynamic response, large torque ripple in the current loop and insufficient disturbance rejection ability of the speed loop in traditional PMSM control schemes, especially under load disturbances and parameter perturbations, this paper proposes a dual-loop cooperative control strategy combining three-vector model predictive current control (three-vector MPCC) and linear active disturbance rejection control (LADRC). The three-vector MPCC is applied in the current loop to reduce current harmonics and torque ripple while improving current tracking and dynamic response, and the LADRC adopted in the speed loop can estimate and compensate internal and external disturbances in real time via a linear extended state observer, resulting in strong disturbance rejection and robustness without requiring an accurate motor model.

title: The Convergence of AIML, Humanoid Robotics, and the Metaverse: A Framework for Next-Generation Consumer Engagement
authors: Pradeep Kumar Mishra, Mritunjay Kr. Ranjan, Ambrish Singh, Ronak Modi, Brahmmanand Sharma, Ankita Patil
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The digital transformation is evolving gradually, where artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), humanoid robots, and Metaverse are becoming equal partners to revolutionize marketing tactics and create a futuristic consumer experience. AI and ML are the technologies that throw marketers off the trail of the consumers, and still, these technologies give the marketers the clearest foresight of consumer behavior thus making customer experiences immediate and responsive. Machine learning improves the efficiencies of decisions, analyze large volumes of data and optimize advertisement placements, while AI-driven chatbots and virtual assistants are revolutionizing customer care by providing quick and tailored solutions. Humanoids, because of their human-like experience, are an entirely new dimension of personalization for brands to get better emotional engagement with consumers. Besides, with the help of Metaverse, the consumer can visit various brand spaces and interact with the selected products and brands in new real ways; for instance, through virtual shops or brand events, which, on the other hand, are presenting marketers with new ways of reaching out to the customer besides the physical world. This paper addresses these technologies and the impact of their interaction on consumers and illustrates how businesses can utilize these technologies for more effective personalized marketing and data assimilation. The amalgamation of AI and ML with humanoid robotics and the Metaverse not only changes the marketing features but also radically changes the conventional marketing strategies and paradigms, thus the consumer is still the one who connects with the brand.

title: Improved CBBA for Multi-AUV Task Allocation Under Weak Acoustic Communication Using a Five-Regime Evaluation with 6-DOF Dynamics
authors: Hailin Wang, Shuo Li, Yiqun Wang, Tianyou Qiu, Yiping Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Cooperative task allocation for multiple autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) is commonly studied under ideal communication assumptions, which seldom hold in underwater acoustic networks. This paper presents an improved ConsensusBased Bundle Algorithm (CBBA) with three main contributions: (i) a communication-robust consensus update that incorporates local conflict resolution, deterministic tie-breaking, and local bundle repair; (ii) a marginal cost model informed by six-degree-offreedom (6-DOF) dynamics that accounts for turning and depthtransition feasibility during task insertion; and (iii) a five-regime evaluation framework spanning IDEAL, GOOD, MODERATE, LIMITED, and SEVERE acoustic communication conditions. The method is evaluated in an integrated simulation environment that couples range-limited, lossy, and delayed acoustic links with 6-DOF AUV dynamics. On a multi-target visit benchmark with 4 AUVs and 12 static tasks, the proposed method maintains at least 99.8 % task completion across all

title: Depression Detection Using Social Media and Multimodal Data: Toward Explainable and Fair Deep Learning Advances
authors: Harikrishna Pydi, P. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Depression is one of the most significant global health issues with millions of people across the globe being affected. Social media offers rich multimodal signals-linguistic, visual, audio, and behavioral-that have made ML and DL-based approaches possible for early detection. The review integrates findings from 32 papers, providing a comprehensive overview of the field. The review covers extensively used datasets (RSDD, SMHD, eRisk, DAIC-WOZ), methodologies (SVM, Random Forest, CNN, RNN, BERT, Transformers), fusion techniques, explainability, fairness, and ethical issues related to privacy. The review identifies various research gaps, suggests various future directions, and defines concrete goals for the development of efficient, fair, and explainable systems for the detection of depression. The findings have the purpose of bridging the computational progress with the field of clinical practice, with the guarantee of the efficient and ethical application of AI-based mental health solutions.

title: How Low Can Medical Llms Go? an Empirical Study of Low-Bit Quantization
authors: Tanha Tasfia, Abu Kaisar Mohammad Masum, Emilien Meyer, Fabio Cumbo, Sercan Aygun
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently shown very promising results on medical tasks such as clinical question answering, report summarization, and decision support. However, their high computational cost makes them difficult to use on resource-constrained devices. Quantization reduces model size and enables lightweight deployment, yet the impact of low-bit precision on medical language understanding remains underexplored. In this work, we present a systematic empirical study of low-bit quantization for medical LLMs. We evaluate multiple 2-, 3-, and 4-bit quantization techniques across several medical natural language processing (NLP) benchmarks and report their results on task accuracy, model size, and inference efficiency. Our study focuses on evaluating different quantization methods on medical data rather than device-specific deployment; the findings show that medical LLMs can be compressed without significant performance degradation, making them strong candidates for future edge and mobile healthcare applications.

title: STAMICS: Splat, Track and Map with Integrated Consistency and Semantics for Dense RGB-D SLAM
authors: Weiyun Yi, Yongxu Wang, Xu Cao, Zhaoxin Fan
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is a critical task in the field of robotics, enabling systems to autonomously navigate and understand complex environments. Current SLAM methods predominantly rely on geometric cues for localization and mapping, but they often struggle to ensure semantic consistency, particularly in dynamic or densely populated scenes. To address this limitation, we propose a novel method called STAMICS, which integrates semantic information with 3D Gaussian representations to enhance the accuracy of localization and mapping. STAMICS consists of three key components: a 3D Gaussian-based scene representation that combines semantic information with 3D Gaussian splatting to ensure that semantic information is preserved and correctly aligned with scene geometry, enabling high-fidelity reconstruction; a graph-based clustering technique that strengthens temporal semantic consistency, ensuring consistent semantic information for objects across different time frames; and an open-vocabulary system that allows the classification of previously unseen objects, improving the system's adaptability to diverse and complex real-world environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that STAMICS improves camera pose estimation and map quality, performing competitively compared to existing methods while reducing reconstruction errors. The code will be made publicly available.

title: Benchmarking the Benchmarks for Quantum Code Generation with LLMs
authors: Yunhe Feng, Xiaokun Yang, Kewei Sha, Junhua Ding
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as quantum coding copilots, assisting researchers by generating complex programs for frameworks such as Qiskit, PennyLane, and OpenQASM. However, accurately evaluating the practical utility of these models remains a significant challenge. Existing benchmarks are highly fragmented, ranging from syntax-centric unit tests to simulator- and hardware-aware metrics, making results difficult to compare across different languages and hardware targets. To address this fragmentation, this paper systematically reviews recent evaluation suites and post-training methodologies for quantum code generation. We distill current evaluation criteria into four practical dimensions: syntax and API validity, semantic and physical correctness, resource and hardware feasibility, and execution-based feedback. Building upon these dimensions, we present a unified taxonomy that maps current benchmarks to emerging model adaptation techniques, specifically retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), fine-tuning, multi-agent workflows, and reinforcement learning (RL). Our analysis highlights a clear shift: moving away from prompt-only baselines and toward agentic workflows driven by quantum-verifiable rewards. This shift directly targets the semantic gap between superficially compilable code and physically executable quantum programs. In addition, we identify key research opportunities-such as standardized semantic metrics and integration with quantum error correction-aimed at fostering a more reproducible, verifiable, and hardware-grounded ecosystem for automated quantum software development.

title: A Smart On-Site Verification System for 220-kV Voltage Transformers With Multi-Source Data Fusion and Credibility Assessment
authors: Kan Zhang, Yufu Zhao, Ke Zheng, Minzhe Tian, Xiaojie Liu, Yuanyuan Gu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: On-site verification of 220-kV voltage transformers is critical to the accuracy of electric energy metering, relay protection, and operational monitoring. However, existing field practices still rely heavily on independent testing instruments and manual data handling, which often leads to fragmented data chains, insufficient support from single-error indicators, and weak result traceability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a smart on-site verification system covering the entire inspection workflow. The system integrates equipment parameters, measurement data, process status information, and historical records into a unified multi-source data organization framework. On this basis, a method set is established, including unified complex error representation, repeated-measurement quality modeling, weighted fusion estimation, stability and operational variation analysis, historical consistency evaluation, comprehensive state scoring, anomaly screening, and result credibility assessment. The proposed system enables integrated execution of data acquisition, analytical evaluation, and report generation. A representative case study on a 220-kV voltage transformer shows that the proposed method can effectively suppress the influence of abnormal measurement rounds on the final conclusion, improve interpretability under boundary operating conditions, and enhance the traceability of report outputs. The proposed approach provides practical support for the digital, standardized, and intelligent upgrading of on-site voltage transformer verification.

title: DCS-Based Coal Feed Control for Variable-Load Power Units
authors: Wenlong Ma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Variable-load operation of coal-fired power units is strongly affected by fluctuations in coal quality, which make accurate coal feed control difficult under conventional design-based fuel scheduling. To address this issue, this paper proposes a DCS-based coal feed control method for variable-load power units. The method estimates the calorific value of the as-fired coal online from unit load, coal feed rate, and the load-dependent coal consumption relation. A representative target heating value is then obtained through time-window-based signal processing and steady-state discrimination. To preserve closed-loop stability, the target value is not directly imposed on the control loop but gradually assigned to the control heating value under a pressure-deviation-constrained updating logic. Based on the updated heating value, the target coal feed rate is calculated in real time for a new load demand. The method provides a practical feedforward compensation mechanism for coordinated control systems and improves the rapidity, accuracy, and stability of coal feed control under variable-load and variable-coal-quality conditions.

title: Smart Cloud and IoT Architectures for AI-Powered Personalization and Scientific Data Systems
authors: Dilip Mehendra Sharma, Manish Ravindra Sharath, Sarath Vankamardhi Nirmala Varadhi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) provides a smooth connection between individuals and items, and its integration with the cloud enhances everyday life. Forecasting in the clinical arena can assist in transforming an adaptive clinical method into an innovative one, since modern artificial intelligence (AI) technologies pervade the market. Deep learning, as a subarea of AI, has the revolutionary ability to reliably analyze enormous amounts of data at high rates, extract smart conclusions, and solve complex problems quickly. Reliable and fast medical data management for monitoring cardiac patients is critical to provide preventative treatment and prompt therapy for those in need. With the increased use of digital medical archives, developing forecasting algorithms with higher accuracy is critical for leveraging recurrent neural network (RNN) types of DL that may handle consecutive time-dependent statistics. The suggested system collects data from IoT gadgets, and digital medical statistics regarding medical conditions are saved in the cloud and used for statistical analysis. The smart cloud and IoT framework for monitoring the medical data system of heart patients developed using Bi-LSTM (bidirectional long short-term memory) exhibits an accuracy of 98.9%, which is significantly higher compared to current smart cardiovascular control systems.

title: Optimizing End-to-End Communication Latency in UAV-Satellite Integrated Networks
authors: Anamika Kumari, Mannat Thakur, Akash Singh, Chandra Mohan, Prince Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The integration of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with satellite communication systems has been identified as an effective solution in providing communication services in a reliable manner in environments that are difficult to access. However, the high end-to-end latency is a major problem in the integration of satellite communication systems with UAVs due to the high satellite propagation delay. In this research, a novel latency-aware communication framework is proposed with the main purpose of optimizing the end-to-end delay in the integration of UAVs and satellite communication systems. In this paper, the proposed communication framework is presented, and the results obtained in the simulation of the proposed communication framework using network simulation tools are presented. In the simulation, the proposed communication framework was evaluated using different UAV density and mobility conditions. In the simulation results, the proposed communication framework was observed to have reduced the average end-to-end delay from 420 ms to 285 ms, the packet delivery ratio was improved from 86.2% to 94.6%, and the throughput was improved from 1.8 Mbps to 2.6 Mbps.

title: Handwritten Morse Code Recognition Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Jad Abbass, Karthikraj Meda Nagaraja, Sujay Grama Suresh Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Even after many decades, Morse code has not disappeared from practical use. Still, recognising it in handwritten form is tricky, as each person’s writing style, spacing, and stroke patterns can differ greatly. The use of deep learning techniques for this purpose has been largely underexplored. In this paper, we evaluate five convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures—standard CNN, VGG16, ResNet50, MobileNet, and MobileNetV2—to address this challenge. These architectures were chosen to represent a range of model complexities and design philosophies—from lightweight mobile networks to deep residual and classical convolutional models. Three datasets were employed: (i) a manually curated dataset collected from 17 individuals with diverse handwriting styles and varying familiarity with Morse code, (ii) an external dataset sourced from the literature, and (iii) a combined dataset comprising approximately 38,976 images with extensive data augmentation. While the standard CNN was trained from scratch, transfer learning was applied to the four established models. ResNet50 consistently achieved the highest accuracies $(95.9 \%, 92.4 \%$, and 93.0%) across the three datasets, while the standard CNN also produced competitive results $(86.3 \%, 91.6 \%$, and $89.8 \%)$, outperforming the remaining models. These highly accurate models could be particularly valuable in educational contexts, where manually correcting learners’ guesses can be time-consuming.

title: Evaluating the Environmental and Occupational Impact of Work-From-Home Practices: A Sustainable Shift in Post-Pandemic Workforce Dynamic
authors: M. Asmath Haseena, P. Shymala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Taking a holistic approach While this research attempts to cover the multifaceted environmental and occupational health impacts associated with work-from-home (WFH) practices in the post-pandemic workforce; it is conducted by using comprehensive lifecycles of assessment and occupational health monitoring tools. Using activity-based 573rganizat and greenhouse gas accounting schemes found that remote workers can potentially lessen their carbon footprint as much as 54% than onsite workers and hybrid options showed its carbon footprint decreased by anywhere from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1}-\mathbf{2 9 \%}$</tex> in the case where a work period contained two to four days of remote work PNAS. However, environmental advantages depend on sustainable configuration of lifestyles, the impacts of information and communication technology are still negligible at the moment when non-commute travel and home energy use are becoming significant factors GreenlyPNAS. This research incorporates carbon footprint measuring resources with virtual ergonomic evaluation resources and comprehensive well-being monitoring systems to provide evidence-based recommendations on sustainable adjustments to the rapport of hybrid workforce crew including optimizing and directive strategies in local energy policy to focus association sustainable commute 573rganizational573, facility downsizing, renewable energy usage and ahead occupational health support systems.

title: Classic and Quantum Task-Based Intelligent Runtime for QIRs Running on Multiple QPUs
authors: Narasinga Rao Miniskar, Elaine Wong, Vicente Leyton-Ortega, Jeffrey S. Vetter, Travis S. Humble
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: High-performance computing systems are rapidly evolving into heterogeneous platforms that fuse quantum accelerators with traditional classical processing units (CPUs) and graphical processing units (GPUs). This convergence calls for runtimes capable of managing both classical and quantum workloads in a unified manner. We introduce an intelligent, task-based runtime that marries the Intelligent RuntIme System (IRIS) asynchronous scheduler with a quantum programming stack through the Quantum Intermediate Representation Execution Engine (QIR-EE). Our design allows programs written in the quantum intermediate representation (QIR) to be dispatched concurrently to a variety of back-ends, including multiple quantum simulators and nascent quantum processors, enabling genuine hybrid execution on a single node. To illustrate its practicality, we partition a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{4}$</tex>-qubit and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 0}$</tex>-qubit circuit into three sub-circuits using quantum circuit cutting via the QCut library. Each sub-circuit is simulated independently by the QIR-EE driver within IRIS, after which a classical post-processing step merges the simulation results to recover the outcome of the original full-circuit computation. This case study demonstrates how finer task granularity can enable the parallel execution and lower the simulation burden per quantum task while preserving overall accuracy, highlighting the feasibility of our hybrid approach.

title: Model of a Linear NEMS Comb Drive Accelerator for MAC Operations
authors: Owen O'Connor, Tarek Elfouly
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: With the growing need for energy-efficient accelerators in edge computing systems, conventional transistor-based designs are no longer an attractive solution due to their high energy consumption. Two alternative technologies that can address this limitation are micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) and the closely related nano-electromechanical systems (NEMS). This paper introduces a mathematical model showing how MEMS and NEMS comb drives could perform multiply and accumulate (MAC) operations, a crucial first step in demonstrating their viability for co-processors in embedded computing.
