However, traditional QCNN adopts a single-depth circuit structure, which has limitations in multi-scale feature extraction, and is prone to the barren plateau phenomenon during training, making it difficult to adapt to high-dimensional real-world scenarios such as network intrusion detection. This paper proposes a QCNN architecture based on a multi-path parallel structure. For the network intrusion detection task, it draws on the Inception module to construct three parallel quantum convolution paths: shallow, medium, and deep, achieving hierarchical extraction of multi-scale features; residual connections are introduced to preserve encoded state information and provide gradient shortcuts; a hybrid optimization strategy combining classical Adam and Quantum Natural Gradient (QNG) is adopted to adapt to the heterogeneous parameter space. Experimental validation shows that, on the UNSW-NB15 network intrusion detection dataset, the proposed Multi-Path Parallel Quantum Convolutional Neural Network (MP-QCNN) improves detection accuracy by 3% compared to traditional single-path QCNN and effectively mitigates the barren plateau phenomenon. This design strictly maintains NISQ depth constraints and quantum parallelism advantages, providing an efficient and stable feature extraction framework for high-dimensional tasks such as network intrusion detection.

title: Effect of Surface Shape on Electric Contact Resistance for High-Current Copper Conductors
authors: Francesco Rasetti, Giuliano Bissacco
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Hairpin winding technology has established itself as an industrial standard in EV motors production. Among the main advantages of hairpins over classic winding technology are higher copper fill factor and predictable wire position, making it possible to improve relevant performance indicators for electric motors, such as torque and power density. The final steps of assembly of hairpin arrays, namely twisting and connecting of the conductors, are the most critical towards the final performance of the motor. The deterministic position of hairpins in lamination slots opens new possibilities for the assembly of the electromagnetic circuit, among others the possibility to have reversible connections between the wires. Reversible winding connections could allow easier assembly process chain, better maintainability and reusability of the different parts of the winding assembly. The main obstacle for reversible connections to provide a valid alternative to laser welding is the onset, upon separation of the circuit into separate parts, of electric contact resistance (ECR). ECR describes the sum of the resistance components introduced when multiple parts of a circuit are connected. Contact resistance is a complex phenomenon, and it is influenced by many factors, including contact pressure, temperature and surface conditions. The objective of this paper is to describe, through a combination of numerical and experimental findings, the main factors affecting ECR in hairpin wire connections, to understand the marginal impact of such factors on the generated resistance, and to present possible strategies for minimization of ECR in motor windings by optimizing conductors’ process chain.

title: Autonomous Multi-Agent Cyber Defense for Mitigating AI-Generated Threats
authors: Prajwalasimha S N, Dilip Kumar Jang Bahadur Saini, Ananya S, Abhiram B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The fast development of AI-based cyber threats has essentially changed the situation regarding the attacker defender. This brings in adaptive, automated and polymorphic attacks behaviors that impact on the old centralized and rule-based security systems. The given paper introduces an independent multi-agent cyber defense system that represents cyber protection as a decentralized and collaborative decision-making system in adversarial uncertainty. In the suggested architecture, every defense entity acts as the intelligent agent with the localized perception, autonomous reasoning, and adaptive learning abilities, and the resilience of the system level becomes a result of the well-organized inter-agent coordination and sharing information. Theoretically, the framework models the critical properties needed to address countering intelligent opponents, such as adaptability to non-stationary threat, scalability to large networked systems, and resilience to partial observability and component failures. In order to prove the proposed formulation, comprehensive experiments with representative Artificial Intelligence (AI)-generated attack cases are used. The findings indicate that instead of centralized and single-agent security solutions, the multi-agent approach has higher detection accuracy and low response latency, and it is more robust. In addition, the experimental discussion proves the scalability and deployment ability of the framework to a wide range of network and threat levels. The paper is also a single and expandable reference framework of next-generation autonomous cyber defense with both theoretical and empirical values to justify the implementation of multi-agent intelligence in contemporary and future cyber infrastructures.

title: Emergence of artificial intelligence in electroencephalogram analysis
authors: Salma El Kake, Fatima Ezahra Mouas, Soukaina Ismouguene, Hanae Terchoune, Taoufiq Fechtali
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is a noninvasive method used in cognitive neuroscience and clinical neurology for the assessment of cerebral activity. Despite significant technological advancements in EEG, it has always depended on manual examination and conventional signal processing, both of which are complexes and laborious. The advent of artificial intelligence (AI) has significantly influenced EEG analysis by allowing automated features extraction and complex modeling of sophisticated brain signals. Machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches now augment EEG-based diagnostics, cognitive evaluations and clinical neurology applications.

title: A numerical study of thermoelectric coupling in a photovoltaic-thermal system with a new heat exchanger compared to a photovoltaic module
authors: Hayat Allam, Moustafa Al-Damook, Yassine El Alami, Hicham El Achouby, Youness Bannour, Zineb Hekss, Charaf Hajjaj, Ahmed Faize, Bachir Benhala, Elhadi Baghaz
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of the performance of a conventional photovoltaic module (PVM) and a photovoltaic thermal system (PVTS) incorporating a new heat exchanger and water cooling. A 3D numerical model was developed to assess the impact of solar irradiance on the thermal and electrical performance of the two systems. The model was validated by comparison with previously published results, demonstrating good agreement and confirming its accuracy and reliability. The results show that the PVTS significantly reduces the temperature of the PV cells, with a maximum difference of $15.21^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ compared to the uncooled PVM. This thermal reduction leads to improved electrical performance, with a decrease in efficiency limited to $0.66 \%$ for the PVTS, compared to $1.74 \%$ for the PVM, as well as higher electrical power reaching 221.67 W. In addition, the PVTS ensures efficient recovery of thermal energy, resulting in an increase in the fluid outlet temperature and an improvement in thermal efficiency of $11.78 \%$. These results confirm the value of the PVTS for the simultaneous recovery of electrical and thermal energy and highlight its potential for improving the overall energy efficiency of solar installations.

title: Vision Transformer-Based Deepfake Detection: A Comprehensive Approach for Identifying Manipulated Video Content
authors: S. Hariharasudhan, P. S. L. Risheeth, G. V. V. Satyanarayana
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Deepfake technology has both rigor and challenges in the domain of digital authenticity as well as information security. Therefore, in this paper, a deepfake detection model will be proposed by using the Vision Transformer model in order to detect the manipulated videos. For this purpose, a ViT model has been utilized, which is “vit_large_patch16_224,” with a high accuracy of 90.01% on the Google Deep Fake Detection dataset. Advanced data augmentation methods, such as geometrical transformations as well as color transformations, have been employed in order to increase robustness against various manipulation techniques. Videos in our system are preprocessed frame by frame, while the final classification decision is obtained by majority voting. Experimental results showed superior performance with 90% precision and recall in both real and manipulated classes and an AUC of 0.954 on the ROC analysis. Also, the model has minimal overfitting with consistent convergence of training and validation accuracies, hence proving to be effective in real-world applications of deepfake detection. These findings contribute to the growing body of research into transformer-based approaches for media forensics and authentication.

title: Offline Photo-Shield Automatic System for Finding and Redacting Privacy-Sensitive Information in Images
authors: Sandhya B Shettennavar, Shefali Sharma, Aswathy J S, Kaluvayi Sai Rakshitha, Karthika R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The Photo-shield Automatic System presents a fully offline, AI-driven image redaction system for protecting sensitive information in digital images. This system automatically detects faces and text using EasyOCR and Media Pipe, respectively. The extracted text is analyzed using Python-based classifiers and REGEX techniques to identify privacy-critical entities. It recognizes Aadhaar, PAN numbers, phone numbers, email IDs, addresses, date of birth, financial details and vehicle registration plates. The detected faces and sensitive text regions are classified into predefined categories. Appropriate masking techniques, such as Gaussian blur or blackout are applied using OpenCV. This entire pipeline operates offline, ensuring data security and eliminating cloud dependency. A predefined risk-scoring module is used to evaluate the severity of privacy for each image. An automated PDF report of risk analysis summarizes the detected and redacted regions. The experimental results show high accuracy across multiple images and environment contexts.

title: Bridge Structural Health Monitoring Using Vision Transformers and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Qasim Aljamal, Mahmoud Aljamal, Mohammad Aljaidi, Ayoub Alsarhan, Maruf Farhan, Wael Hadi, Hayel Khafajeh, Ahmad Abdullah Alshammari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Bridges are important in the transportation infrastructure, yet in the absence of efficient monitoring, they may deteriorate and fail structurally. Some of the most intricate patterns of damage can be very difficult to detect by traditional structural health monitoring (SHM) systems because they rely on manual features and threshold-based analysis. These methods are weak in their adapting to the different conditions of the environment, aging, and nonlinear structural behavior that can result in a late detection of damages, and wrong evaluation of the conditions. These weaknesses bring to the fore the need to have more intelligent and data-driven forms of monitoring that can record the subtle and dynamic structural anomalies in real time. To enhance SHM, a new model combining Vision Transformers (ViTs) and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) is proposed to identify and identify anomalies. The vibration and strain are converted to image-like data of ViTs, which learns long-range correlation. GNN subsequently determines the structural similarities of space. The highest accuracy of this ViT-GNN network was 99.2 percent, and it exceeded the traditional one, which implied its applicability in intelligent and real-time bridge monitoring.

title: Multi Level Functional Connectivity Fusion Classification Framework for Brain Disease Diagnosis
authors: K Venkata Sai Saranya, A Prudhvi Raj, M Raja Kumari, Kolluru Swarnalatha, SK Samshad Begum, G Lakshmi Sowmya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Brain disease diagnosis has emerged as a key area in the intersection of artificial intelligence and neuroscience. Quantitative analysis of functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data provides biomarkers that aid clinical diagnosis, with functional connectivity (FC) analysis being a primary method. However, most existing studies focus on low-order FC features, overlooking the significance of high-order functional relationships among brain regions. To address this, we propose a novel multi-level FC fusion classification framework (MFC) for brain disease diagnosis. Our approach utilizes a deep neural network (DNN) to extract abstract feature representations from low-order and high-order FC patterns. The DNN training incorporates unsupervised and supervised learning, with prototype learning applied during supervised fine-tuning to enhance intra-class compactness and inter-class separability. The resulting multi-level FC features were combined using a hierarchical stacking strategy to train an ensemble classifier for brain disease classification. Experiments on large-scale fMRI datasets demonstrated robust performance across different preprocessing pipelines, brain parcellations, and cross-validation schemes. These findings highlight the efficacy and generality of the MFC model, providing a promising approach to integrate low-order and high-order FC patterns for improved brain disease classification.

title: Hybrid Wind-Solar Grid Integrated Energy System Simulation with MPPT Control In Matlab/Simulink
authors: Indrajeet Kumar, Sonali R.Nandanwar, Saket Bihari, Anand Singh, Sandeep Kumar Wasnik, Sonu Ahirwar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Many countries are switching to renewable energy sources including wind, tidal, and photovoltaic (PV) systems as electricity demand increases and fossil fuel supplies decline. Particularly, PV systems have generated a lot of interest as a means of producing electricity. Integrating PV systems with grid-connected multilevel inverters is essential to get the most of them. In this study, a multilevel inverter (MLI) depending on an area-interconnected T-type inverter topology is used to mimic a grid-connected photovoltaic system. PV sources are coupled in series to increase input voltage in order to accomplish efficient power transmission, which creates difficulties for traditional inverter circuits. The proposed inverter operates in a three-level buck mode and delivers a maximum output of +Vdc/2. This design combines the advantages of conventional two-level inverters, including lower conduction losses and easier operation, with advantages like lower total harmonic distortion (THD), fewer filter size, and lower switching losses. It also ensures reduced voltage stress and peak inverse voltage across switches. Additionally, a hybrid pulse width modulation (PWM) strategy combining level-shifted and phase-shifted techniques is used to simplify gate pulse generation. Simulation results in MATLAB confirm the system’s performance and control effectiveness.

title: Lightweight Machine Learning for Intrusion Detection in Zero-Trust Systems
authors: Yasmein Abaza, Hana' Alrashid
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Zero Trust security architectures have gained attention as an effective approach for mitigating modern cyber threats; however, their practical deployment in intrusion detection systems often relies on computationally intensive deep learning models, which limit their applicability in real-time and resource-constrained environments. This study evaluates whether lightweight machine learning models can provide effective intrusion detection under Zero Trust continuous verification principles. A quantitative experimental approach is adopted using lightweight classifiers, including XGBoost, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, and Gaussian Naïve Bayes, evaluated on the CICIDS2017 intrusion detection dataset. The results demonstrate that ensemble-based lightweight models achieve high detection performance, with XGBoost outperforming other models and reaching an accuracy of 99.88% and an F1-score of 99.89%, while simpler probabilistic models exhibit comparatively weaker performance. In addition to detection accuracy, computational efficiency is assessed using training time and inference time, showing that XGBoost and Random Forest maintain competitive execution times suitable for real-time operation. These findings indicate that high intrusion detection performance can be achieved without relying on complex deep learning architectures and highlight the potential of lightweight machine learning models as practical alternatives for Zero Trust intrusion detection, particularly in resource-constrained environments, and support their suitability for real-time continuous verification.

title: Advancing FCEVs/FCHEVs: Overcoming Challenges for Scalable Zero-Emission Transport
authors: Debani Prasad Mishra, Prabin Kumar Parida, Rupam Samanta, Abhinav Singh, Kumar Pritam Maharana, Bibhu Prasad Nanda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The key to zero emission transportation and combating climate change is the change away from internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles and towards cleaner alternatives. Increasing greenhouse gas emissions, the depletion of fossil fuels, concerns for energy security have grown demand for new technologies including battery electric vehicles (BEVs), Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle (FCEVs) and Fuel Cell hybrid electric vehicles (FCHEVs). Although BEVs are presently the most popular vehicles, they still have certain disadvantages, for example a small range, demand for rare raw materials and lengthy charging times. However, hydrogen FCEVs and FCHEVs are great for heavy-duty and long distance transportation accompanied by their lengths driving ranges and speedy refueling.However, the cost of producing and storing hydrogen is the main issue. With an emphasis on green hydrogen generated by electrolysis using renewable energy sources, this study explores the potential of the hydrogen economy. It examines ways to reduce production costs, improve fuel cell performance, and make storage safer. It will be crucial to overcome these financial and technological obstacles in order to accelerate the transition away from internal combustion engines (ICE) and create a transportation system that is durable, effective, and completely emission-free.

title: Secure Edge–Fog Architecture for Social IoT and Opportunistic Network Applications
authors: Nidhi Mishra, Utkarsh Anand
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: While existing research often surveys edge-fog computing in static or infrastructure-dependent contexts, this study addresses a critical gap by designing and testing a novel, unified secure edge-fog architecture specifically tailored for Social Internet of Things (SIoT) and opportunistic network services. Working in resource-limited and intermittently connected environments, the proposed framework is unique in that it combines social awareness with decentralized security to reduce the use of centralized cloud infrastructure. It designed a multi-layer architecture, which involved socially related IoT devices, local processing nodes (edge nodes), and regional coordination nodes (fog nodes). Its most important contribution is a model of composite trust evaluation and a dynamic social graph that, when combined, make it possible to communicate safely via store-carry-forward capabilities, without any prior trust. In order to have the highest performance, the architecture will enable adaptive computation offloading and privacy-preserving preprocessing of data. The simulation outcomes with different node movement and attack conditions show that there are great improvements compared to cloud-centric architectures and non-social ones. Namely, the proposed system reported an average communication latency of 72 ms (as compared to 185 ms in cloud-centric models) and minimized the energy usage to 53 J as compared to 92 J. Moreover, the packet delivery ratio rose with 94% out of 78% respectively in comparison with cloud-centric systems, whereas the decentralized trust system made it very stable and rapid convergence to isolate malicious nodes. These results are a confirmation that social consciousness and decentralized trust embedded within an edge-fog architecture are an effective solution offering resilience and high performance in real-world use in dynamically changing, infrastructure-constrained SIoT environments.

title: Unveiling the Drivers of E-Grocery Usage Behaviour in Urban Areas
authors: Yuka Maheswara, Yulia Magdalena, Yuli Eni
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: E-grocery has recently expanded in Indonesia because of Covid-19. However, the adoption rate of E-grocery is considerably lower than other e-commerce platforms. In order to investigate this, this study used UTAUT to find what influences the adoption rate of the consumers towards $\mathbf{E}$ Grocery. 103 respondents completed a questionnaire. The results show that the main factors influencing the rate of e-grocery adoption are Social Influence and Facilitating Conditions. While Facilitating Conditions have a significant impact on Use Behaviour, Social Influence has a significant impact on Behavioural Intention. It was discovered that neither Performance Expectancy nor Effort Expectancy significantly affects Behavioural Intention. These findings show that social factors and technological support have a greater influence on e-grocery adoption in Indonesian cities than perceived effectiveness and ease of use.

title: Analysis of Digital Twin Ecosystem for the Renal System: Improving Patient Outcomes in Chronic Renal Disease and Dialysis Need Prediction
authors: A. Jaya Mabel Rani, Raed Alazaidah, V. Samuthira Pandi, Balpreet Singh, J. Alekhya, R. Ruth Shobitha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Chronic Renal Disease (CRD) is a global healthcare issue due to its progression, high treatment costs, and impact on patient quality of life. Early disease progression detection and precise dialysis prediction are crucial yet difficult for clinicians. Traditional clinical assessment methods use periodic laboratory testing and static predictive models, which often miss renal system physiological changes. A Digital Twin ecosystem for the renal system is investigated as a novel way to improve patient outcomes and dialysis requirement prediction. The term "renal digital twin" refers to a computer model of a patient's kidneys that uses real-time simulations of disease progression based on clinical data, physiological factors, and medical history. Early intervention, therapy optimization, and risk stratification are all made possible by the digital twin framework's tailored modeling and predictive analytics. The suggested ecosystem tracks renal function, looks for patterns of decline, and estimates when a patient will need dialysis by integrating real-time data collection, computer modeling, and smart analytics. When compared to more traditional methods, experimental assessments show that analysis based on digital twins provides better prediction accuracy and decision support. This study demonstrates how digital twin technology can revolutionize nephrology by enhancing patient-centered treatment, reducing healthcare burden, and facilitating proactive disease management in the context of chronic renal disease.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Variational Neural Network for IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Aayush Shrivastava, Nikhat Raza Khan, Ashish Singhal, Lalit Kumar, Amit Bindal, Sameeksha Verma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Weaknesses arising from heterogeneous device structure, high-dimensional traffic patterns, and more complex nonlinear attack behaviors. The traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS), which rely on the classical machine learning models, often face the challenges of scalability, feature explosion, and low representational ability when handling large-scale IoT traffic data. To meet these challenges, we propose a Hybrid Quantum-Classical Variational Neural Network (HQCVNN) for effective intrusion detection in IoT systems. The model utilizes a Variational Quantum Circuit (VQC) as a nonlinear quantum feature map followed by dense neural layers for overall classification. Angle embedding with entanglement layers enables the encoding of quantum data and leads to a better separability of features in high-dimensional space, whilst the classical optimization guarantees efficient training on NISQ time scales. The model is implemented using Qiskit and evaluated on classical benchmark intrusion detection datasets. Experimental results show the proposed method has better classification accuracy, F1-score, and complexity reduction than conventional ANNs and SVMs. The results demonstrate the promise of hybrid quantum machine learning systems with end-to-end general-purpose capabilities in future IoT security and provide pragmatic impressions on how to deploy them on near-term quantum processors.

title: LS-Interpret: A Multi-Algorithm Gradient Boosting Framework with Three-Tier SHAP Explainability for Geospatial Landslide Susceptibility Prediction
authors: Ran Huo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Landslide susceptibility mapping (LSM) presents a challenging high-dimensional binary classification problem over heterogeneous geospatial feature spaces derived from remote sensing imagery and geographic information systems (GIS). Despite growing adoption of ensemble learning in this domain, existing machine learning-based LSM pipelines suffer from three critical limitations: (1) the absence of rigorous, unified benchmarking protocols for comparing classical and state-of-the-art gradient boosting architectures is rarely conducted under unified experimental protocols; (2) the distinct optimization mechanisms of ensemble models-including histogram-based splitting in LightGBM, ordered boosting with target-leakage prevention in CatBoost, and L2-regularized additive tree learning in XG-Boost-are insufficiently compared on high-dimensional geospatial feature spaces; and (3) model interpretability is typically restricted to single-level feature importance scores, neglecting nonlinear decision boundaries and pairwise feature interaction effects that are essential for model transparency. To address these gaps, we propose LS-Interpret, a machine learning system that integrates systematic multi-algorithm evaluation with multi-level SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) for explainable geospatial risk prediction. Three algorithmic contributions are presented: (1) a unified benchmarking pipeline evaluating nine classifiers spanning logistic regression, kernel-based SVM, tree ensembles, and gradient boosting variants on 14 remote sensing and GISderived features, with CatBoost achieving the highest test AUC of 0.80 through its ordered boosting strategy; (2) a three-tier SHAP interpretability framework encompassing global feature attribution via mean absolute Shapley values, nonlinear threshold detection through dependence plots, and pairwise interaction decomposition through Shapley interaction value matrices; and (3) a multi-configuration spatial prediction module with crossmodel spatial consistency evaluation, confirming LightGBM as the most robust spatial inference model. Experiments on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 4 4 2}$</tex> balanced samples (721 documented landslide events) validate both predictive performance and computational interpretability of the proposed system.

title: Rethinking AI Ethics in Education: From Restrictive Policies to Learning-Oriented Design
authors: Suhailah Mohamed Noor, Zaid Momani
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The rapid adoption of generative artificial intelligence in education has prompted many institutions to adopt restrictive policies framing AI ethics as compliance and control. While aiming to protect assessment integrity, such approaches risk limiting learning opportunities and fail to cultivate students’ ethical judgment. This conceptual paper argues that AI ethics in education should be reconceptualized as a pedagogical issue rather than purely regulatory. Drawing on learning design and process-oriented assessment principles, we develop Prompt Chaining with Human-in-the-Loop as a structured pedagogical methodology for cultivating ethical AI use. Prompt Chaining is conceptualized as a cognitive practice promoting iterative questioning, critical evaluation, and intellectual responsibility through structured, multi-step interactions. Human-in-the-Loop functions as the integrated oversight mechanism ensuring human judgment and accountability remain central throughout the learning process. By embedding ethical reasoning into everyday learning activities, this approach shifts discourse from restriction to learning and from compliance to responsibility, offering educators a pedagogically grounded pathway for fostering ethical AI use aligned with critical thinking, metacognition, and lifelong learning.

title: Multi-Modal Perception for Intelligent Robotic Systems
authors: Amandeep Nagpal, Sanjeev Kukreti, Pawan Kumar, Sanjoy Das, A. Samatha, Nitika Vats Doohan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Complex evaluations required of the intelligent robots necessitate a solid and contextualized view that cut across limited frameworks of sensory observation. Classical methods have the attributes of not being able to adapt to ambiguity, sensor noise, and no full environmental versatility prohibiting resourcefulness of the system to dynamical real-world circumstances. The paper is a solution to these challenges by introducing AURA-Net (Adaptive Unified Representation and Attention Network) a new framework that ought to be employed in achieving a robust multi-modal perception. The suggested system combines sensor streams into a single intelligent output format in terms of modality-specialized encoders. AURA-Net is based on a rather different paradigm of adaptive attention, though it uses hierarchies instead of conventional early/late fusion, such that modality relative weights are shifted dynamically depending on the environmental conditions. This allows the system to give priority to the most trustful cues, e.g., in noisy environments providing emphasis on tactile sense, or in cluttered ones provide the focus on visual. Furthermore, a hybrid neuro-symbolic reasoning module is added to combine the learned representations with the domain knowledge that will make it more explainable and capable of decision making under uncertainty. More perception and control policy refining is effected by feedback driven by reinforcement in an attempt to achieve the long-term adaptability. The experimental evaluations in all the tasks of navigation, manipulation, and human-robot interaction show that AURA-Net is much more robust, more precise, and tougher to execute specific tasks than the benchmarks of state-of-the-art technologies. The paper sets the stage towards the design of intelligent robots with resilient and human-like perception. The proposed AURA-Net approach attains a general accuracy of 98.8 percent over a variety of robotic perception and decision-making tasks.

title: Direct Multi-Source AoA Tracking for Passive UAV Surveillance Under Heterogeneous Signal Conditions
authors: Jingchao You, Zhikun Chen, Fuzhang Wang, Zhibin Chen, Hao Ye
abstract: Passive tracking of multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) using distributed 5G infrastructure represents a promising alternative approach in GNSS-limited or denied environments. However, in complex urban scenarios, this task remains highly challenging, as passively intercepted UAV signals often exhibit severe signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) imbalances, while occlusions lead to time-varying target cardinality. Moreover, due to the non-cooperative nature of multiple UAVs, measurements at each receiver are inherently aggregated rather than target-resolved. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a direct angle-of-arrival (AoA) tracking framework based on the δ-generalized labeled multi-Bernoulli (δ-GLMB) filter, which operates directly on received array measurements without explicit AoA estimation. A feature-space spectral reconstruction mechanism is first developed to suppress strong-signal dominance at the covariance level, thereby restoring the detectability of weak UAV signals under large SNR disparities. Building upon the reconstructed AoA feature representation, a novel measurement separation method (NMSM) is introduced, enabling reliable construction of association cost matrices despite inherently aggregated array observations. Furthermore, a multi-station fusion scheme with weighted likelihoods is introduced to exploit spatial diversity for three-dimensional position tracking. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves robust and accurate multi-UAV tracking under SNR disparities exceeding 25 dB, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art RFS-based approaches in terms of tracking accuracy, cardinality consistency, and convergence probability.

title: Reconstructing Temporal Heterogeneity: A Multi-domain Collaborative Analysis Framework for Robust Time-Series Forecasting
authors: Hengrui Li, Wenxue Cui, Yifeng Wang, Chunshan Dong, Wenju Li, Tianyi Lu, Jiangpeng Shi, Jianfeng Wang, Yongbing Zhang, Shaohui Liu
abstract: Real-world time series data typically exhibit multi-scale patterns, heterogeneous structures, and non-stationarity. Capturing these multifaceted temporal variations remains a fundamental yet challenging problem in time-series forecasting. Despite considerable advancements in recent years, most existing approaches still depend on conventional decomposition paradigms that separate time series into trend and seasonal components, while neglecting abrupt events, and systemic perturbations in practical applications. This limitation not only introduces forecasting bias but also hinders model generalization and interpretability in complex environments. To tackle these difficulties, we propose a Multi-domain Collaborative Analysis Framework (MCAF). The framework addresses temporal heterogeneity through structural decoupling and domain-specific modeling. Specifically, each time series is decomposed into three distinct components: trend, seasonal, and residual. These components are then processed through specialized modeling pathways, each tailored to capture their unique dynamics in either the time, frequency, or time-frequency domain. A unified fusion mechanism subsequently integrates the extracted multi-domain representations. Extensive experiments show that MCAF consistently delivers high accuracy and robustness across diverse forecasting horizons.

title: Robust Rigid Body Pose Estimation for Sparse Point Sets Under Heteroscedastic Noise
authors: Hao Wang, Taogang Hou, Tianmiao Wang, Buhui Jiang
abstract: Rigid body pose estimation from sparse point correspondences is important in robotics but remains challenging when measurements are affected by heteroscedastic noise and occasional outliers. This issue is particularly evident in marker-based tracking of fast-moving platforms, where localization uncertainty may vary substantially across markers due to motion blur, occlusion, and sensing artifacts. To address this setting, we develop a robust registration framework tailored to sparse and structured point sets by modeling residuals with a heavy-tailed Student's t-distribution. Using its Gaussian scale mixture representation, the estimation problem is formulated as a maximum-likelihood objective with latent correspondence reliabilities, and solved via an iteratively reweighted least-squares procedure with a graduated non-convexity schedule. The resulting method adaptively down-weights unreliable measurements while retaining the efficiency of weighted rigid alignment. Simulation results across multiple noise models and outlier ratios show that the proposed approach achieves consistently low rotation and translation errors in sparse regimes. Real-world experiments on high-speed quadrotor tracking further demonstrate improved geometric consistency and reduced trajectory jerk relative to standard least-squares estimation.

title: Multi-Modal Fine-grained Discriminative Cues Transfer for Efficient Person Re-Identification
authors: Shenyu Xu, Boqian Liu, Hong Zhang, Haojie Li, Yi Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In Person Re-identification(ReID), multi-modal features such as text can complement image features with discriminative semantic cues. Existing methods use CLIP to optimize learnable textual prompts, which are then utilized to enhance the features for ReID. However, these textual prompts are often susceptible to background noise and struggle to capture fine-grained discriminative attributes. Moreover, during the inference phase, networks of the multi-modal, such as the text encoder, must remain operational alongside the image encoder, thereby increasing computational overhead. In this paper, we design a Multi-Modal Fine-grained Discriminative Cues Transfer model for efficient person ReID. To address the limitation of existing text modal in capturing fine-grained discriminative information, we propose a Multi-Modal Fine-grained Knowledge Extraction (MMFE) module. This module leverages pre-trained foundation models to generate detailed person attribute descriptions and anatomical structure characterizations, and encodes both modals into multi-modal representations for enhancing the visual features. For improved inference efficiency, we design an Explicit-Implicit combined Knowledge Injection (EIKI) module. Through explicit feature alignment and implicit multi-modal attention mechanism, EIKI guides the image encoder to focus on fine-grained discriminative semantics and structural information within images. This design enables the method to effectively utilize multi-modal knowledge while discarding auxiliary modalities-related networks during inference. Experimental results on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our approach not only enhances ReID accuracy but also maintains computational efficiency with reduced resource consumption, making it particularly suitable for edge devices.

title: Optical Multi-STAR-IRS Assisted Underwater Wireless Communication System
authors: Rehana Salam, Vivek Ashok Bohara, Anand Srivastava
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Optical intelligent reflecting surfaces (OIRSs) have emerged as a promising solution for enhancing reliability, coverage, and energy efficiency (EE) in optical wireless communication networks. However, their application in underwater wireless optical communication (UWOC) remains limited due to severe attenuation and the lack of adaptive non-line-of-sight (NLOS) support. To address these challenges, this paper proposes two novel optical simultaneously transmitting and reflecting intelligent reflecting surface (OSTAR-IRS) architectures: (i) a hybrid mirror-array and liquid crystal-based design (H-OSTAR-IRS), and (ii) a standalone liquid crystal-based OSTAR-IRS (LC-OSTAR-IRS). The proposed system integrates non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) under energy splitting (ES) and mode switching (MS) operations with optimized power allocation. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed H-OSTAR-IRS architecture achieves higher aggregate spectral efficiency (ASE) compared to LC-OSTAR-IRS, with improvements of up to approximately 20-30% depending on the multiple access scheme. In addition, RSMA consistently improves fairness across users. Furthermore, the MS mode achieves execution times below 3 seconds, highlighting its practical suitability and low computational complexity.

title: Towards Next-Generation SLAM: A Survey on 3DGS-SLAM Focusing on Performance, Robustness, and Future Directions
authors: Li Wang, Ruixuan Gong, Yumo Han, Lei Yang, Lu Yang, Ying Li, Bin Xu, Huaping Liu, Rong Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Traditional Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) systems often face limitations including coarse rendering quality, insufficient recovery of scene details, and poor robustness in dynamic environments. 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), with its efficient explicit representation and high-quality rendering capabilities, offers a new reconstruction paradigm for SLAM. This survey comprehensively reviews key technical approaches for integrating 3DGS with SLAM. We analyze performance optimization of representative methods across four critical dimensions: rendering quality, tracking accuracy, reconstruction speed, and memory consumption, delving into their design principles and breakthroughs. We further discuss the trade-offs among these dimensions and their implications for platform deployment. Furthermore, we examine methods for enhancing the robustness of 3DGS-SLAM in complex environments such as motion blur, dynamic environments, and edge platforms. Finally, we discuss challenges and future trends in this area. This survey aims to provide a technical reference for researchers and foster the development of next-generation SLAM systems characterized by high fidelity, efficiency, and robustness.

title: LCSM: A Local Chart-based Statistical Model for Femoral Shape Analysis
authors: Lujian Zhang, Tianyu Fu, Dejin Yang, Jingyi Liu, Jiaju Zhang, Deqiang Xiao, Jingfan Fan, Danni Ai, Hong Song, Yixin Zhou, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Statistical shape modeling of the femur is vital for clinical applications, yet linear methods struggle with complex geometric variations. While recent chart-based generative models like ChartPointFlow excel at general point cloud reconstruction, their direct application to the nuanced domain of SSM is limited. To bridge this gap, we propose a novel framework that adapts and significantly extends the chart-based paradigm for robust statistical shape analysis. Our core contributions are threefold. First, we are applying this substructure-based generative approach to the statistical modeling of anatomical shapes, moving beyond reconstruction. Second, to overcome the limitations of baseline encoders in capturing intricate anatomical details, we design a Geometric-enhanced Dynamic Graph Convolutional encoder that integrates multi-level geometric feature encoding. Third, to better learn the statistical distribution across a population, we introduce a point-wise feature augmentation module, termed Fine Feature Mixture module, which enriches feature diversity and robustness by fusing fine-grained representations across different samples. Extensive reconstruction and generation experiments on a femur dataset demonstrate our framework’s superiority. Furthermore, evaluations on multiple ShapeNet categories validate its generalization and applicability to diverse 3D shape modeling tasks. Code will be available in https://github.com/LujZhang/LCSM.

title: Geometry-Dependent PCRLB for Target Tracking in Clutter With Radar Measurement Uncertainty
authors: Yifang Shi, Yu Zhang, Lingjiao Fu, Dongliang Peng, Qiang Lu, Haewoon Nam, Alfonso Farina
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In realistic radar tracking scenarios, the target measurement uncertainty (TMU)—encompassing both detection probability and measurement error covariance—is strongly influenced by the target-to-radar (T2R) geometry. However, the existing posterior Cramér–Rao lower bounds (PCRLBs) largely overlook the fundamental impact of T2R geometry on the TMU and, consequently, on the mean square error (MSE) of state estimation, often leading to overly conservative bounds. To bridge this gap, this paper first develops a generalized model of target measurement error covariance for bistatic radar systems with moving transmitters and receivers, in which the impact of T2R geometry on the error covariance is explicitly characterized. Based upon this TMU formulation, we subsequently derive a geometry-dependent PCRLB (GD-PCRLB) that fully incorporates both measurement origin uncertainty and geometry-dependent TMU. In this derivation, both detection probability and measurement error covariance are treated as state-dependent parameters when differentiating the log-likelihood function with respect to the target state. Unlike existing PCRLBs that partially or completely ignore the geometry-dependent nature of TMU, the proposed GD-PCRLB captures substantial performance benefits by extracting additional Fisher information arising from geometry-dependent TMU. The resulted GD-PCRLB provides a significantly less conservative MSE lower bound compared to existing bounds that only partially or fully neglect the influence of T2R geometry on TMU. Numerical results demonstrate that the improvement offered by the GD-PCRLB becomes more pronounced as the level of TMU increases.

title: Diffusion-based Data Augmentation and Knowledge Distillation with Generated Soft Labels for Oil Spill Segmentation Learning on Satellite SAR Images
authors: Jaeho Moon, Jeonghwan Yun, Jaehyun Kim, Jaehyup Lee, Munchurl Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Semantic segmentation often suffers from a compounded scarcity of data, where both input observations and ground-truth labels are limited. This problem is particularly severe in remote sensing tasks such as oil spill segmentation using Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), where rare event occurrence and the difficulty of SAR image acquisition result in limited training data. To address this limitation, we propose a diffusion-based Data Augmentation with Knowledge Transfer (DAKTer) strategy. Our DAKTer strategy enables a diffusion model to generate SAR oil spill images along with soft label pairs, which offer richer class probability distributions than segmentation masks (i.e. hard labels). Also, for reliable joint generation of high-quality SAR images and soft labels with high correspondence, we introduce an SNR-based balancing factor aligning the noise corruption process of both modalities in diffusion models. By leveraging the generated SAR images and soft labels, a student segmentation model can learn robust feature representations without teacher models trained for the same segmentation task, improving its ability to segment oil spill regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DAKTer strategy effectively transfers the knowledge of per-pixel class probabilities to the student segmentation model to distinguish the oil spill regions from other look-alike regions in the SAR images. Our DAKTer strategy boosts various segmentation models to achieve superior performance with large margins compared to other generative data augmentation methods.

title: Lightweight Steganalysis with Low Distortion for Countering Robust Image Steganography
authors: Qianqian Zhang, Yinhong Huang, Duo Ma, Xiangyang Luo, Yuanyuan Ma, Ning Ruan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Robust image steganography in social networks presents significant challenges for steganalysis. Existing methods exploit low payload visibility and resistance to evade conventional passive steganalysis, leading to high false-positive and missed detection rates, which undermines reliable verification and poses a significant security threat to cyberspace. Active steganalysis is designed to eliminate hidden information through controlled modifications. However, existing methods usually encounter challenges such as a decline in the visual quality of processed images, excessive training costs of the models, and dependence on the image size. All these have limited the practical application of active steganalysis. To address this issue, we propose a block-based multi-objective analysis framework, which integrates dual-block constraints with multi-objective loss optimization. This framework achieves adaptive image partitioning by dividing the image into non-overlapping 2×2 pixel blocks. The partitioning simultaneously satisfies distortion boundary constraints and luminance preservation requirements. A multi-objective loss function is proposed to eliminate redundant information effectively while maintaining image visual clarity. The weights of loss function are set based on evolutionary optimization. Furthermore, the performance of the proposed method is enhanced by a linear-time complexity depth-first search algorithm, which minimizes the PSB loss under block-level constraints. The optimization process incorporates perturbation-based refinement to facilitate global convergence. Extensive experiments conducted on the BOSSBase-1.01 and CelebA datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in defending against robust image steganography prevalent in social networks. Evaluations across different payload settings indicate that the proposed method achieves an average PSNR improvement of approximately 34% over AO-Net across both BOSSBase-1.01 and CelebA datasets. Furthermore, the framework processes standard test images with a latency of 6.4ms, without requiring any preset image size.

title: Mechanistic Enhancement of Electrical Performance in PEEK Insulation via Surface Gas-Phase Fluorination
authors: Hanbo Zheng, Pengcheng Zhang, Yi Li, Zhenlian An, Shenglin Zhou, Ziliang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Poly(ether ether ketone) exhibits significant potential for high-voltage insulation applications due to its excellent mechanical strength and thermal stability. However, the material's surface is prone to charge accumulation, which lowers the direct current flashover voltage and compromises its reliability in practical applications. This study systematically investigates the mechanism of performance enhancement in PEEK via surface fluorination, integrating molecular dynamics simulations with high-voltage DC flashover experiments. Simulation results indicate that the introduction of highly polar C–F bonds via fluorination strengthens intermolecular interactions, increases cohesive energy density by approximately 3.5%, enhances elastic modulus by over 15%, and significantly reduces the dielectric constant of PEEK. Experimental findings confirm that fluorination effectively suppresses surface tracking and flashover, with the DC flashover voltage of treated samples increasing by 23.03% relative to untreated ones. This improvement is primarily attributed to the fluorinated layer facilitating more efficient dissipation of surface charges, thereby enhancing resistance to electrical degradation and improving insulation reliability. The study demonstrates that moderate surface vapor-phase fluorination offers a viable strategy to enhance the overall insulation performance of PEEK without compromising its intrinsic properties, providing a robust theoretical basis and specific technical guidance for the rational design of next-generation polymer dielectrics.

title: Geometry-Prior Guided Coarse-to-Fine Stabilization of Full-Disk Solar EUV Image Sequences
authors: Qiyan Luo, Zhongli Fan, Mi Wang, Lekang Wen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Full- disk solar extreme ultraviolet (EUV) image sequences are fundamental for continuous monitoring of coronal dynamics in space-weather applications, but on-orbit platform and instrument disturbances introduce inter-frame jitter that disrupts temporal continuity. Solar EUV sequences further challenge generic stabilization pipelines due to strong nonrigid internal evolution, large low-texture regions, and background artifacts that induce unreliable correspondences. This article addresses full- disk sequence stabilization under a 2D similarity model and proposes a three-stage framework for FY-3E X-EUV sequences. First, we construct an edge-aware geometric representation using Sobel- gradient magnitude and a coarse-to-fine ring search to emphasize stable disk-edge structures. Second, we perform a local geometry-aware coarse correction by estimating a disk-center prior and inner- disk mask with a Gaussian-weighted disk-edge ring template, followed by a sub-pixel translation refinement via patch correlation. Third, we apply a global feature-aware fine correction by robustly estimating a similarity transform with an IGG-III weighted iterative solver. We introduce a FY-3E X-EUV full- disk stabilization benchmark of nine annotated sequences, including quiet and C/M-class flare cases, together with a reproducible evaluation protocol under translation, rotation, and larger frame-interval perturbations. We further report raw-sequence image-domain evaluation, quiet- versus-flare disaggregated analysis, and scale-drift sensitivity analysis. Extensive experiments on the benchmark demonstrate that the proposed method achieves consistently high accuracy and robustness and outperforms existing approaches.

title: LAWNs Meet SWIPT: Beamforming and Power Splitting Optimization for Predictive Control
authors: Jun Wu, Wenchao Liu, Weijie Yuan, Nanchi Su
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling sustainable connectivity in battery-limited low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs). This paper investigates a SWIPT-enabled LAWN system in which a multi-antenna base station (BS) simultaneously delivers control information and wireless energy to a fleet of uncrewed aircraft systems (UASs) via power splitting. In particular, the BS remotely guides the UASs to accurately track predefined reference trajectories toward their destinations while avoiding multiple mobile no-fly zones (NFZs). To guarantee collision-free path planning, we first construct smooth and safe reference trajectories using stream function theory. Then, a real-time optimization problem is formulated, which jointly takes into account the wireless control cost and energy sustainability by optimizing control inputs, transmit beamforming vectors, and the power splitting ratios. To address the resultant non-convex problem, a two-stage optimization framework is proposed. First, we develop a model predictive control (MPC)-based method to generate predictive control inputs. Subsequently, we derive a computationally efficient iterative algorithm to optimize the beamforming vectors and power splitting ratios by applying semidefinite relaxation (SDR) and successive convex approximation (SCA) techniques. We further prove that the SDR is tight for our formulation. Extensive numerical results demonstrate that our proposed design significantly outperforms benchmark schemes in terms of tracking accuracy and harvested energy, thereby validating its effectiveness for sustainable implementation in LAWN systems.

title: Hybrid-CMLP: Hybrid CNN-MLP Networks for Low-to-standard-dose PET Synthesis
authors: Yuxin Xue, Mingyuan Meng, Lei Bi, Jinman Kim
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Positron Emission Tomography (PET) is a critical modality in medical imaging for detecting abnormalities and diagnosing diseases. However, the radiation exposure from the radiotracer remains a significant concern. Synthesizing standard-dose PET (sPET) images from low-dose PET (lPET) offers a compelling solution, enabling high-quality imaging while substantially reducing radiation risks to patients. Existing PET synthesis methods predominantly rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), which are inherently limited by their local receptive fields. Transformer-based architectures have been explored to address this limitation by modeling long-range dependencies through self-attention. However, the high computational and memory costs of self-attention restrict Transformers to downsampled feature maps, limiting their ability to model global anatomical and functional correlations at full resolution. Multilayer Perceptrons (MLPs), in contrast, offer a computationally efficient way to capture long-range dependencies at full resolution. Yet, existing MLP-based methods for PET synthesis lack mechanisms to capture localized features such as subtle tissue textures that are critical for PET functionality. In this study, we propose a hybrid CNN-MLP network (Hybrid-CMLP) architecture for PET synthesis. Central to this architecture is a novel Hybrid-Syn block, which integrates axis-wise MLP branches for capturing global dependencies along spatial dimensions, and dual CNN branches for extracting both fine-grained and broad contextual features. To further enhance fine-grained reasoning, we introduce an Adaptive Fusion Mechanism (AFM) that dynamically integrates global and local features based on spatial context. Extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that Hybrid-CMLP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations.

title: Passivity-Based Pinning Synchronization Control of Switched Directed Networks With Improved Switching Strategy
authors: Chongyi Cui, Hong Sang, Yi Liu, Peng Wang, Shuanghe Yu, Georgi Marko Dimirovski
abstract: This research delves into the passivity-based pinning synchronization problem for switched directed networks (SDNs) within the context of a sampled-data-based event-triggered (SDET) communication environment. An improved state-dependent switching strategy (SDSS) using sampled measurements alongside a novel time-dependent Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional (LKF) approach is constructed. Subsequently, a relaxed passivity analysis framework is then presented, ensuring that the resultant synchronization error system (SES) remains passive, and that the examined SDNs achieve asymptotic synchronization in the absence of external inputs. Unlike conventional strategies, the proposed switching protocol depends exclusively on system states at discrete sampling instants and effectively utilizes historical state data, yielding a less conservative theoretical framework for passivity synthesis in SDNs. Furthermore, the resulting synchronization criterion is independent of the number of network nodes, rendering it suitable for SDNs with large size. Ultimately, two examples involving relevant application in image encryption and decryption are provided to verify the effectiveness and applicability of the proposed scheme, and highlight the distinct advantage of switching behaviors in enhancing encryption confidentiality.

title: Hierarchical Multi-Terminal DC System for Large-Scale Renewable Integration: Topology and Coordinated Control
authors: Tian Hou, Ying Huang, Guoteng Wang
abstract: As DC power projects transition toward phased construction and higher voltage levels, interconnecting existing systems to form a multi-voltage-level DC grid is a pragmatic choice that balances the wide-area integration of large-scale renewable energy with the maximization of legacy asset utilization. While DC/DC transformer (DCT) topologies provide viable technical paths, scaling them to ultra-high-voltage applications involves considerations such as full-scale hardware requirements and isolation transformer design. To facilitate more flexible phased expansion, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Multi-Terminal DC (HMTDC) system with Coordinated Primary and Secondary Control (CPSC). The topology employs Multi-Voltage-Level Interconnecting MMCs (MVLI-MMCs) rated for the inter-system voltage differential rather than full terminal voltage, enabling gigawatt-level power exchange through significantly reduced converter capacity. The CPSC combines distributed droop-based primary control for rapid disturbance rejection with secondary optimization that coordinates power margins and voltage quality across subsystems. Comprehensive time-domain simulations demonstrate that the proposed framework not only optimizes cross-level power flows but also ensures fault ride-through and rapid recovery under severe AC/DC contingencies, thereby providing a techno-economically viable pathway for evolving multi-voltage-level DC grids.

title: Wholesale Market Participation via Competitive DER Aggregation
authors: Cong Chen, Ahmed S. Alahmed, Timothy D. Mount, Lang Tong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: We consider the aggregation of distributed energy resources (DERs), such as solar PV, energy storage, and flexible loads, by a profit-seeking aggregator participating directly in the wholesale market under distribution network access constraints. We propose a competitive DER aggregator (DERA) model that directly controls local DERs to maximize its profits, while ensuring each aggregated customer gains a surplus higher than their surplus under the regulated retail tariff. The DERA participates in the wholesale electricity market as virtual storage with optimized generation offers and consumption bids derived from the proposed competitive aggregation model. Also derived are DERA's bid curves for the distribution network access and DERA's profitability when competing with the regulated retail tariff. We show that, with the same distribution network access, the proposed DERA's wholesale market participation achieves the same welfare-maximizing outcome as when its customers participate directly in the wholesale market. Extensive numerical studies compare the proposed DERA with existing methods in terms of customer surplus and DERA profit. We empirically evaluate how many DERAs can survive in the competition at the long-run equilibrium, and assess the impacts of DER adoption levels and distribution network access on short-run operations.

title: Coupled Half-Bridge LLC Resonant Converter With Reduced Switches and Current Sharing Ability
authors: Xinhui Zhao, Hongliang Wang, Zongheng Wu, Lingling Zhang, Mingyang Han, Yan-Fei Liu, Xiumei Yue
abstract: A novel coupled half-bridge LLC resonant converter is proposed in this paper, which has fewer switches and automatic current sharing ability compared with the traditional parallel half-bridge LLC resonant converter. Since the two resonant networks in the proposed converter share the same power switches, the number of devices is reduced by half, which is beneficial for increasing system power density. In addition, the resonant inductor is shared by the two resonant networks, which means that the uneven current in the two LLC resonant converters, caused by differences in resonant parameters, can be shared. The operation mode of the proposed converter is analyzed, and it has the same characteristics as the traditional LLC resonant converter. Besides, the coupled resonant network is analyzed on the basis of Fundamental Harmonic Analysis (FHA), and the current sharing effect of the proposed converter is analyzed. Further, a 1kW experimental platform is built, and the theoretical analysis and simulation results are validated by experimental results.

title: Distributed Fixed-Time Control With Adaptive Barrier Function in AC Microgrid Under Dynamic Event-Triggered Mechanism
authors: Chanjuan Zhao, Yue Li, Wei Sun, Qingwei Tang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a novel dynamic event-triggered (DET) fixed-time control method to solve the practical voltage and frequency consensus problem in the islanded microgrid. By introducing a new dynamic variable with multi-scale negative self-feedback terms rather than a constant, a more serious and time-variant triggering condition is proposed to reduce triggering instants and controller updates. Additionally, an adaptive barrier function and a disturbance observation-like method are integrated into the fixed-time control to enhance precise convergence control. Furthermore, the microgrid fixed-time consensus analysis and Zeno behavior exclusion are verified by the Lyapunov stability theorem. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of our proposed control.

title: Modeling, Simulation, and Prototype Fabrication of a Saturable-Core Reactor
authors: Ábner C. P. Pacheco, Ivan N. Santos, Elise Saraiva, Guilherme D. Da C. Oliveira, Paulo H. O. Rezende, Gustavo T. A. Da Silva, José A. De S. Brito
abstract: This paper presents the complete development of a 500 kVAr saturable-core reactor prototype, including design, computational modeling, fabrication and laboratory testing. The design was based on classical electromagnetic‐circuit principles, and the resulting data were used by TRAEL Transformers to manufacture the equipment and were implemented computationally in ANSYS. Laboratory tests were conducted to assess its operation and effectiveness; among these, the power–voltage characteristic was used to compare experimental results with those from simulation. The analysis demonstrates that the design exhibits robust performance for power-system applications.

title: Control-Integrated Pipeline for Diseasome Analysis: A Case Study on Hypertension Comorbidities in U.S. Veterans_supp2-3698948.csv
authors: A. MAGNONI

title: DyGIN: Modality-Unified Dynamic Graph Inception Network for Context-Aware Emotion Recognition
authors: Yingxue Gao, Huan Zhao, Zixing Zhang
abstract: Graph representation learning has attracted considerable attention for its ability to generate node representations by aggregating information from neighboring nodes. However, existing graph-based methods for sequential data merely focus on a static graph constructed on an entire unimodal sequence, largely ignoring their dynamic evolution. To overcome this limitation, we introduce a modality-unified Dynamic Graph Inception Network (DyGIN) that models dynamic evolutionary graphs for different modalities. DyGIN constructs dynamic graphs from temporal subsequences using a sliding window, and incorporates a Temporal Graph Evolution Gated Recurrent Unit (TGE-GRU) to update graph weights at each segment. Additionally, we propose a context-aware similarity matrix, which updates node representations based on the degree of neighboring nodes, replacing the traditional averaging method. Our model is optimized with a combination of graph structure loss, classification loss, and a learnable pooling function. We validate DyGIN on emotion recognition tasks across three public datasets—RML, IEMOCAP, and DEAP—where it outperforms state-of-the-art models by 1.6% on RML, 1.81% and 2.33% on IEMOCAP, and 2.78% and 6.81% on DEAP, based on accuracy and weighted F1 score. Our code is available at https://github.com/G22-web/DyGIN.

title: Pulse Repetition Frequency Tuned Low-Intensity Focused Ultrasound Neuromodulation for Tibial Nerve Targeted Bladder Function Modulation
authors: Young-Soo Lim, Hyunhee Kim, Chulhong Kim, Sung-Min Park
abstract: Objective: This study aims to investigate whether the neuromodulatory efficacy of low-intensity focused ultrasound (LIFU) for peripheral nerve stimulation (PNS) can be systematically tuned by the temporal stimulation parameter, pulse repetition frequency (PRF). Methods: LIFU-mediated tibial nerve stimulation (LIFU-TNS) was applied with multiple PRF conditions to evaluate PRF-dependent neuromodulatory effects, with micturition inhibition as the primary outcome. Intra-bladder pressure (IBP) was continuously monitored to evaluate changes in micturition intervals. Neural involvement was confirmed using (1) a lidocaine-induced sciatic nerve (tibial branch) block, and the stimulation paradigm was additionally evaluated in (2) an acetic acid–induced overactive bladder model and (3) freely moving, unconstrained animals to assess robustness across physiological and behavioral conditions. Results: A distinct PRF-dependent neuromodulation was observed, where 100 Hz stimulation elicited the most potent inhibition of micturition, whereas 10 Hz and 1 kHz showed comparatively less effective results. Quantitative analysis of normalized micturition interval (post-stimulation / pre stimulation baseline) confirmed this trend. (10 Hz: 0.98±0.16; 100 Hz: 1.72±0.81; 1 kHz: 0.95±0.35). Importantly, consistent inhibition was replicated across the nerve-block, overactive bladder, and freely moving models, indicating robustness of the effect under diverse physiological and behavioral conditions. Conclusion: The clear PRF-dependent modulation observed in LIFU-TNS establishes PRF as a feasible and tunable control parameter and highlights its potential for guiding precise neuromodulation. Significance: Unlike conventional intensity dependent strategies, this study demonstrates that PRF is essential for effective ultrasound neuromodulation in overactive bladder. These findings establish a systematic parameter-based framework for developing robust peripheral nerve interfaces.

title: Variational Enhancement of Coded Fringes via Bi-directional Coded Content Similarity for Structured-light 3D Measurement
authors: Wei Cao, Xiaodong Na, Tianzhe Bao, Zhan Song, Shengquan Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In structured-light (SL) 3D measurement systems, underexposed coded fringe images (UCFIs) often fail to decode and reconstruct sparse point clouds due to low visibility. Unfortunately, existing low-light enhancement algorithms primarily target natural non-coded images, cannot faithfully preserve the encoded information of the fringe patterns, and are prone to introducing undesirable visual artifacts, noise, and reconstruction distortion. To this end, we first propose a novel bidirectional coded content similarity (B2CS) rule, specifically designed to enhance UCFIs captured by SL systems. Based on this rule, it reveals that the failure reason of existing methods occurs by breaking coded content similarity between under- and well-exposed fringes, and accordingly we construct a hybrid L2-L1-L0 norm-constrained decomposition model with illumination-reflectance-noise priors. Considering the non-convexity caused by the joint priors, we design an effective variational decomposition framework and employ the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) for efficient optimization. Based on the reliable decomposed solution, the enhanced UCFIs can assist the structured light algorithm in recovering structurally rich and high-density point clouds. Extensive experiments on real-world SL datasets demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both subjective and objective evaluation, and significantly improves the completeness of the 3D reconstructed point cloud (by ≥ 20 %).

title: Mechanisms of Central Erosion on the Contact Interface With Convex Rail Structure
authors: Jinghan Xu, Minfu Liao, Xubin Li, Yifan Sun, Shengguo Xia, Lixue Chen, Chengxian Li
abstract: The armature and rail (A/R) interface in railguns experiences extreme conditions of high current and velocity, leading to severe melting erosion. Experimental and numerical studies have demonstrated that convex rails exhibit superior launch performance compared with flat ones, with recovered armatures revealing central erosion patterns distinct from those observed in flat rails. In this article, a coupled Multiphysics numerical model is developed, incorporating variations in contact pressure and area, armature acceleration, and temperature dependence of electrical conductivity. The electromagnetic–mechanical–thermal characteristics of the A/R interface at the beginning stage of the melting erosion are analyzed by investigating the distribution of interfacial heating sources. The results indicate that the melting erosion process is affected by two stages, including the initial stage and the acceleration stage. The interfacial Joule heat plays an important role in the temperature rise during the initial stage, whereas the frictional heat dominates the temperature distribution as the velocity increases. Eventually, central erosion patterns are generated on the A/R interface as the temperature reaches the melting point.

title: Analysis and Optimization Framework for STAR-RIS-aided Short-Packet Systems with Covert Rate-Splitting Signaling
authors: Anh-Tu Le, Thai-Hoc Vu, Thien Huynh-The, Miroslav Voznak
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the performance of simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-enabled short-packet communication (SPC) systems that employ covert rate-splitting (RS) to facilitate applications of Internet-of-Things (IoT). Under the generalized model of the α-η-κ-μ fading, we analyze covertness by first deriving a closed-form expression for the warden’s detection error probability (DEP) and then deducing the optimal detection threshold at which the DEP is minimized. From this optimal DEP, we guide the choice of the feasible power-allocation (PA) region at which the warden’s produced DEP is always beyond the minimal acceptable covertness. On the other hand, we develop mathematical frameworks for evaluating the system’s block-error rate (BLER) and the ergodic rate (ER), as well as providing guidelines on how to access their performance limits at high signal-to-noise ratio, especially the diversity orders and ergodic slopes of the users. Furthermore, we also propose to enhance the system performance by jointly optimizing the PA and RS coefficients in order to: (1) minimize the maximum BLER across private users subject to a minimum covertness requirement and (2) maximize the minimum ER across users subject to covertness and decoding constraints. Numerical results confirm the analytical expressions and show that the proposed optimization efficiently tunes the PA and RS coefficients to achieve the BLER and ER fairness objectives.

title: Signal-Compensated PI Control Based on Concurrent-Learning-Driven Optimal Tuning Parameters for Complex Ore Feeding Processes
authors: Ce Guo, Yajun Zhang, Rui Zheng, Yao Jia, Ning Liu, Tianyou Chai
abstract: Due to the influence of fluctuations in the particle size and throughput of ore from the upstream crushing stage, variations in the return flow of unqualified particles during grinding, and the frequent changes of unknown nonlinearities in the complex ore feeding process, it is difficult to apply existing disturbance-rejection control methods to control the feed rate within the target range. In this article, by utilizing the characteristic that the feed rate fluctuates around the target interval, a dynamic model of the feeding process is established. Based on the obtainable previous-time-step value of the unknown nonlinear dynamic system and the closed-loop tracking error generated by the unknown rate of change of this nonlinear system, and the PI closed-loop equation, a data-driven signal-compensator is designed by combining feedforward control with a one-step optimal control law. A signal-compensated PI controller is proposed. The performance index that minimizes the tracking error at each operating instant is introduced, and a concurrent-learning-driven optimal tuning method for parameters of both the PI controller and the compensator is proposed. Simulation and industrial application experiments demonstrate that the proposed control method is capable of controlling the tracking error within the target interval during the entire operational period.

title: AI-Driven Marketing Transformation: Insights from Bibliometric Mapping and Network Analysis
authors: Abu Bashar, Mariam Juma, Hatem Alsridi, Asma Malallah
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) machine learning and computer vision technologies have been incorporated into marketing applications due to the explosion of consumergenerated data and the need for hyper-personalization. Existing research in this field is still dispersed across theoretical underpinnings methodological philosophies and contextual emphasis despite the fields growing volume of studies. This study addresses this critical gap by combining the Theory-Context-Characteristics-Methodology (TCCM) framework with bibliometric analysis to conduct a thorough review of AI applications in marketing. In order to map the intellectual landscape measure research productivity and pinpoint significant publications authors and developing thematic clusters bibliometric techniques are utilized. To complement this the TCCM framework offers an organized way to evaluate the methodological rigor contextual domains defining traits and theoretical foundation of earlier studies. Findings show that over the past 20 years there has been a notable increase in the body of research on AI-marketing with clear clusters emerging around programmatic advertising, customer relationship management, and AI-driven content production. The results provide insightful advice for scholar’s industry professionals and legislators aiming to understand and progress the rapidly changing field of AIpowered marketing.

title: Self-Supervised Anomaly Detection Models for Continuous Data Quality Monitoring in Enterprise Platforms
authors: Zinkal Thakker, Ramanagiri Kumar Matla, Nikhil Reddy Gaddam, Suraj Eleti, Sindhura Nimmakayala, Siva Koteswara Rao Katta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Enterprise data platforms need strong, scalable mechanisms for outlier detection without labeled datasets. In this paper, we conduct a wide-ranging exploration of self-supervised anomaly detection models that caters to continuous data quality monitoring in large-scale enterprise settings. Leveraging selfsupervised learning paradigms such as contrastive learning, masked autoencoders and predictive coding, the proposed framework does away with relying on grunt work generated by humans to label anomalies, a laborious task that is typically expensive, scarce and inconsistent in real-world scenarios.
