The system can recognize discrepancies of schema integrity, statistical distributions, and temporal patterns across multi-dimensional data streams in heterogeneous data pipelines. Experimental results using enterprise-grade benchmark datasets show that the proposed models outperform traditional supervised and rule-based approaches with respect to anomaly detection accuracy, false-positive rate, adaptability against concept drift. Additionally, the architecture is equipped with lightweight online learning modules, allowing for on-the-fly model updates without requiring complete retraining cycles and promotes continuous performance in dynamically changing data environments. Summary The results highlight that self-supervised techniques have the power to automate data quality assurance, leading to extensive overhead reduction in enterprise platforms while providing data reliability and trust for data-driven decision-making.

title: Sophisticated Geolocation Based Attendance Tracking Mobile Application
authors: Magesh. D, Dharshan. R, Gowtham Kumar. S, Tharun Kumar. A, Bagirathan. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Attendance tracking is a critical function that continues to be a vital part of organizations and institutions, as it has a direct impact on discipline, accountability, and efficiency. Traditional methods like manual attendance systems and biometric systems have limitations in terms of issues such as proxy attendance, dependence on physical infrastructure, and lack of flexibility in mobile work environments. With the growing use of smartphones with geolocation capabilities, geolocation-based systems offer a viable alternative for tracking attendance. However, existing GPS-based systems lack mechanisms for validating location reliability and are vulnerable to spoofing, affecting their accuracy. This paper proposes a Geolocation-Based Attendance Tracking Mobile Application that allows users to mark their attendance by GPS verification. The suggested system uses a hierarchical verification process involving geofencing, confidence index calculation, and device binding for effective and accurate attendance monitoring. The application has a central administrative interface that allows administrators to monitor attendance status, attendance logs, and location-based validation results. The results of implementation show that the proposed method can enhance the authenticity of attendance registration while ensuring ease of use and low deployment cost. The system demonstrates improved accuracy and reduced fraudulent attendance compared to conventional methods. The proposed system offers a feasible and flexible solution for the current attendance system.

title: Momentum-Based Cryptocurrency Trading Strategy : Data Synthesis and Machine Learning for Predictive Signal Classification
authors: Maryam AlBahri, Chinthaka Dinesh
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This study proposes a momentum-based machine learning framework for weekly Bitcoin trading signal classification, whose novelty lies in the unification of economically inspired feature design, temporally aware validation, and interpretable modeling to achieve high predictive accuracy without relying on resource-intensive deep learning architectures, using a multi-source dataset that integrates technical indicators, on-chain metrics, economic variables, and event-driven sentiment features. The task is modeled as a three-class classification problem (Buy, Hold, Sell) under class imbalance and temporal dependencies. Data preprocessing involved manual feature construction and alignment from raw sources, guided by financial domain expertise and economic theory-driven selection. The dataset was partitioned chronologically, allocating $15 \%$ for validation and $15 \%$ for final testing to preserve temporal order and prevent data leakage. Multiple classifiers of varying algorithmic complexity were evaluated using walk-forward and rolling-window validation schemes designed to emulate real deployment conditions. Rolling-window validation consistently yielded superior temporal stability, demonstrating the effectiveness of recency-weighted learning in non-stationary financial environments. Ensemble learning represented in Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) with rolling window training achieved the highest performance, attaining $91.0 \%$ accuracy, an F1-score of 0.87, and a ROC-AUC of 0.98 on the test set. Limitations include class sparsity and imperfect threshold calibration, highlighting the need for future work in probabilistic decision tuning.

title: Fixed-Time Synchronization of Delayed Chaotic Memristive Neural Networks Modeling in Terms of Voltage-Charge-Time
authors: Yanting Huang, Jiening Wu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper addresses fixed-time interlayer synchronization for delayed chaotic duplex memristive neural networks (MNNs) . First, a continuous MNNs model is established using a charge-controlled HP memristor operating in the voltage-charge-time domain (x, q, t), with such memristors are treated as time-continuous and dynamically varying parameters. Subsequently, novel fixed-time (FXT) controllers are developed based on Lyapunov functions and inequality techniques, ensuring complete synchronization of voltage and charge states between the two layers of MNN within a fixed settling time, independent of initial conditions. The correctness and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified by numerical simulation.

title: Machine Learning for Clinical Text Classification in Electronic Medical Records: A Review
authors: Hasan Abdulla, Fatema AlJazeeri, Nabil Hewahi, Wael M El-Medany
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The proliferation of electronic medical records has produced large volumes of narrative clinical text that are difficult to curate through manual abstraction. Advances in machine learning and natural language processing now enable scalable clinical text classification, supporting diagnostic decision-making, safety surveillance, and population analytics. This systematic review synthesizes studies published between 2019 and 2025, analyzing methodological choices across data preprocessing, representation, model families, evaluation practices, and clinical applicability. Reporting follows PRISMA 2020, with a PRISMA-informed protocol guiding the review planning. Searches of crossRef-indexed sources identified 830 records; after de-duplication and screening, 58 studies met the inclusion criteria. Evidence from comparable tasks indicates that transformer-based encoders (BERT family and domainadapted variants) generally outperform conventional baselines for EMR text classification. At the same time, simpler models remain competitive in constrained settings. Persistent challenges include heterogeneous datasets, limited external validation, class imbalance, and incomplete reporting of calibration and interpretability. The review consolidates a deployment pipeline and provides recommendations on governance, transparency, generalization testing, and lifecycle monitoring.

title: Dynamic Neuro-Fuzzy Fusion with Attention-Aware Learning for Robust Cardiovascular Disease Prediction
authors: E. Nithyakala, V. Srikanth, J.K. Kanimozhi, K. Jeevitha, C.A. Kandasamy, P. Kalyanasundaram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The objective of this research work is to enhance the precision of cardiovascular disease predictions through the DNAFN approach and then compare it with that of a LightGBM model. Two experiments have been done in a group manner. Group 1 was for training the LightGBM model on clinical and lifestyle factors associated with cardiovascular disease. Group 2 was for training the designed DNAFN model on neuro-fuzzy learning with the assistance of the attention-weighted feature. The experimental work has been done on the Framingham cardiovascular disease study for training purposes on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 2 4 0}$</tex> samples. Statistical analysis has also been done utilizing the SPSS software with a two-sample t-test. The Dynamic NeuroFuzzy Fusion with Attention-Aware Learning (DNAFN) model was significantly higher at 98 % compared to 90 % for the LightGBM model, in addition to better precision, recall, and F1score. Although the learning mechanism for the DNAFN model is more complex, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-value obtained from the independent samples t-test was found to be less than 0.05, thus showing robust statistical evidence for the significance of performance difference between DNAFN and LightGBM. From this experiment, it has been proven that the proposed DNAFN model is more accurate and precise in making predictions regarding cardiovascular disease than the baseline model LightGBM.

title: Quantum Safe AI Pipelines Securing Next Generation Data Flows Against Post Quantum Threats
authors: Bhumika Shah
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: With the development of the sphere of quantum computing, an unprecedented threat to the old cryptography technology is beginning to emerge and, consequently, the safety of the AI pipelines fed with substantial amounts of data is now jeopardized. The author of the review marks the intersection of post-quantum cryptography and AI architectures and proposes the proposal of quantum-safe AI pipelines as an active measure in response to the new quantum threats. The software that has been reviewed throughout the paper is: Internet of Things (IoT), vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, autonomous navigation, healthcare, 6 G networks and global supply chain. It puts emphasis on the possible implementation of lattice-based, code-based and hash-based cryptography self-protection application directly to the AI systems to make the attempts to provide confidentiality of the information and integrity of the models in the long-term. In addition to that, the paper will be concerned with the application of the quantum machine learning or the hybrid reinforcement learning model as the next step in the enhancement of the nature of threats detection and adaptive defense. It is the quantum resistant systems that make the current systems and future AI safe due to the evolving nature of the post quantum cybersecurity threats.

title: The Autonomous Battlefield: AI Arms Race in Cybersecurity, Offensive Tactics, and the Need for a Global Framework
authors: Rajdip Chatterjee, Shameemul Haque
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence is rapidly growing in cybersecurity as both a strong attack weapon and a necessary defensive weapon. In the first part of the article, Offensive Tactics, we discuss how attackers leverage AI technology as part of their social engineering attacks (e.g. through sophisticated types of phishing based on multiple principles including respecting authority and generating authority). The second part of the article, Intelligent Countermeasures, describes how security professionals utilize AI-based threat hunting to examine large data sets for signs of hidden and persistent threats through the detection of subtle differences and changes in systems. The final section of the article, The Future of Digital Conflict, describes how these two fighting systems will increasingly compete in independent, machine-tomachine warfare without intervention by humans. Finally, the conclusion states that technology on its own cannot create a secure environment. Rather, we need fundamentally different structures that take into account international policies, ethical standards, and use of AI for surveillance in order to manage this global digital conflict.

title: Autonomous Edge AI Solution for Post-Harvest Mango Tommy Classification
authors: Juan Sebastián Villota-Cáceres, Javier Alberto Chaparro-Preciado PhD
abstract: Colombian mango export industry faces significant challenges in post-harvest classification processes, relying predominantly on manual inspection methods that limit competitiveness in international markets. Rural agricultural zones lack reliable internet connectivity and computational infrastructure, preventing the adoption of cloud-based Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions. This paper presents an autonomous edge computing approach for Tommy mango classification that operates entirely on embedded devices without external connectivity requirements. We developed and implemented a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture on the MaixCam platform, achieving 96.33% accuracy in real-world tests while processing images in 70.3ms. The system outperformed human expert classification (87.00%) by 9.33 percentage points and demonstrated 30-fold improvement in processing speed. Our solution addresses critical infrastructure limitations in developing agricultural regions while maintaining export quality standards compliance. The autonomous operation, low power consumption (1.5W), and portable design make this approach viable for widespread adoption in resource-constrained environments.

title: Comparative Analysis of Supervised Machine Learning Techniques for Predicting Student Academic Performance
authors: Bhoomi Gupta, Tarun Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This research study proposes ten different supervised learning algorithms such as Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, XGBoost, and LightGBM, to predict student performance based on different factors that can affect it. For binary classification, we divided the students into two groups, pass and fail, based on their final grades. Along with that, we also worked with multi-class classification to see how the performance changes when the prediction setup becomes more detailed. After evaluating the results, we observed that Logistic Regression performed the best in the binary case with an accuracy of 93.4 %, which shows that even simple models can give very good results when the problem is clearly defined. At the same time, ensemble and boosting models showed great results in the multi-class setup, as they are able to handle more complex patterns in the data. From the results, it seems that how the problem is defined matters a lot more than just using a more complex model.

title: Reassessing Venture Capital’s Role in IPO Outcomes: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Analytical Framework Using Evidence from India
authors: Bhuyashi Talukdar, Rudra P Pradhan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The present paper investigates the effect of venture capital (VC) affiliation on the short-run performance of initial public offerings (IPOs) in India within three months of listing. The study compares two outcome measures, Equity Return (ER) and Benchmark-Adjusted Return (BAR), using a comprehensive dataset of 518 IPOs issued between 2009 and 2023. Unlike existing research that uses only econometric estimates or predictive models, this paper adopts a hybrid framework combining the twostage System Generalized Method of Moments (GMM) with machine learning (ML) techniques, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Shapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP). This approach enables both causal inference and feature-wise interpretability, providing deeper insights into IPO performance. The findings indicate that VC-backed IPOs underperform relative to their non-VC-backed counterparts during the first quarter post-listing. These results challenge the applicability of the certification hypothesis in developing countries. On the other hand, factors like firm age, issue size, and market sentiment are significant positive predictors of IPO success, further justifying the significance of firm fundamentals and market conditions. Using a chronological hold-out approach, the tree-based ML models outperform a logistic baseline, indicating a usable predictive signal in pre-listing features beyond VC affiliation. SHAP analysis further highlights that VC affiliation typically shows a modest negative attribution conditional on other features. This study contributes to the field of entrepreneurial finance by challenging the widely accepted assumptions and building upon a hybrid approach that combines econometric and ML techniques, offering practical insights to entrepreneurs, VCs, investors, and policymakers in emerging markets.

title: ANN–GA–PSO Hybrid Optimization for Enhanced DV-HOP Localization in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Sutapa Mondal, Alok Kumar, Rimjhim Gupta, Ritesh Kumar Upadhyay, Sushanta Mahanty, Dilip Kumar Halder
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Present work proposes a predictive localization outcome when compared to Swarm Artificial Neural Network (SANN), Spatio-Temporal Neural Genetic Algorithm (STNGA) and Linear Ridge Radial Basis Function (LRRBF). To locate node is a pivotal process for providing increased reliability, energy efficiency and performance of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). The traditional approach such as the Distance Vector–Hop (DV–HOP) algorithm are error prone and they have very large localization errors especially in both large-scale networks randomly deployed. It is observed that the HCMSPSO–DV–HOP model enhances hop size estimation using increased level of swarm-based optimization, but there is a scope for further improving its precision and stability among different deployment conditions. A novel Hybrid learning scheme of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), Genetic Algorithms (GAs) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) based on loss-function corrected HCMSPSO-DV-HOP is introduced to improve the localization accuracy in existing framework. During training, PSO drives the solution refinement additionally helping faster and more stable convergence to global optimum as well. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the ANN–GA–PSO fusion model presents a more predictive localization outcome in comparison to SANN, STNGA and LRRBF. Other performance metrics (MSE, RMSE and MAE) show that the new approach not only reduces prediction error but also eases training.

title: Omni-Tracker: An IoT-Based AI Companion for Emergency Alert and Geo-Localization System
authors: Swetha Reddy A, Revathi S, Hema Darshini N, Yagnamurthy S S B Anupama, Saravanan S, Pavithra S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies has enabled the development of intelligent systems capable of improving personal safety and emergency response mechanisms. Conventional emergency alert systems often depend on manual communication or smartphone-based applications, which may not be effective during critical situations where immediate assistance is required. This paper presents Omni-Tracker, an IoT-based context-aware emergency alert and geo-localization system designed to provide rapid communication and accurate location tracking during emergency events. The proposed system integrates an ESP8266 microcontroller with an A9G GPS-GSM module and a touch sensor-based emergency trigger. When the trigger is activated, the system automatically retrieves the geographical location of the user and sends emergency alerts containing GPS coordinates to predefined contacts through SMS communication. The system can also initiate an emergency phone call and transmit contextual information such as timestamp, signal strength, and battery status. Additionally, the device supports remote status monitoring through SMS commands and includes a fallback location mechanism when GPS signals are unavailable. Experimental evaluation shows that the proposed system enables reliable emergency communication with minimal response delay and provides an efficient solution for personal safety, elderly monitoring, and smart city emergency applications.

title: Advancing Eigenvalue Computation: Numerical Techniques and Cross-Disciplinary Applications
authors: Aakash Gadh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Eigenvalue algorithms like Power Iteration, Hessenberg Reduction, and the QR Algorithm with Shift are used widely in today's world. The size, sparsity, and spectral characteristics of the matrix are what determine which method to choose. Through analysis of their relative efficiency, convergence and stability, we assess their validity with reference to applications in engineering and physical sciences. The paper discusses how choosing an appropriate algorithm can enhance performance in various fields, including vibrational analysis in aerospace engineering or stability modelling in astrophysics. The comparison findings can aid researchers in selecting suitable methods for the computing task at hand.

title: Multimodal Transformer-Based Framework for Predicting Student Engagement, Achievement and Creativity
authors: Shweta Goyal, Shakhboz Meyliqulov, Muratova Fotima Normaxmat Qizi, Kutliyeva Feruza, Gayrat Raximov, Mamlakat Jumaniyozova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The fast growth of online learning systems in India has created a variety of data that include academic, behavioural and linguistic aspects of student learning. Multimodal information integration is required to produce complex outcomes, including engagement, accomplishment, and creativity; however, traditional machine learning algorithms, such as decision trees and random forests, only prove their effectiveness when dealing with structured information. Two frameworks would be compared in this research paper: one of them the Transformer-based model (DistilBERT structured features), and the other one the Random Forest with a baseline where only engineered features are involved. A detailed prediction framework was created based on the creation of an over 23,000 student record of performance scores, screen-time trends and forum activities. The results of experiments indicate that the Transformer model achieves considerably greater scores than the Random Forest, to a lesser extent, in classification and regressive problems, particularly in creative evaluation by taking into account semantics and syntax as well as nuanced behavioural indicators of participation. In a move to make the analysis interpretable, SHAP-based analysis has highlighted the impact of the most important predictors, which include exam scores, time spent studying, screen time and lexical diversity. The research demonstrates the usefulness of multimodal AI in educational analytics in practice indicating the way forward to investigate scalable, explainable, and adaptive learning systems.

title: Decoding Automation Narratives in Heritage Education and Tourism: A Google Trends Inquiry into Optimization Dynamics
authors: Mohammad Irfan, Kashish Ajit Singh, Saikat Gochhait, Zafrul Allam
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: An evaluation of integrating automation into heritage education and tourism with the help of Workplace Automation Systems (WAS) effects on Optimization with the help of Cointegration Causality Analysis to measure these effects. The research uses Google Trends data (2020-2025) to understand the Workplace Efficiency (WPE) and Employee Wellbeing (EWB) and Decision Quality (DQ) and Operational Safety (OS) relations through Graphical Trend Analysis and Augmented Dickey Fuller (ADF) Test and Johansen’s Cointegration Test and Granger Causality Test and Multiple Linear Regression Analysis. Automation systems lead to distinct efficiency improvements in workplaces that preserve both employee health and superior decision output. The Granger Causality Test proves that important workplace variables form a reciprocal relationship because automation systems advance performance enhancement. Cointegration analysis shows that automated systems create lasting connections with efficient processes which would benefit performance. Standards regarding workforce transition, ethical considerations and cybersecurity threats still need to be addressed in order to achieve successful implementation. Strategic Artificial Intelligence (AI) implementation together with trained workforce programs along with regulatory guides must be established to produce optimal automation advantages through risk management techniques. The research delivers operational directions to the heritage education and tourism through Human Centered Automation (HCA) for their journey toward lasting workplace transformation. A strategic HCA framework is suggested in the paper to facilitate sustainable and culturally responsive automation for architectural heritage.

title: AI-Powered Personalization for Enhancing Student Engagement in E-Learning
authors: Vikas Nain, Rajeev Prasher
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: E-learning has opened up a lot of choices for easy and flexible education in today's fast-changing digital environment. But even though it has a lot of good points, many students still have trouble staying engaged. Generic course content, excessive screen time, and not getting feedback right away are all things that might make students do poorly in school and lose interest. This study looks at how artificial intelligence (AI) may change that story. By adding AI-powered customization, we can make e-learning systems more flexible, motivating, and tailored to the needs of each student. We studied 120 students from three major online venues. We found that students were far more engaged, felt better, and did better when we employed technology like intelligent feedback systems, personalized learning paths, and real-time data monitoring. Along with a big decline in dropout rates, overall engagement scores went up. These findings show that AI is more than just a technical improvement; it is a transformative force that makes learning better and more focused on people.

title: Impact of Service Efficiency Factors on Buying Behaviour and Satisfaction on Digital Platform
authors: Boola Choudhary, Rajeev Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Understanding customer value is paramount for businesses to thrive in today's competitive market. Rajasthan being the largest state of the country has been the subject matter of various studies but analysis of generic contributors of the buying behavior of the customers in the state still rests in the research gap. This study aims to delve into the perspectives of consumers in Rajasthan, India, to identify the key factors that contribute to their buying behavior. By examining buying behavior from a consumer-centric lens, businesses can gain valuable insights to enhance their offerings and foster long-lasting relationships. This study aimed to investigate the factors influencing customer value perception in Rajasthan. A survey of 270 respondents was conducted in Ajmer, Jaipur, Jodhpur, and Kota. The data was analyzed using factor analysis, descriptive analysis and multiple regression analysis. The study will significantly contribute to the existing body of knowledge on customer value by identifying specific determiners of buying behavior of customers in Rajasthan. The findings will be valuable for businesses operating in or considering entry into the Rajasthani market.

title: Activity concentration measurements of radon gas in indoor environment of Benevento city, Campania region - South Italy
authors: Chiara De Vita, Mariagabriella Pugliese, Giuseppe La Verde, Chiara Imparato, Fulvio Coppola, Misbah Javed, Carlo Sabbarese, Fabrizio Ambrosino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The Italian National Action Plan for radon 2023-2032 (PNAR) published in GU Serie Generale n.43 of 21-02-2024, together with the Italian Legislative Decree (D.Lgs) 101/2020, requires the Italian regions to monitor radon indoor activity concentrations in order to identify radon priority areas. A preliminary map of Campania region radon priority areas, in Italy, has been recently realized, based on indoor radon concentration measurements, by means of solid state nuclear track detectors (CR-39), in workplaces, residential and public buildings. In this work, a focus on the radon measurements performed in the city of Benevento is reported.

title: Optimization and Simulation of a High-Speed Data and Power Transmission System Based on Partial Coil Coupling and DQPSK
authors: Dezhi Zhang, Liyan Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To enable reliable real-time communication between the isolated primary and secondary sides in an inductive wireless power transfer (IWPT) system, this paper proposes a novel synchronous wireless power and data transmission (SWPDT) system utilizing quadrature differential phase shift keying (4DPSK) modulation. To address the severe high-impedance bottleneck faced by high- frequency data signals when passing through the primary power coil, the proposed system introduces a unique partial coil coupling topology. This structure provides a dedicated low-impedance bypass path for the 4 MHz data carrier, significantly improving signal injection and extraction efficiency. On the secondary side, multi- stage LC series resonators and high-pass filter networks are used to separate the data signal from the 85 kHz power signal, followed by accurate data recovery via a coherent I/Q dual-path differential demodulation chain. Simulation results validate the superiority of the proposed system. At kilowatt-level power (output power of 1026 W), the system achieves a high power transmission efficiency of 94.56%.Notably, the data transmission rate reaches 1 Mbps with a bit error rate of 0, demonstrating that the proposed topology significantly enhances system data throughput while effectively suppressing high-power cross-interference.

title: Dynamic Topology Control Strategies for Enhanced Connectivity in Mobile Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Ramy Riad Al-Fatlawy, Saloni Bansal, Mudit Mittal, Arun Pratap Srivastava, Akhilesh Kumar Khan, Navdeep Dhaliwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The burgeoning domain of Mobile Wireless Sensor Networks (MWSNs) is under constant evolution, driven by the need for robust connectivity and coverage in a plethora of applications, from environmental monitoring to critical infrastructure surveillance. Dynamic topology control strategies stand at the forefront of this transformation, ensuring that the inherently mobile sensor nodes maintain effective communication despite their erratic movement patterns. This paper presents a comprehensive review of the existing literature, followed by an in-depth analysis of innovative topology control strategies that promise enhanced connectivity, optimized energy consumption, and improved overall network performance. Through simulations and real-world case studies, we demonstrate the efficacy of these strategies, underscoring the vital role of adaptive, predictive, and efficient network reconfiguration in the dynamic landscape of MWSNs.

title: A Transfer Learning Approach for Detecting Buds of Posterior Teeth
authors: Francesca Angelone, Parisa Soltani, Homa Baninajarian, Shakiba Sadeghizadeh, Amirhossein Vedae, Alessia La Rocca, Armando Ricciardi, Mario Sansone, Maria Romano, Francesco Amato, Alfonso Maria Ponsiglione
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence-based medical image analysis represents a critical step towards more sustainable and prevention-oriented diagnosis and healthcare. Early and accurate identification of dental anomalies in pediatric patients is an underexplored field of study, despite dental pathologies being one of the most costly medical conditions in the world. This study proposes a transfer learning approach for automatic detection of first and second premolar tooth buds on pediatric panoramic radiographs, through a YOLOv8 architecture, optimized on a labeled dataset of 619 images. Evaluation metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP@50 = 0.984), Dice coefficient (0.847), and Structural Similarity Index Metric (SSIM = 0.947) demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. However, the model performance decreases with more stringent localization criteria (mAP@50-95 = 0.610) and is sensitive to image quality, with degraded radiographs negatively impacting the detection accuracy. These limitations highlight the importance of high-quality image acquisition and suggest the need for further studies to improve generalization across different clinical acquisition conditions. Despite these challenges, the results suggest that the model could represent a first step in an automated early diagnosis and treatment planning pipeline.

title: Prediction of Underwater Sound Speed Distribution Based on Liquid Neural Networks
authors: Sitong Liu, Wei Huang, Hao Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Underwater sound speed prediction is an important method of sound speed estimation, and its unique advantage lies in the fact that it does not require on-site underwater data measurement. Traditional sound speed prediction methods are primarily based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks and Transformer neural networks. The parameter scale of these models is large, requiring ample training data, which is difficult to obtain for many ocean regions. To tackle this problem, we establish a lightweight liquid neural network model for predicting sound speed distribution (SSP-LNN). The parameter count of this model is only half of that of the LSTM model, making it less prone to overfitting when trained with a small number of samples, thus exhibiting better generalization ability.

title: Next-Generation Network Architectures: Exploring 6G, VANETs, and Heterogeneous Communication Systems
authors: Astha Gupta, Raj Kumar, Natalya Yaronova, Richa Vijay, P. Sarasu, Harjeet Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The evolution toward the sixth generation (6G) communication networks is a paradigm shift in terms of communications, intelligence, and adaptability. This paper examines architectural aspects of the next generation of communication systems combined with 6G, Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) and heterogeneous (HetNet) frameworks. By combining some of the latest in nomadic structures, artificial intelligence and learning-based optimization and the capabilities of Vehicle-toEverything (V2X) technologies, the study points out how 6G powers dynamic and context aware communication within the terrestrial, aerial and satellite domain. This paper presents a self-adaptive network selection (ANSE) and multi-band data integration carrier aggregation representation to further increase reliability and throughput rates for high-mobility environments. A performance analysis proves the scalability of hybrid 6GVANET systems focusing on the importance of autonomous transport, edge computing, and smart city infrastructures. The results indicate that the combination of AI, reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, and ultra-low-latency networking will be the skeleton of resilient, intelligent, and safe communication systems that are next-generation.

title: Transport Protocol Switching Mechanism for Throughput Optimization in Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Yizhen Pan, Xin Xu, Liangdong Wei, Jun Cai
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Ad Hoc networks face significant challenges for reliable data transport due to limited spectral resources and dynamic channel conditions. This paper investigates the throughput performance of four reliable transport protocols—TCP, SCTP, UDT, and Mockets—under varying packet loss conditions in wireless environments. Experimental results demonstrate that while TCP and SCTP perform well under low-loss conditions (<2%), the middleware-based Mockets protocol significantly outperforms the other three protocols in high-loss environments (>3%). Based on these findings, we propose a protocol switching mechanism using a 2% packet loss threshold. Experimental evaluations show that this mechanism achieves 34MB total data transfer within a 60-second window, outperforming standalone TCP (10MB) and Mockets (24MB), thereby enhancing overall network throughput.

title: MIGformer: A Multivariate Time Series Forecasting Model Based on Multi-scale Interaction and Gating Mechanism
authors: Zeyu Hu, Yuan Jia, Zhenhong Jia, Wu Le
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting faces significant challenges due to non-stationarity, complex variable correlations, and multi-scale noise. Recently, the Inverted Transformer has achieved outstanding performance; however, it still exhibits limitations in multi-scale feature fusion and modeling local variable interactions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel model named MIGformer, which incorporates three core components. First, the Multi-scale Temporal Refinement Module utilizes a pyramid decomposition-fusion strategy and a temporal attention mechanism to refine multi-scale features while explicitly suppressing noise. Second, the Patch-level Channel Enhancement Module leverages Multilayer Perceptrons and channel attention to capture intra-patch interactions, thereby enhancing local contextual semantics. Finally, the Multi-View Gating Mechanism integrates linear, convolutional, and pooling perspectives to generate gating masks for dynamic feature calibration. Experimental results on multiple standard datasets demonstrate that MIGformer achieves superior forecasting accuracy, reducing the Mean Squared Error by an average of 5.36% compared to the backbone model iTransformer, verifying its robustness in complex multivariate scenarios.

title: Machine learning potential and applications in manufacturing sectors
authors: Virendra Kumar Verma, Savita Verma, Sumit Gupta, Deepika Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) has transformational potential in the manufacturing sector. This overview discusses its uses, such as process optimization, quality assurance, and predictive maintenance. Manufacturers may decrease downtime, improve product quality, and boost overall productivity by utilizing data-driven insights. Machine learning algorithms provide real-time decision-making and adaptive production that supports competitiveness and support to minimize and optimize costs. This overview study explores the machine learning potential and applications in manufacturing sectors and key highlighting significant in manufacturing sectors.

title: Design and Implementation of Energy-Efficient Protocols for Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: N Shalini, Ruchira Rawat, Haider Alabdeli, A L N Rao, Akhil Sankhyan, Manish Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the design and implementation of energy-efficient protocols for Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs), aiming to address the significant challenges posed by underwater environments. UWSNs are increasingly used in a variety of applications, including marine ecosystem monitoring, resource exploration, and surveillance. However, the underwater setting introduces unique obstacles such as limited energy resources, harsh acoustic conditions, and complex node deployment, necessitating the development of specialized protocols. Through an extensive review of literature, simulation, and empirical analysis, we propose adaptive communication strategies and energy-aware mechanisms that can lead to a more sustainable UWSN. The paper evaluates the performance of these protocols in terms of energy consumption and network efficiency, offering insights into their scalability and reliability.

title: Structural damage identification through PCA and subspace comparison
authors: Gianna Agrò, Elio Lo Giudice, Giuseppe Mugnos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: A numerical study is presented to assess the sensitivity of a PCA-based subspace comparison method for structural damage detection. Results demonstrate that principal angles between subspaces reliably quantify damage severity, confirming the robustness of the approach under controlled conditions.

title: A Practical Methodology for Implementing and Tuning Analog PID-Based PWM Controllers
authors: Joey Florent, Renee Anderson, Wala Saadeh
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This paper presents a practical, reusable workflow for implementing and tuning analog proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controllers that drive a PWM power stage. The methodology emphasizes continuous-time signal paths and hardware-tunable responses, enabling stable closed-loop control without sampling latency. A constant-current regulation prototype validates the approach using a shunt-sense instrumentation amplifier and an op-amp PID network whose output modulates a comparator-based PWM. With an RC feedback time constant of 5 ms and a switching frequency of 5 kHz, the system achieves steady-state current error as low as 0.2% and critically damped settling of 8 ms under resistive load steps. Tuning guidelines are provided for underdamped, overdamped, and critically damped regimes, together with practical insights on PWM frequency selection, RC filtering, gate-drive considerations, and PCB layout for noise mitigation. Although demonstrated for current regulation, the workflow is directly applicable to other analog PWM-controlled plants such as motor drives, LED drivers, and power regulation loops.

title: Mechanism and Suppression of Low-Frequency Oscillations in Lightly Loaded V/f-Controlled Induction Motors
authors: Min Wu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Light-load V/f-controlled induction motors frequently experience low-frequency oscillations driven by the inverter dead-time effect, specifically through equivalent resistance and zero-current clamping. To mitigate this, a comprehensive pulse-width strategy integrating compensations for pulse width, device forward voltage drop, and zero-current clamping is proposed. Experiments confirm this method effectively reduces phase current harmonics and restores complete system stability.

title: Real-Time Analytics and Scalable Orchestration for Multi-Tenant Personalization Systems
authors: Srivardhan Jalan, Vinod Kumar Thottempudi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: A fundamental change in the provisioning, access, and scaling of computer systems has been brought about by the widespread use of cloud computing. A key component of the cloud framework, multi-tenancy allows several individuals or companies (tenants) to access a common equipment while preserving conceptual statistics and asset distinction. This approach provides particular difficulties in striking a balance between efficiency optimization and safety regulation, despite its significant expense and scalable benefits. The advent of generative AI (GenAI) has introduced transformative possibilities for multi-tenant Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) ecosystems, enabling unprecedented real-time customization and personalization capabilities. This research investigates the application of generative AI in tailoring tenant-specific user interfaces (UIs), workflows, and content within multi-tenant architectures. By leveraging GenAI models such as transformer-based neural networks and diffusion models, the study demonstrates how these technologies facilitate dynamic adaptations of PaaS environments, ensuring seamless alignment with tenant-specific requirements. A critical focus of the paper is the evaluation of performance trade-offs and resource implications associated with GenAI deployment in multi-tenant architectures. The computational duration introduced by realtime AI inferences is analyzed, with proposed optimization techniques. The empirical findings displayed that the method of BERT was the most successful, with the highest accuracy of 94.6, to cut down the number of unauthorized access attempts by 61 % and detection time by 48. The study concludes by identifying future directions for generative AI in multi-tenant PaaS, including the exploration of multimodal generative models capable of synthesizing heterogeneous data sources for comprehensive customization.

title: A Novel LLM-RAG Approach for Risk-Aware Anti-Money Laundering Compliance
authors: Manoja R, Rathika P, Srividhya B, Akshaya M, Hariharan K, Janaki P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This study proposes a novel LLM-RAG based risk-aware anti-money laundering framework to detect phishing links, fake OTPs, suspicious calls, and fraudulent transactions while reducing the false positives rate using content-aware analysis. Group 1: refers Random Forest and LSTM analyse structured transaction features and historical fraud details to detect suspicious activities. Group 2: refers to the novel LLM-RAG framework that integrates structured transaction data with unstructured fraud intelligence to perform contextual reasoning, risk scoring, and generate explainable alerts. The LLM-RAG based Anti-Money Laundering framework outperforms the LSTM and Random Forest models. The accuracy of the framework ranges between 75 % and 92 %, with an average accuracy of 83.75 %, which is greater than the existing methods. The proposed framework also shows better precision, recall, and F1-score values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81.00 \%, 78.25 \%$</tex>, and 79.50 % respectively. Statistical analysis proves that the improvement in performance is highly significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p} {< 0. 0 0 1}$</tex>. The Large Language Models with Retrieval Augmented Generation system provides high accuracy and enhanced security for AML compliance by integrating the analysis of structured and unstructured data with the intelligence of LLM-RAG, which leads to accurate risk assessment, low false positives, and enhanced financial security. This framework supports the Transaction Monitoring system, enables effective risk scoring, and ensures risk-aware compliance through detailed statistical performance analysis.

title: DEFI 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)

title: CloudChain: Decentralized Marketplace for AI Compute Resources
authors: Monika Malik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructures have been centralized leading to limited accessibility, monopoly of computational resources, and an uneven distribution of services. CloudChain is a decentralized AI compute market that is made out of blockchain and can solve these challenges with a transparent, trustless, and fair system. It brings together decentralized storage, smart contracts, as well as token incentives to allow fairness, privacy, and auditing. Privacy is ensured through encryption and zero-knowledge proofs, task allocation, distribution of rewards and enforcement of SLA is automated through smart contracts. The performance metrics measured in a 30-day simulation of the major cloud providers (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, Hetzner, Lambda Labs) and the community nodes included the performance measures of latency, throughput, and SLA compliance, as well as token allocation and resource utilization. Findings indicate that CloudChain does provide the necessary balance in the workloads, high quality in the service delivery, and equitable rewards among heterogeneous members. The suggested framework envisioned will create a democratized, secure, and sustainable platform of decentralized AI, enabling innovation, openness, and diversity of global AI ecosystems.

title: Signal Time-Advance Measurement with LP-NGD Circuit
authors: Idiris Aweis Hussein, Nathan B. Gurgel, Willys Noa, Ali Hamada Damien Fakra, Sébastien Lalléchère, Blaise Ravelo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: This paper investigates on the time-advance test method of arbitrary waveform signal generated by a low-pass (LP) type negative group delay (NGD) circuit. The considered active topology is constituted by RL-network circuit. The design theory of LP-NGD function is recalled for the basic comprehension by expressing the NGD value at very low frequency and the NGD cut-off frequency. The LP-NGD circuit design methodology including the design equations enabling to choose the resistors and inductor constituting components of the proof-of-concept from the targeted time-advance specifications is shortly addressed. The proof-of-concept implemented as a printed circuit board (PCB) prototype is designed by considering the desired time-advance of about -300 ns. Frequency domain simulations confirm the relevance of the LP-NGD theory. After the prototype description, the experimental setup is explained. Despite the noise effect, measured time-advances of about -255 ns in good agreement with simulation were observed. Potential applications of LP-NGD circuit are proposed.

title: Modular Robot-Assisted Haptic Simulation of a Shoulder Exoskeleton
authors: Ramazan Gökay, Seyed Milad Mir Latifi, Alexander Stark, Rajal Nagwekar, Julian Öltjen, Robert Weidner
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: This paper presents a modular robot-assisted exoskeleton haptic simulation environment that uses collaborative robots (cobots) and ROS2 to accelerate the development and testing of shoulder exoskeletons. Traditional exoskeleton development requires multiple physical prototypes, resulting in considerable time and resource expenditure. The proposed system uses reconfigurable cobot formations to physically simulate various exoskeleton types and properties before final product development. Within various formations, different exoskeletons and support scenarios can be simulated. This approach offers a scalable, cost-effective method for exoskeleton design optimization that can be extended to other joint systems beyond the shoulder complex.

title: Neural Network-Based Black Box Modelling for Real-Time Super Capacitor Health Monitoring
authors: Ajib Kumar Kar, A.S.S.Murugan, M Mamatha Laxmi, Vijetha, S. Gnana Prasanna, J Kavitha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: A neural network-based black box modelling technique for real-time supercapacitor health monitoring under fluctuating electrical and thermal circumstances is presented in this research. The nonlinear behaviour of supercapacitors is difficult for conventional equivalent circuit models to adequately represent, especially in dynamic situations with changing load profiles and temperature changes. A Multiple Input Single Output (MISO) feedforward Artificial Neural Network (ANN) is trained to predict the terminal voltage response considering temperature, current and capacitance to overcome these restrictions. Experimental data from supercapacitor topologies evaluated between -20°C and +60°C, namely 2700F, 3700F, and 325F, is used to train and validate the model. A significant correlation between the predicted as well as experimental voltage values, in addition to low average quadratic errors, are outcomes of the effective training employing the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm. By accurately recreating both learned and unusual test cases, the ANN proves its remarkable generalizability. With its compact, precise, and extensible embedded diagnostics solution, this research has tremendous potential for power electronic systems, smart grids and electric vehicles that necessitate adaptive and real-time monitoring of energy storage.

title: How Does the Decoy Effect Enhance Consumers' Purchasing Decisions?
authors: Le Phuoc Cuu Long, Le Thi Huong Trang, Van Ho Than Ai
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: Today, competition for businesses is becoming stronger globally in general and for Agency businesses in particular. That makes product pricing and price for customers an extremely important factor to promote customer purchasing behavior. Based on this concern, it is necessary to implement specific strategies to offer prices that are suitable for consumers while bringing maximum benefits to the company. A pricing strategy that is often used but rarely studied is the decoy effect. This study aims to determine the motivation of the Decoy Effect on consumer purchasing decisions. The theory of the Decoy Effect has been proven to have an impact in marketing and influence on customer behavior, as demonstrated in research in 1982, but in the context of the Agency service industry, it has not been studied yet. Therefore, this study will explore the impact of the Decoy Effect on enhancing customers' purchasing decisions. The collected data were processed using confirmatory factor analysis and exploratory factor analysis. To evaluate the initial standard of the data, the author determined the reliability of the data through the Cronbach's Alpha coefficient of the scales, all of which were greater than 0.7. The research results showed that the Decoy Effect promotes purchasing decisions and creates consumer intentions.

title: The Impact of Short Video Content on TikTok on Gen Z's Purchasing Behavior
authors: Tran Ha Vy, Nguyen Thi Nhu Quynh, Le Trinh Thanh Tam, Ngo Hai Quynh
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: The explosion of short video platforms (e.g. TikTok) has greatly changed consumer behavior, especially among Generation Z, which in turn has greatly affected the way businesses market their products. This study aims to explore and measure the influence of several factors on Generation Z's purchasing behavior in the context of short video marketing by addressing three main research questions: (1) What factors and their influence on Generation Z's purchase intention? (2) What strategic solutions can enhance the positive impact of these factors? Quantitative research methods were conducted with a sample size of 300 people aged 15-30. AMOS 20 was employed to calculate the parameters and evaluate the fit of the proposed model. The results were as expected with the strong influence of short video content, entertainment and social interaction on user attitudes, which in turn lead to their actual purchasing behavior. Interestingly, user trust has no relationship with their attitudes. This highlights the uniqueness of digital marketing, where entertainment, emotional appeal, and short video content and community interaction are providing a more authentic experience, and may overshadow the role of user trust in shaping attitudes. The study boldly reinforces the trend of leveraging user-generated contributions and "Mood broad" short video strategies from marketers to enhance user purchase behavior and engagement. A unique result, integrating emotion, entertainment, and social into platform models, has contributed to expanding the TAM and TPB theories, thereby explaining the nature and decision-making process of Gen Z in the digital environment. Cross-platform and cross-cultural comparative studies to further examine the role of trust in this model are encouraged to conduct a more comprehensive view.

title: MERIT: Multimodal Enhanced Retrieval through Instruction-level Transformer-based Chunking in Product Manuals
authors: Trinh The Minh, Van Duc Cuong, Le Hong Ha Anh, Nguyen Van Son, Huynh Cong Phap, Nguyen Thi Hanh
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: Product manuals are critical in helping users understand and operate consumer products. Yet, their increasing length and reliance on complex graphical elements often frustrate information retrieval. Traditional Product Manual Question Answering (PMQA) systems primarily focus on text and overlook visual content such as icons and diagrams, resulting in incomplete or ambiguous answers. This gap highlights the need for a multimodal approach that integrates textual and visual cues to ensure accurate and contextually grounded support. In this paper, we introduce MERIT, a novel multimodal QA framework that advances beyond page-level and text-only baselines. MERIT segments manuals into fine-grained, semantically coherent instruction chunks and encodes them using a Mixture-of-Experts Q-Former encoder, which effectively fuses textual summaries with graphical features. Trained under a contrastive learning objective, MERIT constructs a unified embedding space optimized for dense retrieval. A vision-language model then processes chunks and their associated graphics to generate accurate, fluent, and visually grounded answers. Extensive experiments on the PM209 dataset demonstrate that MERIT consistently improves retrieval precision and answer quality over state-of-the-art baselines, underscoring the importance of fine-grained multimodal reasoning for practical e-commerce applications.

title: Role of AI in Refugees Status Determination with Challenges of Human Dignity
authors: Randeep Kaur, Bindu Jindal, Shalini Gupta, Manish Yadav, Aastha Agarwal, Kunjana Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The globally refugee problem motivates the desire to create refugee status (RSD) in a more ethical, equitable, and efficient manner. Traditional RSD methods—which are mostly manual and subjective—are finding less and less able to provide the necessary degree as the number of displaced persons rises in response to war, persecution, and climate change. The aim of this paper is to evaluate artificial intelligence (AI) revolutionary prospects in enhancing refugee evaluation systems. Studies prior to this largely focus on the moral and legal implications of such integration. The article assesses four artificial intelligence tools—NLP Screening, Biometric Matching, AI Fraud Detection, and Automated Decision Scoring—in a range of geographical areas using a mixed-methods research approach. The research also takes ethical issues of these technologies into account. Especially with respect to biometric systems, the results expose significant accuracy and efficiency benefits; nonetheless, they also highlight important ethical issues like the risk of dehumanization, algorithmic prejudice, and lack of openness. By means of statistical analysis and thematic interpretation, the research emphasizes the need to preserve human dignity and control over the treatment of refugees driven by artificial intelligence. This study aims to propose the use of artificial intelligence technologies protecting refugee rights as well as utilize technological innovations to raise the effectiveness of humanitarian activities in a manner both cautious and proactive.

title: State-Aware Multi-Service Scheduling for VLC/WiFi Heterogeneous Networks Based on Reinforcement Learning(SAMSS-QL)
authors: Menghan Ju, Zhuoxiu Wei, Liwei Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Addressing the challenges of fairness, latency, and throughput balancing in multi-service scheduling within VLC/WiFi heterogeneous networks, as well as the limited adaptability of existing algorithms, this paper proposes a state-aware multi-service scheduling algorithm (SAMSS-QL) based on reinforcement learning. This algorithm models the scheduler as an agent, constructing a state space by discretizing queue head delay urgency, queue congestion status, and channel quality. It integrates four classical scheduling strategies to form the action space and designs a composite reward function incorporating incentive mechanisms, achieving dynamic optimization through Q-Learning. Simulation results demonstrate that SAMSS-QL maintains a Jain fairness index consistently above 0.985, achieves average packet delays as low as 0.004–0.008 ms, and attains a system throughput approaching 4000 Mbps. By ensuring fairness and low latency while sacrificing only minimal throughput, it provides an efficient solution for resource scheduling in heterogeneous networks.

title: Lung Nodule Detection and Segmentation using Linear Karnal SVM
authors: Maruf-Uz-Zaman, Prabhat Kumar Ravi, Nitin Chakole, Rajeev Ranjan, T. V. Hyma Lakshmi, Sonu Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Lung cancer is mostly popular cancer in the world. Different technique used to find accuracy, speed and sensitivity. Computer Aided Detection (CAD) system is easy and common technique to detect lung cancer. In this work discuss about normal nodule, small nodule and micro nodule lung image and their segmentation. Feature extraction, pre-processing, data analysis and classifier are common step to detect lung nodules including linear-kernel function and different support vector machine. Accuracy compares with kernel function accuracy and discus about kernel function algorithms. Proposed work normal lung nodules accuracy 85.7% and after kernel function accuracy improved to 88.5% and small lung nodules accuracy 84.7% and after filter 89.5% and micro lung nodules accuracy is 89.7% and improved accuracy rate is 91.6% and also discuss about their comparative analysis.

title: AI-Driven Security In Cloud Networks
authors: Yogesh Ramaswamy, Vikram Nattamai Sankaran, Vinoth Thoguluva Santharam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The research centers on a comprehensive security system that aims to enhance data security via a four-pronged approach: Activity Driver, Analytics Engine, Activity Aggregator, and Activity Feeder. Logs from users, data from applications, and information from services associated with the identity provider are all pieces of information that it uses to build a common graph of entities and persons. The system's centralized data production of detailed user profiles, risk evaluations, and policy recommendations is powered by large language models and natural language processing (NLP) for analytics. Important use cases include identifying compromised accounts, identifying privilege abuse, and exfiltrating sensitive data. Scenarios for testing include data exfiltration, privilege abuse, and credential theft. The result can be used to demonstrate how the detection and response systems function. Constructing comparisons between peer and user data access activities can reveal security vulnerabilities and offer a critical level of knowledge into interaction patterns. As a result, the system may take preventive security steps and remediate incidents in real time.

title: Early Warning System for Systemic Financial Risk in Indian Financial Markets Using Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Murugan P, Suresh K S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: In contrast to dangers that are linked with particular firms, systemic financial risk has the potential to result in the collapse or instability of the full financial system or its whole. It is plausible for the Indian financial market to be sensitive to systemic risk, particularly during times of domestic or global financial crises, because of the various unique instruments and regulatory dynamics that it contains. The nonlinear and ever-changing interdependencies among financial data make it tough for traditional statistical methodologies to identify warning symptoms of systemic instability. The reason for this is that these markers work together. With this in mind, it is highly vital to create a prediction model that can uncover sophisticated patterns of risk that is also very dependable. The purpose of this research is to use artificial neural networks (ANNs) to build an early warning system that can find systemic financial danger in India. Market indices, interest rates, volatility indices, and nonperforming asset ratios from the past are used to train the model.

title: Leveraging NLP and AI for Optimizing Energy Efficiency in Smart Cities: A Green Technology Perspective
authors: Praveen Singh, Arjun Suresh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the pursuit of sustainable urban development, smart cities are increasingly adopting green technologies to reduce energy consumption and minimize environmental impact. However, the integration and optimization of these technologies require intelligent systems capable of analysing complex, heterogeneous data. This paper proposes a novel framework that leverages Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance energy efficiency in smart cities. By utilizing NLP techniques to analyse citizen feedback, social media discourse, and government reports, city planners can better understand public sentiment and identify opportunities for green policy improvements. Additionally, AI-based predictive models are employed to manage smart grid systems, optimize public transportation, and reduce carbon emissions. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate the potential of combining NLP and AI to drive energy-efficient decision-making in urban environments. This work aims to bridge the gap between technological innovation and environmental sustainability, providing actionable insights for policymakers and researchers.

title: AI-Powered Analysis of Machine Learning Techniques for Fake News Classification
authors: Saily Suresh Patil, Devyani Bhamre
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Fake news has emerged as a critical challenge in the digital era, influencing public opinion, political processes, and social harmony. The rapid growth of social media platforms has enabled misinformation to spread faster than ever before, necessitating automated mechanisms for detection and mitigation. This study analyses various machine learning techniques used for fake news classification, comparing their strengths, weaknesses, and applicability across different datasets. The research evaluates classical algorithms such as Naïve Bayes, Support Vector Machines (SVM), Logistic Regression, and Random Forest, as well as advanced deep learning models including LSTM and Transformers. A structured methodology involving data preprocessing, feature extraction using TF–IDF and word embeddings, and performance measurement based on accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score is implemented. Theoretical insights and empirical results indicate that deep learning models outperform traditional classifiers, particularly when dealing with complex linguistic features and large datasets. However, traditional algorithms remain efficient for small, balanced datasets with clear textual patterns. The findings emphasize the importance of combining linguistic, semantic, and contextual representations for robust fake news detection. This research contributes to the ongoing development of automated misinformation detection systems, ultimately supporting safer and more trustworthy online environments.

title: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
authors: N/A

title: A Hybrid ML Framework Integrating CAI and EFRI Metrics for Smart Predictive Maintenance of Power Transformers
authors: T.S.Suganya, S. Rukmani Devi, A. Belin Jude, Kada Tulasi, Sameer Yadav, Preshni Shrivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Power transformers produce rich multimodal telemetry (SCADA, DGA, FRA/EFRI, thermal imaging) that can enable predictive maintenance, but practical adoption is hindered by delayed fault detection, fragile performance when sensors are missing or asynchronous, and poorly calibrated risk outputs. The study presents Synapse-PdM, a physics-aware, causal, Bayesian multimodal Transformer that fuses modality-specific encoders with a causal disentanglement module, a hierarchical cross-modal mixture-of-experts Transformer, and a Temporal Graph Neural Network to capture topology-driven propagation. Synapse-PdM issues Bayesian survival-based EFRI curves and conformalized RUL quantiles, trained under a composite loss combining censored survival likelihood, quantile RUL losses, physics-informed regularization, causal penalties, self-supervised pretraining, and a decision-aware cost term. On held-out time-split evaluations Synapse-PdM achieves AUC-ROC 0.995, PR-AUC 0.968, RUL-MAE 4.8 days, and detects 82% of failures at least seven days ahead; Brier score is 0.060 and expected annual maintenance cost under the test cost model reduces to ₹9.15M. These results translate to earlier, better-calibrated alerts, fewer false alarms, and substantive operational savings, supporting pilot deployment in utility environments. The model’s interpretability and uncertainty outputs enable actionable maintenance decisions and facilitate regulator acceptance and scalability.

title: Detection of Credit Card Fraud: An Analytical Accounting Perspective Using Classification Algorithms
authors: Shaik Khaja Mohiddin Basha, G. Sathya, D.Vinoth, S Naveen Kumar, M Kalidas, G. Deepa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Detecting credit card fraud remains a significant challenge for financial institutions and auditors as digital payment volumes are increasing and fraud schemes are becoming increasingly complex. This paper reframes fraud detection through an analytical accounting lens by integrating ledger-aware features, multimodal evidence, and auditor-oriented explanations to improve triage and regulatory compliance. LedgerGuard-TransGNN is introduced: a Transformer + Graph Neural Network (GNN) hybrid with a Random Forest (RF) sensing frontend, hybrid feature selection, CIAE calibration, multimodal inputs (transactions, ledgers, Optical Character Recognition (OCR)), and blockchain provenance. Evaluation uses a combined IEEE-CIS and LedgerSim Indian corpus of about 2.1 million transactions. LedgerGuard-TransGNN achieved AUC = 0.97, F1 = 0.84, and MCC = 0.76, reduced false-alarm review costs to ₹1.2M, prevented an estimated ₹8.4M in fraud, and delivered 60% auditor time savings. Results demonstrate that embedding accounting artefacts into classification algorithms produces calibrated, auditable risk bins and measurable economic benefits for audit prioritisation and compliance. Policy-weighted Cost-Impact Aware Evaluation (CIAE) thresholds, auditor-feedback loops, and published LedgerSim configurations enable local adaptation and reproducibility. Operational pilots are recommended to validate long-term performance and human–machine interaction effects.

title: Corporate Governance’s Impact on Financial Performance of Banks
authors: Devinderpreet Kaur, Garima, Ashima Grag, Jaya Kaushik, Suman Khanna, Bhupinder Bajaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Corporate governance in the banking business is more vital than ever as the world's economies grow more connected and the financial system gets more complicated. The people who authored this study looked at how variables like the independence of boards of directors, the work of audit committees, concentration, and openness affect how effectively banks handle their money. The research looks for trends and explanations using descriptive statistics, correlation analysis, multiple regression, and analysis of variance (ANOVA). We apply the methods mentioned above to a large set of governance and financial data from a number of different commercial banks. The findings show that better governance frameworks are closely linked to better financial measures like Return on Assets (ROA), Return on Equity (ROE), and Net Interest Margin (NIM). Also, they are extremely closely linked to better governance. The audit committee met often, and the board of directors was independent, both of which were positive markers of solid financial performance. On the other side, a high level of ownership concentration was a bad sign. This paper is meant to provide governments, investors, and banks that want to improve governance as a strategy to generate more money and be more stable useful information.

title: Resilient Smart Grids: Digital Twin-Driven Predictive Control for Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Layth Hussein, Navdeep Singh, Arti Badhoutiya, Gudapalli Karuna, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on resilient smart grids enabled by digital twin–driven predictive control for seamless integration of renewable energy resources. We propose a layered framework that combines high-fidelity digital twins of grid assets, real-time state estimation, probabilistic renewable generation forecasting, and model-predictive control (MPC) enriched with learning-based surrogate models to achieve adaptive, robust, and low-latency operational decisions. The framework explicitly addresses multi-timescale coordination between transmission, distribution, and distributed energy resources (DERs), and incorporates resilience metrics to quantify and optimize the grid’s ability to withstand and recover from disturbances (e.g., extreme weather, cyber incidents, and component failures). Key technical contributions include (1) a hybrid physics-informed/data-driven digital twin architecture that fuses physics models with online machine-learning updates for drift correction; (2) a predictive-control algorithm that integrates probabilistic forecasts and constraint tightening to guarantee safe operation under uncertainty; (3) a scalable data-federation and communications design tailored for near-real-time twinning and closed-loop control; and (4) resilience-aware objective formulations and recovery strategies that minimize service disruption while respecting DER and network constraints. We validate the proposed approach via case studies on a benchmark test feeder and a meshed transmission–distribution test system under high renewable penetrations, demonstrating improvements in renewable curtailment reduction, frequency and voltage regulation, and faster recovery from contingencies compared with baseline control strategies. The results show that digital twin–driven predictive control substantially enhances operational flexibility and resilience, while highlighting open challenges in scalability, interoperability, cybersecurity, and standards for practical deployment.

title: Physics-Informed Neural and Graph Learning Framework for Operational Amplifier Performance Prediction and Sizing
authors: Rejith Kumar G, Yukti Kumar, Achyutha S, Sunita M S
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This paper presents a neural network approach to designing and optimizing operational amplifiers. The novelty of the work lies in the approach that combines a PhysicsInformed Neural Network (PINN), which enforces steady-state differential-algebraic circuit equations, with a topology-aware Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network (PI-GNN) derived from physics-embedded circuit graphs. This dual-path architecture enables accurate forward performance prediction as well as inverse design, directly mapping target specifications to optimal transistor dimensions. The framework is trained and validated using high-fidelity Cadence Virtuoso simulations. Experimental results demonstrate prediction errors below 5% for key metrics, which include DC gain, unity-gain bandwidth, phase margin, slew rate, and CMRR. The inverse sizing achieves specification matching within 5% without iterative SPICE optimization, highlighting the effectiveness of physics-guided neural and graph learning for scalable analog IC design automation.

title: An Improved Multi-Target Tracking Algorithm for Intelligent Transportation Perception Based on Adaptive Association and Trajectory Smoothing
authors: Ru-Ping Li, Fang-Sheng Wu, Dong-Dong Shi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the issues of trajectory jitter, weak data association robustness, and limited tracking accuracy in multi-target tracking for intelligent transportation perception systems, this paper proposes an improved algorithm named SORT++, which is built upon the standard SORT (Simple Online and Realtime Tracking) framework. The improvements include: (1) an observation-noise-aware adaptive association threshold to enhance data association accuracy under dynamic sensor noise; (2) a velocity moving average smoothing mechanism to suppress trajectory fluctuations caused by sensor noise and model mismatch; (3) a weighted correction strategy in the Kalman filter update stage to improve state estimation stability.
