Experimental results on simulated traffic scenarios show that compared with KF, EKF, and standard SORT, the proposed SORT++ reduces the average root mean square error (RMSE) by 40.8%–56.5% and the average speed standard deviation (SSD) by 52.0%–68.4%, while maintaining a real-time inference speed of 1.6 ms per frame, which is suitable for vehicle-mounted or roadside embedded perception devices.

title: Explainable AI Engine for Cross-Platform Transaction Trust Evaluation
authors: Madhav Saraswat, Savita Saini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: This paper introduces an explainable AI engine to cross platform transaction trust evaluation based on a hybrid explainable AI method with Federated learning when deployed on Microsoft Azure machine learning. The proposed solution will employ explainable AI methods like SHAP and LIME to make trust analysis transparent and allow users to realize the most important factors to make trust decisions on various platforms. Federated learning is applied to maintain privacy through training the model on decentralized data without the necessity of sharing sensitive transaction information. The engine is 95 % accurate with trust predictions with a high scalability allowing the processing of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> transactions per minute. The system is found to record 90 percent the compliance to privacy and this makes it appropriate when dealing with sensitive transactions. Relative studies indicate that the hybrid model has been better than the traditional machine learning, deep learning, and rule-based models in its accuracy, interpretability, and scalability providing a strong solution to the cross-platform trust assessment.

title: A Bias-Resistant Framework for Misinformation Detection in Code-Mixed Telugu Using Semantic Graph Neural Networks
authors: PDSS Lakshmi Kumari, Chappidi Sravani, C. D. S. Divya Manju, Cherukuri Sindhu, Dude Lakshmi Tejaswini, Kadali Supriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Detecting false information in regional Indian languages is a great issue for natural language processing, and is made worse by code-mixing and an absence of annotated data. Our study analysis suggests that, rather than learning a fully semantic understanding of truth, fine-tuned language models can show sensitivity to structural patterns such as text length biases and vocabulary artifacts, even when achieving very high classification performance on regional datasets. A robust, biasaware framework for classifying fake news in code-mixed Telugu (Tenglish) is presented in this paper. To differentiate semantic information from dataset-specific formatting, we use a selective transliteration pipeline combined with aggressive sequence normalization. The Multilingual Representations for Indian Languages (MuRIL) encoder is then used to extract deep contextual representations. We use a Graph Attention Network v2 (GATv2) for final classification, mapping these embeddings into a semantic k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) graph weighted by cosine similarity instead of depending on isolated sequence classification. Our method is designed to reduce sensitivity to structural artifacts by encouraging the model to assess localized semantic neighborhoods. Experimental results show that the proposed semantic graph architecture achieves an F1-score of 0.944, while baseline classifiers such as TF-IDF with Random Forest can attain near-perfect F1-scores (up to 0.997), potentially reflecting strong lexical cues present in the dataset. The GATv2 layers provide a promising methodology for misinformation detection in lowresource environments by facilitating improved separation of verifiable news from false claims in the semantic vector space, further supported by qualitative cluster analysis.

title: Memristor-Based Spiking Neural Network Accelerator for Bio-Inspired Interception Task
authors: Qianhou Qu, Sheng Lu, Liuting Shang, Jaihan Utailawon, Sungyong Jung, Qilian Liang, Chenyun Pan
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) provide event-driven and low-power computation inspired by biological neural systems, but current implementations rely on von Neumann graphics processing units (GPUs) and central processing units (CPUs) platforms, where memory and computation bottlenecks limit energy efficiency. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an analog memristor-based spiking neural network (SNN) accelerator that integrates in-memory synaptic computation with analog integrate-and-fire (IF) neurons, eliminating multi-transistor CMOS synapse circuits and enabling asynchronous event-driven operation at the 45 nm technology node. Additionally, a digital SNN accelerator is designed and optimized at the 5 nm technology node for comparison. The proposed architecture is evaluated using a predator-prey tracking task that emulates pursuit behavior. In this task, the analog SNN accelerator's inference closely matches the ideal software inference with a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.004. HSPICE simulation results show that the proposed analog SNN accelerator achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.7 \times$</tex> lower energy consumption and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.26 \times$</tex> lower delay compared to the digital baseline, demonstrating the potential of memristor-based neuromorphic circuits for energy-efficient real-time edge intelligence.

title: SYNE: A Biologically-Inspired Decision Neural Network via Stochastic Delay Differential Equations
authors: Langhua Ju, Xinling Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Modeling the neural mechanisms of decision-making is a major challenge in developing brain-inspired intelligence. Existing computational models, such as drift-diffusion models (DDM) and reinforcement learning (RL), address only subsets of these aspects in isolation, lacking a unified dynamical framework. Here we propose SYNE (Synaptic Nexus Entropy Network), a biologically-inspired decision neural network based on stochastic delay differential equations. SYNE integrates four core biological principles—dynamic homeostasis, competitive selection, history dependence, and intrinsic noise—into a single system. Theoretical analysis proves the existence, uniqueness, and local exponential stability of the solution. Experiments on multi-armed bandits, mouse maze navigation, and multi-agent market games show that SYNE achieves or exceeds state-of-the-art methods in exploration efficiency, risk adaptation, and dynamic coordination. Sensitivity analyses reveal functional specialization of components and provide parameter guidance.

title: Cloud-Driven Deep Learning Applications in Smart Cities
authors: Jasjeet Singh, Harshit Agarwal, Srinivasa Reddy, Isan Sahoo, Syed Hauider Abbas, Pankaj Kunekar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The concept of global urbanization poses very difficult challenges of managing urban systems. The report considers the ability of cloud based deep learning paradigm to act as key to designing smart cities by making them smart, efficient and sustainable. In this case, we present a series of actions of developing and implementing a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) network on a cloud computing platform to forecast urban energy usage according to the multi-dimensional ISONE Smart City Energy Dataset which is present in Kaggle. The model is quantitatively compared to a statistical baseline on significant regression measures. The results indicate that deep learning method possesses a more powerful predictive capability and this justify the power of this synergy. The report finally ends with the debate of the technical, ethical and practical implications of such a framework and recommendations on the next line of research.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Thwarting Impact of Vampire Nodes in WSNs
authors: Pediredla Srilatha, Suneel Pappala, Sripada Rama Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), the resource depletion attacks termed vampire attack influence the network factors such as energy consumption, bandwidth and link quality. This vampire attack performs route depletion which has the probability of draining the battery from the nodes that eventually resulted in poor network performance. In this paper, a Weight-Optimized Extreme Gradient Boosting-based Machine learning (WOEGBML) model is proposed for determining potential features from the network and explore it extensively for detecting and isolating vampire attacks. It specifically used an adaptive weight determination method which computed weights associated with the network factors considered for detection such that vampire attack is accurately isolated from the network. It further used k-fold validation strategy for handling the problem of overfitting and enhancing the degree of generalization. It is proposed with the capability of exploring multiple numbers of features that contributed towards vampire attack detection and quantify their impact during the launching process. It specifically handled the limitations of class imbalance during the use of datasets which are considered for experimental evaluation. The results of this machine learning model under different density of vampire attacks maximized packet delivery rate and throughput with minimized latency and energy utilization rate.

title: Cloud-Enabled Chatbots and Virtual Assistants: Redefining Digital Marketing Strategies
authors: Anil Pandurang Gaikwad, Ankita Tandon, C. Vijai, Vijaya Lakshmi V, Himanshu Sanghavi, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Online marketing is being transformed by the use of cloud-based chatbots and virtual assistants, which provide the ability to connect with consumers in real-time, provide personalized recommendations, and offer help on a large scale. When they are developed on Flask and MongoDB, these applications of AI models, such as Google Gemini, are useful in the large-scale processing of data within a short period of time. They also provide multi-page interactive user interfaces and secure authentication. The analytics module's foundation is the ability for administrators to enhance campaigns, user engagement, and the experience of reaching maximum load in addition to answering commonly asked user inquiries. This paper illustrates how AI-driven cloud technology can help improve the efficacy of marketing and consumer experience.

title: The Role of Time-Sensitive Networking in Enhancing Real-Time Clock Synchronization for Power Monitoring Devices
authors: Jinghao Hei, Xiaowen Chen, Bing Liu, Yong Wang, Lan Zhang, Qiongqiong Yao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Real-time clock synchronisation to achieve the accurate measurement, data stability and the system stability is important and required in smart grids and industrial automation. In this study, Time Sensitive Networking (TSN) is explored to enhance accuracy and stability of real-time clock-synchronisation by minimising synchronisation errors. The quantitative analysis is done using Python (Google Colab) in order to simulate network conditions (latency, jitter, and packet loss). Machine learning models with advanced machine learning, XGBoost, LightGBM and LSTM are used to forecast the synchronization accuracy and benchmarking is conducted to IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol (PTP) in the absence of TSN. The findings show that a much smaller median error (0.5 ms) is obtained with TSN-enabled synchronization than with non-TSN (1.5 ms) as shown in Figure 1. The statistical analyses prove a very significant decrease in synchronization errors (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}< 0.0001$</tex>) proving that TSN is more effective in reducing timing differences introduced by networks. LSTM is more successful than XGBoost and LightGBM to predict synchronization errors (Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of LSTM is 0.7033 compared to XGBoost (0.7335) and LightGBM (0.7243)). Through real time power monitoring systems, accuracy and reliability in synchronisation can be achieved which is very good as compared to other solutions such as estimation of frequency and generation. Thus, these systems can be served by TSN. The power utilities and the industrial networks should be made to operate using synchronisation based on TSN, and machine learning can be used to optimize dynamically. It leads to practical implications in order to clean-up the event logging, to detect faults better, and to improve the accuracy of the energy distribution. Although the viability is demonstrated in the case of simulation, the capability of the given study is constrained by simulation-based method and a real TSN hardware implementation study would need to be further confirmed.

title: A Temperature Compensation Method for MSR Monitoring under In-Service Condition using EMAT
authors: Yiyang Xu, Tao Zhang, Liang Tang, Sanao Huang, Zhenyi Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: The Moisture Separator and Reheater (MSR) of the nuclear power plant operates under high temperature conditions. A compensation method was developed to improve the accuracy of electromagnetic acoustic transducer (EMAT) technology in monitoring MSR corrosion. Firstly, an EMAT high-temperature test system was developed to simulate the ultrasonic propagation characteristics in 20 steel, 304 stainless steel, 6061 aluminum, and T2 copper at 350°C. A polynomial fitting based sound velocity compensation method was proposed to effectively reduce corrosion monitoring errors in high-temperature environments. In addition, by combining the multimodal wave characteristics of EMAT, the monitoring of the Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio of metal materials in high-temperature environments has been achieved. The experimental results show that the compensated high-temperature measurement error is less than 1%, and the accuracy of Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio is higher than 95%, which verifies the effectiveness of the method and provides support for improving the monitoring reliability and engineering application value of EMAT technology under high-temperature environments.

title: Toxicological Impact of Heavy Metals During Sustainable Brick Production from Bagasse, Fly Ash, and Cement
authors: Pratik Krishna, Santosh Verma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: This study looks at the effects of heavy metals during the production of sustainable bricks made from fly ash, bagasse, and cement. Fly ash and bagasse, which are industrial and agricultural by-products, have a lot of potential for eco-friendly construction because they are abundant and help reduce waste. However, trace heavy metals like Pb, Cd, Cr, As, and Zn must be carefully evaluated to ensure safety for the environment and human health. We conducted tests on the raw materials and the final brick samples. The results showed that heavy metal levels remained within safe limits because they were effectively trapped in the cement mix. The final bricks had acceptable compressive strength (3.65 N/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and and did not show any harmful or toxic properties. This study concludes that bricks made from fly ash, bagasse, and cement are safe for the environment, non-toxic, and promote sustainable construction while lowering the carbon footprint and addressing waste disposal issues.

title: Collaborative Optimal Scheduling Method for Microgrid Clusters Based on Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient
authors: Han He
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the high integration of renewable energy and the enhanced coupling between microgrids, the collaborative optimal scheduling of microgrid clusters faces higher requirements in terms of economy, grid absorption capacity, and operational safety. Addressing the high computational complexity and insufficient robustness of traditional centralized optimization methods, as well as the inadequate representation of continuous action spaces, multiple constraint coupling. In this model, each microgrid is treated as an independent agent and adopts a centralized training, decentralized execution mechanism. It introduces a multi-dimensional state space that includes renewable energy output, load demand, state of charge of energy storage, time-of-use electricity price, and power exchange constraints of interconnection lines, and designs a composite reward function that integrates operational cost, wind and solar curtailment penalties, power balance deviation, and equipment safety constraints. At the same time, it combines a double critic evaluation mechanism, prioritized experience replay, and an inter-agent attention communication module to improve the stability of policy learning, global collaboration, and adaptability to uncertain scenarios. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms comparison models in terms of system operational cost, renewable energy utilization rate, and scheduling stability.

title: Trajectory Tracking Control for Inspection Robots via Tube-Based Linear Model Predictive Control
authors: Haojie Cheng, Wei Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Conventional Linear Model Predictive Control (LMPC) strategies often suffer from trajectory divergence under persistent external disturbances, potentially leading to violations of safety constraints. To address this challenge in the context of unmanned inspection robots, this paper proposes a robust Tube-Based Linear Model Predictive Control (Tube-LMPC) framework. By incorporating uncertainty tubes into the LMPC formulation, the proposed method constrains the system states within a time-varying tubular region surrounding the nominal trajectory. This mechanism guarantees that the actual tracking trajectory remains bounded within the tube despite the presence of disturbances and model uncertainties. Simulation results demonstrate that the Tube-LMPC controller significantly reduces trajectory deviations and enhances tracking stability compared to traditional approaches. Furthermore, the method ensures that the robot operates strictly within safe boundaries, validating its effectiveness for substation inspection scenarios where high safety standards and strong disturbance rejection capabilities are paramount.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Pipeline for Multimodal Biomedical Signal Interpretation
authors: Dhannya J, Pallavi B. Narkhede, Chennupalle Venkatasai Raghavendra Reddy, Parnapalli Maruthi, K.Suresh Babu, Aswathy N Rajan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: This research presents an explainable deep learning signal interpretation pipeline of multi-modal data of biomedical signals comprising of more than one mode (EEG, ECG and physiological sensors). For this, we pose a Transformer based multifunction fusion architecture and cross-modal attention utilities which is helpful in resolving temporal dependencies and signal-to-signal correlations. The pipeline utilises both GradCAM++ and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations), for interpretability of the model, and will provide transparent insights in the decision making process of the model to clinicians. Our framework is build on PyTorch Lightning to ease and efficient model development and uncertainty quantification will be Monte Carlo dropout. The architecture has 94.3% accuracy in performing the task of classification of diseases and at the same time retaining its computational efficiency. Explainablity modules that are used to generate the visualization of attention maps showing the critical sections of the signals as well as feature importance values. Extensive validation on MIMIC-IV and PhysioNet database prove the superiority of this autonomy on compared to the conventional machine learning approaches establishing such framework to be a robust tool of automated biomedical signal analysis in precision medicine applications.

title: Ensemble-Based Learning Algorithm for Multi-Morbidity Prediction
authors: Subham, Anjana Mishra, Gyanendra, Abhishek Kumar, Nishant Kumar, Mayank
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Interventions such as diagnosis and management at an early age are significant because they can curb the severity of the disease and the outcomes of the patients. The different diseases including Diabetes, Cancer, Parkinson, Heart and Liver disease accurate diagnosis explained in this paper using multimorbidity prediction algorithm. In the system, a combination of machine learning models such as random forest, decision tree, k-nearest neighbors (KNN) and naive bayes, were used. In order to enhance the accuracy of prediction, the model used the approaches of ensemble learning including voting (hard voting) and gradient boosting which involved the combination of predictions of numerous models to get a more credible outcome. The developed model incorporated the data provided by various sources including electronic health records (EHRs), biomedical imaging, patient-generated health data (PGHD), and genomic data, which offered the comprehensive approach to the disease in the individual. Others have reported similar results that ensemble learning was better than the traditional machine learning models, with gradient boosting at 79.88 per cent of diabetes, 88.52 per cent of heart disease, 0.82 per cent of Parkinson's disease, 74.58 per cent of liver and at 98 per cent of cancers. The multimorbidity forecasting engine confirmed the ability of ensemble learning to restructure health care through the help of enabling early prognosis and tailored solutions. Machine learning algorithms coupled with ensemble learning offer a scalable, powerful approach to predicting numerous diseases, which ultimately can contribute to the enhancement of patient care and results.

title: Integrated Digital Advisory System for Real-Time Agricultural Decision Support in Rural India
authors: Siri Ramineni, G Mary Swarnalatha, Umapathi Nagalla, Neha Gugulothu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Farmers in rural India continue to struggle against uncertain weather conditions, marketplace fluctuations, and lack of access to reliable agricultural information. In this paper, an intelligent digital system for realtime agricultural intelligence through informed decisions is proposed. It includes weather reports, market rates, crop advisories, and best practices, providing area-specific recommendations in the local language to solve literacy challenges. The service also offers up agricultural news and advice and tools for analyzing crop health and market trends. Unlike spot solutions, it allows farmers to make crop calendar plans in order to use resources judiciously and optimize yields. Preliminary assessments indicate enhanced awareness, better decision-making, and greater market participation, demonstrating the possibility that digital interventions could help to enhance sustainable rural development and food security.

title: Two-Stage Variable-Decoupling Framework for Trajectory Optimization of Industrial Manipulators
authors: Gu Li, Jin Huang, Zichuan Wu, Qi Gao, Yong Jia, Gang Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper presents a two-stage trajectory optimization framework for point-to-point motions of industrial manipulators. The problem is formulated as a nonconvex optimization model with kinematic constraints, in which segment durations and waypoint offsets are treated as decision variables. In the first stage, TVAC-PSO performs a global joint search over both variable groups. In the second stage, the segment durations are fixed and the waypoint offsets are further refined by L-BFGS-B, thereby improving smoothness without altering the execution time obtained in the first stage. Experiments over three representative scenarios and 30 independent runs show that, relative to TVAC-PSO alone, the proposed method reduces the composite objective by 4.66%–4.77% and the average RMS jerk of the six joints by 20.61%–22.18%.

title: The CRISP Framework: Enhancing AI Explainability Through Context-Aware Prompts for Clearer and More Interpretable Responses
authors: Yvonne Jegede, Dorcas Addo, Atef Mohamed Shalan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of the CRISP (Context, Role, Iteration, Specificity, and Precision) prompt engineering framework in enhancing the interpretability, accuracy, and user alignment of AIgenerated responses. Four key metrics: precision, specificity, relevance and clarity, were employed to compare responses generated from CRISP-based prompts against those from generic prompts. The results indicate a substantial improvement in relevance, with a 46.82% increase, highlighting CRISP’s strong ability to produce contextually grounded and semantically aligned outputs. Precision and specificity improved by 3.91%, reflecting the capacity of CRISP to strengthen in generating focused, context-rich outputs. However, clarity declined by 2.10%, suggesting that the increased detail and structure introduced by CRISP may result in denser, less easily digestible responses. These findings demonstrate the impact of CRISP on response quality and point to the need for future refinement that better balance depth with clarity. Limitations include the small prompt sample, limited domain diversity, and reliance on automated metrics (TFIDF and cosine similarity) rather than human evaluation, which may not fully capture perceived clarity. Future research should integrate human and automated assessments to better evaluate CRISP’s real-world performance

title: Coordinated Optimization Strategy for Distributed PV Distribution Networks Based on Hierarchical Power Allocation
authors: Haoran Ma, Jianfeng Yang, Hong Li, Nan Hu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources such as photovoltaics (PV) in distribution networks, real-time power adjustment is essential to maintain system balance due to the inherent fluctuations in both load demand and renewable generation. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a hierarchical power regulation strategy that integrates Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) theory with a consensus-based consensus error algorithm. First, Monte Carlo simulation is employed to generate random scenarios representing uncertainty in PV output and load demand. CVaR is then used to quantify the expected risk under these uncertain conditions, enabling robust operational decisions. In the upper layer, an economic dispatch optimization problem is formulated to minimize the total operating cost of distributed generators while satisfying risk constraints. The lower layer implements real-time power control through a modified consensus algorithm based on tracking local consensus errors. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves faster convergence compared to leaderless or fixed-leader schemes, especially under dynamic load changes or distributed source disturbances. Economic performance is validated via comparative experiments, confirming its effectiveness in enhancing both stability and efficiency.

title: Data-Driven Modeling of Soil Moisture in Irrigated Fields
authors: Abinava Yeshwanth Kalayambakam Janardhan, Sumon Datta, Saleh Taghvaeian, Christopher Crick, Palash Kumar Kundu
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Commercially available sensors measuring soil moisture (SM) are expensive, labor-intensive and may perform differently across soils with varying clay content and salinity. As an alternative, this study evaluated three machine learning (ML) models: Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM) to predict SM at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ c m}$</tex> soil depth at 10 irrigated fields in Oklahoma using soil temperature, weather variables, irrigation, soil texture, and crop data. The models were trained and tested on data from 9 sites <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(70 \%-30 \%$</tex> split) and validated on one unseen site. The trained models with sensor-based initialization and calculated SM-based initialization with incorporation of 1-hour, 3-hour, and 6-hour lookback (LB) periods: LB1, LB3, and LB6, respectively revealed that the inclusion of LB increased prediction accuracy. LGBM performed well among evaluated models under sensor-based initialization – demonstrating the importance of inclusion of antecedent SM, while calculated SM initialization showed slightly lower performance but remained promising for practical use, especially after major watering events.

title: A Fine Balance: Understanding Anti-Technosolutionist Strategies of Environmental Justice Advocates
authors: Lydia Stamato, Hasan Mahmud Prottoy Prottoy, Yi Khoo, Foad Hamidi
publish: ACM Journal on Computing and Sustainable Societies

title: PredComp: Predicting Compiler Optimization Options with Multi-stage Learning
authors: Bingyu Gao, Ziming Wang, Mengyu Yao, Zhihong Xue, Xiangqun Chen, Ding Li, Yao Guo
publish: ACM Transactions on Architecture and Code Optimization
abstract: None

title: HBA-VIO: High-Precision Direct Sparse Monocular Visual-Inertial Odometry via Hybrid Bundle Adjustment
authors: Zhiyu Zhou, Han Zhang, Feng Hui, Junyi Wu, Yu Liu
abstract: Visual-inertial odometry (VIO) is a core perception module for autonomous robots, where high-precision pose estimation is essential for reliable navigation. However, low-cost setups equipped with inertial measurement units (IMUs) and monocular cameras suffer from cumulative trajectory drift. In this article, we propose HBA-VIO, a high-precision direct sparse monocular VIO system based on hybrid bundle adjustment (HBA). First, to address initialization challenges in IMU-based systems, a multistage visual-inertial initialization strategy is proposed, consisting of static initialization and coarse-to-fine visual-inertial alignment. This ensures precise calibration between monocular visual odometry and inertial measurements across diverse scenarios. Second, for high-accuracy trajectory estimation, we design an HBA framework integrating backward-decoupled preintegration residuals, inertial-only prior residuals, cross-frame preintegration residuals, and a scale refinement module, which effectively refines the global scale and suppresses cumulative trajectory drift. Extensive evaluations are performed on real-world challenging scenarios. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that HBA-VIO achieves state-of-the-art accuracy, validating the effectiveness of each technical component and the system’s strong robustness to real-world disturbances.

title: AI-Augmented Healthcare Communication: A Review of Large Language Model Healthcare Agents and Generative AI in EHR Integration
authors: Shivam Tyagi, Amit Kumar Goyal, Deepti Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) and large language models (LLMs) are among artificial intelligence (AI) innovations that have reshaped the concept of healthcare communication. Such technologies are being used to facilitate both synchronous and asynchronous communications: LLCM-based healthcare agents making structured consultations with patients, and GenAI-generated drafts helping clinicians respond to electronic health records (EHR) messages. The current review brings together recent research literature on these two paradigms that are complementary with regard to their effectiveness in clinical work, implications on workflow, and dilemmas related to them. An experimental and randomized research shows that healthcare agents significantly improve the inquiry proactivity, diagnostic relevance, and communication safety over baseline LLMs, whereas GenAI drafts augment reply empathy and length, but less frequently at the expense of increased review time. A comparative study shows that neither of the two paradigms can fully address issues of physician workload, personalization and medico-legal accountability. Rather, an intermediate system between patient-facing actors and physician-facing EHR draft can provide a moderate solution. Other challenges that are open to review in the paper are the personalization of physician style, integration of multi-modal data, and adherence to privacy rules including HIPAA, and GDPR. We conclude by a research roadmap highlighting the aspects of hybrid integration, personalization, and stringent assessment schemes of AI-assisted healthcare communication.

title: Comprehensive Analysis of Induction Motor Harmonics for Sensorless Speed Estimation in its Application to MCSA
authors: Jorge Bonet-Jara, Joan Pons-Llinares
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In recent years, companies have been implementing smart sensors for autonomous asset monitoring. In this context, Motor Current Signature Analysis (MCSA) is ideal to monitor induction motors due to its non-invasive and remote measurement capability. An autonomous MCSA algorithm requires precise, automatic and non-invasive knowledge of the motor speed to accurately locate the speed-dependent fault harmonics. Regarding this, harmonic-based Sensorless Speed Estimation (SSE) methods are the most compatible with MCSA, since they are based on the same principles. Among them, Mixed Eccentricity Harmonics (MEHs) are the most commonly used, due to their non-invasive implementation. However, recent studies have also shown the non-invasiveness of Rotor Slot Harmonics (RSHs) techniques. Therefore, the potential industrial use of both types of harmonics makes it necessary to determine which are more reliable for an autonomous MCSA algorithm. Hence, this paper aims to definitely answer this question by conducting an in-depth analysis of RSHs- and MEHs-based techniques attending to the most critical features of these methods: the detectability and accuracy of the harmonic used. For that, several related aspects are studied using three databases containing respectively: construction data of 3643 motors, 100 industrial measurements of 100 different motors, and 135 signals generated in a test-bench. The variety and extension of the databases allows for solid and general conclusions to be drawn that are applicable to industry.

title: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
authors: N/A

title: Maximum Entropy Inverse RL for Cyber Attack Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Mohamed Massaoudi, Maymouna Ez Eddin
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has significantly expanded the attack surface for cyber threats, making effective intrusion detection systems (IDS) critical for network security. Traditional anomaly detection methods often struggle with high-dimensional network traffic data and evolving attack patterns. This paper presents a novel approach for cyber attack detection using Maximum Entropy Inverse Reinforcement Learning (MaxEnt IRL). The proposed method learns reward functions from normal network traffic patterns, enabling the identification of anomalous behaviors that deviate from expected normal operations. By leveraging the maximum entropy principle, the approach captures the underlying distribution of normal traffic states without requiring labeled attack samples during training. The study evaluates the performance with multiple attack types, including Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS), Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) poisoning, and Message Queue Telemetry Transport (MQTT) based attacks. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 91.18% accuracy, outperforming traditional machine learning approaches, deep learning methods, and other RL attack detection techniques. The results highlight the potential of MaxEnt IRL for robust and adaptive cyber attack detection in IoT environments. All the related codes and datasets publicly available at: https://github.com/Mmassaoudi-cell/maxent-irl-iot-ids.

title: Performance, Adaptability, and Security in AI-Driven IIoT: A Survey
authors: Uddin Md. Borhan, Arif Raza, Taki Uddin, Mahbubul Islam, A.K.M. Muzahidul Islam, Jie Chen
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) integrates cyber-physical systems, edge-cloud computing, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and advanced connectivity to enable intelligent, data-driven operations across manufacturing, energy, transportation, and healthcare. The convergence of Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT), together with pervasive AI-driven automation, complicates meeting the joint requirements of performance, adaptability, and security (PAS). This survey adopts an explicitly AI-centric view of PAS and reviews IIoT research spanning deep reinforcement learning (DRL), multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), digital twins (DTs), federated and collaborative learning, graph neural networks (GNNs), blockchain-assisted analytics, and lightweight and post-quantum cryptography. The reviewed approaches are analyzed across device, edge, fog, cloud, and application layers under industrial constraints such as latency, reliability, resource limits, safety requirements, and regulatory compliance. A PAS-centric taxonomy is introduced that links technique families to deployment layers and application domains, and recurring trade-offs among PAS dimensions are summarized. Open challenges are identified in multi-objective optimization, semantic interoperability, lifecycle validation, and governance, with directions outlined toward secure and sustainable Industry 5.0 IIoT ecosystems.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp8-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: Cross-Behavior Learning with Object Flow Prediction for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Longrui Chen, David Russell, Yulei Qiu, Mehmet Dogar
abstract: Cross-embodiment learning enables robots to acquire manipulation skills by learning from demonstrations provided by different embodiments. However, most existing research on cross-embodiment learning focuses on transferring similar manipulation behaviors. For the same task, different embodiments may need different behaviors; e.g., it may be easier for a human to push an object to a goal position, while a robot may use a pick-and-place to perform the same task. Making use of such cross-embodiment and cross-behavior demonstrations becomes essential for large-scale imitation learning. In this work, we propose a novel framework for cross-behavior learning based on object flow. Object flow represents the task in a manner that is independent from the embodiment and the particular behavior used during the demonstration. By shifting the focus from the manipulator to objects, our framework enables learning from cross-behavior manipulation data rather than merely imitating the behavior of a specific embodiment. Our results on task representations show that, with only 20 robot demonstrations, integrating object flow prediction improves success rates by up to 91% in-domain and 87% out-of-domain. Adding 40 human demonstrations in addition to the 20 robot demonstrations further boosts out-of-domain performance by 143%.

title: Online Coreset Selection for Learning Dynamic Systems
authors: Jingyuan Li, Dawei Shi, Ling Shi
abstract: With the increasing availability of streaming data in dynamic systems, a critical challenge in data-driven modeling for control is how to efficiently select informative data to characterize system dynamics. In this work, we develop an online coreset selection method for set-membership identification in the presence of process disturbances, improving data efficiency while preserving convergence guarantees. Specifically, we derive a stacked polyhedral representation that over-approximates the feasible parameter set. Based on this representation, we propose a geometric selection criterion that retains a data point only if it induces a sufficient contraction of the feasible set. Theoretically, the feasible-set volume is shown to converge to zero almost surely under persistently exciting data and a tight disturbance bound. When the disturbance bound is mismatched, an explicit Hausdorff-distance bound is derived to quantify the resulting identification error. In addition, an upper bound on the expected coreset size is established and extensions to bounded measurement noise and nonlinear systems are discussed. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through comprehensive simulation studies.

title: IoT-Enabled SnO₂-Based Humidity Sensor for Real-Time Monitoring in Neonatal Incubators
authors: Poundoss Chellamuthu, Kirubaveni Savarimuthu, Gulam Nabi Alsath Mohammed, Biancaa Ramesh
abstract: This research presents the development and optimization of a Tin Oxide (SnO₂)-based resistive humidity sensor for accurate monitoring in incubator systems. SnO₂ nanomaterials were synthesized using hydrothermal synthesis technique, followed by comprehensive characterization through X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM), current-voltage (J-V) analysis, ultravioletvisible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy, and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). The best-performing sensor exhibited a flower-like nanostructure with a response time of 18 s, a recovery time of 14 s, and a sensitivity of 72.4%. The sensor functioned effectively within the required incubator humidity range of 40–60% relative humidity (RH) and demonstrated superior performance compared to the commercial DHT11 sensor. To ensure accuracy and reliability, machine learning algorithms were applied to estimate error and validate the correctness of the sensed humidity data. The optimized SnO₂ sensor was integrated into an incubator system, with real-time data transmission enabled through an Internet of Things (IoT) platform. These findings highlight the potential of SnO₂-based sensors for high-precision humidity monitoring in biomedical applications, particularly for neonatal care where stable humidity levels are critical.

title: A Continuous Finite-Time Tracking Controller for Nonholonomic-Unicycle Vehicles
authors: Angel I. Paredes, Emmanuel Cruz-Zavala, Antonio Loría, Emmanuel Nuño
abstract: This letter proposes a tracking controller for first-order nonholonomic-unicycle vehicles that guarantees stabilization in finite time. Besides the perks of finite-time stabilization (such as robustness), a significant advantage of our controller is its mathematical simplicity; while existing finite-time controllers often rely on discontinuous switching terms ours is continuous. This is important since the robot is velocity-controlled. Furthermore, in contrast to other controllers achieving finite-time stabilization ours relies only on the knowledge of the desired reference velocities and the system maintains stable in the presence of unknown, non-zero reference accelerations. We establish uniform global finite-time stability (UGFTS) via Lyapunov theory and illustrate our theoretical findings with numerical simulations.

title: Wavelength Dependence and Transient Photoresponse Mechanisms in Aerosol-Jet Printed ZnO Nanowire Ultraviolet Photodetectors
authors: Chenkun Lyu, Haoyi Lin, Joseph Andrews
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation poses significant health risks, motivating the development of reliable UV sensing systems. This paper presents an aerosol-jet-printed ZnO nanowire UV photodetector fabricated on a Si/SiO₂ substrate using printed interdigitated Ag electrodes and a ZnO nanowire sensing layer. The device exhibits strong wavelength- and intensity-dependent photoresponse under 340 nm and 375 nm UV illumination at a 1 V bias. At the same irradiance of 60 μW·cm⁻², the photocurrent reached ∼0.9 mA at 340 nm and ∼0.015 mA at 375 nm, corresponding to responsivities of 31.71 A/W and 3.67 A/W, respectively. Transient measurements reveal pronounced persistent photoconductivity (PPC) and strong dependence of recovery behavior on illumination conditions. In addition, thermal cycling experiments and a 10-nm Al₂O₃ atomic layer deposition (ALD) encapsulation layer were used to investigate the role of oxygen-related surface processes in carrier transport and recovery dynamics. These results provide insight into the transient photoresponse mechanisms of ZnO nanowire UV photodetectors and the influence of surface interactions on device behavior.

title: Motion analysis of a multi-stiffness concentric tube magnetic continuum robot and its remote constraint application for minimally invasive surgical operations_supp2-3699226.mp4
authors: Wenguang Yang
abstract: Conventional magnetic continuum robots (MCRs) show great potential for minimally invasive surgery due to their simple structure and ease of manipulation. However, their monolithic continuous structure and single-stiffness design limit kinematic performance and manipulation accuracy. To address these limitations, we developed a concentric tube magnetic continuum robot (CT-MCR) system with replaceable concentric tubes and adjustable magnetic particle positions. Motion analysis and modeling were performed using the Cosserat rod model to optimize structural characteristics and enhance manipulation accuracy. Within a 270° driving range, the operating end-effector achieves stable trajectory following with a fit rate exceeding 90%. The distal constraint effect minimizes end-effector hopping, improving stability by over 50% compared to conventional MCRs. Navigation and foreign body retrieval experiments in a complex branching vascular model within a 3D Helmholtz coil workspace demonstrate the CT-MCR’s potential for targeted drug delivery and minimally invasive cardiovascular surgery.

title: Trans-Intestinal Port for Periodic Drug Refilling: Bridging Ingestible Capsules and Implantable Devices_supp7-3699210.mp4
authors: Leonardo Ricotti
abstract: Implantable devices for controlled drug release are extremely promising for the treatment of chronic diseases, such as type 1 diabetes, chronic pain, and others. However, these systems still require periodic invasive refills, which can result in clinical complications and patient discomfort. Previous works introduced the opportunity for a non-invasive refilling of these devices through ingestible capsules. However, concerns remain regarding repeated tissue perforation of the intestinal wall and the corresponding risk of fistula formation. This study presents a novel trans-intestinal port system enabling non-invasive refilling of implanted devices designed to create a stable and controlled interface between the device and the intestinal lumen. The system aims to avoid tissue damage due to repeated needle insertions, ensure proper alignment between the device and capsule, and prevent leakage of digestive fluids into the peritoneal cavity. The design and fabrication of this trans-intestinal port are presented, together with the results of bench tests and a preliminary short-term in vivo test. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed trans-intestinal port as an innovative solution to enable a non-invasive periodic refilling of implantable drug delivery systems, paving the way to a full validation of this technology in the future, which will require long-term in vivo experiments.

title: Differentiated Warning Method for Wildfires in Transmission Corridors Based on Multi-Dimensional Visual Features
authors: Aihui Wen, Jun Wu, Enze Zhou, Siyan Lin, You Zhou
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To overcome the limitations of single-dimensional warning, this paper proposes a vision-based differentiated warning method for wildfire in transmission corridors. First, an improved lightweight YOLOv8, incorporating DySConv and DyHead, is developed to enhance the detection of small smoke and fire targets in complex backgrounds. Second, a multi-feature extraction algorithm, fusing monocular spatial ranging with RGB+YCbCr/HSV color spaces, is designed to accurately quantify flame distance, height, and intensity. Finally, by integrating these visual features into the Byram combustion and three-interval insulation tripping models, a comprehensive warning matrix based on "arrival time margin" and "dynamic tripping probability" is established. This approach provides proactive decision support for differentiated emergency response in power dispatch centers.

title: Real-Time MPPT Optimization for PV-Battery Hybrid Systems in EV Fast Charging Infrastructure
authors: Madhumathi R M, Bharath M, Mahadevan V, Thanagan K, Udayan K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The development of electric vehicle (EV) fast charging infrastructure is required that uses renewable energy sources as the source of power." This paper addresses the research work carried out with the objective of developing a real-time maximum power point tracking (MPPT) optimization technique for solar photovoltaic (PV) – battery storage systems for electric vehicle fast charging applications. A single DC–DC converter is used as part of the proposed system for MPPT tracking as well as battery charging control, making the complete system more efficient as well as cost-effective for developing electric vehicle fast charging infrastructure.

title: EchoGuard: Biometric Voice-based Audio Watermarking with Blockchain-Enabled Ownership Verification
authors: Padmini Devi B, Vishnu M S, Saaidarun S, Vinith M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital audio content has made ownership verification and tamper detection increasingly challenging. Existing approaches, including conventional watermarking and blockchain-based timestamping, fail to establish a reliable and identity-linked association between audio content and its creator. To address this limitation, this paper proposes EchoGuard, a hybrid AI–blockchain framework for secure audio ownership verification. The proposed system integrates biometric speaker embeddings, neural audio watermarking, and decentralized blockchain registration into a unified pipeline. A creator-specific voiceprint is first extracted using a deep speaker representation model and converted into a compact watermark payload. This payload is then embedded into the host audio using a deep learning-based steganographic model, ensuring imperceptibility while maintaining robustness against common signal processing attacks. The system further stores cryptographic hashes of the watermarked audio on a blockchain to provide immutable and verifiable ownership records. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves high watermark extraction accuracy and strong robustness under compression, noise, and resampling attacks, while preserving audio quality. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining biometric watermarking with blockchain to enable reliable, tamper-resistant audio authentication.

title: Optimizing Linux Kernel Scheduler Parameters Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hitesh Makka, Kartikey, Preeti Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The Linux kernel’s Completely Fair Scheduler (CFS) is a cornerstone of modern operating systems, but its static, heuristic-based design often fails to deliver optimal performance for specialized, high-demand workloads. This paper investigates the application of a modern reinforcement learning algorithm, Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), to dynamically tune nine critical CFS parameters. An agent was trained in a live kernel environment on a PostgreSQL OLTP workload to learn an optimal scheduling policy. The evaluation reveals a significant performance trade-off. The A3C-optimized parameters significantly improved throughput for CPU-intensive tasks, achieving a 47.4% faster completion time on the Hackbench scheduler stress test and a 14.8% increase in requests-per-second for an Apache high-concurrency test. However, this gain came at a significant cost to latency-sensitive applications, with the same policy causing a 7.6% performance regression and a seven-fold increase in instability on the Redis benchmark. The principal contribution of this work is the empirical evaluation of the A3C algorithm for live kernel parameter tuning, characterizing its effects and revealing trade-offs between throughput gains and low-latency performance.

title: Effect of Dipole Length on the Design and Performance of High Permittivity Materials in 7 T MRI
authors: Vincenzo Miranda, Giuseppe Carluccio, Giuseppe Ruello, Riccardo Lattanzi, Daniele Riccio
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: At 7T MRI, radiofrequency field inhomogeneity and limited penetration arise from wavelength shortening in biological tissues. High permittivity materials (HPMs) have been proposed as a passive solution to improve transmit efficiency and signal-to-noise ratio by reshaping the electromagnetic field distribution. However, their design remains largely simulation-driven, with limited physical insight into their interaction with realistic RF sources. An analytical scattering framework based on canonical geometries has recently been introduced to provide a physics-based interpretation of HPM behavior and to identify optimal material parameters. Here, we investigate the robustness of this framework under different RF excitations by analyzing the effect of dipole length on the performance of a spherical HPM at 7T. Analytical predictions obtained with an elementary electric dipole are compared with full-wave simulations using a realistic half-wavelength dipole. Results show that, at the sphere center, the optimal permittivity remains essentially invariant to substantial variations in dipole length, despite differences in the transmit magnetic field distribution. This behavior is attributed to spherical symmetry and to the modal nature of the scattering process governing field enhancement. These findings support the proposed analytical framework as a robust and physically grounded tool for HPM design in ultra-high-field MRI.

title: Toward Portable and Energy-Efficient Home Hemodialysis: Clinical, Economic, and Technical Review with Engineering Perspectives
authors: Ilyasse Boujraf, Yassine Zahidi, Mohamed El Moufid, Hassan Gziri, Wafaa Dachry, Hicham Medromi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Home hemodialysis (HHD) offers a promising alternative to in-center hemodialysis (ICHD), providing improved quality of life and treatment flexibility. This review presents a comparative analysis of HHD versus ICHD with respect to cost-effectiveness and quality of life, based on a structured literature search in the Scopus database. In addition, a comprehensive technical evaluation of four commercial HHD systems (Quanta SC+, NxStage System One, Physidia $\mathrm{S}^{3}$, and Tablo) is conducted, focusing on key design parameters including weight and dimensions, dialysate delivery strategies, circuit configurations, consumables requirements, and portability characteristics. The analysis highlights three major, interconnected technological challenges in current HHD systems: high dialysate consumption, significant energy demand, and extensive use of disposable consumables, collectively impacting system cost and portability. Proposed directions are intended to serve as a structured engineering perspective to guide future research. Experimental validation, system modeling, and prototype implementation are identified as necessary steps toward developing cost-effective, energyefficient, and portable HHD technologies.

title: Optimal Control for Managing the Dynamics of Two Computer Virus Co-Infection
authors: Bashir Abdullahi Baba
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a mathematical model describing the simultaneous spread of two distinct computer viruses within a networked system, taking into account vertical transmission for one virus as well as treatment strategies for both infections. The model is used to derive the basic reproduction number of the combined system, which helps determine the conditions under which the infections either die out or persist. We analyze the disease-free and endemic equilibrium states to better understand the longterm dynamics of the system. To identify effective mitigation strategies, optimal control theory is incorporated into the model, and Pontryagin’s Maximum Principle is applied to establish the necessary conditions for minimizing infection levels and associated intervention costs. Numerical simulations are performed to demonstrate how the proposed control measures influence the spread of infection. The results indicate that combining equilibrium analysis with carefully designed control strategies can substantially reduce infection prevalence while keeping costs at a reasonable level, providing useful insights for managing co-infections in computer networks.

title: Influence of Electric Field Strength on the Dielectric Response of Epoxy Resin foe Electrical Insulation
authors: Qian Wang, Ting Lei, Qiulin Chen, Jiahe Yu, Haohan Zhou, Chao Wu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Epoxy resin is one of the most widely-used polymer dielectrics for electrical insulation. Polarization properties are vital to the electric field distribution and the heat management of equipment. Polarization of dielectric is usually investigated by dielectric response, while the voltage applied in previous research is much lower to that in practical operation, which affects the universality of conclusions. Therefore, in this research, the dielectric response of typical epoxy resin under different field strengths is measured, which reaches the magnitude of actual operation. The influence of field strength on different micro polarization processes is revealed, which suggests a linear polarization trend. The charges involved in the polarization processes are found to be mainly thermal-activated, which means the predominant factor of polarization performance is temperature. The findings herein would prominently benefit the design and the maintenance of epoxy-based high voltage equipment.

title: Learning-Driven Job Recommendation Systems: A Comprehensive Review of Deep Semantic, Transformer-Enhanced, Ontology-based and NLP-Bidirectional Approaches
authors: Velusamy A, Aishwarya S, Chellam N, Devipriya R, Prabhu R, Vasudevan I
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Deep learning, semantic matching, natural language processing (NLP), and transformer-based systems have significantly improved job recommendation systems. Such developments respond to the increased complexity of the job market, the large data about high dimensional skills, dynamically changing employed requirements, and the need of increasingly individualized employment support. The review is based on the contributions of recent literature in the area of matched representation learning, semantic structure modeling, attention mechanisms, transformer-based framework, reciprocal recommendation model, ontology-enhanced system, and resume fusion system. Ten advanced methodologies are compared in terms of data modeling strategy, interpretability, bidirectionality, scalability, adaptability to labor-market development, and relevance to different groups of people such as college graduates, migrants, and vocational learners. The paper also outlines some of the major constraints of classical job-matching pipelines and suggests a hybrid architecture, which combines semantic skill extraction, contextual modeling through transformers, ontology-based filtering, and the two-way resume-job matching. Comparative findings demonstrate higher accuracy, relevance ranking, explainability, and robustness. Future directions include multimodal-resumes-understanding, adaptivity through reinforcement-learning, cross-regional-labor-market-intelligence-engine-based.

title: A Coefficient Feature Matching–Based Method for Single-Phase-to-Ground Fault Location in Active Distribution Networks
authors: Shoudong Xu, Zixin Li, Jinxin Ouyang, Xiaofu Xiong, Bofei Yang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The large-scale integration of distributed generation alters the fault current distribution characteristics of distribution networks, which significantly increases the difficulty of locating single-phase-to-ground faults. This paper proposes a coefficient feature matching–based method for single-phase-to-ground fault location in active distribution networks. By constructing the positive-sequence augmented network of an active distribution system, the intrinsic relationship among the fault location, the feeder outlet, and the positive-sequence current injected by distributed generation is analytically derived, and a generalized fault location model is established. The proposed method formulates location criteria by exploiting the distinctive features of current vector proportion coefficients under different faulted sections, thereby enabling rapid identification of the faulted section and accurate distance estimation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method is insensitive to fault location, transition resistance, and the number of distributed generation units, exhibiting superior location performance and providing effective technical support for the rapid handling of faults in active distribution networks.

title: Data-driven Prediction of California Bearing Ratio Using Fuzzy C-Means Clustering
authors: Yogesh Iyer Murthy, Nikhat Raza Khan, Syed Rameez Ali, Rajesh Tiwari, Shalini Dubey, Priya Shrivastava
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The current research presents the application of Fuzzy Clustering Method (FCM) with Sugeno Type I to develop the prediction model for the soaked California Bearing Ratio (CBR) of black cotton soil. In this research, 211 samples of soil were investigated experimentally. The prediction models were developed using proportion of fractions below and above 0.75 mm in size, optimum moisture content (OMC), maximum dry density (MDD), plasticity index (PI), shrinkage limit (SL), and swell pressure as input parameters. Soaked CBR values were considered as output. The defuzzification of rules was carried out using weighted average approach. The results thus obtained were further tuned using genetic algorithm with 1050 configurable parameters. The results obtained by this hybrid algorithm presented excellent predictability with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.945.

title: Comparative Analysis of Enhanced Weighted A* and Theta* Search Algorithms on Pacman Mazes
authors: Shlok Burmi, Sujal Kishore, Shweta Malwe, Prachi Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Pathfinding is a fundamental element in a number of AI applications in the form of games and navigation. The algorithms that we tested in this study are DFS, BFS, UCS, A, as well as Weighted A* and Theta* with the help of the Berkeley Pac-Man Environment. Our results justify the claim that the effect of heuristic is used in Weighted A* to perform better in terms of speed, since with a negligible decrease in the quality of the path, we were able to achieve speed improvement by almost 30 percent. Theta* services acquire smoothing paths at the expense of increased computation by path line-of-sight checks. The tests used varied in the sizes of the mazes to use. The comparison was done according to the path cost, time, memory and the nodes explored. All approaches had a trade off between speed and accuracy. By looking at the results in general the average run times with small margin of difference (+-2% ) indicate that both the algorithms operate in a consistent manner. The process of determining the usage of memory in all the sizes of the maze assists us in comparing the cost as well as the real comparison of the most feasible method.

title: Intelligent Diagnosis of Transformer Partial Discharge Based on ALEF-SSG Signal Enhancement and Multi-Domain Feature Fusion
authors: Jun Deng, Dui Liu, Haibin Zhou, Jinquan Tang, Zhicheng Xie, Xingzi Liu, Heng Wu, Zhicheng Pan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Partial discharge serves as the primary early indicator of insulation degradation in power transformers, and its accurate diagnosis is critical to ensuring the safe and stable operation of power grids. Aiming at the engineering bottlenecks of existing methods in weak feature identification, sample imbalance, and model generalization, this paper presents a complete intelligent diagnosis framework. First, an ALEF-SSG signal enhancement scheme and an ACM channel hybrid augmentation strategy are proposed to effectively suppress noise, enrich sample diversity, and mitigate overfitting under small-sample conditions. Second, a time-frequency-entropy coupled multi-domain feature set is constructed, followed by improved Relief-F selection and feature enhancement to achieve dimensionality reduction and discriminability reinforcement of high-dimensional features. Finally, an AMR-MSA-Net network is devised together with a class-balanced training strategy to realize accurate classification of four typical fault types. Validation on related dataset demonstrates that the core evaluation metrics of the proposed model exceed 0.94. Compared with conventional models, the proposed method exhibits significant improvements in diagnostic accuracy, generalization ability, and anti-interference performance, with strong engineering applicability.

title: Ontology-Enriched Abstract Topic Modelling for Biomedical Trend Discovery: A MeSH-based LDA Framework with BI Visual Analytics
authors: Ahmad Altarawneh, Stephen Swift, Allan Tucker, Mahir Arzoky
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The explosive growth of biomedical literature increasingly makes it difficult to identify incipient topics and long-term developments. Citation-based indicators are slow to capture developments and often fail to represent influence. Whilst Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) supports content-based trend discovery, its bag-of-words assumption can overlook synonymy, polysemy, and hierarchical relations that are central to biomedical terminology. Moreover, recent work provides limited direct evidence on how far MeSH-enriched abstract representations improve LDA-based trend analysis when compared against the original abstract text. To fill this gap, an ontology-driven methodology for the pre-topic modelling of article abstracts by enriching them with hierarchical Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) terms is introduced in this study. We evaluate four representations, namely original abstracts, MeSH parent categories, MeSH-matched terms with parents, and a fully enriched representation, using a decade-long Scopus corpus of 624,486 biomedical journal articles (2015–2024). Model performance is assessed using coherence, distinctiveness, perplexity, and stability. The fully enriched representation achieves the strongest overall results (coherence 0.606, distinctiveness 0.996, stability 0.725, perplexity 1782.040) relative to the original abstract baseline. Temporal topic analysis further reveals interpretable patterns, including the COVID-19 surge and longer-term structural shifts such as the rise of machine-learning diagnostics. These findings indicate that MeSH-guided abstract enrichment can strengthen topic quality and support more effective biomedical trend discovery through business-intelligence visual analytics.

title: A Method for Elevation Calculation of Offshore Islands and Reefs Far from the Mainland Based on Water Level Derivation Technology
authors: Caixia Yu, Yiman Yang, Yan Lu, Haifeng Lin
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Spaceborne or aerial photogrammetry enables rapid acquisition of image data of uninhabited or special islands and reefs far from the mainland. However, providing a consistent absolute vertical datum for such image data remains a challenging issue. Based on the principle of water level derivation, this paper proposes a method for calculating the instantaneous sea surface elevation by using tidal models, stationary sea surface topography models, and residual water levels transferred from coastal tide gauges. In the China seas, the derivation accuracy of the instantaneous sea surface elevation is assessed using current regional tidal models, stationary sea surface topography models, and residual water level data. Under the worst-case condition without support from coastal tide gauges, the root-mean-square error of the derivation is approximately 18.77 cm. Taking Qianliyan Island in the North Yellow Sea and Weizhou Island in the Beibu Gulf, both 50 km offshore, as an example, the evaluation results show that the root-mean-square error of the estimation under the most unfavorable conditions are 15.22 cm and 14.02 cm, respectively. With the support of coastal tide gauges, the root-mean-square errors are reduced to 8.26 cm and 7.29 cm, respectively.

title: Robust Speed Regulation of BLDC Motors through a Buck Boost Assisted Super Twisting SMC
authors: A. Hasni, H. El Fadil, A. Lassioui, T. Bouanou, M. El Ancary, Y. El Asri, M. Chiheb
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel control strategy for Brushless DC (BLDC) motor drives based on a Buck-Boost converter, aiming to enhance dynamic performance, reduce speed ripples, and suppress chattering effects. The proposed approach combines an Enhanced Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Controller (STSMC) for precise motor speed regulation with a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller dedicated to stabilizing the converter voltage. While the STSMC provides strong robustness against disturbances and parameter variations, it inherently produces chattering phenomena that can deteriorate system smoothness. To mitigate this issue, the PID controller regulates the Buck-Boost converter voltage, ensuring smoother torque and minimizing current oscillations. The hybrid STSMC-PID dual control structure improves both transient and steady-state performance, achieving faster dynamic response and higher control precision. MATLAB/Simulink simulations confirm the efficiency of the proposed method, showing a remarkable reduction of the speed ripple rate to $0.12 \%$ and a fast recovery time of 0.05 s after load perturbations. These results demonstrate that integrating a Buck-Boost converter with the dual STSMC-PID control significantly enhances the stability and responsiveness of BLDC motor drives, providing a promising solution for electric vehicle propulsion and advanced industrial motion systems.

title: Utilizing Academic Analytic Dashboards in Improving Student Graduation Rates
authors: Walid Ibrahim
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing complexity of higher education institutions has elevated the need for academic analytics dashboards as a critical tool for enhancing institutional effectiveness, facilitating evidence-based decision-making, and supporting continuous quality improvement. This paper discusses the importance of academic analytic dashboards and presents the United Arab Emirates University (UAEU) as a case study of effective institutional implementation. The UAEU dashboard provides a comprehensive suite of interactive pages designed to support data-informed decision-making across multiple dimensions of academic performance. Among these, this paper focuses on three core pages that are particularly critical for academic planning and enhancing student graduation rate. These pages deliver real-time, insights into course offerings, student registration, as well as graduation, and retention patterns. The dashboard provides academic administrators with the needed insights to adjust course capacities, add or consolidate sections, and allocate instructional resources more efficiently based on emerging trends. Furthermore, the timely detection of registration anomalies or at-risk patterns allows for targeted interventions that help students stay on track with their academic plans. Collectively, these features enhance institutional responsiveness, promote better resource allocation, and ultimately contribute to improving student progression and graduation outcomes.

title: Optimization potential in the production and end-of-life-management of electric drive units to increase resource efficiency
authors: Thorsten Ihne, Alexander Vogel, Manuela Ockel, Mario Sprenger, Roman Hahn, Marcel Baader, Nils Thielen, Jörg Franke, Florian Risch
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The electrification of road traffic enables significant reductions in greenhouse gas emissions and is therefore a key aspect of the industrial transformation strategy of many industrialized countries. A central component of electric vehicles is the electric traction drive, which is often designed as a functional unit consisting of the motor, a gearbox, and power electronics. The focus of product development for electric drives is particularly motivated by the maximization of energy efficiency during utilization, as this aspect has a major ecological and economic impact over the entire service life of the electric vehicle. However, to further improve ecological and economic sustainability, the entire product life cycle must be considered holistically. This applies to the resource and energy-efficient production of the drive unit and the processing of the end-of-life product by circular economy approaches. In this context, the present paper identifies and discusses optimization potentials regarding the production and the end-of-life-management of electric traction units. The focus here is on the electric motor and the inverter. Both subsystems have a complex manufacturing process chain and may contain critical raw materials. One main aspect that is often neglected in industrial practice is design for recycling or remanufacturing, where there are close interactions between product and process aspects. In this context, the conducted study aims to systematically identify and evaluate complications for component and material recovery using a reference series drive. Based on this, product and process innovations are discussed that allow sustainable and circular production of the drives without affecting the quality-relevant product properties.

title: A Sustainable Approach to Climate Change Mitigation and Energy Security Enhancement Through Biofuels Integrated with IoT
authors: Mohammad Maroof Siddiqui, Piyush Charan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Biofuels consumes approached to be referred to as an substitution and renewable source of energy that has led to a decrease in the production of carbon dioxide besides the improvement in energy security in the world. The given paper presents the details on biofuels and the potential of applying IoT in the role of a smart technology to enhance the production of biofuels, distribution, and environmental impact analysis thereof. IoT sensors, cloud analytics, and real-time data processing can be used to enhance the efficiency of the biofuel systems and make them more transparent and adaptable. It has an accounting of different types of biofuels, state of art production routes, environmental effects, trends in technology and a case study on biofuel development in India. The paper has also proposed a smart biofuel systems methodology in IoT environment, which demonstrates the challenges, opportunities and future perspective towards research and implementation.
