The interrelation of IoT and biofuels is a game changer towards a more green, intelligent and secure energy infrastructure in the world.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Large-Scale Water Quality Assessment
authors: Anuj Kumar Jain, Manu Midha, Narinder Kaur, Geetanjali Sharma, Arushi Vaish, Shaveta Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for environmental sustainability as well as public health and traditional laboratory-based water quality assessment methods are time-consuming and costly and difficult to scale. This study introduces a machine learning based framework for efficient classification of water quality using a large scale environmental data. A dataset with more than 2.5 million data records after cleaning containing 8 most important physicochemical parameters, namely: pH, temperature, turbidity, nitrate, phosphate, hardness, chlorides and dissolved oxygen is used. Comprehensive preprocessing steps such as Cleaning of the data, Normalizing of data, Feature selection and Handling Imbalance in classes are taken up to make the data set rely-able data. Multiple machine learning models namely Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) are evaluated. Experimental results show that the LightGBM model is better than other methods and its accuracy is 98.6% and other evaluation indexes (precision, recall and F1-score) are better. Comparative analysis is a method to prove the robustness and scalability principle of the proposed mechanism in the case of big data. Furthermore, the trained model deployment is done using a Streamlit-based web interface for performing the water quality prediction in real-time. The proposed framework is a timely and cost-effective way of tackling the issue of water quality assessment with possible applications in environmental monitoring, policy making and public health management.

title: From Data to Value: Assessing the Role of Digital Approaches in Enhancing Battery Cell Manufacturing through Expert Perspectives
authors: Marcel Drescher, Rui Yan Li, Clemens Scherer, Paul Lingohr, Heiner Heimes, Achim Kampker
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Battery cell manufacturing represents a critical technology for the future of energy storage and electrified mobility, yet continues to face substantial challenges in process stability, yield optimization, and industrial scalability. Data-driven optimization approaches offer significant potential to address these challenges by leveraging advanced analytics, machine learning, and adaptive control systems. However, their practical implementation remains limited due to systemic barriers. This study investigates the current state, technological readiness, and key obstacles associated with data-driven optimization in battery cell production through a structured expert survey involving 46 professionals from industry and academia. The analysis is structured along three core dimensions: (i) general digitalization challenges, (ii) traceability as a prerequisite for adaptive control, and (iii) cross-process optimization. The results confirm that deficits in process data availability, traceability, and cross-process knowledge are the primary obstacles to industrial adoption of data-driven manufacturing. Despite these challenges, experts unanimously recognize the high potential of advanced traceability and integrated control systems to significantly improve production efficiency and flexibility. The findings underline the need for coordinated research and development efforts addressing technical traceability solutions, data infrastructure harmonization, and the systematic generation of high-quality process data to advance data-driven battery manufacturing towards industrial maturity.

title: A Critical Comparative Survey of ML/DL Approaches for Late Blight Detection and Prediction for Guinean Agriculture
authors: Mohamed Diallo, Demba Faye, Idy Diop, Alie Ndiaye, Salifou Touré
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Late blight (Phytophthora infestans) remains one of the most devastating diseases for staple crops. This survey benchmarks CNN (AlexNet, VGG-16, ResNet-50, DenseNet) and RNN architectures (LSTM, GRU) and hybrid multimodal frameworks. Our analysis reveals that ResNet-50 offers the best accuracy-to-parameter trade-off for image detection (90-99 %), while LSTM outperforms GRU for long-term temporal dependencies in disease risk prediction. We identify four structural limitations: over-reliance on the PlantVillage benchmark, near-total absence of West African training data (the rare African studies (Tanzania, Cameroon) confirm a $\mathbf{3 2 \%} \mathbf{F 1}$-score drop in real-world conditions), neglect of deployment constraints for resource-limited environments, and insufficient integration of image and climate modalities. Based on this analysis, we propose a hybrid CNN-LSTM reference architecture and formulate concrete recommendations for its adaptation to the Guinean agricultural context.

title: Enhancing Small and Medium Sized Enterprises Sustainability: Material Flow Cost Accounting as an Enabler for Circular Economy Thinking
authors: Rahajeng Simpony Santoso, Ang Swat Lin Lindawati, Agustinus Winoto
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of Material Flow Cost Accounting (MFCA) and Green Accounting on circular economy practices among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in West Jakarta, Indonesia. Data were collected from 410 SME owners and managers through a structured questionnaire and analyzed using the Smart- PLS technique. The results reveal that both MFCA and Green Accounting exert positive and significant effects on SMEs’ adoption of circular economy principles. The integration of these two accounting approaches enhances operational efficiency, reduces production waste, and strengthens environmental accountability. The study contributes to sustainability accounting literature by simultaneously testing MFCA and Green Accounting within the SME context-an area that remains underexplored in developing economies. Practically, the findings provide insights for SMEs, policymakers, and academics in promoting resource-efficient and environmentally responsible business practices

title: Revisiting Deepfake Image Detection: An Analysis of Cross-Dataset Generalization Limits
authors: Charri Meriem, Zaydi Hayat, Assoul Saliha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid development of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) has enabled very realistic photorealistic faces to easily be generated in the manipulations, which are widely known as deepfakes, thereby creating significant obstacles for digital forensics to confirm media authenticity. Although high accuracy has been reported by the latest deep learning solutions on standardized evaluation datasets, their robustness to diverse data distributions remains to be challenging tasks for now. The study considers an analysis on the crossdataset evaluation performance on models designed for general image detection, especially focusing on deepfake images, and two models are evaluated in this study because they are very representative in deepfake detection tasks, one is the generic Convolutional Neural Network with ResNet structure, and the other one is designed to detect the artifacts in the images generated by GANs in digital manipulations, which is specifically referred to as the GAN-CNN model. The two models are evaluated in this work based on two publicly accessible deepfake imagery datasets based on intra- and crossdataset testing protocols, according to which significant performance loss has been found in models when they are evaluated in datasets that were not yet seen in the testing tasks, varying to some degrees according to the datasets in question regarding the deepfake detection performance loss due to domain gap, whereas the performance loss can be lessened with the use of manipulation-focused representations by observing the cross-dataset performance improvement based on detection accuracy in the study on the two models.

title: The effects of climate change on the spread of the common bean diseases
authors: Juvénal Havyarimana, Demba Faye, Messikely Anoman, Doudou Dione, Idy Diop
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) is extensively cultivated across Africa and Latin America, particularly in regions of high and medium altitudes. It represents a major staple food crop in developing countries, owing to its substantial nutritional and agronomic value. Despite its importance, the crop is highly susceptible to both biotic and abiotic stresses, which adversely affect plant growth and overall productivity. Climate change has further exacerbated these constraints through rising temperatures, increased rainfall variability, and a higher frequency of extreme weather events. Such environmental pressures significantly compromise plant health and reduce bean yields. In the context of rapid global population growth, it is imperative to enhance bean production to ensure food security. This study presents a literature review encompassing research conducted between 2014 and 2025, with particular focus on the impact of climate variability on the prevalence and spread of bean diseases. The review also synthesizes proposed strategies for mitigating the effects of climate-related stresses. The findings underscore persistent challenges, notably the insufficiently explored influence of climate change on the dynamics of plant diseases.

title: Digital Transformation Readiness and SME Performance: An Information Systems Model Using TOE and Social Media Adoption
authors: Gromyko Bongso, Nicholas Salim, Imelda Citra Anggraini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Following the COVID-19 pandemic, studies shown the barriers issues related to the adoption of new technology. In particular, this research studies the social media effect. The authors apply a strict quantitative approach, utilizing structural equation modeling to analyze survey data from SME owners and managers in Indonesia. The research findings illustrate that, within the context of SMEs, social media adoption affect positively the SME performance and is positively influenced by the SMEs’ technological and organizational readiness. The authors state that social media adoption is a mediator between the TOE related factors and performance outcomes. This research shows that social media adoption in SMEs has a positive impact and, therefore, contributes to the literature pertaining to digital transformation.

title: A Multi-LLM Ensemble Approach for Evaluating Software Development Technologies
authors: Billal El Maalmi, Mohammed Lahmer
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Choosing a framework for a project is not obvious. Although surveys, expert panels, and targeted benchmarks provide guidance, those approaches are costly and difficult to implement. Moreover, they often fail to synchronise with evolving trends. To overcome this limitation, the MultiEval approach automates the evaluation protocol by leveraging six large language models (LLMs): GPT-5, Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Gemini 2.5 Pro, DeepSeek-V3.2, Qwen3-Max, and Grok 3. The approach uses a shared set of standardised prompts to generate ratings on a scale of 1 to 5 across six key dimensions: adoption, community support, learning curve, performance, security, and maturity. We then correlate those scores against GitHub and Stack Overflow signals to assess how well they mirror real-world ecosystem data. For backend frameworks, the six-model ensemble aligns with expert judgments 92% of the time ($\kappa=0.85$ [95% BCI: 0.81-0.88]) and outperforms every single model. Frontend scores exhibit higher variance because developer experience is inherently subjective, yet they remain strongly correlated with public ecosystem data ($r=0.98$ [95% BCI: 0.95-0.99]). A retrospective backtest that withheld all post-2020 information demonstrates the ensemble’s ability to identify long-term winners early (78% top-3 accuracy [95% BCI: 68-86 %]). Taken together, a heterogeneous model ensemble combined with a fixed scoring protocol offers a fast, repeatable method for rating software frameworks at scale.

title: Early Detection of Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome Through Vocal Biomarker Analysis Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Rohan Dhumane, Rahul Bhandekar, Prashant Tarone, Nilesh Nagrale, Jayesh Bisen, Jiwan N. Dehankar, Nutan Dhande
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome leaves vocal traces before visible symptoms appear. This study demonstrates how artificial intelligence detects these changes, enabling earlier diagnosis. We analyzed 400 voice recordings examining 23 acoustic characteristics revealing hidden disease patterns. Using multiple machine learning approaches, Convolutional Neural Networks achieved 97% accuracy distinguishing healthy from Parkinson's-affected voices, outperforming K- Nearest Neighbors and Support Vector Machines. CNNs overcome classical limitations by automatically learning complex patterns without handcrafted features. Voice recordings are non-invasive, inexpensive, and easily collected, ideal for widespread screening and telemedicine. While data standardization and validation challenges remain, this research advances accessible early detection tools deployable via smartphones, giving patients crucial intervention time.

title: Hybrid Essay Assessment Framework: Integrating Neural Content Analysis with Reference-Based Similarity Evaluation
authors: Eman Alduweib, Essa Hezzam, Mohammed Noman, Abdel Khalik Alhammouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Essay grading is one of the most important applications of artificial intelligence in education. As digital learning platforms grow and are expanded, automated grading is said to offer students consistent immediate feedback and reduce teacher's assessment workload. This research provides a theoretical foundation for an automated grading model based on both reference-based similarity evaluation and content-based neural analysis of essays. The two methods are currently being used separately by most automated grading systems. The proposed framework uses three key components: dual pathway representations to maintain semantic depth and structural similarities, dynamic integration of evaluation signals through adaptive integration mechanisms, and plagiarism aware evaluation (to distinguish quality from plagiarism). We draw upon transformer-based language models, Information Retrieval Theory, and Educational Measurement to provide a basis for addressing long standing issues of fairness, consistency and interpretability. this framework provides a comprehensive assessment of a student's writing quality through its dual pathways. In addition, the adaptive integration mechanism in the framework allows for adjustments to be made to the assessment process based on the context in which the assessment is being conducted.

title: ALLMS-Modified Jaya Optimized Unified Power Quality Conditioner to Improve Power Quality
authors: B. Pavankumar, Saroj Pradhan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: In this paper, a PV fed UPQC is constructed to address the Power Quality (PQ) problem in a distribution system. The suggested UPQC combines a series filter and a shunt filter, and an appropriate controller is developed in order to operate both filters effectively. At first, the controller is made to produce the reference injection signal for both filters. The reference for the shunt part is provided by combining an indirect current controller technique with adaptive leaky least mean square (ALLMS) and a modified Jaya algorithm based proportional integral (PI) technique with the active component of PV. The same method is proposed for the series but instead of current signal in shunt, voltage signal is chosen for series. The actual injection and reference injection are compared, and the error is fed to the hysteresis for the UPQC's switching operation. THD analysis is carried out for both current harmonics and voltage sag problem. The acceptable THD value as per IEEE 519 of source current after filtration is 4.31 % for current harmonics and 3.98 % for voltage sag is obtained, which shows the effectiveness of the technique. System performance is analyzed with MATLAB /Simulink platform

title: Secure and Optimized Cyber-Physical Architecture for Smart Grid and IoT-Enabled Energy Systems
authors: Anjali Goswami, Anjali Krushna Kadao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: One of the strong aspects of smart grids is increasing the use of Internet of things (IoT) technologies to enhance monitoring and control of energy, but also poses serious cybersecurity and operational risks. The proposed study is a safe and efficient cyber-physical architecture of IoT-based smart grid systems aimed at increasing data security, operation efficiency, and scalability. The framework suggested incorporates secure communication standards such as AES-256 encryption, TLS communication and MQTT messaging in a manner that promotes a stable and secure transmission of data between grid devices and control systems. Moreover, predictive machine learning algorithms will be implemented that will help predict loads and optimize the grid in real-time. The architecture would also enable the combination of renewable energy sources and energy storage systems that will enhance the use of energy in a sustainable manner. Evaluation conducted with the help of simulating proves that the proposed method would enhance energy efficiency by approximately 15%, communication latency is reduced to 30 ms, and the rate of breaches of security is reduced to 0.2% in comparison to traditional grid systems. The findings are important in how cybersecurity mechanisms and energy optimization strategies can be combined in cyber-physical smart grid infrastructures. The suggested architecture offers a secure and scalable platform of the upcoming smart grid installation and industrial IoT deployments.

title: A Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model for Bias-Aware Decision Making in Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Yuvaraj Duraisamy, Rabins Porwal, Kshamta Chauhan, P. Ramya, Kunal, Sribidhya Mohanty
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Loss of transparency and bias in the detection of financial fraud presents two significant problems, namely, poor adherence to the standards of fraud detection, and lost user confidence. In order to address these issues, we have come up with a Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model that is used to deal with bias-conscious decision making in fraud detection. We use a combination of three aspects Transformers to identify complex pattern of temporal frauds, adversarial debiasing to make fair predictions across the various demographic groups and explainability by visualization of attention and SHAP analysis. We achieve 97.3% accuracy, 98.1% recall, and 99.2% AUC-ROC, better than current algorithms, such as XGBoost (95.1%) and Random Forests (93.8%). Better still, we maintained demographic parity disparity at less than 0.02 which reduced bias by 89.8 per cent relative to conventional systems. The SHAP analysis revealed that the most decisions are made basing on the Transaction Amount, Time of Transaction, and Merchant Category which provides the stakeholders with a clear understanding of the operation of the model. Such a balance between explainability and bias minimization forms a fraud protection system that is both non-discriminatory and credible enough, as required by the regulations and ethical principles of the fintech sector.

title: Integrating Literature and Learning: A Graph Neural Network Framework for Herb-Drug Interaction Prediction Toward Sustainable and Clinically Informed Pharmaceutical Safety
authors: Eyad Mallah, Ahmad Altarawneh, Khaled Omari, Stephen Swift, Mahir Arzoky
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The increasing combined use of herbal medicines and conventional drugs has increased the need for the reliable prediction and mitigation of herb-drug interactions globally. Prediction is difficult because herbal products are chemically complex mixtures, and their constituents can act across multiple biological pathways simultaneously. Conventional computational approaches, including quantitative structure activity relationship models and standard machine learning (ML) classifiers, often underperform in this setting because they represent the problem as isolated entities rather than as an interaction network. This work proposes a more appropriate herb-drug interaction prediction model, called Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network (H-GAN), which is a Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework. By building a heterogeneous graph for herbal components, drugs co-administered, and significant biological targets (e.g., metabolic enzymes, transporters), the proposed model can consider the possible synergistic or antagonistic effects of multiple constituents that are responsible for the interaction outcomes. This approach provides a mechanism for enhancing patient safety and facilitates the inclusion of safety in the sustainable development of pharmaceuticals following the principles of green chemistry. Accordingly, this paper offers a conceptual, literature-grounded framework for herb–drug interaction prediction using heterogeneous graph attention networks, outlining the proposed graph schema, biologically motivated meta-paths, and interpretability considerations to guide future implementation and validation. This contribution is conceptual and framework-orientated; it does not include implementation, dataset construction, or quantitative validation.

title: A Vector-Free Global Terrain Analysis Algorithm and its Application: Extraction of Constructible Areas for Navigation in Wild and Extreme Disaster-Stricken Zones
authors: Boyang Guo, Mianqing Zhong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Aiming at the inefficiency and heavy multi-source data reliance of traditional methods for constructible area extraction in vector-free extreme scenarios (e.g., war, disaster zones), a DEM-only vector-free global terrain analysis algorithm is proposed. The algorithm constructs a directed weighted slope topological graph integrating slope, azimuth and terrain flow direction via DEM preprocessing and regular sampling, then realizes candidate node preliminary screening, terrain-constrained 8-neighborhood DFS connected domain retrieval, and secondary screening by azimuth consistency and area threshold to automatically extract qualified contiguous constructible areas. Lightweight, globally adaptable and reproducible, it abandons auxiliary data and large GIS tools, and can be rapidly applied to vector-free scenarios to provide efficient technical support for emergency engineering terrain decision-making.

title: Effect IoT based-RFID on Stock-Recording Efficiency in Indonesian Retail Industry: T-O-E Model with Workflow Optimization
authors: Bryan Lewis, Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya, Hendry Hartono
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study analyzes effect IoT integration based on RFID technology, system integration, and employee digital competence on stock recording efficiency in the Indonesian retail industry. Internet of Things (IoT) technology has encouraged the adoption of Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology in inventory management to improve stock recording efficiency. However, stock recording efficiency is not only influenced by technology implementation, but also by human, organizational, process, and system factors. The results aim to analyze effect of IoT-based RFID adoption, system integration, and employee digital competence on stock recording efficiency in the Indonesian retail industry, with workflow optimization as a mediating variable and organizational change readiness as a moderating variable. This study uses a quantitative research approach using the SmartPLS application with the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) model, following the methodological guidelines of Hair et al. (2019). The results indicate that the adoption of IoT-based RFID, system integration, and employee digital competence have a positive and significant impact on workflow optimization. In addition, workflow optimization has a significant positive impact on stock recording efficiency and mediates the relationship between independent variables and stock recording efficiency. Organizational change readiness significantly moderates the relationship between workflow optimization and stock recording efficiency. These results indicate that improving stock recording efficiency requires not only technology adoption, but also integrated systems, employee digital competence, optimal workflows, and organizational readiness to manage change.

title:
Numerical Investigation of MASnBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /CsSnGeI
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /SnO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Solar Cell: Understanding the Impact of Absorber Layer Parameters
authors: Abhijit Kumar Kar, Mnisha, Chandni Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The study of solar cell based on perovskite materials have been reported numerously in past few years. However, the most reported paper has used lead as one of the constituent elements of perovskite which pose toxicity and environmental challenges. To address this issue, this study is focused on investigating a lead-free substitute cesium tin– germanium triiodide (CsSnGeI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) which offers better performance. Tin dioxide (SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) and MASnBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> were selected as the electron transport layer (ETL) and hole transport layer (HTL), respectively, because of their suitable energy band alignment and superior carrier mobility. We have investigated the performance of solar cell by varying the absorber thickness and electron affinity. The device has also been investigated for variation in bulk defect and interface defects with ETL and HTL. The software used here is SCAPS 1D for the numerical investigations. The studied solar cell depicted a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 30.61%, with an open-circuit voltage (Voc) of 1.2 V, a fill factor (FF) of 89.8%, and a short-circuit current density (Jsc) of 28.31 mA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Clustering of Equipment for Maintenance in BIM 7D Using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) Approach Applied to Data Reduced by t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding)
authors: Adnane Abdellaoui Alaoui, Mouna El Mkhalet, Nouzha Lamdouar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Building Information Modeling (BIM) in its 7D dimension incorporates maintenance management and life cycle cost of technical equipment. Optimizing this maintenance requires intelligent methods capable of grouping equipment according to their operational behaviors and characteristics. This article proposes a supervised clustering approach using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm applied to dimensionally reduced data using the t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) technique. The objective is to visualize and classify equipment based on aging criteria, failure frequency, temperature, duration, and intensity of use. The results show that this $t-S N E+K N N$ combination allows for distinguishing three main groups of equipment with similar maintenance behaviors, paving the way for predictive models integrated into 7D BIM.)

title: Beyond information: how smart systems manage consumer knowledge
authors: Yasmine Lebbadi, Nisrine El Ayat
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Smart systems, including artificial intelligence driven platforms, recommender systems, conversational agents, and algorithmic decision-support tools, are increasingly embedded in consumer decision-making processes. While prior research has primarily conceptualized these systems as advanced information providers (Shankar, 2018; Huang & Rust, 2021), this article argues that their role extends far beyond information delivery. Drawing on knowledge management theory (Nonaka & Takeuchi, 1995; Davenport & Prusak, 1998), consumer learning (Alba & Hutchinson, 1987; Bettman, Luce, & Payne, 1998), and algorithmic governance (Gillespie, 2014; Pasquale, 2015), we propose that smart systems function as active consumer knowledge managers. We develop a conceptual framework explaining how smart systems manage consumer knowledge through three interdependent processes: knowledge acquisition, via algorithmic curation, personalization, and selective exposure (Thorson & Wells, 2016; Wedel & Kannan, 2016); knowledge transformation, through sensemaking support, cognitive scaffolding, and experiential learning facilitation (Weick, 1995; Hoffman & Novak, 2018); and knowledge governance, whereby algorithmic authority, trust calibration, and opacity shape epistemic dependence and perceived expertise (Luhmann, 1979; Logg, Minson, & Moore, 2019). We argue that smart systems progressively reconfigure consumer knowledge from an individually constructed cognitive asset toward an algorithmically mediated form of knowing, altering autonomy, decision confidence, and perceived control. While these systems enhance cognitive efficiency and reduce information overload (Eppler & Mengis, 2004), they simultaneously raise concerns related to knowledge asymmetry, cognitive passivity, and ethical responsibility. By reframing smart systems as knowledge management actors rather than neutral informational tools, this research advances marketing information systems literature and provides strategic guidance for designing smart systems that support sustainable consumer knowledge development rather than mere behavioral optimization.

title: Study on Cutting of Rectangular Copper Hollow Wire for the Use in Hairpin Stator Electric Traction Motors
authors: Till Backes, Achim Kampker, Henrik Born, Michael Nankemann, Simon Krusenbaum, Matthias Hörder
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The increasing demand for efficient electric traction motors requires innovative approaches in design and production. Hairpin stators have established themselves as a promising technology for increasing the power density and efficiency of electric motors. The use of rectangular hollow copper wires offers additional advantages in performance through significantly improved heat dissipation and weight reduction. However, the precise cutting of these hollow wires poses a technical challenge, as conventional cutting methods can lead to deformation or damage. In this study, the cutting process for rectangular hollow copper wires is analyzed and optimized. Various cutting methods, including mechanical sawing and laser cutting were analyzed in terms of their cutting quality, process speed and effects on material properties. The results show that sawing offers the highest precision with minimal thermal impact on the material. Optimized cutting processes enable improved integration of the hollow copper wires in hairpin stators. The findings of this study contribute to the further development of production technologies for future-oriented electric motors and thus support the transition to sustainable mobility.

title: The Influence of AI-Powered Reviews on Consumer Purchase Intention and Decision in E-Commerce: An Adapted UTAUT2 Approach
authors: Amanda Saphira, Cadelina Cassandra, Danang Prihandoko
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The increasing adoption of AI-powered reviews in e-commerce platforms has changed how consumers evaluate products and make purchase decisions. This study aims to examine the influence of AI-powered reviews on purchase intention and purchase decision by adapting the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology 2 (UTAUT2) framework. This research investigates the effect of effort expectancy, performance expectancy, hedonic motivation, personal innovativeness and trust on consumers' purchase intention as well as the effect of purchase intention on purchase decision. The data were collected using an online survey method and 382 Indonesian e-commerce consumers participated in the study. Data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) with SmartPLS version 4.1.1.6. The result shows that all variables significantly influence purchase intention toward AI-powered reviews. Furthermore, purchase intention is found to significantly affect purchase decisions. This study, however is restricted to Indonesian consumers and uses self-reported data, suggesting that future research might examine cross-cultural contexts and long-term behavioral impacts.

title: Quantum-Resilient Cryptography and Safe Guarding Post Quantum Cryptographic Protocols Against Quantum Threats
authors: S Hemajothi, Keerthana B, Janani R, Farzana E
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The Hybrid approach in Quantum Cryptography creates adaptable security model which provides the safest Communication in the emerging quantum era. The development of quantum computing raise questions about the long-term security. Traditional algorithms, such as RSA, ECC, and Diffie-Hellman, which is seemed computationally secure but it can be effectively solved by quantum algorithms such as Shor's and Grover's. This problem leads to the origination of two key trends in current studies, namely Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), designing mathematical approaches to encryption resistant to quantum attacks; Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) using the principles of quantum mechanics to assure security in communications. Accordingly, this research proposes a hybrid security framework including reliable data encryption and authentication using PQC, combined with the safe key- exchange method of QKD. The model is implemented in Python, Qiskit, and PQClean's Kyber and Dilithium algorithms to show how these two paradigms can cooperate toward quantum- resilient communication. To present the system performance, key exchange efficiency, computational overhead, and security strength have been considered. According to the results, this hybrid approach gives superior defense against quantum threats while maintaining practical effectiveness for real-world applications such as cloud security, 6G networks, and the Internet of Things.

title: Affective Intelligence-Enhanced Visual Content Summarization for Context-Rich Video Understanding
authors: Kumar P, Hari Sainath C
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Combining emotion recognition technology with video to text summarization brings about a totally novel innovative avenue for automated content analysis. Here we present the conceptualization and implementation of the Emotion Infused Visual Content Summarizer (EIVCS) which is emotionally intelligent regarding the summarization methods used in the textual domain. EIVCS takes advantage of the emini API to produce brief video to text summaries and incorporates emotion recognition to determine the emotional states shown by visual content. EIVCS text interpretation and emotional derivation come from the same video frame to generate texts that are not only the summarization of the recorded videos but also the representations of the emotions experienced, the full and contextualized representation of video content. This paper demonstrates the set up the plan, and the components of the EIVCS besides the combination of emotion detection algorithms with novel summarization models. The scheme reveals how the system's ability to interpret linguistic and affective signals may result in improved user content understanding feedback which is the strength of automatic systems that claim to analyze the users' content understanding. Moreover, the potential implementation areas related to this line of inquiry are media analysis, user experience improvement, content recommendation systems, and other areas at the interface where emotional context could be critical to user engagement. By integrating video-based content summarization with emotion recognition, the EIVCS is a new and more human-centered perspective on videos that can not only enhance user experience but also make video content analysis more nuanced and meaningful.

title: Towards Robust Closed-Loop Control for Aerosol Jet Printing Using Laser Transmission Feedback
authors: Sagni Majumdar, Florian Risch, Jorg Franke, Sebastian Reitelshofer
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Aerosol Jet Printing (AJP) is an emerging direct-write technology for fabricating high-resolution functional materials. Despite its advantages, the absence of robust process control remains a major barrier to widespread industrial adoption. This paper addresses the challenge of implementing closed-loop control in AJP by presenting an approach based on laser transmission measurements. Building on previous research employing camera and optical scattering-based feedback mechanisms, this work presents a control architecture that enables real-time, accurate, and non-intrusive monitoring of the aerosol stream. Expanding upon foundational studies, this method facilitates localized process control by employing a single beam splitter with dual photodetectors for measurement and reference signals. This work reviews the current state of AJP process monitoring, identifies limitations of existing control solutions, and details a scalable architecture designed to enhance precision and adaptability in future implementations.

title: Big Data-Driven Monitoring and Risk Early Warning for Mountainous River Waterways
authors: Zhihao Yao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Mountainous river waterways are characterized by complex terrain, abrupt hydrological variation, and coupled navigation-geology hazards, while conventional monitoring and alarm systems remain constrained by fragmented data, low precision, and delayed response. This study proposes a big-datadriven monitoring and risk early warning framework for mountainous river waterways. A heterogeneous data fusion architecture is first established to integrate hydrological, meteorological, navigation, geological, and dispatch-related streams. A dynamic warning model is then developed using an optimized long short-term memory (LSTM) network to encode temporal dependency, cross-source interaction, and hazard escalation patterns. On this basis, an intelligent platform integrating acquisition, analysis, diagnosis, and warning push is designed for closed-loop operation. A representative Wujiang mountainous waterway reach is selected for validation using three years of measured data. Results show that the optimized model achieves an overall warning accuracy of 92.7%, improving by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1. 3 {\%}}$</tex> over a conventional threshold-based method. The false alarm rate decreases to 4.1%, and warning latency under extreme hydrological conditions is controlled within 8 s. The framework accurately identifies high-risk shoals, rapid-flow reaches, and landslide-prone banks, demonstrating a practical route toward safer inland navigation governance and smart water transport upgrading.

title: AI-Based Predictive Energy Management of a Hybrid Solar-Wind System for Green Hydrogen Production: Case Study of Tarfaya, Morocco
authors: Fatima Zahra El-Yazidi, Hamid Mounir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Combining renewable energy systems and the production of green hydrogen presents a major opportunity to enable significant decarbonisation of both the energy and industrial sectors. However, due to the intermittent nature of both solar (PV) and wind resources, achieving and maintaining the necessary conditions for electrolyzers to operate continuously and reliably remains a major hurdle. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an artificial intelligence (AI)based predictive energy management system to manage a hybrid solar-wind-battery system that produces hydrogen. The target location for this application is the coastal city of Tarfaya (Morocco) where the authors conducted an earlier data-driven analysis that determined the location had a very high level of suitability for deploying hybrid renewable systems. Along with the system’s renewable energy harvesting component, on-site climatic data are employed for renewable energy production simulation and in AI-based prediction & intelligent control technique development. A forecasting machine learning model is developed to predict the solar and wind generation on a dayahead basis, and an RL-based energy management strategy is proposed in order to coordinate renewable generation, battery storage as well as grid interaction. The objective of the proposed approach is to reduce RE intermittency and energy loss, as well as guaranteeing the electrolyzer continuous operation.

title: AI-Driven Learning Analytics for STEM Education: Insights from Primary School Admission
authors: Dina Fitria Murad, Shahnorbanun Sahran, Bambang Dwi Wijanarko, Erick Fernando, Siti Maryam, Silvia Ayunda Murad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) education in the context of elementary school admissions. Through the development of an AI-based learning analytics framework named AISA-P (AI-Based STEM Admission Framework for Elementary Education), this study investigates the technical and pedagogical implications of applying AI to evaluate student readiness for STEM learning. The students involved were in Grade 11, Level 2, at a high school in Pariaman City, totaling 200 students (4 classes). Using a mixed-methods empirical design, this study combined quantitative machine learning techniques-such as Random Forest and Decision Tree algorithms-with qualitative interviews involving teachers and parents. The findings indicate that AI can predict STEM readiness with $\mathbf{8 7. 4 \%}$ accuracy while maintaining interpretability and fairness. However, this study also highlights ethical and pedagogical concerns regarding algorithm transparency, teacher autonomy, and data privacy. By combining analytical rigor with human-centered educational values, this study provides new conceptual and empirical understanding of how AI can support equitable, data-driven, and inclusive elementary education. This study provides a framework that combines empirical evidence and human-centered design principles to inform equitable, transparent, and personalized admissions practices in elementary STEM education.

title: Foundational Study of Topological Indicators and Neural Vision Models for Enhanced UAV Navigation
authors: Kush Saraf, Rudra Patel, Lithika Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Traditional SLAM based pipelines have high computational costs, while purely learning based methods have limited generalization capabilities. Neither approach is suitable for autonomous UAV navigation in conditions where GPS is unavailable and where resources are limited. Therefore, lightweight navigation methods that combine robustness and efficiency will be very important for small UAV platforms. This research will evaluate the implementation of a dual layer visual navigation system that combines topological place recognition with a learning based vision based local correction model, in a lightweight form that provides robust navigation with reduced processing overhead by separating the local and global aspects of motion refinement. Visual motion information and cues from the current viewpoint and destination node are integrated with the neural layer for geometric adjustments, whereas the topology layer creates a compact graph like structure of visual input keyframes for rough localization and route, destination selection. Preliminary results show that the proposed dual layer configuration enables models to remain smaller, which is particularly beneficial for resource constrained platforms, and that the proposed technique provides greater overall stability and path following than the existing single topology navigation approach. This effort serves to validate the proposed design as well as establish a foundation for additional comprehensive testing and practical development.

title: Optimizing Efficiency and Performance of Axial Flux Machines for Dual-Drive Systems with a variable Air gap
authors: Niklas Kolb, Thomas Albrecht, Stefan Gretzinger, Jan-Niklas Molan, Lennart Kopp, Markus Kley
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Battery electric vehicles are gaining increasing importance, particularly in terms of sustainability and global competitiveness. Beyond the widely used radial flux machines (RFMs), axial flux machines (AFMs) offer new possibilities for drive topologies due to their compact design. Their small axial package enables compact dual drives, making them the subject of this research. This paper focuses on enhancing the efficiency and performance of AFMs through the implementation of a variable air gap.

title: Subcellular Microdosimetry in a Virtual 3D HaCaT Cell Exposed to 26.5 GHz Millimeter Waves
authors: Sara Fontana, Noemi Dolciotti, Giulia Renna, Anna Sannino, Gianmarco Ronza, Stefania Romeo, Olga Zeni, Maria Rosaria Scarfî, Francesca Apollonio, Micaela Liberti
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The deployment of fifth-generation (5G) has renewed interest in the assessment of human exposure to radiofrequency electromagnetic fields (RF-EMF). This study presents the development of an advanced virtual 3D HaCaT cell, reconstructed from confocal microscopy images. These models are employed for microdosimetric simulations at 26.5 GHz to evaluate electric field distributions and volumetric power dissipation density at cellular and subcellular levels.

title: Exploration of Engineering Educational Models based on CDIO and TPACK
authors: Meriem El Batoul Eddaif, Mohamed Tabaa, Souad Ahriz, Es-Saadia Aoula
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The 21st century has witnessed a profound digital transformation in higher education, particularly within engineering education institutions. While the CDIO (Conceive–Design–Implement–Operate) framework promotes experiential and project-based learning grounded in real engineering practice, there is no clear approach for operationalizing technology, pedagogy, and content knowledge to enhance these experiential learning opportunities in a way that aligns seamlessly with CDIO standards. Conversely, the TPACK (technological, pedagogical and content knowledge) framework offers a theoretical foundation for teaching but lacks contextual anchoring in authentic engineering workflows. In order to facilitate the methodical creation of successful engineering learning experiences, this paper proposes an integrated CDIO–TPACK pedagogical model that integrates TPACK competencies with CDIO standards. The model provides educators with an organised approach to curriculum design, instructional planning, and assessment by clearly mapping CDIO standards to content knowledge, pedagogical knowledge and technological knowledge. A structured workflow for a Python programming course given to first-year engineering students demonstrates the applicability of the proposed framework into instructional designs that promote active learning and close the gap between theory and practice.

title: PSAR: Progressive Structure-Aware Refinement for Salient Object Detection via Spectral-Biased Dynamic Curriculum
authors: Aarsh Muley, Manjusha Behera, Rukmabha Mukherjee, Tapas Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: State-of-the-art SOD models like F3Net often face high computational demands due to complex feature aggregation modules. Here, we introduce PSAR, a streamlined Feature Pyramid Network that replaces these complex modules with a Dy namic Resolution Curriculum (DRC). Inspired by the Frequency Principle, DRC scales inputs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224^{2} \rightarrow 352^{2}$</tex>) to stabilize global patterns before refining high-frequency details. On the DUTSTE benchmark, against a reproduced F3Net (32 epochs), PSAR achieves a superior MAE of 0.0354 (vs. 0.0421), indicating better global consistency. While we observe a trade-off in boundary precision (lower Max- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{\beta}$</tex>) due to smoothing, PSAR operates with 46% fewer Inference FLOPs and significantly reduced training costs. This confirms that curriculum learning enables lighter models to achieve faster convergence and lower global error rates under constrained budgets.

title: Parameter Estimation of Intermittent Sampling Repeater Jamming Based on Orthogonal Block-Concatenated Waveform Design
authors: Chang Zhou, Wenfeng Chen, Zhu Tang, ALei Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Intermittent Sampling Repeater Jamming (ISRJ) achieves both deception and suppression effects by flexibly setting jamming parameters. Therefore, fast and high-precision estimation of ISRJ parameters is the premise for radar to implement effective anti-jamming. Existing estimation methods based on time-frequency analysis and ambiguity function transformation have problems such as high computational complexity and poor robustness at low jamming-to-noise ratios. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight jamming parameter estimation method based on radar transmitter waveform optimization and receiver-side multi-block energy center calculation. This method uses deep learning to design a set of orthogonal phase-coded block sub-blocks with low autocorrelation sidelobes and low cross-correlation peaks, and splices them into a radar transmit waveform. When affected by ISRJ, based on the peak position offset and amplitude distribution of the matched filtering output of each sub-block, the Multi-block Energy Center Calculation (MEC) algorithm is used to calculate the jamming sampling period and duty cycle, avoiding complex transform domain operations. Simulation results show that Monte Carlo experiments verify the effectiveness of the proposed parameter estimation algorithm, which can provide an engineering implementation path for radar to sense ISRJ.

title: Battery Management Systems for Lithium-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles: Architectures, Modeling, and SOC Estimation Methods - A State of the Art
authors: A. Fhail, H. El Fadil, A. Lassioui, H. Abbade, A. Hasni, S. El Meslouhih
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) rely on lithium-ion batteries as their main energy source, making battery safety, efficiency, and reliability critical design issues. To ensure proper battery operation under varying load and environmental conditions, battery management systems (BMS) are integrated into EV battery packs. This paper provides a concise state-of-the-art review of BMS technologies for lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicle applications. The review covers common BMS architectures and implementation topologies, as well as equivalent circuit models used for battery behavior representation. Key BMS functions, including state-of-charge (SOC), state-of-health (SOH), and state-of-power (SOP) estimation methods, are also discussed. In addition, recent trends such as artificial intelligence-based techniques and wireless battery management systems (wBMS) are briefly highlighted. The paper aims to offer an application-oriented overview that supports the selection of suitable BMS solutions for electric vehicle systems.

title: Deep Learning Framework Integrating Self-Supervised Learning and Attention for Landslide Susceptibility Mapping
authors: Yanwei Zhang, Lina Yu, Qiwen Dong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The field of landslide susceptibility mapping has long faced a lack of high-precision labeled data. This paper proposes a deep learning framework that combines self-supervised learning with an attention mechanism. The framework utilizes the classic DeepLabv3+ model as its backbone and pre-trains it using a self-supervised learning strategy to enhance feature representation. At the same time, it introduces the CBAM module to enhance the model's sensitivity to spatial and channel information in the input features, thereby strengthening its ability to extract information from remote sensing data. This paper conducts standardized comparative experiments between the proposed deep learning framework and several classical models commonly used in landslide susceptibility studies. The results show that the proposed method achieves an F1 score of 0.820 and a mIoU of 0.846 on the test set, and it outperforms the comparison models overall. In addition, ablation studies are carried out to demonstrate that both self-supervised pre-training and the introduction of the attention mechanism contribute positively to improving model accuracy. Finally, the paper extracts and analyzes the channel attention scores from CBAM, which reveal the relative importance of different influencing factors in the proposed model. The findings of this study provide a new framework for applying deep learning models to regional landslide susceptibility analysis. Furthermore, the proposed framework shows the potential to improve performance by making effective use of limited high-precision labeled data.

title: Oral-Mamba vs. Swin-UNet: A Comparative Deep Learning Analysis for Gingivitis, Dental Caries, and Dental Calculus Segmentation
authors: Remya R Prabhu, Neeraja K, Vimina E R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Gingivitis, dental caries, and dental calculus are some common oral health issues that many people face. Identifying these problems early through intraoral photographic images is crucial for early diagnosis and computer-assisted dental checks. This study compares two deep learning (DL) models, Oral-Mamba and Swin-UNet, for analyzing oral images to identify gum disease, tooth decay, and plaque. The focus is on training the models to recognize only disease-affected areas effectively. The models are trained and tested using a dataset of 3,365 annotated oral images in a 3-fold cross-validation setup. Evaluation metrics include Intersection over Union (IoU), Precision, Recall, and Accuracy. Results show that the Swin-UNet model achieves an average IoU of 0.8909, an average precision of 0.8989, an average recall of 0.8860, and an average accuracy of 0.9633, while the Oral-Mamba model boasts an average IoU of 0.8477, an average precision of 0.9112, an average recall of 0.9197, and an average accuracy of 0.9743. The experimental results show the comparative performance of the Swin-UNet and the Oral-Mamba model for the segmentation of oral diseases. The Oral-Mamba model shows higher precision, recall, and accuracy compared to the Swin-UNet model, which indicates the improved performance of the proposed model for accurately identifying the disease region in the intraoral image. The results clearly show the effectiveness of the architecture and the increasing importance of deep learning models for dental diagnosis.

title: Generative AI for Structural Design of Earthquake-Resistant Civil–Mechanical Systems
authors: Qasim Aljamal, Mohammad Q. Al-Jamal, Jamal Al Sadi, Mohammad Aljaidi, Mahmoud Al-Jamal, Ayoub Alsarhan, Maruf Farhan, Wael Hadi, Ahmad Abdullah Alshammari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The article discusses a machine learning-based earthquake-resistant building generative architecture, more than the traditional deterministic architectures and finite element analysis. This model is a system composed of physics-based generative models and structural dynamics to maximize the stiffness, flexibility,, and energy dissipation designs. It applies a mixture of variational autoencoders and generative adversarial networks as the input, and a series of seismic load histories and material properties. The structure revealed a significant increment: the 32.8% growth in the energy intake, the 24.5% drop in the peak movement and the incredible 99.92% achievement in declaring the designs as either seismically secure or unsafe. The results point to the opportunities of generative AI to create strong and effective structural solutions.

title: Energy Management and DC Bus regulation of an isolated DC Microgrid using Backstepping Control and ANN-Based MPPT
authors: Hajar Akli, Abdelowahed Hajjaji, Hassan Abouobaida, Amina El Aaoud, Safeer Ullah, Ghulam Hafeez, Khadija Oualifi, Abdelmoghit Fathelkhair
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a backstepping-based control and energy management strategy for an isolated DC microgrid integrating photovoltaic, wind, and battery energy storage systems. Due to the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources, advanced maximum power point tracking (MPPT) techniques are required to ensure efficient energy extraction under variable environmental conditions. In this work, an artificial neural network (ANN)-based MPPT algorithm is employed to generate optimal voltage references for the $\mathbf{P V}$ and wind subsystems. An energy management system (EMS) is developed to coordinate power sharing and generate the reference battery current, ensuring DC bus voltage stability and balanced operation. The proposed backstepping control scheme is designed to regulate the PV and wind boost converters as well as the bidirectional battery converter, enabling accurate reference tracking and robust performance. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in maintaining DC bus voltage stability and improving energy utilization under renewable and load variations.

title: Ensemble Strategies for LLM-based Text Summarization: A Timing-Based Framework
authors: Rooz Munjal, Sonali Goyal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: One of the biggest problems with big language models (LLMs) is finding the right balance between accuracy, cost, and latency while also cutting down on hallucinations. This research presents a temporal categorization of ensemble techniques (EMs) into Ensemble Before Inference (EBI), Ensemble During Inference (EDI), and Ensemble After Inference (EAI). A systematic review of 11 ensemble studies (2022–2025) employing transformers in multimodal domains reveals distinct performance–cost trade-offs. Ensemble approaches significantly enhance hallucination detection, medical and legal summarization, multi-domain tasks, and low-resource language processing, yielding performance improvements between 2% to 33% compared to single models. The temporal taxonomy shows that each strategy has its own strengths: EBI reduces runtime overhead while keeping quality, EDI improves output quality at a higher computational cost, and EAI is a balanced and versatile option. This framework gives academics and practitioners a structured way to choose the best ensemble techniques depending on the needs of the application and the resources available.

title: Heterophily-Robust Patient-Similarity Graphs for Parkinson’s Screening from Voice
authors: Ekta Srivastava, Siddhant Ujjain, Tapan Kumar Gandhi, Sandeep Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Voice-based Parkinson’s disease (PD) screening is a data-analytics task requiring robust and well-calibrated risk estimates from small, correlated tabular features, and aligns with SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-Being). We propose a lightweight patient-similarity pipeline that builds a k-nearest-neighbor graph on standardized acoustic descriptors and applies Personalized PageRank (PPR) with restart to aggregate multi-hop evidence under heterophily (label-discordant neighborhoods). The method is fully specified for reproducibility (graph construction and propagation settings) and supports decision support via calibrated probabilities and transparent explanations. We provide dual interpretability using SHAP feature attributions and influential-neighbor inspection, and apply post-hoc temperature scaling to improve calibration. On the UCI Parkinsons dataset (n=195; PD= 147, HC= 48), PPR achieves 0.974 AUROC, 0.953 F1, and Brier 0.084, reducing ECE by 0.047. The most significant improvements occur in low to intermediate homophily settings, and ablation studies over k ∈{3,5,10}, cosine and Euclidean distance metrics, and the restart weight α show robustness. Running a full 5-fold pipeline takes approximately ~45 s on a laptop while giving a clear probability score that can be used for screening in clinics.

title: Assessing the Efficacy of Pre-trained Deep Learning Models for Facial Expression Recognition
authors: Maryam Alkandari, Alaa Eleyan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) is a fundamental problem in affective computing and human– computer interaction, with applications in healthcare, surveillance, and intelligent user interfaces. Recent advances in deep learning, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have significantly improved FER performance by leveraging transfer learning from large-scale image datasets. This paper presents a comparative performance analysis of four state-of-the-art pre-trained CNN architectures, namely, ResNet152, EfficientNetV2-L, Xception, and DenseNet201 which are applied to facial expression recognition using the Extended Cohn–Kanade (CK+) dataset. The experiments were conducted on eight emotion classes with data augmentation applied to enhance generalization. All models were fine-tuned using the Adam optimizer under identical experimental conditions. Performance evaluation was carried out using confusion matrices and class-wise, micro-averaged, and weighted-averaged metrics. Among the evaluated models, DenseNet201 achieved the highest accuracy of 89.25%, outperforming EffiecientNetV2-L by 1.08% under identical training conditions. This indicates that dense feature reuse is particularly effective for capturing subtle facial expression patterns, while EfficientNetV2-L provides a balanced trade-off between accuracy and model efficiency

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Improving Multilingual Neural Machine Translation using Semantic and Linguistic Features
authors: Yaxi Su, Mohamad Yusof Darus, Norizan Mat Diah, Yuxia Du, Ling Li, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study presents a novel semantic-linguistic feature decoupling framework designed to significantly enhance the performance and generalization capabilities of multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT). By employing a specialized dual-channel feedforward network, the proposed approach explicitly separates language-agnostic semantic representations from language-specific linguistic features. This structural decoupling facilitates more robust cross-lingual knowledge transfer and effectively mitigates interference between diverse languages. Furthermore, the framework is integrated with a lightweight Transformer architecture that leverages dynamic sparse attention mechanisms and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). This combination ensures both exceptional computational efficiency and high-quality translation outputs. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on standard benchmarks such as IWSLT2017 demonstrate remarkable gains: the model achieves a substantial BLEU score improvement of +3.74 in zero-shot translation directions. Simultaneously, it optimizes operational efficiency by reducing memory usage by 62.5% and increasing inference speed threefold. Ultimately, this framework successfully addresses the critical issue of off-target generation, offering a scalable solution suitable for deployment in diverse resource-constrained environments.

title: Cognitive Networking using Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Optimization
authors: Jobin Thomas, Riya Sanjesh, Devi Priya V.S., Galiveeti Poornima
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Modern communication networks are becoming more complex due to factors such as the rapid proliferation of varied devices, changing traffic patterns, and tight quality-of-service standards. It is challenging to respond in real time with rule-based or model-driven network management in such dynamic contexts. Cognitive networking holds great promise as it enables networks to see, understand, and adjust autonomously. This study presents a cognitive networking framework for optimising networks in real-time that is based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Intelligent and adaptive optimisation of routing, resource allocation, and congestion control is possible when network control is viewed as a sequential decision-making issue. By interacting with the network environment and learning the necessary rules from experience, the framework adapts to new scenarios without network models. The experimental results demonstrate that cognitive networking based on DRL outperforms typical optimisation methods in terms of latency, throughput, and packet loss, all while being scalable and stable.

title: Research on Gridded Reconstruction and Generalization Capability of Sunshine Duration in Hubei Province
authors: Fei He, Chuandong Zhu, Peihua Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Obtaining high-resolution gridded sunshine duration data in complex terrain is challenging due to the sparse distribution of ground stations. To overcome the extrapolation errors of traditional models, this paper proposes a two-step gridded reconstruction framework integrating astronomical physical constraints with machine learning. Driven by CLDAS high-resolution radiation products, the framework first establishes theoretical sunshine limits and truncation thresholds based on celestial mechanics. Subsequently, Random Forest (RF) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) are utilized to construct non-linear mappings of multi-dimensional spatiotemporal features. We innovatively evaluated the models using both temporal and rigorous spatial hold-out validations. Results indicate that XGBoost excels in temporal historical imputation at known coordinates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{R}=0.857)$</tex>. However, in ungauged spatial estimation, multiple linear regression suffers catastrophic extrapolation collapse, whereas RF demonstrates superior spatial generalization robustness without topographical overfitting (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.842$</tex> hours). Ultimately, a high-precision 0.0625° gridded sunshine duration product for Hubei Province was successfully generated using the RF algorithm, providing a robust scientific paradigm for large-scale meteorological grid reconstruction.

title: Deep Semantic Feature Learning and Efficient Indexing for Content-Based Image Retrieval
authors: Khushbu Devi Tiwari, Swati Khanve, Nitya Khare, Vinay Lowanshi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A lot of organizations and people have huge amounts of images. These pictures can be used for training data, visualization, marketing, etc. Searching through picture archives can become an overwhelming task because of the amount of data. Deep features require efficient indexing and storage methods for image retrieval that is scalable and efficient. Content-based image retrieval (CBIR) is the system that allows access to and search for images based on their contents. CBIR makes it easier for people to look for information or products by efficiently searching and browsing the world of images on the internet. Manually extracting images of interest as per query is a tedious process. CBIR plays an important role a better alternative to retrieval of images on a large scale. It also helps for quicker and easier detection than manual detection. The system generates feature vectors for every pixel in the image for further analysis of the image and the object. The system does not rely on any content (text or metadata) surrounding the image but on the actual content in the image.Systems based on handcrafted features of CBIR do not have sufficient semantic representation and scalability. We need to find a better way to extract content from images. Additionally, efficient and scalable algorithms for indexing are also needed.

title: Design and Optimization of a Waveguide Applicator for Cell Cultures Exposure in the FR1 Band of 5G Signals
authors: Stefania Romeo, Olga Zeni, Anna Sannino, Micaela Liberti, Francesca Apollonio, Alessandra Paffi, Maria Rosaria Scarfî
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a waveguide applicator for exposure of cell cultures to RF-EMF at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 ~ G H z}$</tex>, an operative frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> technologies. In accordance with the requirements for an RF applicator, numerical dosimetry analysis was carried out to maximize uniformity of SAR distribution in the cell monolayer and the number of samples to be exposed simultaneously.

title: Decision letter for "End-to-End Depositional Microfacies Identification from Variable-Log Inputs Using CNN and Attention-CRF"
authors: N/A

title: Cpp2Rust: Automatic Translation of C++ to Safe Rust
authors: Lucian Popescu, Francisco Gouveia, Henrique Preto, João Silveira, Dmytro Hrybenko, José Fragoso Santos, Nuno P. Lopes
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Contextual Embeddings: Implementing Bound Variables through Instance Resolution
authors: Samantha Frohlich, Jessica Foster, G. A. Kavvos, Meng Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: An Intelligent AI-Based Multimodal Platform for Adaptive Interview Simulation and Candidate Assessment
authors: Kanukula Anshu, Yugandhar Manchala, Battula Bhoomika Naga Sai, Mungi Vverendhra Kumar, Gedipudi Abijith, Nirmal Keshari Swain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This research work introduces an AI-based mock interview performance analysis system developed using Streamlit to simulate and analyze actual interview situations. The system combines computer vision, audio processing, and natural language processing to analyze candidate answers from different perspectives, such as communication effectiveness, facial expressions, and emotional undertones. The system allows users to get real-time feedback to aid them in preparing for interviews through sentiment analysis, speech rate tracking, and visual analysis. For academic and professional interview preparation, the system provides an interactive and holistic self-analysis tool that accepts text, audio, and video inputs with real-time insights analysis.

title: Automated Solar Cell Surface Defect Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: R. Santhosh Kumar, Bachu Hemanth, Anagani Sai Deepthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Renewable energy resources, especially solar energy, play a crucial role in fulfilling the increasing demand for energy while addressing environmental concerns. Nevertheless, solar cell efficiency reduces with time owing to surface defects caused by environmental and usage factors. Electroluminescence imaging helps to highlight these defects, but manual processing of these images is a time-consuming, errorprone, and inefficient process. This paper presents a completely automated deep learning-based system for the detection and localization of solar cell surface defects. The system includes a series of preprocessing tasks such as distortion correction, segmentation, and perspective transformation, followed by convolutional neural network for defect detection. To handle the issue of imbalanced data, methods such as minority oversampling and data augmentation are used. The proposed system is trained and tested on a real-world electroluminescence image dataset, with an overall classification accuracy of 87.4% for the detection of common solar cell surface defects such as cracks, scratches, and impurities. Moreover, a Faster Region-Based Convolutional Neural Network (Faster R-CNN) model with a Feature Pyramid Network (RFPN) is designed to facilitate efficient detection and localization of surface defects. The system minimizes the time required for manual inspection, improves quality control, and facilitates the automation of solar cell manufacturing. Experimental results show that deep learning approaches are a trustworthy and scalable solution for solar cell surface defect detection in the photovoltaic industry.

title: News Under the Microscope: Exposing Fakes with Classic Machine Learning
authors: Naeema Salah Alazemi, Nabil M. Hewahi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The rapid spread of misinformation through online news platforms poses significant societal challenges, making fake news detection an urgent area of study. This paper presents a machine learning-based framework for classifying fake and real news articles using classical algorithms combined with TFIDF vectorization. Utilizing the publicly available Fake and Real News Dataset from Kaggle, we apply thorough text preprocessing including lemmatization, stopword removal, and vectorization to transform raw news content into structured features.We evaluate five supervised learning models: Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Multinomial Naïve Bayes, Random Forest, and a Voting Classifier. On a held-out test set and via 5-fold cross-validation, Random Forest and SVM deliver near perfect performance, while Naïve Bayes trails but remains competitive given its speed. We further analyze computational cost and potential overfitting, and discuss why tree ensembles excel for this dataset. Finally, we position classical models relative to recent transformer-based approaches and outline conditions where lightweight models are preferable.While transformer-based architectures such as BERT and DistilBERT have achieved strong results in fake news detection, they are discussed in this paper only for contextual comparison. Our findings demonstrate that well-engineered classical models remain highly competitive, achieving near-perfect accuracy at a fraction of the computational cost typically associated with deep learning approaches.

title: Temperature Compensation in RF Reflectometry for Crop Quality Assessment
authors: Adeoluwa Oyinlola, Temitope Odedeyi
abstract: This study examines the impact of temperature on RF reflectometry (RF-R) measurements in four root crops: cassava, carrot, sweet potato, and potato. Using a dual-pin RF probe and a vector network analyzer, reflection coefficient, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>, was measured across 401 frequency range of 30 kHz to 200MHz as samples cooled from 40 °C to 25 °C. A linear relationship between S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and temperature was observed across all crops. Temperature correction factors (TCFs) were derived to normalise measurements, with the aim of improving the accuracy of RF-R-based quality characterisation in food and crop analysis, particularly under field conditions.

title: A Unified AI-Based Approach for Performance Optimization and Threat Detection in Telecommunication Systems
authors: Prashant Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
