abstract: The rapid growth in telecommunication systems is resulting in increased complexity in these systems, thus creating problems in optimizing their performances and detecting intrusions in real-time. Conventional machine learning techniques and rule-based systems face difficulties in dealing with high-dimensional data and class imbalance problems. In this paper, an intelligent system called EATIO-Net (Ensemble Attention-Based Telecom Intrusion and Optimization Network) is presented for optimizing the performances of telecommunication systems and detecting intrusions simultaneously. Various data preprocessing techniques are applied in EATIO-Net, including outlier removal using IQR and Z-score methods, categorical encoding, normalization, class balancing using SMOTE, and text preprocessing using stopword removal and lemmatization techniques. Three deep learning models, named “Attention-based Multi-Layer Perceptron” (Attention-MLP), “TabNet,” and “CNN-LSTM hybrid,” are implemented on a balanced data set consisting of 159,926 training samples, 39,982 testing samples, and 78 features, where the ensemble technique is applied for improving the robustness of the predictions made by the models. The accuracy of the proposed framework is validated by the results obtained, where 98% accuracy, 97% precision, recall, and F1-score are achieved, making it suitable for modern high-speed telecommunication environments.

title: Synthetic Twins: Toward Sustainable, Tunable 3D Printed Models of Human Tissues
authors: Gianmarco Dolino, Damiano Coato, Alice Berardo, Emanuele Luigi Carniel, Paolo Gargiulo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The concept of the digital twin has already transformed engineering and medicine by enabling virtual replicas of organs and tissues for simulation, diagnostics, and treatment planning. Yet, many biomedical applications require physical counterparts that replicate not only the anatomy but also the mechanical behavior of living tissues. To address this gap, we introduce the Synthetic Twin: a sustainable, physical analog of human tissue, fabricated via advanced 3D printing with tunable microstructure that reproduce native mechanical properties. Unlike conventional phantoms, which depend exclusively on material composition, Synthetic Twins leverage microstructural engineering (e.g., fiber reinforcement, layering, porosity modulation) to replicate a wide spectrum of healthy and pathological conditions. As a proof-of-concept, we present the design and characterization of 3D printed fiber-reinforced cartilage phantoms, demonstrating how microstructural tuning enables anisotropic behavior that closely resembles native tissue. Using mechanical testing combined with Digital Image Correlation (DIC), we evaluated samples with single fiber orientations (superficial, middle, deep) as well as a novel multilayered construct mimicking the stratification of native cartilage. Results show that microstructural design governs stiffness and Poisson’s ratio in a depth-dependent manner, with multilayered constructs displaying the highest fidelity. This work demonstrates that Synthetic Twins can provide reproducible, customizable, and ethically sustainable alternatives to animal and cadaveric models, with applications in diagnostics, surgical training, and personalized healthcare.

title: Identification of vulnerable lines in power system considering the randomness of wind power
authors: Ning Wang, Lixia Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To deal with the uncertain challenges brought by the high proportion of wind power integration, this paper proposes a fragile line identification method considering the randomness of wind power. Firstly, based on the scene generation technology combining Latin hypercube, K-means clustering and Monte Carlo sampling, the random scene model of wind power is constructed; Furthermore, the network structure vulnerability, load impact vulnerability and branch breaking vulnerability are proposed, and the final vulnerability ranking is obtained by TOPSIS method. The simulation in IEEE 39 and IEEE 118 bus system shows that this method can effectively identify the vulnerable lines of the system in the random wind power scenario, and analyses the impact of wind power penetration on the vulnerability, which provides a theoretical basis for power grid security assessment.

title: Trustworthy Big Data Pipelines: Fairness-Aware and Privacy-Preserving Knowledge Discovery Framework
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Prasanta Parida, Manish Gupta, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: It can be indicated that the given paper deals with the design, implementation, and evaluation of reliable big-data pipelines that would also be fair and privacy conscious when knowledge is being discovered. Modern data pipelines are mediating an increasing set of high-stakes decisions across a variety of domains (healthcare, finance, public policy), but that are susceptible to privacy leakage, representational and procedural bias, and opaquitas that erode trust between the stakeholders. Our proposed conceptual framework (i) models the data pipeline as a process where each stage is handled in an accountable manner (data acquisition, cleaning, entity matching, model training, and deployment); (ii) incorporates interventions that are cautious of fairness (preprocessing, in-pipeline auditing, model selection, and post-processing); and (iii) incorporates provable privacy mechanisms (differential privacy, secure multi-party computation, and privacy-related generation of synthetic data) without impairing analytics utility. The framework also suggests the provision of instrumentation to support constant surveillance, provenance, and human-in-the-loop governance so as to enforce remedial measures and legal regulations. The paper formulates research problems and evaluation standards and an experimental roadmap to compare tradeoffs between privacy and fairness metrics and downstream utility by synthesizing recent contributions in the areas of privacy-preserving generative modelling, pipeline-sensitive fairness studies, and operational trustful AI practices. The work is based on the increasingly common demands regarding the pipeline-level implementation of fairness and the comprehensive consideration of privacyinterfairness dependencies in machine learning systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Explainable AI Framework for Predicting Customer Churn in Telecom Networks
authors: Shivakumar Nethani, Sharfunisa. N, Kalneedi Rambabu, N Subbu Krishna, Manjula Mallya M, Deepali Yewale
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Customer churn in telecommunications imposes substantial revenue risk and necessitates predictive models that are both discriminative and operationally interpretable; existing high-capacity learners often deliver opaque outputs or implausible prescriptive recommendations. This study aimed to develop and validate a latency-aware, neurosymbolic explainable AI pipeline that concurrently maximizes churn discrimination, calibration and actionable interpretability. A large-scale experimental study was conducted using a 2.15M-record telecom corpus; the architecture fuses gradient boosters (LightGBM/CatBoost/XGBoost), an FT-Transformer for tabular interactions, and a GNN head for call/SMS relational signals within a calibrated stacking meta-learner. Preprocessing combined iterative Bayesian imputation, Boruta selection and SMOTE balancing. Explainability comprised TreeSHAP, Integrated Gradients/DeepSHAP, TCAV concept scores and constrained DiCE counterfactuals; fidelity, stability and sparsity metrics plus bootstrap/DeLong statistical tests were employed. The stacked hybrid achieved superior discrimination and calibration (AUC-ROC = 0.993, 95% CI [0.990,0.995]; PR-AUC = 0.972; Brier = 0.028) and concentrated churn risk (lift@10% = 3.15). Ablations show SMOTE and Boruta materially drive PR-AUC and F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> gains. The explainability stack produced high local fidelity (TreeSHAP) and coherent concept alignment (TCAV ρ ≈ 0.72) with sparse, plausible counterfactuals (mean Δ features ≈ 2.2; plausibility ≈ 0.92). Integrating neurosymbolic concept explanations with hybrid predictive stacking yields both state-of-the-art churn performance and operationally actionable, audit-ready explanations, supporting deployable retention interventions and governance.

title: Optimisation of Phase Processing And Noise Reduction In Speech Enhancement Using CSM and Adaptive Filters
authors: Savita More, Jagdish Helonde, Prakash G. Burade
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The significance of noise cancellation: Noise cancellation is very important to many applications, such as teleconferencing, VoIP [1], hearing aids, and automatic speech recognition. Noise in the environment, particularly room noise, is often a significant contributor to poor intelligibility of speech. The present work is focused on combating such interference through the use of adaptive filtering methodologies, like Recursive Least Squares (RLS) and Normalized Least Mean Squares (NLMS), with the aim of enhancing speech intelligibility for the listener. Furthermore, much research has been done on phase-relevant processing for improving the perceptual quality of generated speech. The traditional way of spectrogram-masking and phase reconstruction often ignores the problems with consistency constraint, which is likely causing audible artefacts as well as complicating the reconstruction process. To achieve this goal, we introduce CSM, a new framework for imposing consistency-based constraints to optimize spectrogram estimation at the complex-valued level. Embed the phase estimation into speech enhancement models for better time frequency domain representations, e.g., \ common ratio mask (CRM). Experimental results show that the CSM can effectively improve speech quality and accelerate model convergence. Our approaches might provide adaptation algorithms of speech for denoising/mic array-based speech denoising for noise reduction applications.

title: Hybrid Lexical-Topic Feature Framework for Machine Learning-Based Suicidal Ideation Prediction
authors: Maya Gharat, Manivel Kandasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Suicidal ideation is a significant public health issue, and the early detection of such ideation from text data could potentially allow for early intervention and the saving of lives. However, the automatic detection of suicidal ideation from text data is difficult because of the subtle and varied ways in which people express their distress through text. To tackle this issue, this paper proposes a hybrid lexical-topic feature framework for machine learning-based suicidal ideation prediction, using the TF-IDF n-gram approach and Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic features to model the lexical and topic-level semantic structures of user-generated text. The experiments are performed on the publicly available Suicide Watch dataset, which includes posts that have been annotated for the presence of suicidal ideation. Various machine learning classifiers are trained and tested using standard evaluation metrics. The hybrid feature-based approach outperforms the individual feature-based approaches, emphasizing the significance of modeling the lexical and topic-level semantic structures of usergenerated text for the detection of subtle suicidal ideation cues.

title: SMS Spam Detection Using Hybrid BERT + XGBoost Model
authors: K. Jeevitha, B. Jayasurya, N. Prakash, E. Loganathan, P. Anitha, R. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: AIM: The aim of the study is to develop a SMS spam detection system using a Hybrid BERT + XGBoost model that can predict the spam messages from SMS communication. Materials and Methods: In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to a baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model while Group 2 refers to a proposed and advanced BERT + XGBoost Model algorithm. Each group consists of 200 samples, with the SMS providing more accurate and flexible spam classification. The total dataset is 10,962 samples. The power value is 97 % the significance threshold is 0.05, with a confidence interval of 99 %. Result: The BERT + XGBoost model is a faster prediction method and significantly higher accuracy than the TF-IDF. BERT + XGBoost algorithm attains a validation accuracy range from 98.68 % to 99.12 %, but the baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model reaches an accuracy range from 96.65 % to 97.63 %. Statistical analysis indicates a significant improvement in performance, with the proposed model demonstrating higher accuracy compared to the baseline approach, and a significant improvement in the statistical analysis (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> less than 0.001) in the detection of all sms spam categories. Precision, recall, and F1 scores are noticeably higher withBERT + XGBoost Model. Conclusion: In this work, it is observed that BERT + XGBoost achieves a greater improvement in spam detection compared to the Random Forest model methods. It provides an effective security for spam messages in text classification.

title: EAS-PQC: Analyzing Post Quantum Cryptography Algorithm Selection for IoT Device Classes
authors: Aisha Nasser Ahmed, Norziana Jamil, Nadia Badawi, Berini Aymen Dia Eddine, Yasir Amer Abbas
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The migration of Internet of Things (IoT) systems to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is essential to ensure long-term security against quantum enabled adversaries. However, the large computational, memory, and communication overheads of PQC algorithms pose challenges for resource-constrained IoT environments. In practice, IoT platforms span a wide range of capabilities, from battery-powered sensors to gateway devices, making a one-size-fits-all configuration impractical. This paper presents a new methodology for selecting suitable PQC schemes based on RFC 7228 device classes. We introduce the Efficiency-Adjusted Security for Post-Quantum Cryptography (EAS-PQC) metric that considers security strength, latency, energy consumption, memory footprint, communication overhead, and protocol reliability. Device-class-specific constraints and weighting profiles are incorporated to reflect the operational priorities of Class 0, Class 1, and Class 2 IoT devices. Our evaluation primarily uses performance benchmarks from the PQM4 framework, which are based on ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller profiles, and performance results from several work on similar embedded platforms. Energy consumption is estimated using a constant active power model derived from Cortex-M4 power characteristics. We evaluate multiple PQC candidates, including ML-KEM and ML-DSA, and the results show a quantitative mapping between IoT device classes and PQC schemes. Specifically, Saber-LightSaber is identified as the most suitable option for Class 0 devices, while ML-DSA-87 combined with ML-KEM-768 achieves the highest overall scores for Class 1 and Class 2 devices. Our findings improve feasibility and efficiency by up to 30–40% compared to static baseline configurations. With these findings, we recommend EAS-PQC as a useful tool for the assessment of post-quantum security in industrial IoT systems.

title: Dynamic Trust AI: Uncertainty-Driven Decision Adaptation for Trustworthy Human–AI Collaboration in High-Risk Imbalanced Systems
authors: Nour Ahmad Kaymih, Hajar Dib, Noor Aldeen Alawad
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: In many highly imbalanced domains, such as detecting credit card fraud, setting a threshold on the output of a classifier is a common and critical task. However, the common static threshold and uniform treatment of model predictions generally result in low sensitivity when detecting rare events.Dynamic Trust AI is a lightweight framework that is model agnostic, and it transforms the model’s uncertainty into dynamic, instance-specific decision controls. The framework uses predictive entropy to determine dynamic decision thresholds at runtime.On the European credit card fraud dataset, Dynamic Trust AI achieves a recall of 0.880 and an F1 score of 0.880, outperforming the baseline model (recall 0.720, F1 0.800). Moreover, the model achieves a PR-AUC of 0.849, indicating that it reduces the number of false negatives while maintaining precision.These results show that Dynamic Trust AI can significantly improve the reliability of AI models and the human–AI teaming in safety-critical settings.

title: LLM-Enhanced Braille-to-Speech Learning Assistant for Visually Impaired Users Using AraT5
authors: Alya Alahmed, Al Anood Saleh, Fatna Belqasmi, Manar Alkhatib
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: Braille literacy remains essential for visually impaired individuals to access written information and achieve independence in education and daily communication. However, learning Braille can be challenging due to the lack of immediate feedback when interpreting Braille dot patterns. Traditional assistive technologies often focus only on recognizing Braille characters and converting them into speech without providing contextual explanations that support learning.This paper proposes an intelligent Braille-to-Speech learning assistant that integrates rule-based Braille decoding with the Arabic transformer language model AraT5. The system converts Braille symbols into text, generates contextual explanations using a large language model, and provides auditory feedback through text-to-speech synthesis.Experiments conducted using a Kaggle Braille dataset demonstrate strong performance, achieving 93% Braille decoding accuracy, 0.84 token-level F1 score, ROUGE-1 score of 0.79, ROUGE-L score of 0.76, and BLEU score of 0.71. These results indicate that combining symbolic Braille decoding with large language models significantly improves accessibility and enhances interactive Braille learning for visually impaired users.

title: Neuro-Symbolic AI for Cognitive Interaction in Humanitarian Voice-Based Interfaces
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Manish Gupta, B. Sireesha, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The proliferation of voice-based interfaces (VBIs) offers a transformative opportunity for humanitarian assistance, enabling interaction with vulnerable populations irrespective of literacy or digital literacy levels. However, conventional deep learning-based VBIs often struggle in these high-stakes, low-resource contexts, exhibiting critical limitations in reasoning, contextual understanding, and operational transparency. This paper posits that Neuro-Symbolic AI (NeSyAI), which integrates the statistical pattern recognition of neural networks with the explicit, logical reasoning of symbolic AI, presents a seminal framework for overcoming these challenges. We propose a novel architecture for humanitarian VBIs that leverages neural networks for robust speech recognition and intent classification, while a symbolic reasoning engine manages task decomposition, contextual grounding, and ethical constraint validation. This synthesis facilitates not only improved accuracy in noisy, multilingual environments but also engenders crucial cognitive interactions—such as explainable decision-making, adaptive dialogue management, and trust-building through verifiable reasoning chains. The paper delineates the theoretical underpinnings of this approach, outlines a prototypical system design for a disaster response scenario, and discusses the significant implications for developing ethically aligned, cognitively resonant, and deployable humanitarian technologies.

title: Hardware-Aware Dynamic Range Calibration for Post-Training Quantization on FPGAs
authors: Shuo Liu, Zhiming Li, He Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) enables reduced-precision inference without retraining and is often used to deploy deep neural networks on field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs). At low bit-widths, accuracy can drop sharply due to activation outliers and calibration schemes that depend on floating-point or runtime rescaling, which are inconvenient for integer-only FPGA datapaths. This work introduces Hardware-Aware Dynamic Range Calibration (HADRC), a PTQ calibration scheme designed for FPGA constraints. HADRC adapts to the target bit-width and restricts range redistribution factors to power-of-two (PoT) values, so rescaling can be folded offline and implemented as shifts at inference time. On CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-1K, HADRC improves W4/A8 accuracy over round-to-nearest baselines while remaining comparable at W8/A8. We also implement an end-to-end FPGA prototype to verify efficient, deterministic integer-only execution.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Digital Credentialing: An Interoperable Hybrid Architecture for Academic Integrity and Verification
authors: Swarna Surekha, B Santhosh Kumar, P.K.Dhanalakshmi, R.Usha, N. Dhasarathan, R. Senthamil Selvan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Digital credentialing provided by blockchain technology offers portable proof and constant audit trails; however, utilizing public ledgers raises unique causes questions with cost, solitude, and the speed accompanying that revocations are processed. To attain a balance between immutability, solitude-maintaining discriminating disclosure, active annulment, and uniform affordability, this study assesses a pertinent composite architecture for instructional dependability and proof. A permissioned consortium ledger handles issuer dossier and credential annulment, while a permissionless coating anchors Merkle ancestries. The suggested design uses a nothing-information proof method, an established PLONK-style selective announcement to claim consumers' privacy. An inclusive evaluation of conduct, security, and interoperability is created likely by the whole end-to-end original, which is supported by a dataset of 100,000 synthetic attestations, containing 25,000 adversarial sketches. With a maximum throughput of 420 verifications/second, an average evidence length of 2.4 KB, and a cap of 42 bytes for exposed traits, the system achieves a misleading acceptance rate of 0.12%. The permissioned ledger's revocation transactions take 1.2 seconds, obtain interoperability of 98.7 allotment, and cost an average of 1.94 rupees to confirm. This cost-effective, secure, and solitude-continuing plan enables organizations to conduct lengthy credential proof accompanying little functional burden, internationally recognized honor, and strong privacy protection.

title: Adaptive Lévy Flight-Based Optimization for Fetal ECG Recovery and Arrhythmia Detection
authors: Shivangi Diwan, Mridu Sahu, Varun Bajaj
abstract: Early detection of fetal cardiac abnormality facilitates timely intervention and management. Existing noninvasive fetal electrocardiogram (fECG) extraction techniques often rely on fixed or heuristic optimization parameters and segment-level evaluation strategies, limiting robustness under nonstationary maternal–fetal signal overlap and introducing potential information leakage. This work presents an adaptive optimization framework utilizing the adaptive Lévy flight-based cuckoo search (ALCS) method for improved fECG extraction and detection of fetal arrhythmias, contributing to intelligent human–machine systems in obstetric monitoring. The framework employs ALCS as an outer loop optimizer to determine an optimal initialization of the least mean square (LMS) filter coefficients for adaptive noise cancellation (ANC), where a convergence-aware Lévy flight parameter governs the search process. The Lévy parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lambda$</tex-math></inline-formula> evolves adaptively within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[1.5, 2.0]$</tex-math></inline-formula> enabling an effective transition from exploration to exploitation. The proposed optimizer achieves approximately 1.8–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.0\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> faster convergence than standard LMS, reducing the mean-squared error by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>74% to 0.10 within 50 iterations. A comprehensive feature set exceeding 100 descriptors is extracted from fECG signals and evaluated using ensemble classifiers. Ensuring a leakage-safe evaluation protocol, the experiments were conducted using subjectwise grouped cross-validation with foldwise feature selection on the noninvasive fetal ECG arrhythmia database available from PhysioNet. The proposed framework achieves robust arrhythmia detection, attaining 91.2% accuracy and 95.0% area under the curve across unseen subjects. To quantify systematic bias and agreement across evaluation stages, Bland–Altman analysis is employed to provide clinical interpretability beyond aggregate scores. Overall, the results demonstrate that convergence-aware Lévy-based optimization improves ANC performance for fECG analysis, while acknowledging dataset scale and offline evaluation limitations.

title: BliChain: A Blockchain-Assisted and Lightweight Cross-Domain Authentication Scheme for 6G-Enabled VANET
authors: Jiaxin Li, Hong Zhong, Yun Shen, Lu Wei, Irina Bolodurina, Jie Cui
abstract: In the evolution of 6G-based Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANETs) from closed single-domain environments to open cross-domain environments, several security challenges arise, including heterogeneous domain trust barriers, vehicle identity privacy leakage, and high authentication overhead in dynamic scenarios. Existing schemes based on centralized public key infrastructure (PKI) suffer from single point failure risks and have difficulty balancing low latency with conditional privacy preservation. Meanwhile, blockchain-assisted solutions are often constrained by on-chain storage expansion and high computational overhead. To address these issues, this paper proposes a blockchain-assisted anonymous authentication scheme for 6G-VANETs. The proposed scheme uses blockchain to construct distributed trust anchors and enable secure sharing of public parameters across domains. By generating lightweight authentication parameters based on Lagrange interpolation polynomials, the scheme supports direct mutual authentication and session key agreement between vehicles without requiring massive certificate-related on-chain transactions. In addition, a pseudo-identity mechanism is introduced to achieve conditional privacy preservation, thereby balancing vehicle identity privacy and traceability. Security analysis demonstrates that the proposed scheme satisfies the required security properties under the random oracle model. Performance evaluation shows that compared with existing solutions, the proposed scheme significantly reduces computational overhead by 22.31%, 11.02%, and 29.19%, respectively, and communication overhead by 51.2%, 20.08%, and 24.61%, respectively.

title: Energy-saving Optimization Control of PEMFC systems for Plateau Railway
authors: Shuqi Xie, Qi Li, Liangzhen Yin, Leshan He, Weirong Chen
abstract: The harsh environment and high-power and high dynamic traction load along the plateau railway led to severe energy loss, limited output capacity and even stack halt of proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) systems. To this end, an energy-saving optimization control (ESOC) method is proposed to simultaneously enhance energy recovery and output capacity of PEMFC systems. Based on the close energy interaction between the system and environment, a plateau PEMFC system model is established, and an expander is integrated to achieve the conversion of gas waste heat energy. The contradictory characteristics between the energy recovery capacity and output capacity of the system in the plateau environment are analyzed. Because maximum net power and maximum energy recovery cannot be achieved at the same oxygen excess ratio (OER), a dual-objective optimization method is proposed to determine the optimal OER while strictly adhering to safety constraints. To realize rapid response under complex traction loads, the OER is dynamically adjusted through an active disturbance rejection controller. The comparative experiments on the hardware-in-the loop platform show that the proposed ESOC method can effectively ensure the stable operation of PEMFC systems in plateau areas, and achieve energy recovery and output capacity improvement.

title: Temporal Irregularity-Aware Attention Mechanism for Industry 5.0 Intrusion Detection
authors: Danish Javeed, Muhammad Adil, Prabhat Kumar, Hongbo Liu
abstract: The evolution of Industry 5.0 introduces complex, temporally irregular network behaviors, challenging intrusion detection in heterogeneous industrial environments. Existing Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) often struggle with asynchronous traffic and lack interpretability. To overcome these limitations, we propose AegisNet, a novel sequence-based IDS that reformulates intrusion detection as a sequence classification task on fixed-length windows of T consecutive observations (sequence length), augmented with explicit time-delta features to capture irregular traffic intervals. AegisNet introduces a novel Temporal-Aware and Self-Adaptive Multi-Head Attention (TA-SAMHA) mechanism. It introduces learnable temporal bias projections to directly encode irregular time intervals into the attention process, enabling the system to capture fine-grained temporal dependencies and evolving behaviors. It also employs a Dual-Gate Residual Encoder (DGRE) for bidirectional sequence modeling with dual gating, enabling enriched contextual representations and stable learning across deep temporal layers. Additionally, we introduce the IDS-specific adaptation of Temporal SHAP (T-SHAP), enabling time-resolved feature attributions that expose how influential factors shift during sequential intrusion detection, offering a new dimension of temporally aware explainability for evolving attack patterns. Experimental results demonstrate that AegisNet achieves a detection accuracy of 99.63% on the CICIoV2024 dataset and 99.13% on the CICIoT2023 dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness for securing Industrial environments.

title: Core Values for Ethical AI: A Systematic Review of Frameworks, Contexts, and Applications
authors: Hannah Louise Mulvihill, Taís Fernanda Blauth, Oskar Josef Gstrein, Andrej Zwitter
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems increasingly support decisions with significant ethical and societal implications, necessitating systematic approaches to value-aware design and governance. However, it remains unclear which normative values are most consistently emphasized in AI design research and how these priorities vary across domains and research objectives. This study presents a systematic literature review of 46 peer-reviewed publications identified through six major scholarly databases. The analysis distinguishes theoretical and applied contributions and examines Value Sensitive Design (VSD) alongside related value-protection frameworks to identify recurrent value patterns. Results reveal a convergent core set of values including privacy, fairness/equality, transparency/explicability, accountability/responsibility, and autonomy, that dominate the discourse. In contrast, interpretability, safety, and reliability receive comparatively limited attention, while collective goods and ecological sustainability are rarely addressed. Domain-specific differences are evident: safety-critical and embodied systems prioritize safety and interpretability; data-intensive public sector applications emphasize fairness and privacy; and industrial contexts foreground security, control, and responsibility. Furthermore, framework-oriented studies tend to emphasize procedural values, whereas empirical case studies focus on domain-specific risks such as safety and reliability. Across the corpus, a persistent gap is identified between articulated value commitments and mechanisms for post-deployment verification of value realization. Based on the synthesis of 21 recurrent values, this review derives nine illustrative system-level requirements intended to support the design, evaluation, and governance of value-aware AI systems.

title: Radio-Coverage-Aware Path Planning for Cooperative Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Giuseppe Baruffa, Luca Rugini, Francesco Binucci, Fabrizio Frescura, Paolo Banelli, Renzo Perfetti
abstract: Fleets of autonomous vehicles (AV) often are at the core of intelligent transportation scenarios for smart cities, and may require a wireless Internet connection to offload computer vision tasks to data centers located either in the edge or the cloud section of the network. Cooperation among AVs is successful when the environment is unknown, or changes dynamically, so as to improve coverage and trip time, and minimize the traveled distance. The AVs, while mapping the environment with range-based sensors, move across the wireless coverage areas, with consequences on the experienced access bit rate, latency, and handover rate. In this paper, we propose to modify the cost of common path planning algorithms such as Dijkstra and A*, so that the best path solution takes into account not only the traveled distance, but also the radio coverage experience. To this aim, several radio-related cost-weighting functions are introduced and tested, to assess the performance of the proposed approaches with extensive simulations. The proposed mapping algorithm can achieve a mapping error probability below 2%, while the proposed path-planning algorithms extend the radio coverage of the AVs, with only a limited increase in traveled distance with respect to shortest-path existing methods, such as conventional Dijkstra and A* algorithms.

title: MG-YOLO: A Lightweight Deep Learning Model for Detecting Blueberry Fruits at Different Maturities
authors: Qin Ling, Cui Kai, Wen Zelong, Zhang Wenyuan
abstract: The yield and quality of blueberry cultivation depend heavily on effective fruit harvesting. Accurate and real-time identification of blueberries at various maturity stages is a prerequisite for precise harvesting. Accordingly, a lightweight network named MG-YOLO is developed for blueberry fruit detection in this study, through multiple enhancements on YOLO11-nano. First, the GBC block integrating gated convolutions and Bottleneck convolutions is adopted to improve adaptive feature discrimination ability, especially for densely occluded blueberries. Second, a novel MG-C3k2 module incorporating multi-scale Ghost convolution is proposed to strengthen feature representation while maintaining model efficiency. Finally, IF-CIoU loss, focusing on inner overlapping areas of bounding boxes and the balance between hard and easy samples, is designed to mitigate the class imbalance issue and accelerate bounding box regression, improving localisation precision. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed MG-YOLO attains 85.7% mAP@0.5 and 60.6% mAP@0.5:0.95, representing remarkable improvements of 4.0% and 5.1% over the baseline, respectively. Furthermore, the parameter count is reduced by 12%, and the model exhibits a detection speed of 51 FPS measured on an NVIDIA RTX 3060 GPU, indicating its potential for for real-time automated harvesting applications.

title: HMFM: A Robust Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module to Enhance Map Learning With Additional Information
authors: Wenjie Gao, Entao Chang, Jiawei Fu, Shitao Chen, Nanning Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: High-definition (HD) maps, rich in geometric and semantic information, are essential for autonomous driving systems. Recent online map learning methods, predicting maps directly from onboard sensor data, offer promising alternatives to traditional manual approaches. However, they are limited in long-range detection and complex environments due to onboard sensors’ inherent constraints. While some works incorporate external priors, they often lack generality due to tight coupling with specific architectures and poor robustness to imperfect data. To address these gaps, we propose the Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module (HMFM), a robust, generalizable plug-and-play module that fuses onboard data with external information at two levels. At the feature level, the Masked Attention Module (MAM) selectively enhances features to boost fusion efficiency and minimize interference. At the Bird’s Eye View (BEV) level, the Alignment Module (AM) resolves spatial discrepancies; then, the Unified Attention Module (UAM) adaptively weighs external priors to effectively mitigate inaccuracies from imperfect data. We also introduce a distillation framework to distill benefits from additional information into the model without affecting runtime efficiency. Extensive experiments validate HMFM’s generality, leveraging satellite and SD maps for significant gains across baselines (e.g., +22.0 mIoU on HDMapNet, +12.3 mAP on MapTR). Furthermore, the distillation framework boosts the original HDMapNet by 2.8 mIoU without affecting runtime efficiency. The code and data processing pipeline for this work are publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/xjtu-cs-gao/HMFM</uri>

title: Integrated Planning of Virtual Coupling Train Convoy Formation Schemes and Timetables With Dynamic Coupling&#x2013;Decoupling_supp1-3698131.docx
authors: Dingjun Chen
abstract: Virtual coupling technology provides a novel approach to increasing the transport capacity of freight railway corridors. To enable the dynamic formation and adjustment of train convoys during a trip from origin to destination, this study develops an integrated planning model for virtual coupling train convoy formation schemes and timetables. The model simultaneously incorporates infrastructure characteristics and the coupling–decoupling process, which is described by a “waiting and sequential braking” strategy. The objective is to maximize the residual transport capacity, while considering constraints related to convoy formation, train timetables, convoy adjustment and arrival–departure times, coupling–decoupling within sections, and station capacity. Considering the characteristics of the problem, we design a heuristic optimization framework combining the Advantage Actor–Critic algorithm and the Gurobi solver. Numerical experiments based on the Ningwuxi–Diliudeng segment of the Shuohuang Railway reveal that, compared with Moving Block systems, virtual coupling trains achieve a 9.9% reduction in operating energy consumption with only a 1.5% increase in travel time. In the tested scenarios, the transport capacity can be increased by 25%, provided the coupling–decoupling time penalty remains within a reasonable range. In addition, the maximum convoy length, track availability, and section length are found to significantly affect the efficiency of virtual coupling trains.

title: Machine Learning and AI Applied to fNIRS Data Reveals Novel Brain Activity Biomarkers in Stable Subclinical Multiple Sclerosis_supp1-3700987.pdf
authors: Xianta Jiang

title: Hyperparameter-Invariant Deep Spiking Neural Networks: A Triple Synergy Approach
authors: Donglin Xiong, Jianwei Zhao, Jintao Li, Yun Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) promise energyefficient neuromorphic computing but suffer from extreme sensitivity to hyperparameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tau, \theta)$</tex>, necessitating costly trial-and-error tuning. To address this issue, we integrate reset-free dynamics (Accumulator-LIF) with Layer Normalization and SelfAttention, yielding hyperparameter invariance: across extensive search of neuronal parameters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau, \theta$</tex>), the accuracy variance is strictly zero (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma=0.000 \%$</tex>). Under this tuning-free paradigm, we develop an SNN architecture reliably to achieve 77.20 % on CIFAR-100, and a stable average accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.73 \% (\pm 0.16 \%$</tex> across random seeds) on CIFAR-10, peaking at 94.03 %. We formalize this via the Triple Synergy Mechanism: (1) Reset-free dynamics linearize gradients, enabling scale-invariant normalization; (2) Softmax's relative encoding decouples sparsity from accuracy; (3) Adaptive attention compensates residual variations. Extensive validation shows 6.85 % robustness advantage under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> noise, stable feature representations (Procrustes distance: 4.49 vs 23.10), and flat loss landscapes (std: 0.021 vs 0.098). This work eliminates hyperparameter tuning, clearing a major hurdle for plug-and-play SNN deployment on edge devices and embodied intelligent systems.

title: Co-Designing Quantum Codes with Transversal Diagonal Gates via Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Xi He, Sirui Lu, Bei Zeng
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: We present a multi-agent, human-in-the-loop workflow that co-designs quantum error-correcting codes with pre-scribed transversal diagonal gates. It builds on the Subset-Sum Linear Programming (SSLP) framework, which partitions basis strings by modular residues and enforces <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex>-marginal Knill-Laflamme (KL) equalities via small LPs. The workflow is powered by GPT-5 and implemented within TeXRA, a multi-agent research assistant platform where agents collaborate in a shared LATEX-Python workspace synchronized with Git/Overleaf. Three specialized agents formulate constraints, sweep and screen candidate codes, exactify numerical solutions into rationals, and independently audit all KL equalities and induced logical actions. Focusing on distance-two codes with nondegenerate residues, we catalogue new nonadditive codes for dimensions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K \in\{2,3,4\}$</tex> on up to six qubits, including high-order diagonal transversals, yielding 14, 116 new codes. From these data, the system abstracts closed-form families and constructs a residue-degenerate ((6, 4, 2)) code implementing a transversal controlled-phase <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{diag}(1,1,1, i)$</tex>, illustrating how AI orchestration can drive rigorous, scalable code discovery.

title: PosePart-MLP: 3D Human Pose Estimation via Hierarchical MLP and Cross-Part Attention
authors: Junna Gao, Guohui Wang, Hongxue Yang, Lei Ma, Jianxing Yang, Chuanhao Ma, Dake Guo, Siyuan He
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Estimating 3D human pose from 2D joint coordinates is an important task in computer vision, but traditional methods often produce significant errors on distant body joints due to the articulated structure of the human skeleton and its high degree of freedom. Existing MLP-based models typically process joint features independently, failing to unify the modeling of local joint dependencies and global pose structure. To address these issues, this paper proposes PosePart-MLP, a novel end-toend network for 3D human pose estimation from 2D inputs. Our method first introduce a multi-level feature extraction module to fuse global spatial context and local joint features. We then divide human skeleton into a hierarchical structure based on body topology, and employ a hierarchical MLP pose block (HMPB) module to model intra-region dependencies and inter-region feature interactions. Finally, we use a prediction head to generate the final 3D pose. Experimental results on the Human3.6M dataset demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches, and exhibits significantly enhanced robustness on distant body joints.

title: TAC-DHER: Tactical-Consistency-Aware Confidence-Gated Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay for Cooperative UAV Air Combat
authors: Wenxin Li, Yongxin Feng
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Dynamic-target cooperative unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) air combat poses substantial challenges to reinforcement learning due to continuously evolving tactical objectives, sparse rewards, and poor reuse of failed episodes. To tackle these issues, a tactical-consistency-aware confidence-gated dynamic hindsight experience replay method, termed TAC-DHER, is proposed. TAC-DHER replaces purely geometric goals with joint tactical goals that jointly encode mission mode, cooperative role, engagement geometry, resource status, and safety constraints. During failed-trajectory reconstruction, tactical soft matching is introduced to replace the rigid goal correspondence used in the original Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay (DHER), thereby expanding the set of high-value failed segments that can be relabeled. During replay, confidence gating and adaptive scheduling are further incorporated to ensure reliable sample injection and stable training. Experiments in a 2 -vs- 2 heterogeneous cooperative UAV air combat environment show that TAC-DHER consistently outperforms the original DHER across multiple dynamic combat scenarios, including standard dynamic-target settings, constrained training configurations, riskconstrained cooperation, and extremely sparse-reward conditions. Ablation studies confirm the complementary contributions of tactical soft matching, confidence gating, and adaptive replay scheduling, while trajectory cases further demonstrate that TACDHER induces more stable cooperative suppression and targetelimination behaviors.

title: Unsteady Motion Parameter Extraction for Shipborne High-Frequency Surface Wave Radar Using Distributed Reference Sources
authors: Yuze Wang, Yuhan Xia, Ruizhe Zhou, Xianzhou Yi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Shipborne high-frequency surface wave radar (HFSWR) operating under unsteady motion conditions faces critical challenges in motion parameter extraction, which is a prerequisite for reliable ocean surface current inversion. The integrated navigation system suffers from significant translational parameter degradation during non-stationary platform motion, necessitating alternative approaches. This paper proposes a cascaded motion parameter extraction framework based on distributed reference sources for shipborne HFSWR under unsteady motion. The proposed architecture decouples rotation and translation parameter extraction into two independent stages. First, rotational parameters (yaw, pitch, and roll) are estimated via multi-source maximum-likelihood search using the measured steering vectors of reference signal sources relative to the shipborne receiving array. Second, translational parameters (east and north displacements) are extracted through a differential reference source architecture that eliminates the clock synchronization error between shore-based transmitters and the shipborne radar. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves yaw root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.2°, pitch RMSE of 0.8°, and roll RMSE of 1.7° for attitude estimation, and east/north RMSE of 1.7 m and 2.5 m for translation estimation, with a trajectory recovery ratio of 95.3%.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Voltage Control in Active Distribution Networks
authors: YongZe Ma, Hong Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The secure and economic operation of Active Distribution Networks (ADNs) is increasingly challenged by the high penetration of renewable energy and electric vehicles, which introduces significant source-load uncertainties leading to voltage violations and increased network losses. This paper proposes an intelligent voltage dispatching strategy based on a Sequential Deep Double Q-Network (SDDQN). The dispatching problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) within the Gym-ANM framework. The SDDQN algorithm decomposes the high-dimensional, continuous action space into an ordered sequence of one-dimensional sub-decisions, enabling the problem to be solved using only a value function and circumventing the convergence difficulties of Actor-Critic architectures. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate that the proposed SDDQN achieves a more stable training process with faster convergence compared to both DDPG and TD3 algorithms. The strategy ensures all nodal voltages remain within permissible limits while maintaining lower network losses throughout the dispatching period.

title: VaST-Conv: Variable-Strided Tiled Convolution Framework for Resource-Efficient CNN Acceleration
authors: Bindu G Gowda, Pradyumna G, Madhav Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Convolution is the backbone of modern computer vision, yet traditional CNNs incur high computational cost, limiting deployment on constrained hardware. This work introduces the VaST-Conv strategy, where the input feature map is tiled, and each tile is convolved with potentially different stride values to balance resolution and computation. Non-uniform striding improves flexibility and reduces MAC operations, with outputs aligned through interpolation for seamless processing. The approach is evaluated on emotion classification and YOLO-based object detection, along with FP16 MAC hardware cost analysis. Results show that variable-strided tiled convolution provides a scalable and efficient alternative to conventional CNN processing.

title: Research on Early Fault Detection Method of 10kV Cable Based on Adaptive Fusion of Multimodal Features
authors: Zikang Li, Haoran Liu, Xiaomin Lu, Jifeng Zhang, Ziyi Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: One-sided acquisition of cable operation information leads to low accuracy in early fault detection of 10kV cables. To address this issue, a research method for early fault detection of 10kV cables based on adaptive fusion of multimodal features is proposed. A high-frequency current sensor is used to acquire signals, which are then processed by wavelet threshold denoising, standardization, and sliding window segmentation of the 10kV cable operation signal. Based on the local signal segments processed by the sliding window, multimodal features in the time domain, frequency domain, and time-energy domain are extracted to reflect the cable operation status. The multimodal features are fused through adaptive weight calculation and input into the LOF model to achieve early fault detection of 10kV cables. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves a high fault detection accuracy of 98.32%. Compared with the comparative method, it significantly improves the detection sensitivity under various cable load rates, with an average improvement of over 10%, and significantly reduces the number of misclassified samples, thus improving the accuracy of early fault detection of 10kV cables.

title: Optimization of Low-Cost Flex Sensor Performance Using Machine Learning
authors: Carla Zuccarini, Bhargavi Rakesh Savani, William Luu, Ava Hedayatipour
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Flex sensors are pivotal in wearable technology, robotics, and gesture recognition due to their affordability and flexibility. However, their performance is often hindered by non-linear behavior, environmental sensitivity, and variability. This paper explores the enhancement of low-cost flex sensor performance through machine learning regression models. A system was designed using an Arduino-based setup for automated data collection, integrating a servo motor for precise angle control and a voltage divider for resistance measurement. Several regression models, including Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), a Stacked Ensemble, and an uncertainty-aware fusion model, were evaluated based on Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> scores. Results showed that GPR achieved the highest prediction accuracy with an MAE of 0.82° and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.9981, outperforming traditional methods. These findings demonstrate that applying advanced machine learning techniques significantly enhances the predictive reliability of flex sensors, making them more viable for precision applications in wearable and embedded systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Handcrafted Feature - Based Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Automated Mango Grading
authors: E. Loganathan, M. Bhavatharani, C.A. Kandasamy, A. Yoganathan, P. Kalyanasundaram, S. Nivetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Mango grading is an essential process for quality assessment and prevention of post-harvest losses. However, manual grading is the one of the most time-consuming processes. This proposed system uses a CNN-based deep learning model to extract deep feature and combines with the handcrafted features such as color, size, texture, and surface defect attributes using ensemble machine learning. The experiment consists of two groups. Group1 uses a traditional Random Forest model and Group 2 uses a Hybrid Ensemble model. The Experiments were performed on mango images originally taken from a Kaggle dataset. The mango grading image samples are 1200. The result Indicate that traditional Random Forest model achieved an accuracy of 88.64% while the proposed hybrid ensemble models achieve a higher accuracy of 94% and also hybrid models produce a better precision of 97.0%–97.9% and overall recall value for all grades of mangoes. Additionally, proposed hybrid models produce a low overall error or RMSE of 0.87 compared to Random Forest models and have less variability or error. Statistical analysis of the proposed hybrid model shows the overall accuracy significant value of P < 0.001 compared to traditional Random Forest models. This proposed hybrid model can give a reliable and efficient automated grading process in mangoes that helps in real-word applications.

title: A Scalable Agentic Artificial Intelligence Architecture for Autonomous Decision-Making and Self-Optimization in Complex Cyberphysical Systems
authors: Satpalsing Devising Rajput, Kiran Veernapu, Minakshi Sati, Suresh Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Modern Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) must be autonomously adaptable to deal with growing complexity and uncertainty in the environment. In this paper, the author proposes a scalable agentic AI architecture that will allow CPS to sense, think, and act in autonomy. The framework combined both local edge intelligence and global coordination to solve this by balancing the distributed agents with the hierarchical reinforcement learning. By contrast with the traditional centralized models, our architecture employs the use of selfmonitoring and feedback loops in the optimization of performance in real time and without human intervention. Assessment of various CPS conditions reveals major gains in decision quality, latency, and resiliency, providing a solid base to the future of the industrial automation and intelligent infrastructure.

title: Smart Control System with NodeMCU-Controlled Relays and MobileNet-SSD for Person Detection
authors: Sunil Kumar G., Surya Kavitha T., Vamsidhar A., Sankara Rao P., Sridhar G. V., Ravi Tejesvi V.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The inception of intelligent context-aware systems has been made possible over the past decade by the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with Internet of Things (IoT) devices. This study describes a real-time, vision-based human detection system to autonomously operate numerous electrical appliances utilizing AI and IoT hardware. The proposed system uses a laptop webcam to continuously record video data, which is then analyzed by OpenCV in Python using a pretrained Mobile-SSD deep learning model to identify human presence. Upon recognizing a person, the control commands were delivered via serial-communication to an ESP8266-based NodeMCU microcontroller integrated on a Kiwi-IoT V1 development board. This board connects with a 4-channel relay module that operates a 12V DC fan, serial lamp and a submersible pump. A 3-cell LiPo battery powers the system, and an L298 motor driver controls the power distribution. For smart environments such as homes, farms and industrial areas, this proposed integrated architecture provides a low-cost, portable solution by enabling smooth interaction between AI-based detection and physical control of electrical equipment. The experimental results demonstrate high reliability and responsiveness in real-time scenarios, making the system a promising model for intelligent automation using edge AI and Internet of Things convergence.

title: Propagation Characteristics and Parameters Optimization of Laser Ultrasonic Waves in C130 Post Insulators
authors: Bichuan Xu, Zhenhua Xu, Tao Lai, Yanglin Li, Fangji Liu, Hui Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Laser ultrasonic non-destructive testing (NDT) technology has the unique advantages of ultrasonic testing method and optical method, which improves the temporal and spatial resolution while realizing non-contact and non-destructive testing. In order to improve the excitation efficiency and defect detection accuracy of laser source ultrasonic non-destructive excitation, it is necessary to optimize the parameters of laser source to improve the energy conversion efficiency and reduce the testing cost. This paper mainly researched the propagation characteristics of laser ultrasonic waves under different conditions and simulated the process of laser ultrasonic action on C130 high-strength aluminum post insulators by using the finite element analysis software COMSOL Multiphysics 6.3. The parametric relationship between width of laser pulse 10-40ns, radius of beam 0.3-0.6mm, and resultant ultrasonic waves characteristics was quantitatively established. Simulation results revealed that a pulse width of 10ns combined with a beam radius of 0.3mm achieved optimal ultrasonic excitation efficiency. This result provided an important basis for the analysis of the internal ultrasonic waves propagation of C130 high-strength aluminum ceramic post insulators in the future.

title: Bidirectional Covertness and Security Enhancement in FD-ISAC Systems
authors: Jiajie Jian, Min Liu, Shu Fu, Yongming Huang
abstract: To mitigate diverse security threats in 6G full-duplex integrated sensing and communication (FD-ISAC) systems, this paper presents a bidirectional security enhancement framework. This framework simultaneously supports heterogeneous services involving uplink (UL) public and covert access, downlink (DL) secure transmission, and continuous radar sensing within a unified spectrum. Specifically, we exploit the intrinsic high-power radar and public user signals to mask UL covert transmissions while strategically embedding artificial noise into DL radar beams to degrade eavesdropping channels. We establish a joint beamforming and power optimization problem to maximize the aggregate covert and secrecy spectral efficiency. To address channel state information (CSI) uncertainty in the surveillance link, a worst-case constraint based on the S-procedure is derived to strictly guarantee covertness. The resulting non-convex problem is efficiently solved via successive convex approximation (SCA) and rank relaxation. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effective covert communication capability and robust security performance achieved by our proposed scheme.

title: Multi-Scale Symmetric Difference Attention-Based Transformer for Panchromatic and Multispectral Classification
authors: Yuantian Zhu, Hao Zhu, Zeyu Lei, Xiaotong Li, Huihui Dong, Biao Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Nowadays, effectively exploiting multispectral (MS) and panchromatic (PAN) imagery for their joint classification task has attracted wide attention. However, due to their heterogeneous resolutions and distinct modality characteristics, the precise alignment of different-scale semantic features and the compact fusion of modality-specific information remain challenging. To tackle these issues, we propose a multi-scale symmetric difference attention-based Transformer (MSAFormer). It comprises two key modules: texture-aware multi-scale feature generation (TMFG) module and symmetric difference feature divergence interaction (SDFD) module. By exploiting PAN’s high-resolution texture to bridge the inherent texture deficiency of MS at continuous resolutions, TMFG builds a multi-scale space with precise texture alignment. This module constructs a robust foundation for the comprehensive extraction of dual-source specific features during interaction. Subsequently, by applying the concept of symmetric difference between sets to the aligned dual-modality groups of different scales, SDFD generates compact and comprehensive multi-scale fusion representations in a non-decoupled manner. These lightweight fusion features integrate the enhanced modality-specific features and eliminate the overlapped redundancy of shared information, ultimately yielding superior classification performance. Several experimental results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the network. Codes are available at https://github.com/Xidian-AIGroup190726/MSAformer.

title: BMS-MFZS: A Background Modeling and Suppression Method based on Multi-Feature Generalized Zero-Shot in Remote Sensing
authors: Na Li, Shuoyu Zhang, Huijie Zhao, Wen Ou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Reliable background suppression remains a key challenge in infrared imaging for space and aerial scientific visual learning, as image data is often affected by multiple complex and intertwined factors such as environmental variability, radiometric inconsistencies, and sensor noise, which make it difficult for models to effectively learn background characteristics for robust suppression. To address this, we propose a Background Modeling and Suppression method based on Multi-Feature Generalized Zero-Shot Learning (BMS-MFZS), which innovatively introduces Generalized Zero-Shot Learning(GZSL) into background modeling. By leveraging both target features and seen background features, the model reversely infers background representations to suppress the impact of complex backgrounds on recognition. Moreover, this work is the first to incorporate the physical characteristics of both targets and backgrounds as auxiliary features within the GZSL framework, thereby enhancing feature effectiveness and improving the model’s target–background discrimination capability. Finally, a joint semantic–pixel background reconstruction strategy is introduced to enable effective suppression of complex infrared backgrounds by combining global structural modeling with fine-detail refinement. The effectiveness of the proposed BMS-MFZS model is validated through extensive experiments on both real, semi-physical simulation and simulated datasets, demonstrating strong adaptability and suppression performance across multiple complex scenarios.

title: F-POI: A Lightweight, Noise-Resilient Parallel Side-Channel Attack on Kyber
authors: Peng Chen, Laifan Pei, Shaoqing Guo, Chi Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Plaintext-checking (PC) oracle-based side-channel attacks represent a well-established category of methods, recognized for their broad applicability and operational efficiency. At TCHES 2023, Rajendran et al. and Tanaka et al. independently introduced the concept of a parallel PC oracle, which significantly lowers the number of traces required for successful key recovery. However, Rajendran et al.’s approach, though lightweight, is highly sensitive to noise, while the method proposed by Tanaka et al. depends on a resource-intensive TensorFlow framework. In this work, we present an F-score based points of interest (F-POI) selection method and a plug-in P-parallel distinguisher architecture. This architecture, which combines a feature extractor and a classifier, resolves the trade-off between noise robustness and computational overhead. The feature extractor replaces recursive T-tests with a single 2P -class F-test, eliminating cumulative errors. The classifier provides flexible backend options, including Euclidean distance and various machine learning models, to optimize the balance among speed, accuracy, and deployment overhead. Experiments on an STM32F407G running pqm4-Kyber-512 show that our approach enables full key recovery with an unprecedentedly low error rate. Our Top-accuracy variant (using scikit-learn, ≈300 MB) achieves this with only 576 traces, resulting in just 0.08 incorrect coefficients (out of 512). Our Lightweight variant (Numpy-only, ≈50 MB) also significantly outperforms prior recursive methods across all tested parallelism levels. Crucially, this performance is achieved without the TensorFlow stack (≈2.1 GB), offering a practical and scalable alternative for security evaluation.

title: Generalized Signal Design for RF and Optical SWIPT in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks
authors: Shuaishuai Guo, Kaiqian Qu, Chenhao Qi, Anbang Zhang, Chenyuan Feng, Geyong Min
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) has emerged as a cornerstone technology for sustainable connectivity in sixth-generation (6G) space–air–ground integrated networks (SAGIN). This paper proposes a unified signal design framework that jointly supports radio-frequency (RF) coherent reception and noncoherent energy detection with colocated or separated deployments of information/energy receivers (IR/ER), thereby bridging RF and intensity-modulation/direct-detection (IM/DD) optical links. Guided by reliability, spectral efficiency, and harvested power metrics, we develop a generalized signal design framework based on a refined sphere-packing aimed at constructing high-dimensional symbols across space-time-frequency resources subject to symbol-level wireless power transfer constraints and an average power budget. The resulting nonconvex quadratically constrained quadratic program (QCQP) is efficiently solved using an alternating semidefinite programming–linear programming (ASDP–LP) method and an augmented Lagrangian dual-ascent (ALDA) algorithm. Analytical and simulation results confirm that the proposed approach achieves significant gains in communication-power trade-offs compared with conventional schemes, providing a foundation for sustainable RF–optical SWIPT in future 6G integrated networks.

title: A Neuromorphic Image Sensor with In-Sensor Spiking Neural Network Computing
authors: Jung-Hye Hwang, Jong-Beom Kim, Jubin Kang, Kieop Hong, Heein Yoon, Seong-Jin Kim
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper presents a neuromorphic image sensor (NIS) that integrates in-sensor spiking neural network (SNN) computation combined with a single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) array. The proposed NIS directly performs spike-domain convolution on integrate-and-fire (IF) neurons integrated into the sensor, eliminating the need for conventional multi-bit analog-to-digital conversion (ADC). A two-step IF computing scheme hierarchically partitions the processing into pixel-level temporal integration and column-level spatial accumulation, enabling efficient spike-based feature extraction with minimal data movement. The pixel circuit employs analog-counter-based convolution and a switched-capacitor IF neuron, achieving low power consumption and a compact implementation while maintaining a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Fabricated in a 110-nm BSI process, the prototype sensor with a 64×64 SPAD pixel array conducts first-layer SNN processing in an ADC-less hardware platform. The proposed NIS achieves a frame rate of 1,525 fps with five time steps and six convolution filters, achieving 95.9% classification accuracy on the MNIST dataset. The measured power consumption is 1.43 mW, corresponding to a figure of merit (FoM) of 7.63 pJ/pix·frame·filter and computing efficiency of up to 1.27 TOPS/W. These results verify the feasibility of energy-efficient, high-speed, and ADC-free spike-domain vision processing for future edge and event-driven AI applications.

title: A Unified Multi-Dynamics Framework for Perception-Oriented Modeling in Tendon-Driven Continuum Robots
authors: Ibrahim Alsarraj, Yuhao Wang, Abdalla Swikir, Cesare Stefanini, Dezhen Song, Zhanchi Wang, Ke Wu
abstract: Tendon-driven continuum robots offer intrinsically safe and contact-rich interactions owing to their kinematic redundancy and structural compliance. However, their perception often depends on external sensors, which increase hardware complexity and limit scalability. This work introduces a unified deterministic multi-dynamics modeling framework for tendon-driven continuum robotic systems, exemplified by a spiral-inspired robot named Spirob. The framework integrates motor electrical dynamics, motor–winch dynamics, and continuum robot dynamics into a coherent system model. Within this framework, motor signals such as current and angular displacement are modeled to expose the electromechanical signatures of external interactions, enabling perception grounded in intrinsic dynamics. The model captures and validates key physical behaviors of the real system, including actuation hysteresis and self-contact at motion limits. Building on this foundation, the framework is applied to environmental interaction: first for passive contact detection, verified experimentally against simulation data; then for active contact sensing, where control and perception strategies from simulation are successfully applied to the real robot; and finally for object size estimation, where a policy learned in simulation is directly deployed on hardware. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides a physically grounded way to interpret interaction signatures from intrinsic motor signals in tendon-driven continuum robots.
