abstract: In our work, we propose an Sliding Mode Control combined with Linear Matrix Inequality (SMC-LMI) control strategy designed for the vehicle active suspension system to enhance the ride comfort of conductors. Our developed method combines LMI-based state-feedback design with sliding mode control to ensure closed-loop stability and enhanced robustness against road disturbances and modeling uncertainties. Simulation results obtained under sinusoidal road excitation demonstrate a significant reduction in vibrations propagated to the vehicle body compared to the passive suspension case, while ensuring bounded control effort. Our obtained results highlight the effectiveness, robustness and practical feasibility of the proposed approach for active suspension systems.

title: Detecting and Mitigating Bias in Resume using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: A. Suhana Nafais, G.J. Sripriya, M. Subitson
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Biases in resume screening processes may compromise both hiring fairness and the diversity of employees hired if evaluations of candidates rely on subjective assessments or keyword matching only. The proposed resume screening method is based on NLP and machine learning to rank candidates according to the degree of semantic relevance between their resume and a job description, and at the same time to detect any instances of potential bias in resumes and job postings. Resumes are first extracted from PDF or DOCX files and then preprocessed using a series of operations followed by generation of embeddings using the Sentence-BERT method. Cosine similarity between resumes and job postings is used for matching jobs and resumes, while at the same time a list of discriminatory phrases together with an anonymizer help detect bias and remove names, gender-specific and age-related language from resumes. The suggested solution proved to be 90% accurate in resume-job matching and achieved 85% bias recall.

title: An Interval-Valued Fuzzy Inference System for Disease Diagnosis
authors: Adnan Shaout, Sai Arunanshu Govindarajula, Neha Basugade
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Clinical data that is noisy, partial, and overlapping is frequently used by medical diagnostic systems. A lot of traditional diagnostic models produce clear results. A false sense of security may result from these results. Classical fuzzy inference systems also have this drawback. This paper will demonstrate that interval valued fuzzy inference system provide a more practical and informative approach of medical diagnosis than the conventional fuzzy or non-fuzzy methods. To explicitly model uncertainty in disease diagnosis, this paper presents an Interval-Valued Fuzzy Inference System (IVFIS). The Pima Indians Diabetes Dataset is used to evaluate the system. As baseline models, traditional Mamdani and Sugeno fuzzy inference systems are used. Mesiar's interval-valued fuzzy set methodology is then used to build an interval-valued extension. The propose IVFIS enhances robustness in borderline situations, as simulation data show. Additionally, it improves interpretability. Instead of using single numerical values to describe illness risk, the system uses intervals to produce more meaningful diagnostic findings.

title: Perforations as a Multifunctional Mechanism for Enhancing Piezoelectric Cantilever Energy Harvester Performance: A Critical Review
authors: Aymen Ezzahri, Abdelmajid Bybi, Ouadia Mouhat, Lahoucine Elmaimouni, Noureddine Bouhaddi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This review explores the strategy of incorporating perforations into piezoelectric cantilever beams to enhance their energy harvesting performance. While traditional and geometrically modified cantilevers often improve one characteristic at the expense of another, this review synthesizes evidence showing that substrate perforations simultaneously address four major limitations: they lower the resonant frequency, improve strain distribution, increase power output, and broaden the bandwidth. The paper reviews the fundamental principles of piezoelectric cantilevers, outlines the limitations of conventional designs, and provides a detailed analysis of how perforations alter the mechanical behavior of the beam. Findings indicate that perforation remains highly promising yet underexplored. It identifies a critical gap in the literature, as no previous review has covered perforated piezoelectric cantilevers, and concludes by emphasizing the need for experimental validation and the exploration of more complex perforation geometries.

title: Intelligent Multi-Source Data Fusion for AI-based Precision Crop Recommendation using Soil, Environmental, and Market Intelligence
authors: S.Vinoth Kumar, E. Rama Seshu, G. Guru Gopi Pavan Harsha Vardhan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The classical crop recommendation methods mainly use soil and climatic factors without taking into account important economic factors like demand in the market and fluctuation of prices. This constraint commonly leads to agronomical but not economical recommendations. To fill this gap, this research proposes a market-conscious crop recommendation model, that is, incorporating soil nutrients, environmental factors, and past market patterns into a single machine learning model. The formulation of the problem is a supervised multi-class classification problem based on a random forest classifier. The model is trained using a multi-source dataset with soil, climatic and market characteristics. Experimental findings indicate that the use of market intelligence can greatly enhance the performance of prediction with an overall accuracy of 95.2%. The analysis of the feature importance shows the role of both environmental and economic characteristics in making a decision. The suggested framework offers a viable, and cost-effective decision-support system in precision agriculture, which allows farmers to make effective decisions in crop planning in dynamic environmental and market conditions.

title: Skin Lesion Segmentation and Cancer Classification using U -Net and Vision Transformer Models
authors: Shantini K, Shalini V, E Ahila Devi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Skin cancer is one of the most common and dangerous dermatological conditions in the world, where early and correct diagnosis is very important for increasing the survival rate among patients. Manual analysis of dermoscopic images is time-consuming and prone to human error, thus sparking interest in the use of automated diagnosis techniques. In this paper, a two-stage deep learning model is proposed for the segmentation of lesions and probabilistic diagnosis of skin cancer from dermoscopic images using the U-Net and Vision Transformer model. The proposed work utilizes the U-Net model for precise lesion segmentation by eliminating background noises along with retaining necessary structural details. The resultant lesion images are then further processed with Vision Transformer for calculating the probability score for malignancy instead of obtaining a crisp diagnosis result. The proposed model is evaluated using publicly available ISIC datasets, and it is shown that lesion segmentation improves the result for risk prediction and that the proposed work is superior to results attained using convolutional neural networks.

title: In Silico Study of Resonant Magnetoelectric Micro-Transducers for Implantable Magnetic Sensing
authors: Giulia Caiani, Valentina Galletta, Marta Bonato, Emma Chiaramello, Serena Fiocchi
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The detection of weak biomagnetic signals remains underexplored in clinical practice due to the limitations of current bulky and expensive measurements system. Recently, magnetoelectric (ME) materials have emerged as compelling alternatives for highly sensitive, room-temperature sensing. This work presents a computational investigation of resonant ME micro-transducers designed for fully wireless operation via the delta-E effect. We analyse and compare the performance of core-shell microparticles and thin-film disks composed of Cobalt Ferrite and Barium Titanate, specifically optimized for MHz and kHz frequency regimes to minimize electromagnetic attenuation in biological tissues. Finite element simulations reveal that thin film disk can achieve maximum sensitivities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 5 ~ H z} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{n T}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 1. 5 ~ m V} \boldsymbol{/}$</tex> Oe, for the first radial mode, at low bias field <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<60 \text{Oe})$</tex>, while the core-shell configuration a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$214 \text{mV} / \text{Oe}$</tex> at 350 Oe. Collectively, these results pave the way for the development of minimally invasive, wireless microsensors that overcome the limitations of current wired technologies in clinical diagnostics.

title: Overcoming Data Scarcity in EV Battery Disassembly with Augmented Deep Learning and Structural Reasoning
authors: Marina Baucks, Haoran Sun, Florian Koessler, Juergen Fleischer
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The steadily increasing demand for electric vehicles (EVs) has led to a heightened need for efficient, automated battery disassembly systems for sustainable recycling. However, the variability of battery designs and the insufficient availability of training data pose significant challenges for machine learning in the context of disassembly automation. In this study, we propose an image-based approach that integrates deep learning and structured reasoning to enable the autonomous generation of disassembly sequences for EV battery packs. A YOLOv8-based pipeline for object detection and instance segmentation is trained using two different approaches to data augmentation: conventional image transformations are compared with synthetic image generation using Segment Anything Models (SAM). Object-specific augmentation using SAM leads to higher precision in object recognition than general, conventional augmentation techniques. Structural relationships between components are then derived using both bounding box heuristics and pixel-level segmentation masks. This enables reliable extraction of spatial and connection data. The information extracted from the image data can then be further processed to derive well-founded and adaptable disassembly sequences. This process is possible without the use of CAD models or extensive prior knowledge. The experimental results showed improved recognition accuracy by using SAM-based data augmentation compared to data augmentation with conventional image transformation (mean Average Precision at an Intersection-over- Union threshold of 0.5 (mAP@0.5) increase: 2.0 %) and compared to not using augmentation techniques (mAP@0.5 increase: 3.3 %) on a small-sized dataset containing 190 images (original size, + 50 images for SAM-based augmentation; + 160 images for conventional augmentation).

title: Lightweight Deep Learning for Concealed Person Detection and Tracking in Airplane Cargo Holds
authors: Bachir Khalfi, Rim Gasmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: One of the major security challenges, particularly in airports with high passenger and cargo traffic, is the concealment of humans inside aircraft cargo holds as a means of illegal immigration. Traditional preventive methods, such as manual inspections of cargo areas and the use of canine units to detect human presence, are often insufficient or difficult to apply effectively. To address this issue, the present work proposes an artificial intelligence–based approach that employs lightweight YOLO models—such as YOLOv8n, YOLOv9t, YOLOv10n, YOLOv11n, and YOLOv12n—as well as an SSD-based lightweight detector (SSDLite320 with a MobileNetV3 backbone),to detect individuals attempting to hide in cargo holds under varying lighting conditions, viewing angles, and positions. The selected model was further integrated with a tracking algorithm and virtual counting lines to monitor entry and exit events, after being optimized to balance detection accuracy with computational efficiency. Experimental results show that YOLOv9t provides the best trade-off between mean average precision and inference speed, making it well suited for real-time deployment. The proposed solution demonstrates strong potential to improve airport security protocols and reduce the risks associated with illegal immigration.

title: Auditory Landscape Identification of Endangered Bird Species using Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: Ramachandran Vijayarani, G. Karthika, S. Manjineeshwari, I. Sandhya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The swift extinction of threatened bird species driven by habitat destruction and climate change calls for effective and automated surveillance. In this work we present a hybrid deep learning model for bird species identification based on acoustic signals. The proposed approach is Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) with Deep Neural Network (DNN) in which temporal and spectral characteristics of bird calls can be well modeled. Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) and spectrogram-based features are derived to represent the audio signals. The model is learned and tested on a heterogeneous dataset that contains 2162 audio recordings from 219 species. Experimental results show that the proposed hybrid model obtains a classification accuracy of 95.53% which is higher than both single BiLSTM and DNN model. It also demonstrates improved recall and F1-score which makes it effective for detecting rare and endangered species. This work paves the way to scalable and real-time biodiversity monitoring systems, providing a valuable tool to assist in conservation and ecological studies.

title: A Heterogeneous Ensemble for Robust Deepfake Detection: Synergistic Integration of Convolutional and Transformer Architectures
authors: Aswin Bala R, Infant Sam Anton J, S Shanthini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Modern approaches to detecting deepfake videos mostly use traditional architectures that utilize a single deep- learning paradigm, thus restricting their ability to detect the vast spectrum of artifacts produced by advanced generative methodologies. In order to mitigate these natural deficiencies, we propose a heterogeneous ensemble architecture that integrates three complementary deep-learning streams, permitting a new analytical perspective for identifying synthetic forgery cues. The proposed solution overcomes the shortcomings of conventional forensic techniques, which rely on fixed signature repositories, by dynamically combining the forgery properties extracted by each paradigm. Experimental evaluations indicate that our multi- stream ensemble outperforms strong single-method baselines, achieving a total accuracy of 94.0% and a false-positive rate of just 1.3%. This confirms the effectiveness of combining local, global, and structural representations to create a highly resilient synthetic media detector.

title: Intelligent Network Intrusion Detection for IoT Devices using Semi-Supervised Learning and Random Forest
authors: K.Chandrakumari, R.K.Kavya, PC.Godlina, A.Sultan Saleem, V.Sriram, G.Selva Cyril
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: With the fast development and growth of Internet of Things (IoT) networks, there is an increased threat of cyber-attack and it poses a significant demand on intelligent and real-time security. This project suggests some multi language machine learning framework, which will be used to design the cloud-based intrusion detection which involves the implementation of IoT devices to monitor both the environment and networks at all times. Three MCU boards are implemented: the DHT11 sensor on one of them measures the temperature and humidity, the LDR sensor on another one measures the intensity of the light, and the third one is the simulated node of the attacker. Gathered information is sent to a cloud system, and machine learning algorithms trained on the NSL-KDD dataset estimate and identify possible intrusions. Experimental outcomes prove high detection quality, low response time, as well as scalable integration with can be provided irrespective of heterogeneous platforms, and the framework has the potential to ensure IoT and cloud security by means of proactive automated intrusion detection in dynamic networks.

title: Exploring the Moderating Effect of Artificial Intelligence Adoption on Technological Self-Efficacy and Digital Communication in Relation to Digital Leadership Competencies
authors: Vania Zeisa Ardini, Gita Chinara Maharani, Kenni Natali Talaata Anggraeni, Maria Grace Herlina
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) is changing how organizations work. It means organizations need to be ready for the technology and have good leadership and communication. This research examines the relationships among digital leadership, digital communication, technological self-efficacy, and the use of AI in organizational settings. Utilizing survey data from 241 employees across various sectors in Greater Jakarta, the study employs structural equation modeling (SEM) to evaluate both mediating and moderating mechanisms. The results demonstrate that digital leadership can significantly enhance digital communication, further increasing employees’ technological self-efficacy. Digital communication is further validated as a critical mediating mechanism through which digital leadership impacts technological self-efficacy. It confirms the essential role of social and communicative processes in digital transformation. Moreover, the utilization of AI significantly impacts the relationship between digital communication and technological self-efficacy, exhibiting a negative interaction effect. This outcome suggests that excessive dependence on $A I$ might harm the confidence-enhancing function of human-centered digital communication. It highlights a significant boundary condition in AI-integrated workplaces. The study enhances the literature on digital leadership by illustrating that AI adoption does not consistently improve psychological outcomes and must be complemented by leadership-driven communication. The findings indicate that organizations should incorporate AI as an augmentative tool while maintaining meaningful leadership communication to preserve employees’ technological confidence.

title: AUV Path Planning Under Ocean Current Disturbances Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Bingbing Cai, Fanghua Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address three-dimensional path planning for autonomous underwater vehicles in marine environments with seabed, obstacles, and ocean-current disturbances, this paper proposes an improved deep reinforcement learning method termed NPD-D3QN. A three-dimensional marine environment is constructed using a bathymetric digital elevation model, spherical obstacles, and real ocean-current data with a first-order GaussMarkov disturbance term. The path-planning problem is formulated as a Markov decision process with a designed state space, discrete action space, and reward function. Based on D3QN, NoisyNet, prioritized experience replay, and a distillation replay pool are introduced to enhance exploration, sample efficiency, and training stability. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves faster convergence, higher success rates, and lower overall cost than the comparison methods. Dynamicobstacle experiments further demonstrate its adaptability and response capability in time-varying environments. Therefore, the proposed method provides an effective solution for threedimensional AUV path planning in complex marine environments.

title: A Digital Twin of Schooling Instability: Active Matter and Machine Learning for Climate-Resilient Marine Systems
authors: Fatima-Ez-Zahra Grini, Souad Tayane, Jaafar Gaber, Walid Abouzoul, Safa El Kordy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Climate change is increasingly destabilizing marine ecosystems, yet existing ecological models often fail to capture the nonlinear collapse of collective animal behavior under environmental stress. This paper presents an integrated framework combining active-matter physics and machine learning to model and predict climate-induced instability in fish schooling, with a regional focus on the Red Sea one of the fastestwarming marine basins globally. Using a Vicsek-type model of self-propelled agents, we simulate fish school dynamics across a grid of density $\rho$ and climate stress $\eta$ parameters, capturing transitions between ordered, milling, and disordered states. We introduce a Schooling Stability Index (SSI) that couples collective alignment (polarization) with environmental variability to quantify resilience. A Random Forest classifier predicts schooling phases with $\mathbf{9 6. 8 \%}$ accuracy, supporting ML-assisted earlywarning indicators, supporting ML-assisted early-warning indicators. Results reveal sharp thresholds beyond which behavioral collapse occurs, particularly under high-stress regimes consistent with climate-forcing scenarios reported for warming basins. This physics-informed digital twin prototype contributes to climate-resilient monitoring and AI-for-sustainability applications, with scalable relevance to vulnerable coastal regions.

title: A Review of Error Analysis and Compensation Methods Affecting the Measurement Performance of All-Optical Current Transformers
authors: Wenxing Sun, Haiyun Tang, Duanjiao Li, Congwei Yao, Kunyu Song, Wei Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The All-Fibre Optic Current Transformer (AFOCT) is an innovative electrical measurement device based on the Faraday magneto-optic effect. It offers distinct advantages including excellent insulation properties, strong resistance to electromagnetic interference, a broad measurement bandwidth, compact size, and ease of digitalisation. It holds significant application potential in fields such as ultra-high voltage transmission, renewable energy grid integration, power electronics equipment, and traction power supply for rail transit. However, during prolonged operation and under complex conditions, the measurement performance of AFCTs is susceptible to multiple factors including fibre material characteristics, optical component drift, environmental disturbances, and signal processing algorithms. These factors induce non-negligible measurement errors, limiting their further adoption in high-precision metering and protection applications.

title: Cascaded FOC-Based Speed Control of a PMSM Traction Drive for Electric Vehicles
authors: Mohamed Bounagui, Tamou Nasser, Ahmed Essadki
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper focuses on the control of high-power Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) speed for traction drive in electric vehicles. The electromechanical dynamic behavior is formulated in state space representation that can be readily controlled. A cascaded Field-Oriented Control (FOC) architecture is then applied, where the outer speed loop generates the torque producing current, and inner current regulators ensure a decoupled current. The PMSM is fed by a voltage source inverter with a Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) control, and an output filter is connected to increase the quality of the motor terminal voltage. Simulation results under variation speed reference and load torque disturbance are provided to show tracking, disturbance rejection, stable regulation, and balanced three phase stator currents. The inverter phase voltages show the expected switching behavior, while the filtered waveforms are very close to sinusoidal, confirming the effectiveness of the modulation and filtering block.

title: Nonlinear Integral Terminal Sliding Mode Control for DFIG-Based Wind Energy Conversion System
authors: Abdelmoghit Fathelkhair, Hassan Abouobaida, Younes Abouelmahjoub, Khadija Oualifi, Hajar Akli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This article focuses on the nonlinear control of a wind energy conversion system (WECS) based on a doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG). A fixed-time observer is developed to estimate the mechanical torque of the generator. Based on this, an Integral Terminal Sliding Mode Controller (ITSMC) is designed to control the Rotor Side Converter (RSC) and Grid Side Converter (GSC), with the aim of ensuring Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) as well as precise tracking of the control variables to their reference values. The stability of the proposed approach has been demonstrated using Lyapunov theory. The overall system is simulated in MATLAB/Simulink. The obtained results demonstrate a satisfactory dynamic response and accurate mechanical torque estimation, as well as the performance of the developed control strategy, particularly in terms of accuracy and robustness.

title: Adaptive Resource Allocation in Edge Computing Using Intelligent System Response
authors: Jamil K. J. Bataineh, Rula M. Tawalbeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Edge computing has in recent times been identified as a vital paradigm in the management of the ever-increasing challenges of bandwidth, latency, and resource capabilities in cloud computing. Nevertheless, the diversity of edge computing resources and the intricacy of computational tasks have posed a great challenge in the management of these computing paradigms. In this study, we have presented an adaptive resource management framework using intelligent systems’ approach to a system’s response. Through the formulation of a predictive resource management approach in a learning-based intelligent system, we have presented a solution to the management of diverse and intricate edge computing paradigms. The solution’s architecture and extensive experimental results have shown promising results in improving performance metrics such as throughput, latency, energy, and task acceptance rates. This study’s results have shown the viability of the presented resource management framework in the management of diverse computing paradigms in the context of the Internet of Things, Industry 4.0, and the next-generation wireless network.

title: TuRA-UNet: A Tumor-Aware Residual Attention U-Net for Brain Tumor MRI Segmentation
authors: Shuyi Li, Mohammed Ayoub Alaoui Mhamdi, Madjid Allili
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Segmenting brain tumors from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is vital for supporting diagnosis, treatment planning, and disease monitoring. However, the heterogeneous appearance, irregular boundaries, and varying sizes of tumors make automated segmentation a challenging task. In this paper, we propose TuRA-UNet, a tumor-aware residual attention UNet architecture designed to improve Dice-based segmentation accuracy for brain tumor MRI images. The proposed model extends the standard U-Net by incorporating residual learning to mitigate performance degradation in deeper networks and by integrating attention mechanisms to capture long-range spatial dependencies and contextual information relevant to tumor regions. Residual blocks are employed in both the encoding and decoding paths to preserve feature representations and ensure stable gradient propagation, while attention-guided skip connections selectively emphasize tumor-related features and suppress background noise. The model is evaluated on a publicly available multi-modal brain tumor MRI dataset using the Dice similarity coefficient as the primary accuracy measure. Experimental results demonstrate that TuRA-UNet consistently outperforms the baseline U-Net and representative variants, particularly in delineating complex tumor boundaries and small pathological regions. These findings indicate that the proposed tumor-aware residual attention framework provides an effective and computationally efficient solution for brain tumor segmentation in MRI images, with strong potential for integration into clinical decision-support systems.

title: Consistency Knobs in Practice: Latency, Availability, and Staleness Across Cassandra and MongoDB Under Workload and Faults
authors: Vinay Soni, Neha Agrawal, Madhushree Kumari, Viswanathan Ranganathan, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Backend teams routinely tune database consistency and routing knobs to hit latency targets, but the same knob can behave very differently under different workloads and fault modes. This paper presents a reproducible laptop-scale benchmarking workflow that sweeps consistency and routing settings for Apache Cassandra and MongoDB across four workload shapes and a multi-fault suite, with three repeats per scenario (648 total runs). We report tail latency (p99), staleness (sliding-window stale read rate), read-your-writes failures, multi-key batch staleness, and availability errors. Using an SLO-driven selector, we show which settings are fast and safe, which are fast but incorrect, and which are correct but unavailable under hard faults. Key findings include: (i) MongoDB secondary reads can stay below 1% staleness for read-heavy and hot-key cache patterns even under moderate induced lag, but can exceed 30% staleness under write-intensive workloads; (ii) Cassandra achieves near-zero staleness across several non-crash fault modes in our harness, yet node kill events drive error rates high enough that no tested knob setting meets the availability constraint.

title: IoT Enabled Contaminated Food Detection in Warehouse Storage
authors: M. Kavitha, Kavitha B.C, Pallavi Singh, Dr.P.Sankar, A.Pavan Venkata Durga Prasad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Ensuring food quality is crucial to guarantee safety and freshness throughout storage and transport. The IoT Enabled Contaminated Food Detection system monitors the quality of the food which employs a Raspberry Pi integrated with multiple sensors to track critical parameters affecting food quality. The variation of the temperature and hazardous gases spoiling the food can be identified by the MQ135 gas sensor and Dallas temperature sensor, pH sensor. Tracking of the location and monitoring enabled by the GPS module embedded with the system. All the sensors are also connected to the GSM module to share the alert message and location information. By comparing the threshold limits the degradation of the food can be detected. This system can be employed during food transportation to monitor the food quality and the challenging situation of maintaining food quality.

title: Affective-Aware Learning Systems: Bridging Emotion and Education through AI
authors: Khalil Omar, Jorge Marx Gómez
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Most digital learning platforms fail to address the critical role of emotions in educational outcomes. This study presents an emotionally adaptive learning framework that uses multi-modal emotion recognition to dynamically adjust instructional content and interaction patterns based on real-time student affective states. Our system achieves 87.3% accuracy in recognizing seven key learning-related emotions across diverse datasets including DAiSEE (9,068 educational videos), AffectNet (450,000 facial images), and MOOC behavioral data (200,000+ learners). The hierarchical decision-making architecture balances immediate emotional responses with long-term pedagogical objectives, demonstrating a 34% improvement in predicted knowledge retention and 42% reduction in stress indicators compared to static interfaces. Cross-dataset validation shows significant improvements in engagement metrics (p < 0.001). The framework employs privacy-preserving edge computing and cultural adaptation mechanisms to address key challenges in non-invasive emotion sensing. These findings establish foundations for next-generation learning systems that respond to both cognitive and affective dimensions of individual learners.

title: Sensing Before Failure: A Predictive IoT Architecture for Resilient Infrastructure Systems
authors: Savitha N, M. Jayaprakash
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Modern infrastructure systems are increasingly exposed to failures due to aging components, environmental variability, and dynamic operational loads. Traditional monitoring approaches rely on reactive strategies, resulting in delayed fault detection and increased system downtime. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a predictive IoT-based architecture that integrates distributed sensing, edge intelligence, and machine learning for early failure detection. The framework employs a closed-loop feedback mechanism to continuously monitor environmental and structural parameters, enabling real-time adaptive response. A machine learning-based prediction model is incorporated to identify abnormal patterns and forecast potential failures before occurrence. The system is evaluated using a simulated infrastructure dataset, demonstrating significant improvements in performance. Experimental results indicate that the proposed approach reduces response time by over 60% and improves prediction accuracy to 94.8% compared to conventional open-loop systems. The integration of predictive analytics with edge-based decision-making enhances system resilience, reliability, and resource efficiency. The proposed framework provides a scalable and sustainable solution for next-generation smart infrastructure monitoring.

title: Systematic Literature Review of Business Continuity Plan Methodologies: Components and Limitations
authors: Yusrida Muflihah, Apol Pribadi Subriadi, Reny Nadlifatin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The escalating frequency of global disruptions, including ransomware attacks, data breaches, cyberattacks, and supply chain vulnerabilities driven by geopolitical tensions, has elevated Business Continuity Planning (BCP) from operational contingency to strategic imperative. Therefore, Business Continuity Management (BCM) has evolved to address cyber-physical risks and supply chain dependencies. However, contemporary BCP methodologies show significant inconsistencies, limited validation, and unclear integration with organizational resilience frameworks, hindering implementation. This Systematic Literature Review (SLR) examines BCP methodologies published between 2021 and 2025 to identify core components and limitations. Searches were done on Scopus, ScienceDirect, ACM Digital Library, SpringerLink, and Google Scholar, following the PRISMA 2020 guidelines. The inclusion criteria consisted of peer-reviewed English journal articles published between 2021 and 2025. From 1,981 records, removing 885 duplicates resulted in 1,096 records for screening, yielding 49 eligible articles. The synthesis reveals five BCP components: risk and business impact analysis, continuity strategy design, implementation and operationalization, testing and maintenance, and integration with organizational resilience frameworks. Evidence limitations include scarce empirical validation, with 33 studies lacking field testing, heterogeneous study designs, and unclear resilience operationalization.

title: Lightweight Edge based Anomaly Detection Framework for Real Time Industrial IoT Monitoring
authors: A. Jerrin Simla, Hayel Khafajeh, N Krishna Chythanya, Varalatchoumy M, S.Agnes Shifani, Ramya M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Real-time anomaly detection in Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) environments is critical for minimizing equipment failure, improving operational efficiency, and ensuring predictive maintenance. Traditional models often require heavy computational resources and centralized architectures, making them unsuitable for lightweight edge deployment. The paper presents LEAD-XNet, a Lightweight Edge-based Anomaly Detection system, which can directly execute the system on resource-restrained edge devices. The model proposed incorporates such rare and new things like the Temporal Heterogeneous Information Network (T-HIN) embeddings, spiking graph neural layers, and a hybrid Tiny Temporal Conformer module to be able to capture the temporal-topological patterns in the most effective way possible. A large number of experiments was carried out based on real-world IIoT datasets, which included multivariate time-series data of machinery work. LEAD-XNet obtained the highest accuracy of 96.7% compared to the base models such as GRU-AE (91.3%), LSTM-VAE (93.5%), and Edge-AttentionNet (94.1%). It also showed granted minimal inference latency (22.5 ms) and decreased model size (6.9 MB), which validated its suitability as an edge. The model also has a drift-aware retraining scheme, which is founded on Wasserstein distance, which is resilient to environmental and operational shifts. These findings confirm the usefulness of LEAD-XNet in industrial anomaly detection in real-time with scalability. Its hybrid architecture guarantees high detection accuracy and cost-efficiency, which makes it a solid option to use on the modern smart factories and Industry 4.0 ecosystems.

title: Detecting DDoS: A Communication Efficient Approach in Distributed Environments
authors: Batool Armouti, Mouhammd Alkasassbseh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) environments are inherently dynamic and distributed. With the rapid increase in edge devices connected to the internet, vulnerabilities to cyber attacks have also grown. Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks are particularly critical, as they can severely degrade service performance for legitimate devices. Detecting such attacks is essential for maintaining network performance. Federated learning offers a promising solution by overcoming the privacy constraints associated with sharing raw data. Instead of training a centralized machine learning model, local clients are trained directly on devices, and their updates are aggregated into a single global model. Federated ensemble learning combines multiple weak local models at the server side. In this paper, a federated random forest model is implemented to detect DDoS attacks in distributed IoT environments. To reduce model size, both cost-complexity pruning and quantization techniques are applied. The combination of 16-bit quantization and pruning resulted in a significant reduction in model size retaining only 2.16% of the original size, while maintaining strong performance. This came at the cost of only a 0.79% drop in accuracy, along with substantial reductions in both uplink and downlink communication overhead.

title: Adaptive Cloud Resource Management using IBI Logic and Learning-based Optimization
authors: Subash Chandra Tripathy, Suvendu Chandan Nayak, Rekha Sahu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Scheduling large, diverse, and dynamically arriving tasks effectively while preserving performance, resource usage, and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance is becoming more and more difficult in cloud computing environments. Drawing inspiration from the human mind’s adaptive and learning-driven characteristics, this paper presents the ILA-ML Scheduler, a novel cloud task scheduling framework that combines predictive machine learning models with the Incomprehensible but Intelligible-in-time (IbI) Logics Algorithm (ILA). The suggested scheduler makes use of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to forecast workload dynamics and XGBoost to predict task execution time. The ILA optimization process uses these predictions as a guide to make proactive, flexible, and human-inspired task allocation choices. When compared to traditional scheduling algorithms like Min-Min, Round Robin, and Genetic Algorithm-based schedulers, extensive tests using a workload taken from the Google Cluster Workload Trace show that the ILA-ML Scheduler dramatically lowers SLA violation rates, improves virtual machine utilization, and reduces makespan. The outcomes demonstrate how well IbI logic-driven optimization and predictive machine learning work together to create next-generation cloud scheduling systems.

title: Hybrid Heuristic and Zero-Knowledge Proof Framework for Patient Data Sanitization and Selective Disclosure in Smart Contracts
authors: M.Lakshmanan, Gaurav Londhe, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, Sriramkumar R, R.Tamilamuthan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: In order to ensure the transparent and immutable maintenance of healthcare data, Blockchain technology has been proposed, but it places privacy against core requirements—one of which is privacy demanded by law. Anonymization techniques present today are useful in providing privacy, however they fall short in this sense in terms of guarantee. While zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) are one of the strongest cryptographic attestations, they come with hefty computational costs. This paper proposes an HH-ZKP model within a patient-specific-scope and selective disclosure on a smart contract. On a preliminary note, energetic sorting of electronic health records is actually done where anonymization is achieved using hybrid heuristic methods adhering to the constraints of k-anonymity and l-diversity. This is later followed by succinct ZKPs, ensuring that privacy is being obeyed without exposure of any of the hiding values. Again, the aggregations of these proofs are put onto the block with the smart contract, optimized for gas usage, to increase the scalability to a higher level. It is shown through experimental evaluations on the 10 K synthetic EHR dataset that the proposed scheme shows about a 75% reduced on-chain cost four times reduced proof sizes fully meeting HIPAA Safe Harbor compliance. The HH-ZKP model, by yielding a hybrid of heuristic-preprocessing and formal-principled cryptographic verification, is paving the way for scalable and regulator-aware blockchain health applications.

title: Enhancing Finite-Element-Informed Neural Networks via Metaheuristic Optimization: A Case Study in Structural Mechanics
authors: Ouadie El Mrimar, Loukmane El Khaldi, Zakaria El Haddad, Ibrahim Saadouni, Imane El Mrimar, Bousselham Samoudi, Othmane Bendaou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This article presents an optimized version of the finite element informed neural network (FEI-NN) based on the bio-inspired Harris Hawks optimization (HHO) algorithm for automatic hyperparameter tuning. FEI-NN combines the approximation capabilities of deep neural networks with the residuals from the weak formulation of the finite element method to impose physical consistency during learning. The HHO algorithm is used to optimize the learning rate, the physical penalty coefficient and the network size, leading to a hybrid model called HHO-FEI-NN. The method is validated on the 72 -bar space truss reference problem. The numerical results show a significant improvement in predictive accuracy, numerical stability and convergence speed compared to the standard FEI-NN and purely data-driven models.

title: Plant Leaf Disease Classification Using a Multi-Scale Attention Network
authors: Fatimah Shamsulddin Abdulsattar, Nadia Shamsulddin Abdulsattar, Faiq Sabar Baji
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Monitoring plant health and increasing crop productivity depend on the prompt identification of plant diseases. The diagnostic process is challenging due to the fact that infection symptoms might vary in size. This study addresses this issue by introducing a small-sized and multi-scale convolutional neural network (CNN) model designed for the identification of plant diseases in leaf images. In order to capture disease symptoms at various resolutions, this model uses multi-scale attention blocks, each of which has two convolutional branches with different kernel sizes. To enforce concentration on key features, a channel attention block is added to improve the representational power of the model. To achieve a balance between model performance and complexity, a few layers are used to create a small-sized architecture. The model has nearly one million parameters. Two plant disease datasets were used for evaluating the performance of the developed model. Compared to recent models, our model recorded SOTA performance with lower computational complexity. The accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were 99.68%, 99.51%, 99.62%, and 99.56%, respectively, on the PlantVillage dataset, and 96.64%, 96.63%, 96.64%, and 96.63% on the SAR-CLD-2024 cotton disease dataset. Further analyses highlighted the importance of a multi-scale framework and the incorporation of the attention block.

title: Flood Inundation Extent Extraction Based on Multi-source Synergistic Features from GF-3 Dual-polarization SAR Data
authors: Rong Li, Denghua Yan, Wei Jiang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In the context of global climate change, flood disasters triggered by extreme rainstorms occur frequently, posing a severe threat to the safety of people's lives and property. Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), with its all-time and all-weather imaging capabilities, has become a critical tool for emergency flood monitoring. However, complex terrain and interference from ground features often limit the accuracy of traditional singlefeature extraction methods. This paper proposes a technical framework for the precise extraction of flood inundation extents based on Gaofen-3 (GF-3) dual-polarization SAR data, following a “preprocessing - multidimensional feature extraction - machine learning classification - post-processing optimization” workflow. Building upon radiometric calibration and terrain correction, this method constructs a 21-dimensional multi-source collaborative feature vector that includes polarimetric scattering, texture features, temporal changes, and terrain constraints. An optimized Random Forest algorithm is then utilized to achieve probabilistic water body classification. Focusing on the “ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23 \cdot 7$</tex> ” catastrophic flood event in the Haihe River Basin in 2023, the Dongdian Flood Storage and Detention Area was selected as the study area, and GF-3 imagery from August 11, 2023, was used for experimental validation. The results indicate that this method effectively overcomes interference from complex backgrounds, and the extracted inundation extent aligns closely with the geographical boundaries of the storage and detention area. Compared with concurrent high-resolution optical imagery, the Overall Accuracy (OA) reached 94.67 %, with a Kappa coefficient of 0.89. The findings confirm that this technical scheme possesses both high accuracy and high timeliness under extreme weather conditions, providing reliable spatial information support for flood control decision-making and post-disaster assessment.

title: A Comparative Study of Marker-Based and Markerless Augmented Reality Technologies
authors: Younes El Jonhy, Soulaiman El Hazzat, Majid Ben Yakhlef
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) technologies have transformed the way digital information is integrated into real environments. This paper presents a comparative analysis of two major AR paradigms: marker-based and markerless systems. Markerbased AR employs predefined visual cues, such as QR codes or images, to anchor virtual objects within physical space, ensuring high positional accuracy and tracking stability. In contrast, markerless AR leverages sensors, including GPS, accelerometers, and computer vision algorithms, to detect and interpret the environment without physical markers, providing greater flexibility and immersion. The study examines the operational principles, advantages, and limitations of each approach across multiple application domains, including education, cultural heritage, and e-commerce. Experimental observations highlight that while marker-based AR offers superior accuracy under controlled conditions, markerless AR provides enhanced user engagement and adaptability. The insights derived from this comparison aim to guide developers and researchers in selecting suitable AR frameworks according to specific design and interaction requirements.

title: Cloud-Enabled Backup and Recovery Architecture for High-Availability Data Management
authors: Yash Narayan, Kashish Gupta, Yogesh Rajput, Swastik, Simran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rise in the usage of cloud computing and data-driven applications makes it imperative to provide adequate backup and recovery solutions to address potential system failures. In this regard, on-premises backup systems tend to lack scalability, incur heavy maintenance costs, and fail to address disaster recovery efficiently. This paper aims to develop an effective backup and recovery solution in the cloud to provide long-term accessibility, integrity, and fast system recovery from various system crashes, cyber-attacks, natural disasters, and other unforeseen circumstances. The proposed solution focuses on utilizing cloud storage to reduce data loss as well as increase recovery time objectives in the system. The security of systems is further improved using different encryption techniques to emphasize data privacy and secure access to systems from unauthorized platforms. The system’s performance is measured through parameters like backup delay time, recovery time, storage capacity, system dependability, and various workload parameters on the system. The result clearly indicates improved system recovery time, system dependability, cost-effectiveness, and effectiveness of the backup system compared to traditional systems.

title: TinyML-Based Hand Gesture Recognition for Device Control
authors: Chaymae Yahyati, Ismail Lamaakal, Khalid El Makkaoui, Ibrahim Ouahbi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Hand gesture recognition enables intuitive and contactless device control by translating specific hand movements into commands. This allows users to effortlessly operate smart home systems, consumer electronics, and industrial machinery through natural gestures, enhancing accessibility and user experience without physical interfaces. This paper presents a robust TinyML-based framework for real-time hand gesture recognition, designed for efficient deployment on resource-constrained embedded devices. The system translates eight distinct hand gestures into device control commands using a vision-based approach. A dataset of 8,000 images was collected under varied lighting and angles to enhance generalization. The preprocessing pipeline incorporates hand isolation via MediaPipe, resizing, selective brightness enhancement, and normalization to optimize input data. Three lightweight convolutional neural networks, MobileNetV3Small, NASNetMobile, and DenseNet121, were evaluated and optimized using pruning and full integer quantization. Results show that all models retained high accuracy above $97 \%$ post-compression, with MobileNetV3Small achieving the best performance: $98.7 \%$ accuracy, an inference time of 4 ms, and a model size of 195 KB. This work demonstrates the viability of TinyML for enabling low-latency, privacy-preserving, and energy-efficient gesture-controlled interfaces on microcontrollers.

title: Mamba-SHAP Hybrid Framework for Explainable PCOS Detection from Clinical and Hormonal Data
authors: Mathu Mozhi S P, Nithyasri B, Hepsi Ajibah A S
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome is a complex endocrine disease that should possess precise and interpretational systems of diagnosis. Convolutional machine learning models are small in terms of temporal modeling capacity and they cannot be easily explained, hence they do not work well in clinical decision making. In this discussion, offer Mamba-SHAP Hybrid Framework of explainable PCOS detection according to clinical features, metabolic features, and hormonal features. The given system will be based on Mamba State Space Model (SSM) to model long-range dependencies and sequentiality in patient data, which is more efficient than recurrent architectures such as LSTM and GRU. To represent the clinical records, they are tabularized, and the sequence construction module of a feature normalization and patient-wise sequence construction is used to convert the clinical records into temporal embeddings, which can be processed with Mamba. To achieve transparency and clinical trust, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) is introduced to identify the contribution made by individual hormonal and metabolic parameter when making the prediction. It can be observed that the experimental analysis of the PCOS data demonstrates that it categorizes much better, more consistent with imbalanced samples, and has a better extraction of time-related details than baseline deep learning models. The biomarkers that are clinically validated such as AMH, LH / FSH ratio, BMI, and fasting insulin are mentioned in SHAP visualizations which demonstrate the medical importance of the model. The proposed Mamba-SHAP strategy is also well-predictive and its diagnostics are interpretable, thus the choice to be implemented in actual health care.

title: Parkinson’s Disease Classification from Smartwatch Non-Motor Symptom Questionnaires in the PADS Dataset
authors: Asma Ouabd, Mohamed Sraitih, Achraf Benba, Abdelilhah Jilbab, Amir Hajjam El Hassani, Ahmed Hammouch
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) manifests through both motor and non-motor symptoms (NMS), the latter playing an increasingly recognized role in early and differential diagnosis. Although recent works using the Parkinson’s Disease Smartwatch (PADS) dataset have focused primarily on multimodal fusion strategies combining wearable sensor data with clinical questionnaire responses, the standalone diagnostic capability of NMS questionnaires has not been thoroughly investigated. This paper presents a systematic evaluation of PD classification relying exclusively on the $\mathbf{3 0}$-item PDNMS questionnaire from the PADS dataset, without any smartwatch sensor data. We employ three supervised learning classifiers: CatBoost, Random Forest, and XGBoost combined with SelectKBest feature selection (ANOVA F-test, $\mathrm{k}=17$), SMOTE based class balancing on training data only, and 5-fold stratified cross-validation with hyperparameter grid search. The questionnaire only approach achieves balanced accuracies of 93.24% for PD vs. Healthy Controls (HC) and 78.22% for PD vs. Differential Diagnosis (DD), equaling or surpassing prior questionnaire only baselines and rivaling sensor-based multimodal approaches. These results establish NMS questionnaires as a powerful, cost-effective, and hardware-free alternative for large scale PD screening.

title: A Review on Advancing Immersive Experiences through Animation Technology in Virtual Reality
authors: Faris Abuhashish, Mohammed Atoum, Waheeb Abu-Ulbeh, Isma H. Ismail
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Animation in virtual reality is often conceptualized as a layer on top of content, empirical observations suggest that the virtual reality animation is infrastructure which takes up latency budgets, stabilizes sensorimotor synchrony, and which determines whether a virtual environment is experienced as if inhabited or be simply observed. Drawing upon a Scopus/ERIC-based systematic review of VR-animation investigations, we characterized animation technology as a coupled pipeline that involves motion generation, constraint management, multimodal synchronization, and real-time rendering within some stringent frame-time constraints. The consequences of this group of pipelines, reported, fall into three fields: user experience (presence, immersion, engagement), educational and training contexts in which animation is embedded in interactive tasks, in industrial applications for collaboration, configuration and decision support. The evidence basis is heterogeneous; many studies make assuming designs but make use of non-comparable measures, and are constrained because of hardware, space, or methodological differences. To address this, we put forward a taxonomy that separates between perception-coupled motion and purely decorative motion and propose an evaluation framework for performing an animation selection based on the latency variance, risk of cybersickness and impact on the execution of the related task. The following research outline presents the focus on reproducibility, cross-device strength, and privacy-conscious avatar animation.

title: A Multi-Task Cross-Attention Network for Driver Fatigue Detection: A Tobacco Logistics Case Study
authors: Nan Chen, Shichao Yan, Yuanxiang Ji, Yanghua Gao, Yuanfeng Zhu, Rui Wang, Yi Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: Fatigue driving poses a significant safety risk in real-world driving scenarios, while conventional visionbased methods often struggle with limited accuracy under complex conditions. This paper proposes a multimodal fatigue detection approach based on a Multi-Task Cross-Attention Network (MT-CAN). The model jointly represents static visual features, inter-frame variations, structured facial keypoints, and temporal information, and employs a cross-attention mechanism to enable effective multimodal fusion and multi-task learning. Four fatigue-related tasks, including blinking, yawning, head deviation, and fatigue state recognition, are collaboratively modeled within a unified framework.Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves an average F1-score of 0.868 and an mAP@0.5 of 0.905, outperforming several baseline methods. The model demonstrates strong robustness and real-time performance (48.9 FPS), making it suiTable for practical deployment in real-world driving scenarios. Although validated in tobacco logistics scenarios, the proposed method is applicable to general driver fatigue detection tasks.

title: Entity Guided Natural Language Generation for Targeted Review Summarization
authors: Prachi P, Malavika T, Mahendran P, M. Rajkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Analyzing large volumes of hotel reviews is challenging due to unstructured text and complex language. This paper proposes an entity-guided natural language generation framework for generating targeted and factually accurate summaries from customer feedback. The system performs clause-level segmentation, aspect-based sentiment classification, and statistical aggregation to construct structured evidence for summary generation. A large language model is guided using this evidence to produce coherent summaries, while a numeric validation mechanism ensures factual consistency and reduces hallucinations. The proposed approach enables scalable and data-driven analysis of customer reviews for improved decision-making.

title: Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Algorithms for Depression Risk Prediction using PHQ-9 Questionnaire
authors: Chandni Magoo, Roshi Saxena, Parth Sharma, Nishant Kumar Chauhan, Ranveer Chauhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Among university students, depression can be seen as a growing problem. Objective screening of depression should be considered beyond conventional clinical assessments. The Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9) is a popular tool widely used for assessing depressive symptoms that provides both a numerical score and a categorical severity level. This work portrays a comparative analysis of various machine learning algorithms for two predictive tasks: classification of depression severity categories and regression-based estimation of PHQ-9 numerical scores. For classification models, all PHQ-9 items were used, while regression models were intentionally limited to non-PHQ psychosocial features in order to avoid trivial score reconstruction. Among the classification algorithms, Random Forest had the best performance with an accuracy of 75.91% and good cross-validation performance. For the tasks of regression, Gradient Boosting model showed the best results with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.522 and a mean absolute error of 4.04. These results confirm that machine learning approaches, especially tree-based models, help in early depression risk screening.

title: Safety-Enhanced Hybrid A* Path Planning for Unclassified Roads Based on Hierarchical Map
authors: Xiyu Huang, Guoliang Zhang, Wenjie Yu, Ping Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address the issue of unreasonable paths generated by the traditional Hybrid A* algorithm when searching on unclassified roads with complex roadside environments, this paper proposes a safety-enhanced Hybrid A* path planning method based on a hierarchical map. First, a hierarchical map encompassing a geometric layer, an obstacle layer, a semantic layer, and a safety coefficient layer for unclassified roads is constructed. Subsequently, the hierarchical map is integrated with the Hybrid A* algorithm by incorporating the grid safety coefficient into the cost function as an additional cost term to enhance path safety. Furthermore, the Voronoi diagram is utilized to extract the road centerline, which is then resampled using spline interpolation. The deviation between the vehicle’s heading angle and the heading angle of the centerline sampling points is introduced into the actual cost value to improve path smoothness. Finally, by delineating straight and curved road segments, different step sizes are adopted for expansion in different segments to enhance algorithm efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method not only ensures feasibility and stability but also offers advantages in safety and algorithm efficiency for path planning in unclassified road environments.

title: From Adoption To Execution Why Many Indonesian MSMEs Fail After Going Digital A Systematic Literature Review Of Strategic And Organizational Barriers
authors: Dandy Darmawan, Muhammad Hanif, Enggal Sriwardiningsih
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Digital Transformation has been essential for strengthening competitiveness in Indonesian Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs), yet many fail to execute technology adoption into improved performance, financial gain, or competitive advantage. This Systematic Literature Review (SLR) consists of twenty peer reviewed articles published between 2020 and 2025 to examine the strategic and organizational barriers that prevent effective digital transformation execution. The findings show that strategic barriers arise when MSMEs adopt digital technology without clear objectives, structured planning, or well-defined performance indicators. While organizational barriers such as limited digital skill, insufficient training, weak leadership readiness, and resistance to change business culture will prevent technology from being integrated into daily operations. These interconnected barriers create what researchers described as implementation gaps. The review concludes that digital transformation must be approached as a comprehensive organizational change process. This review also provides practical implications for policymakers, MSMEs owners, and digital platforms, while also identifying future research direction for MSMEs with limited resources.

title: A Comparative Study of Various Urban Change Detection Techniques in Northern Jordan using Sentinel and TerraSAR-X images
authors: Nawras Shatnawi, Bashar Altheeb, Aslam Al-Omari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The high rate of urbanization in the northern governorates in Jordan has necessitated the need to detect urban change in time with the planning being needed by the high numbers of refugees entering the country. Hence, it has been assessed that a multi-temporal Sentinel-2 (optical) and SAR (Sentinel-1 and historical TerraSAR-X/ASAR) multi-image analysis compares urban growth in the Northern Jordan in 2003 and 2024 to quantify the growth. The image preprocessing, which involves radiometric calibration, speckle filtering and terrain correction was used to meet this aim and object-based classification was done in eCognition Developer. Further, spectral indices have been utilized including: Normalized Difference Built-up Index (NDBI) and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and a Built-up Area Index (BAI), as attributes in a supervised maximum likelihood classification and in unsupervised (K-means) clustering. The coded results were then transformed into raster to calculate built-up area (BUA). The findings reveal that the BUA expanded by 53.8 km 2 (3.3%) in 2003 to $84.6 \mathrm{~km} 2(4.8 (\%))$ in 2024 with most of it being located at the urban Belt and thus turning agricultural lands into built space, increasing the population demand on infrastructure and services. Moreover, similar maps were obtained by the SAR-based and the optical-based methods, and even supervised classifications tended to be more accurate than unsupervised because with the increase in control of the definition of classes performance improved. Therefore, the results demonstrate that urban sprawl can be effectively monitored by satellite change detection that will guide land-use policy. Moreover, the research points at the implication of the findings on the planning of studies because the profound growth of study territory sets the importance of a sustainable development strategy and free access to high-resolution images and analysis tools to continue monitoring of the city. Lastly, the research paper suggests the need to avail free satellite images, which spans a vast region within the area, by government and private organizations that support such kind of researches.

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Classification Using Handcrafted Features and Machine Learning on MRI Data
authors: Amina Lakbiri, Abdelilah Jilbab, Ahmed Hammouch
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that has a considerable impact on a patient’s daily function and cognitive ability. Therefore, it is vital to identify AD through early diagnosis in order to properly treat the patient. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is an important tool for examining brain structure and pathological changes, and it provides valuable information for determining how an individual is affected by this disorder. The implementation of machine learning for detecting stages of Alzheimer’s disease could improve the precision of diagnoses and increase the chances of early intervention. In this study, we propose a machine learning framework based on handcrafted descriptors to better extract information from MRI scans. Three machine learning classifiers (Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Decision Tree (DT)) were used to classify MRI scans into different categories. The experimental results show that the highest classification rate was achieved using SVM, which obtained an accuracy of 84.27%, with KNN scoring 83.98% and DT yielding 64.16%. It is evident that effective classification of MRI data related to AD depends on the combination of welldefined texture features and the choice of machine learning classifier. This approach is less expensive than deep learning methods, requires fewer computational resources, and still produces reliable diagnostic performance.
