title: Prakriti: Human Body-Type Identification to Recommend Lifestyle Using IoT and ML
authors: Mahesh Pawaskar, Suraj Vishwakarma, Shravan Thakur, Sameer Singh, Ritesh Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The skyrocketing prevalence of lifestyle-related disorders and chronic non-communicable diseases has seen preventive healthcare getting a major boost. Modern healthcare systems are effective, which is effective in the diagnosis and treatment of patients, but they usually do not have the mechanisms of continuous monitoring and personal wellness advice. An ancient Indian approach to medicine Ayurveda, which is more focused on prevention, is based on the study of individualized constitutions called Prakriti which is regulated by three types of Doshas: Vata, Pitta, and Kapha. This paper introduces a system of healthcare called Prakriti that incorporates the Internet of Things (IoT) technology with the use of the Machine Learning (ML) models to recognize the Ayurvedic type of the body of an individ and prescribe personal lifestyle changes. IoT sensors can be used to record real-time physiological data like heart rate, electro-cardiogram (ECG) body temperature, and oxygen sat- uration (SpO2) and process them using supervised learning algorithms such as Random Forest and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The system combines biometric patterns following the Ayurvedic principles to create individualized advice regarding diet, exercise, yoga, and daily practices. High prediction accuracy, low latency, and reliability have been proven via experimental assessment and it is true that the challenge to employ traditional medical knowlknowledge and the present computational intelligence is attainable. The suggested model facilitates wellness, early disease diagnosis, and preventive care.

title: A Compact Population-Activity Detector for Event-Driven Gating in Neuromorphic Pipelines
authors: Gourab Datta, Sumaia Jahan Mishu, Sarah Safura Sharif, Yaser Mike Banad
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Event-driven systems benefit from suppressing unnecessary downstream computation when input activity is low. This paper presents a compact population-activity detector for event-driven gating, implemented as a purely combinational digital primitive (popcount + programmable threshold compare) in Cadence using a 90 nm process library. The circuit accepts five binary activity inputs and asserts an enable signal when the instantaneous population count exceeds a configurable threshold. We formulate gating as a trade-off between compute suppression and activation fidelity, and we provide an evaluation protocol based on duty-cycle, false activation/suppression rates, and activation-latency statistics under controlled activity/noise conditions. The proposed detector serves as a reusable low-overhead building block for gating expensive stages in neuromorphic and event-based processing pipelines.

title: A Detecting Method For Line-Spectrum Target Based on Time-Frequency Signal Presence Probability Sensing
authors: Lekai Chen, Xiang Pan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This study introduces a line-spectrum target recognition methodology utilizing time-frequency signal presence probability (TFSPP) sensing, aimed at estimating the bearings of weak targets in underwater settings characterized by significant interference. The proposed method utilizes the narrowband characteristics and long-term stability of line-spectrum signals to estimate the probability of signal presence at each frequency bin and time frame, employing these probabilities as weights for a weighted summation of the spatial power spectra across various beam directions. This amplifies the target's line-spectrum elements while attenuating noise and interference. Simulation results and maritime experimental data indicate that the TFSPP based conventional beamforming(TFSPP_CBF) surpasses traditional CBF, narrowband CBF, and variance-of-frequency weight based conventional beamforming(VFW_CBF) under low signal-to-noise ratio(SNR) and high-interference conditions. The suggested method efficiently emphasizes target bearings and mitigates interfering beams, thereby enhancing detection performance greatly.

title: Fuzzy Controller Assisted DVR for Grid-Connected PV Nanogrid Under Severe Grid Faults
authors: Maheshwari Thippanolla, Sobhana Obulareddy, J. Viswanatha Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The increasing adoption of photovoltaic based nano grids necessities a critical need for fault management system to ensure uninterrupted supply to critical loads. This paper presents a Fuzzy assisted Dynamic Voltage Restorer (DVR) to mitigate power quality issues within the distribution system. The proposed PV nano grid has a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm, a three-phase voltage source inverter with fuzzy controller assisted Dynamic Voltage Restorer (DVR). The MPPT controller sustains optimal photovoltaic output voltage under varying irradiance conditions during standard operational procedure. The analysis is carried out with significant three-phase faults and sensitive loads. As a series-connected FACTS device, the DVR regulates voltage by feeding a compensating voltage directly into the line. By adopting a novel FLC-assisted control strategy, the DVR achieves a faster response to system disturbances compared with conventional approaches. The proposed controller was modeled and validated using MATLAB/Simulink, and a comparative study was presented with and without DVR protection. The results indicate the FLC-DVR provides superior accuracy and performance over conventional PI controllers.

title: Research on PCB Defect Detection Algorithm Based on YOLOv5
authors: Lu Gao, Kim Young Chul
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of electronic information technology, electronic products are evolving toward higher precision and smaller dimensions. Meanwhile, manufacturing quality directly determines product reliability and stability. As core components of electronic devices, printed circuit boards (PCBs) require rigorous quality inspections to ensure production integrity, yet PCB manufacturing processes are prone to various defects. To address challenges such as small target dimensions, diverse defect types, complex background interference, and difficult recognition of tiny defects in PCB defect detection, this study proposes an improved YOLOv5 algorithm. The proposed approach first incorporates a SENet network to autonomously learn feature channel importance weights, enhancing target detection accuracy for small and weak defects. It then integrates decoupled detection head architecture into the YOLOv5 network to eliminate task conflicts between classification and bounding box regression, while improving defect detection precision and accelerating network convergence. Furthermore, the original activation function is replaced with FReLU to enhance spatial feature capture capability. Experimental results demonstrate that the anchor-based algorithm achieves an accuracy rate (P) of 87.8%, representing a 1 percentage point improvement over the original YOLOv5 algorithm, and mAP@0.5 is increased by 3 percentage points, further validating the effectiveness of this optimization.

title: A CNN Framework for Accurate Fatty Liver Disease Prediction Using EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121
authors: Karthi V, Manikandan K, Hariomprakaas M, Madhu Mitha M, Pradeep S, Saik Muhamed Aslam J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The primary goal of this work is to construct and apply an automated system leveraging deep learning methods to classify liver ultrasound images into Healthy and Hepatic Steatosis and to compare the performance of EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121 transfer learning models. Materials and Methods: Data samples were allocated, where Group 1 utilized the existing single pretrained CNN approach and Group 2 utilized the proposed transfer learning model with Global Average Pooling. Result: The performance matrix at a 95% confidence level, and the proposed approach attained an accuracy of about 95–96%, whereas the existing approach attained an accuracy of about 90–91%. Conclusion: The proposed framework integrates EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121 with a two-stage transfer learning strategy and statistical validation, achieving improved accuracy and reliability for early detection of hepatic steatosis.

title: Front Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)

title: Quantum Computing-Powered Error Correction System for Secure Satellite Communication
authors: Sudhakiran Ponnuru, Siva Kumar. M, Sai Santhosh Ramadugu, M.S. Sivagama Sundari, Kalpana Umapathy, K. Rakesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In this research a new framework is proposed for the quantum computing based error correction of the secure satellite communication systems. Leveraging on quantum error correction codes (QECC) that are drawn with advanced machine learning architectures, we develop a system that is a hybrid (classical and quantum) sufficient and using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum Libraries (TfQ). The proposed system is transformer based proudly practicing neural networks with prowded consideration activity mechanisms for decimating the syndrome size and formation of error bars for real-time pattern recognition for noisy quantum channels. We integrate in a distributed way the federated learning for satellite networks and provide reinforcement learning agents which take part in the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm for adaptive optimal error correction strategy choice depending on channel conditions. Research validation with PyTorch Lightning and Weights & Biases provide some experimental validations showing 34 % improvement on bit error rate (BER) and 28 % less decoding latency as compared to the conventional LDPC codes. The system has quantum-resilient security which is founded upon BB84 protocol implementation by neural network enhanced key distributing.

title: WayWaker: A Location-Based Alarm System for Travelers Using Transformer-Based Context-Aware Travel Recommendation
authors: Vallimmai C K, Sujitha P, Sherlin Jenefa I M, M B Anushlin Leena, M Robinson Joel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Analysts and specialists from diverse sectors, including customisation, data recovery, ubiquitous and adaptable computing, data mining, and management, have duly recognised the importance of contextualized data. While a detailed study was recently carried out in the domain of recommendation systems, most of the current approaches emphasize the recommendation of the best-suited products to consumers. Most of the additional contextual data such as time, space, climate, or the attractiveness of other places is usually ignored. To maximise the use of contextual information and avoid the mistakes of conventional sequential recommendation systems, we suggest a Transformerbased Context-Aware Travel Recommendation (TCATR) paradigm. Our model effectively captures the contextual fluctuations and long-range relationships in user trip behaviour by utilising a selfattention mechanism instead of recurrent structures. A ranking prediction module, a contextual feature embedding unit, and a sequential behaviour encoder are the three main parts of the model.

title: Smart Water Management System for Multi-Storey Residential Buildings
authors: Godlin Debby K. J, Eswar A, Jayasurya S, Naveen Kumar R, Suradhish R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This research work proposes a Smart Water Management System (SWMS) based on the use of Internet of Things (IoT) technology, embedded controllers, real, time sensors and cloud, based data sculping to automate and enhance water distribution in multi, storey residential buildings. The utilitarian of the scheme is intelligent pump automation, smart metering at individual flats, real, time leak detection, pressure monitoring, and cloud, based dashboards accessible through mobile and web applications. User alerts and consumption analytics foster transparency and facilitate responsible water usage. Simulation and experimental tests demonstrated that the proposes model has the potential to greatly increase water utilization efficiency, lower cost of operations, reduce water losses due to leakages and overflows, and simultaneously support sustainable urban water management.

title: Analysis of Multiple-Output Integrated Receivers for Wireless Power Transfer in Biomedical Implants
authors: Yifan Yao, Shilong Shen, Alice S. Lee, Hoi Lee, Jin Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) via inductive coupling is widely used to power different biomedical implants. Traditionally, a WPT receiver consists of a first-stage rectifier for AC-DC conversion and a second-stage single-inductor multipleoutput DC-DC converter that provides different supply rails to the analog, digital, and stimulation modules inside the implant. Recently, multiple-output-regulating (MOR) rectifiers have been reported to perform the same function as the conventional twostage architecture with a single power stage, significantly improving system efficiency and reducing receiver size. The paper reviews state-of-the-art MOR rectifiers and analyzes their pros and cons with respect to power efficiency and scalability. We then provide design suggestions to help researchers and engineers design a new high-performance MOR rectifier.

title: Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) by Workers: Extending Theory of Planned Behaviour (TPB) with IT Knowledge and Price Value
authors: Ahmad Bilal Almagribi, Bambang Purnomosidi Dwi Putranto
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology has seen widespread adoption across various sectors, including by employees within numerous organizations. Despite its many benefits, accessing the full features of AI often involves a cost for these workers. Consequently, it is crucial to understand their behavioral responses to this technology. The purpose of this study was to evaluate the impact of the Theory of Planned Behaviour (TPB), IT Knowledge, and Price Value on the Behavioral Intention to Adopt AI. The study employed a quantitative method, collecting primary data from 169 Indonesian workers across various professions who were selected through purposive sampling. Subsequent analysis of the data was conducted using partial least squares-structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM) with the SmartPLS 4 software. The results indicated that both Attitude and Subjective Norms significantly influenced the Behavioral Intention to Adopt AI. The researchers recommend that organizations should embrace this technology but must also establish clear regulations and provide guidance to employees on its wise and ethical use. Future studies are encouraged to expand the sample size, explore alternative analytical techniques such as path analysis or multiple regression, and incorporate other potential moderating or mediating variables.

title: IoT Data Analytics with Deep Neural Networks: Trends and Future Directions
authors: Jayamangala Sristi, Vinay Kumar Nassa, Isan Sahoo, M. K. Sharma, Maddikera Kalyan Chakravarthi, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The study has shown how “deep neural networks” can enhance predictive maintenance according to the “Internet of Things (IoT)”. “The artificial neural networks (ANN) and the Long Short-term memory (LSTM) models are developed and tested analyzing data related to performance. These findings showed that, in spite of the LSTM being more efficient at identifying temporal relationships, the ANN is more precise and accurate overall, and appropriate for sequencing IoT data. These findings indicate that deep learning can streamline the work of the industry, minimize downtime, and significantly increase the ability to identify failures. Regardless of the favorable results, the existence of disproportional classes of failures hampered recall and model generalization. Therefore, deep neural networks become an effective tool for analytics of the IoT, providing a foundation to develop predictive maintenance systems that are more robust, reliable, and current.

title: Autonomous Agent-Based Intelligence for Continuous Learning and Adaptation in Cyberphysical Systems
authors: Vivek Sanjay Thorat, Sri Krishna Ravulapalli, Shambhu Shankar Rai, Nirmala Soren, Rajesh Kumar Tiwari, Abhishek M. Dhore
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) experience great operational complexity in the stochastic and non-stationary contexts when the control logic can be considered static. The current paper introduces an autonomous agent-based intelligence framework, which can be used in perpetually adaptive and decentralized organization of heterogeneous CPS systems. The given framework implements a multi-agent system (MAS) to bridge the gap between the cyber and physical layers with the help of built-in perception-action loops and planning modules. With the help of online reinforcement learning (RL) and predictive analytics, individual agents change control policies in real-time to alleviate disturbances in a system and changing operational constraints. A shared knowledge layer that is distributed is put in place to coordinate inter-agent coordination and policy refinement making the system-wide scalable and fault-tolerant. Experimental validation in a wide range of CPS situations proves that the suggested autonomous structure can be much more efficient and self-optimizing than centralized baseline frameworks, providing a stable channel of self-evolutionary industrial and robotic systems.

title: AI-Based Daily Heart Risk Guardian (DHRG) Using XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Aishweryam Jofin J, Elakkiya E
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: CVD is still the global leader of deaths and therefore, we need to detect those at risk of suffering a heart attack or other cardiovascular disease early, so that we can prevent them from developing into a serious health risk. The proposed DHRG system will take raw data from multiple sources, process this data into appropriate formats (i.e. standard units), then combine the processed data into a single database. Then the machine learning algorithm will use this single database to train the XGBoost model to predict a user's chance of having a heart problem. Finally, it will generate a risk level (low, medium, high) based on the user's daily activity levels (i.e., inactivity, active, very active, etc.) and give them personalized recommendations on what to do next (e.g., eat better, start exercising, etc.). Unlike current technologies, which rely almost exclusively on either ECG data or lifestyle data alone, the DHRG system integrates lifestyle, physiological, and environmental information into one area, thus providing health care providers and patients an integrated, detailed daily assessment of the risk of developing CVD and the ability to help prevent it.

title: Enhancing Employee Data Security with Blockchain Solutions
authors: Priyanka Rani, A. S. Kavitha, V. Vimala, Simranpreet Kaur, Shekhar Singh, Pankaj Kunekar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The secrecy of the information of the employees has become one of the most significant dilemmas of the contemporary organization, and the blockchain is one of the opportunities that are under consideration. The article cannot be called the abstract one as it is based on the recent research and practical examples to investigate the spectrum further through the contribution of blockchain to the appearance of data security. It has been demonstrated that accuracy and access control and transparency could be enhanced, with the help of a hybrid company, where certified blockchain is applied alongside the traditional off-chain databases. Even this system will be delivering the tradeoff between the raw nature of blockchain and the necessity to be regulated and an easy method of ensuring the security of HR data.

title: Deep Learning-Based Solar Energy Forecasting Using Environmental Time-Series Data
authors: Geethanjali Guthi, Pavan Kumar Didde, Zuber Shaik, Siva Mohan Yadav Vangala, Muralidhar Kurni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: With the global transition toward renewable energy, accurately forecasting solar energy production is pivotal for enhancing grid reliability, optimizing energy storage, and facilitating sustainable distribution. This project presents a comprehensive analysis of energy prediction using three distinct models: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, Linear Regression, and Random Forest. Leveraging environmental inputs such as temperature and humidity, hourly time-series data were processed and normalized using a 24-hour input sequence to capture daily patterns. Model performance was rigorously evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). Comparative results highlight the superior accuracy of the LSTM model, reflecting its ability to learn long-term dependencies in sequential weather data. Furthermore, the project includes a 7-day (168-hour) future energy forecast and aggregated daily estimates, offering actionable insights for solar energy planning and optimization. This work demonstrates how deep learning approaches can outperform traditional models in complex, time-sensitive domains, such as energy forecasting.

title: A 12.5mW Deep Neural Network Accelerator for Continuous Seizure Forecasting in Edge Devices
authors: Bailey Heynsten, Muhammad Awais Bin Altaf
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Early prediction of epileptic seizures can significantly improve patient safety and enable proactive interventions. However, achieving accurate forecasting on wearable devices is challenging due to stringent power and area constraints. This paper presents a hardware-efficient Deep Neural Network (DNN) accelerator for continuous seizure prediction, capable of forecasting seizure risk up to 60 seconds (s) before onset. The proposed design leverages a hybrid feature set combining spectral ratios and temporal dynamics with patient-specific normalization to enhance sensitivity to pre-ictal transitions. A compact DNN architecture with quantized weights and a fixedexponent sigmoid activation, implemented via a 16-entry lookup table, achieves 31.4% area reduction and 15.4% lower energy per operation compared to IEEE-754 floating-point designs. FPGAbased validation using the CHB-MIT EEG database demonstrates prediction accuracies of 96.5%, 94.3%, 91.8%, and 89.7% for 10 s, 20 s, 30 s, and 60 s horizons, respectively, with an end-to-end latency of 0.8 s. Post-synthesis results in 180 nm CMOS report 12.5 mW power consumption, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.33 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex> area, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.33 \mu \mathbf{J}$</tex> per inference, making the accelerator well-suited for resource-constrained wearable applications. These results establish a practical pathway toward real-time, energy-efficient seizure forecasting at the edge.

title: Blockchain Technology for Enhancing Transparency and Security in Financial Management Systems
authors: Bini Marin V, Santanu Roy, Sandeep Banerjee, Lokeshwari CK, Neeraj S Bakshi, Avinash Hanmant Ghadage
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Blockchain innovation is revolutionizing distributed networks by enhancing security and transparency. Blockchain has become an innovation that may be used in many fields, especially in financial services, over the past decade. It has been emphasized as an innovation that will make financial transactions easy, efficient, secure, and affordable. This work presents a blockchain system powered by cutting-edge artificial intelligence (AI) methods that will improve transparency and safety across the transaction system. The conceptual methodology used in this research depends on federated learning for Blockchain Inclusion, in which dispersed databases from various blockchain sites add to a worldwide AI system without exchanging unprocessed data specimens. Increasingly better safety standards for fraud and anomaly identification are developed with LSTM automatic encoders. This study suggests Smart Contractual Financial Control for AI algorithms in a distributed setting to achieve this goal. CNNs use the secure worldwide paradigm for examining streaming transactional statistics, generating prompt notifications, and taking appropriate action when fraud is identified. The system is considerably more dependable because of its accurate time. Based on the preliminary results, the suggested model is successful when regional frameworks are aggregated without data leaks, outliers are recognized effectively, algorithms are handled transparently, data confidentiality is maintained at an extremely advanced stage, and illicit transactions are easily identified.

title: Research on Human-Following Control of a Smart Walker Based on IMU and Pressure Sensor Fusion
authors: Zirui Zhang, Shimin Wei, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: To address the high cost and environmental sensitivity of existing LiDAR-based walkers, this paper proposes a low-cost, lightweight <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<2 \text{kg})$</tex> human-following control scheme based on the fusion of an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) and Force Sensing Resistors (FSR). The system utilizes a distributed control architecture with Field Oriented Control (FOC) in-wheel motors. An adaptive gain Attitude and Heading Reference System (AHRS) algorithm is proposed to address IMU drift during dynamic walking, while a multi-source fusion strategy combines gait characteristics with handrail interaction forces to achieve compliant motion control. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves a gait cycle identification accuracy of 98% and a response latency of 24ms, with hardware costs only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 5 {\%} - 2 0 {\%}$</tex> of traditional solutions, demonstrating significant engineering value.

title: Apple Grading Based on Bagging Ensemble of Ordinal Evidential Neural Network
authors: Yiyang Wang, Wenxiao Guan, Shuhui Bi, Liyao Ma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Apple grading is essentially an ordinal classification problem, in which adjacent grades exhibit natural continuity and boundary uncertainty. To improve the efficiency and performance of automatic apple grading, this study adopts a Bagging ensemble method based on ordinal evidential neural network. Within an ordinal framework, this paper designs an ordinal evidential neural network based on Dempster–Shafer evidence theory to output mass functions, thereby effectively characterizing ordinal relationships and grading uncertainty. Meanwhile, a Bagging ensemble strategy is adopted to further improve the grading performance of the model. Experimental results on the Red Fuji apple dataset show that the proposed Bagging–ORENN model achieves an average accuracy of 89.19%.

title: Design and Development of 8 - Legged Robot Using Theo Jansen Mechanism
authors: K. Venkatesh, B Sudhakara Rao, Shaik Bazani, Durga Bhavani P, Jhansi Rani K, P.S.V. Ramana Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In rough and rocky paths, wheels have been shown to be ineffective and legged robots using the Theo Jansen mechanism is a better solution to enhanced walking vehicles. This process allows the robots to overcome curbs, climb stairs and move through places where wheeled vehicles cannot reach. Theo Jansen mechanism is one of the most essential approaches since it does not need the microprocessor control or a sophisticated system of actuators. Rather, it uses pivot joints to connect the links to convert the rotational movement of a crank into a walking movement just like that of animals. The links proportions are also calculated to maximize the foot linearity during half a crank rotation to assure a smooth forward movement. In the other half-cycle, the foot is raised to an agreed height and brought back to the initial position and the process is repeated. Fitting two linkages to the crank one-half cycle in phase difference enables the vehicle frame to be kept parallel to the ground during motion. Hazardous material handling, mine field cleaning and the ability to secure the high-risk zones without risking the lives of personnel are big applications of this technology. Mechanical spider robots would be of great use to military, law enforcement, Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) units and even to private security firms since they are good at stair climbing and going over obstacles which are difficult to the wheeled or tracked vehicles.

title: An Intelligent Ventilation Constant-Pressure Control Method Based on State Awareness and a Hybrid Optimization Algorithm
authors: Guang Yang, Zhongshun Su
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the nonlinear, time-varying, and disturbance-sensitive characteristics of intelligent ventilation constant-pressure systems under complex operating conditions, this paper proposes a hybrid optimization method, IPSO-BAS-SA, for fuzzy PID parameter tuning. First, the ventilation constant-pressure plant is equivalently linearized, and a unified second-order transfer-function model is established to provide a consistent mathematical basis for controller design and simulation comparison. Second, a fuzzy PID controller with the error and the rate of change of error as inputs is constructed, and a hybrid algorithm composed of an improved particle swarm optimization algorithm, chaotic-map initialization, beetle antennae search local refinement, and a simulated annealing acceptance mechanism is employed to optimize the key parameters. Finally, under a 50 Pa step input, a 100 Pa variable-condition step input, and pulse disturbance conditions, the proposed optimized fuzzy PID controller is compared with a conventional PID controller and a manually tuned fuzzy PID controller. In addition, ablation experiments are conducted to verify the effectiveness of each component in the proposed optimization framework. The results show that the proposed method outperforms the comparison methods in response speed, overshoot suppression, settling time, and disturbance recovery capability, demonstrating superior control performance and robustness.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Work-Life Balance on Job Performance Among Women Police Personnel in Chennai City
authors: Nithya M, Kavitha M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Work-life balance (WLB) has emerged as a critical concern in the high-pressure occupations like policing, especially for women who often tends to face dual responsibilities at work and the home. In India, specifically in the urban settings like Chennai, where women police personnel tends to the encounter unique challenges in the balancing personal and the professional demands. There is a lack of an empirical data that examines if work-life balance tends to the affect the job performance of the women police officers in the metropolitan regions. This study aims to the address this gap by the assessment of the relationship between the WLB and the job performance among women in the Chennai police force. A mixed-methods approach was employed. Primary data were collected through the structured questionnaires (n=150) and the semi-structured interviews (n=20). The WLB was assessed using the Work-Life Balance Scale (WLB-S) and the job performance was measured using a modified version of the Police Job Performance Scale. Quantitative data were analyzed using SPSS, applying regression analysis, correlation, and the ANOVA. Sentiment analysis of qualitative data was performed using NVivo. The study helps to the find a moderate to the strong positive correlation (r = 0.68, p < 0.01) between the WLB and the job performance. Key WLB components such as flexible shifts, spousal support, and the access to the childcare services significantly influence job performance indicators like punctuality, task completion, and the stress management. Regression analysis shows that 52% of the variance in the job performance is explained by WLB-related factors.

title: Latency-Budgeted Terminal–Edge Collaborative Video Analytics with Adaptive Masking and Content-Aware Encoding
authors: Lixin Jin, Kangli Zhao, Penglin Dai, Xincao Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Real-time edge video analytics is critical for latency-sensitive applications but remains challenging under dynamic video content and fluctuating wireless bandwidth. Existing terminal–edge collaborative systems often rely on static offloading, fragile Region of Interest (RoI) extraction, or coarse-grained compression, leading to unstable latency or inefficient bandwidth usage. This paper proposes SCORE, an adaptive terminal–edge collaborative framework built on a batch-based processing model. SCORE integrates three tightly coupled modules: a Latency-Aware Keyframe Scheduler that dynamically selects keyframes under strict latency budgets, a Adaptive Foreground Masker that adaptively refines RoI extraction based on detection feedback, and a Joint Resolution–Quality Optimizer that jointly optimizes spatial resolution and encoding quality for RoI transmission. Experiments on a real hardware testbed with traffic surveillance datasets show that SCORE achieves up to 2.8× higher throughput than state-of-the-art methods while maintaining comparable detection accuracy and significantly reducing bandwidth consumption.

title: Computational Engineering Based on the Intersection of Data Protection and Cybersecurity
authors: Vratika Singh, Bindu Jindal, Sujata Dahiya, Pranav Ranga, Anil Vats, Ankita Saini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: As our world becomes more digital and data-driven, the protection of personal and sensitive information has taken center stage. From healthcare records to financial transactions, organizations today are under increasing pressure to not only secure their systems but also comply with evolving data protection laws. This study explores the intersection of cybersecurity and data protection, showing how these two fields must work together to ensure both technical safety and legal accountability. Drawing on real-world data across industries like healthcare, finance, and education, the research analyzes how factors like compliance scores, cybersecurity investment, and breach response impact the scale and cost of cyber incidents. The results are clear: organizations with stronger compliance frameworks consistently face lower breach costs and recover more effectively. The study highlights the need for a unified approach—where legal awareness and technical defense are part of the same strategy—to build digital trust in an increasingly connected world.

title: Architecting Ethical and Scalable Artificial Intelligence Systems: A Secure Deployment Model with Real-World Benchmarking
authors: Arvinder Singh, Mandeep Singh, Manpreet Kaur, Vikas Attri, Sakshi Chauhan, Rajinder Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The sheer rampant growth of artificial intelligence (AI) systems in the safety-critical, regulated, and large-scale areas of infrastructures has enhanced the demand of deployment models that are secure, ethical compliant, and scalable in terms of operation. The proposed research aims to develop a layered AI system architecture with verifiable privacy, federated orchestration, and explanation of decision-making as the first principles, as opposed to an afterthought. The framework consists of three modular parts, namely a privacy preserving computational substrate based on holomorphic encryption (HE) and differential privacy (DP), a distributed trust model that is enforced by a zero knowledge proofs (ZKP) and decentralized authentication protocols, and an ethical governance layer that includes policy-aware execution and explainable AI (XAI) reasoning. In contrast to the isolated sandbox setup, this architecture was tried in the realistic environment of a deployment using the representative of the domain data, such as OpenAQ sensor telemetry, MIMIC-III health logs, or smart energy grid datasets. The simulations were performed on 60-node Docker Swarm cluster using orchestrated adversarial attacks such as input reconstruction, consensus disruption, and metadata inference. Based on empirical evidence, the results indicate the latency stabilization at less than 140 ms, the cryptographic computation overhead at less than 22 percent and the privacy leakage reduction at more than 83 percent compared to centralized conventional baselines. These results confirm the correctness of implementing scalable AI solutions and not compromising ethical outcomes, regulatory guidance, and computational performance. This is the stepping block of future AI infrastructures that will have to be at scale, constrained, and in a similar spirit as the human-centric values.

title: Mindfulness Meditation in Workplace: Satisfaction and Harmonious Employee-Employer Relationships
authors: Aparna Vajpayee, Emery Bokey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This research investigates the transformative impact of mindfulness practices within organizational contexts, focusing on their influence on employee satisfaction, organizational culture, and overall well-being. Mindfulness, encompassing both individual and collective dimensions, is explored for its role in fostering adaptability and mental resilience in navigating life's challenges. The study employs a comprehensive research design, drawing on both qualitative and quantitative methods. Survey questionnaires are utilized to gather data from a diverse sample within organizational settings. The research design incorporates a multilevel approach, simultaneously exploring individual and collective mindfulness to provide a nuanced understanding of their effects. The measurements encompass a range of factors, including individual well-being, organizational culture, and employee satisfaction. The findings reveal the measurable and tangible impact of mindfulness practices. Individual and collective mindfulness are linked to positive outcomes at various levels, emphasizing their significance in shaping organizational dynamics. Clear connections are established between mindfulness and enhanced adaptability, mental resilience, and overall well-being. The study emphasizes the need for ongoing multilevel mindfulness research to comprehensively grasp the implications of these practices in organizational psychology and behavior.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Analytics Framework for Urban Heat Island (UHI) Mitigation
authors: Sana Alam, Manshi Chauhan, Ishaan Saxena, Akshaya Shukla, Lakshita Aggarwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The UHI effect refers to cities being hotter than their rural counterparts, with a corresponding uptick in risk for health, energy, and food systems. Existing strategies reactively respond to events, while urban resilience situates predictive intervention on a data-driven assessment. This paper presents an AI-based framework that integrates multi-source datasets: satellite imagery, weather data, geospatial data, and socio-demographics, to create a predictive model. It considers the biological activities taking place around us, due to which the urban lands are more prone. The latest state-of-the-art shows the biological and chemical effects that become the primitive reasons for the same. Machine learning algorithms such as XGBoost, produces the best achievable accuracy for predicting land surface temperature. Causality can be explicitly touted using indicators from an Explainable AI module (SHAP), which quantifies the contribution of local drivers of UHI intensity using weights. The framework can also serve as a simulator where planners can model different interventions, such as green infrastructure, or reflectance, to assess impacts prior to implementing those interventions. They integrate prediction, explanation and simulation into a decision support system to create new and equitable racing routes for cities to improve resilience to climate change in terms of urban planners.

title: Data-Driven Insights in Consumer and Workforce Analytics Using AI and Natural Language Processing
authors: Ajay Narayan Shukla, Meghna Sharma, Raj Kumar, Ulugmurodova Nargiza, Richa Vijay, Harjeet Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have become major pillars in modern enterprise intelligence - transforming the way in which organizations analyze and act on large amounts of information. In this paper, data-driven methods to provide actionable insights through consumer behavior, workforce data by AI and Natural Language Processing models are reviewed. It explains how sentiment analysis, predictive modeling and machine learning enabled HR analytics plays an important role in improving HR decision making, engagement and productivity. The proposed framework addresses the structured and unstructured processing for strategic planning in the context of marketing and HR. A thorough review of the state-of-the-art available studies shows that real-time monitoring of the plume, feedback assimilation, and performance prediction are possible based on AI-based analytics. Recommendations for impacts suggest the development of more transparency and better operational efficiency and market competitiveness due to AI-NLP implementation. Future directions are multimodal forms of analytics that fuse text, speech and behavioral information to improve how a company understands and grammatical learning throughout omers and organizational ecosystems.

title: Integrated Power Line Fault Detection Using Arduino With GSM
authors: Krosuri Lakshmi Revathi, Varagani Pavan Roshan Krishna, Thanniru Sudarshan, Rajulapati Dhanush
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Transmission Line Protection is a major problem in today’s power systems. In the modern era, more than 80 percent of the power transmission faults occur because of the transmission lines. In this Identifying the fault’s source location is very time consuming process and increased risk in the equipment damage. Sometimes due to natural phenomena the transmission line may be cut and these hanging electrical wires have caused multiple deaths. Identifying the fault location detection will reduce the time required for repairs also and minimize deaths. With the help of GSM Modem we can identify the fault location detection. The system also integrates fire detection sensor (IR sensor) and monitors the resistance variation to detect short circuits. A capacitor is added to measure the impedance, aiding in detection of the open-circuit faults. Transmission Line status displays on the LCD Board and if there is a fault an SMS alert is sent to predefined numbers through GSM modem, ensuring the notification. This is a low cost and efficient solution which provides better safety near power transmission systems.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Predictive Maintenance of Power Transformers Using CAI and EFRI Metrics
authors: C V P R Prasad, Shyam Kishore G, Parvathy K, N.Jyothsna, Senthilkumar Aandi, Anuradha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: A machine intelligence foundation for predictive perpetuation of capacity transformers utilizing CAI and EFRI versification is proposed to address postponed discovery and damaging unplanned outages in new grids. Motivated by assorted analyst like SCADA telemetry, Condition-Aware Indicators (CAI) calculations, Frequency Response Analysis (FRA)/EFRI spectral sweeps, and warm metaphors, the objective search out fuse multimodal signals to enhance early-collapse discovery, staying useful existence guess, and cost-knowledgeable decision-making. The Multimodal Cost-Aware Transformer for Predictive Maintenance (MCA-TransPdM) model joins windowed multimodal inputs and engages parallel encoders (Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM), Transformer) whose embeddings are melded into a meta-classifier; strength is enhanced by way of opposing preparation, denoising, modality disguising, and BRITS-style pretraining for uneven samples, while a conclusion-cost piece selects deployment thresholds. Evaluation on classical datasets manifests forceful performance: AUC ≈0.990, PR-AUC ≈0.952, RUL MAE ≈6.3 days, RMSE ≈ 8.1 days, and anticipated annual cost diminished to ≈ 13,396,200 INR at the optimum beginning versus ≈21,513,600 INR (SoTA-2024) and ≈ 43,707,800 INR (telemetry-only). Implementation analyses containing preprocessing, the earth's features-aware face, and approach people present at event are given to support reproducibility and integration accompanying existent advantage-management schemes, and manipulator-in-the-loop confirmation tests. These results indicate concrete functional harvests, fewer dishonest alarms, and former interferences, supporting arranged field pilots, routine recalibration, and connected to the internet incessant education to ensure arrangement eagerness across various sites

title: Enhancing Healthcare Waste Management Through Deep Learning-Based Classification
authors: Gayathri G, Visaka P, Chitra P, Priya Thiagarajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This project explores the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL), to improve medical waste classification in healthcare settings. Classifying medical waste manually can pose significant health risks, result in inconsistent sorting, and heighten environmental damage due to human errors and inefficiencies. Automating this process ensures precise segregation, minimizes health hazards, enhances compliance with regulations, and fosters safer and more sustainable waste-management practices. In comparison, a test was conducted for three deep learning models: Vanilla Neural Network, EfficientNet-B0, and EfficientNet-B3, using a set of 2,769 images categorized into five types of medical waste. The aim is to classify two main types of waste: sharp waste, such as syringes and tweezers, and infectious waste, such as gloves, gauze, and masks. All images underwent preprocessing, including resizing and normalization, in addition to augmentation techniques. Among the models tested, EfficientNet-B3 performed well, with an accuracy of 98.44%. EfficientNet-B0 also performed well, with an accuracy of 97.92%. EfficientNet-B0 has high efficiency using minimal computational resources, and the Vanilla Neural Network reached 97%. Based on these results, a prototype system was developed for real-time classification of medical waste. This system improves the waste segregation efficiency, reduces contamination, and enhances the overall waste disposal process. Future improvements to this system include incorporating user feedback, expanding classification categories, and improving integration into healthcare settings. This project shows how AI-driven solutions can significantly contribute to safer and more efficient medical waste management, help to protect healthcare environments, and support workers in ensuring proper waste disposal.
