title: Blockchain for Secure Management of International Student Exchange Records: Opportunities, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: RajKumar, Sayam Mattoo, Vaishnavi Sharma, Krishika Thakur, Harshit Tomar, Archit Khanna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The blockchain technology is becoming a standout solution to the international student exchange records that must be managed safely. The Diploma forgery, cross-border verification, inefficiency and lack of interoperability and high administrative costs are some of the challenges associated with traditional centralized systems. In this review, the author evaluates the available literature on blockchain-based learning models, pointing to such technologies as Ethereum, Hyperledger Fabric, and models based on NFTs. The study is conducted in a systematic methodology, which starts with the analysis of the topic, literature review and the identification of past outputs, moving on to explore future opportunities and make a comparative analysis. The results indicate that blockchain increases credential management transparency, efficiency, and security, and tackles fraudulent behavior and increases trust in academic mobility. Nevertheless, there are still challenges like scalability, obstacles to adoption, and regulatory challenges. The future prospects are on hybrid blockchain frameworks, privacy verifying blockchain frameworks and the global standardisation of credential frameworks. It is summarized in the review that blockchain has a great potential to improve the management of academic records, and it is a potential avenue to a secure, decentralized, and internationally accepted model of international student exchanges.

title: Secure Link Scheduling for UAV Swarms: From Perspective of Covert Communication
authors: Li Yang, Xufeng Zhan, Jia Yu, Jiguo Yu, Dongxiao Yu
abstract: The link scheduling is to determine which links should be scheduled at what times, each of which is a (sender, receiver) communication pair used to realize wireless transmission of a message. It is an appealing solution to fulfill the resource-constrained and latency requirements of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms. However, most existing link scheduling algorithms primarily focus on the throughput or latency in wireless communication, neglecting the protection against the eavesdropping attack due to the openness of UAV swarms, thereby leading to unsafe message dissemination. To address this issue, we consider integrating the covert communication into the link scheduling by concealing the very existence of message transmission. We first present a successive interference cancellation-based link diversity partition (SIC-LDP) algorithm in the context of the physical interference model, to maximize the number of concurrently transmitting links in a single time round, also called the maximum link scheduling (MLS) issue. Then, using a friendly jammer, we further propose a covertness-aware SIC-LDP algorithm (named SIC-CLDP) to resolve the MLS, as well as to defend against the eavesdropping attack. Both SIC-LDP and SIC-CLDP algorithms are proven to have constant approximation ratios. Next, implementing SIC-LDP and SIC-CLDP repeatedly can minimize the number of time rounds scheduling n participating links, termed the shortest link scheduling (SLS) issue, with an approximation ratio of O(ln n). Lastly, theoretical proofs and simulation results demonstrate the correctness, covertness, and efficiency of our proposed algorithms under the eavesdropping attack.

title: Deduplication-while-Storage: A New Encrypted Deduplication Storage Paradigm
authors: Yufei Yao, Zhongyun Hua, Mingyang Song, Yifeng Zheng, Zhaoquan Gu, Qing Liao
abstract: Ensuring data confidentiality while achieving efficient deduplication is critical for cloud storage services. Existing works on encrypted data deduplication operate under a deduplication-before-storage (DbS) model, identifying duplicates only during the data uploading phase. They require the owners of duplicate data or additional key servers to assist in the data uploading phase for enabling encrypted deduplication. This makes it hard to balance data security and deduplication effectiveness. This paper introduces a new paradigm named deduplication-while-storage (DwS), which differs from the existing DbS model by enabling dynamic deduplication throughout the entire storage lifecycle, activated whenever owners of potential duplicate data are available. This paradigm aims to significantly boost deduplication ratio without compromising the security of outsourced data. To instantiate the DwS paradigm, we develop SGX-DwS, a shielded storage system that utilizes Intel SGX to support encrypted data deduplication. SGX-DwS builds on an ownership-based encryption mechanism that enables secure equality checks on ciphertexts with the cooperation of owners of duplicate data. It adopts a two-phase deduplication strategy. The first phase processes duplicates during uploading if a previous uploader of duplicate data is online and willing to assist (with incentives provided for participation), while the second phase addresses remaining duplicates during the storage phase when owners of the potential duplicates are simultaneously online and cooperative. Our experimental results demonstrate that SGX-DwS achieves high deduplication ratio with modest overhead, even in large-scale cloud storage environments. Specifically, the improvement in deduplication ratio achieved by SGX-DwS is up to 92.49% as compared to that in the DbS model.

title: Wearable-Based Emotion Recognition Using Electrocardiogram and Galvanic Skin Response and Instrumentation Audit: A Systematic Review
authors: Ayse Kosal Bulbul, Muhammad Irfan, Maria K. Jaakkola, Abdulhamit Subasi, Riku Klén
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Simple and unobtrusive wearables, particularly watches and wristbands, now provide continuous monitoring with no inconvenience and are widely used by elderly people. Effective emotion recognition relies not only on advances in models but also on methods for signal acquisition and specification. In response to this imperative, we performed a systematic review of emotion recognition utilising wearable physiological signals, focusing on two practical modalities: electrocardiography (ECG), which indicates cardiac dynamics and emotional arousal, and galvanic skin response (GSR), a measure of electrodermal activity (EDA) reflecting sympathetic nervous system activation and conveniently recordable at the wrist, which reflects sympathetic nervous system activation and can be conveniently recorded at the wrist. We examined four prominent publishers (ScienceDirect, SpringerLink, Taylor & Francis Online, IEEE Xplore) and identified 549 papers; after excluding 90 review articles and 75 papers focused solely on electroencephalography (EEG), 384 were subjected to thorough screening. After excluding 20 studies focused solely on stress and 316 papers without ECG/GSR data, 48 studies utilising machine learning were identified (2019–2025). Traditional models like support vector machines remain prevalent, though deep learning techniques, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and hybrids, are more effective at discerning complex temporal patterns. Simultaneously, we conduct an instrumentation and measurement (I&M) audit on commonly utilised datasets. Protocols and sampling rates are generally documented; however, essential measurement details are frequently absent: electrode type and positioning, skin–electrode impedance, signal quality indices (SQIs), calibration, repeatability, and interdevice comparability. These limitations hinder equitable performance attribution (sensing versus modelling), limit uncertainty assessment, and complicate hardware transfer and cross-study evaluation. Standardising I&M metadata in public releases, when combined with AI evaluation practices, improves comparability and dependability in healthcare predictions based on wearable physiological information.

title: Enhancing Credit Scoring Accuracy Using Machine Learning and Alternative Data Sources in a Credit Card Company in Bahrain
authors: Mohamed Al-Shakhoori, Abdulrahman A. Shakh, Minwir Al-Shammari
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the effects of introducing alternative internal variables into credit scoring models to improve forecast accuracy and interpretability. The ML models were assessed using cross validation and a held-out test set, with performance measured by the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC), a performance metric for binary classification models that assesses a model’s ability to distinguish between positive and negative classes across all possible thresholds of AUC-ROC and related classification metrics. The findings show that models integrating alternative data consistently outperform those using only traditional variables. The Random Forest and CatBoost classifiers continued to be top performers, achieving AUC ROC scores of approximately 1 in the enhanced scenario. To improve interpretability and identify the leading causes of default risk, the study applied SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis. SHAP values are a method for explaining the output of any machine learning model. It employs a game-theoretic approach that measures each player’s contribution to the outcome. The overall findings highlight the importance of incorporating internal behavioral data to improve default prediction accuracy. This study contributes to the growing body of research in credit risk modeling, offering valuable insights and measurable evidence for credit organizations seeking to update and modernize their scoring methodologies and systems.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Dynamic State Abstractions
authors: Shripad Joshi, Bhavana Santosh Pansare, Anandakumar Haldorai, Donamol Joseph, Shaziya Islam, D. Vetrithangam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Generally, the performance of RL in generalized state space and the non-adaptive modes of representation in complex environments are poor. The current paper can offer a solution to the problem by coming up with a dynamic state abstraction scheme according to which the agent can revise its internal representations by the changing task structure and environmental feedback. Unlike traditional methods that rely on fixed or non-adaptive abstractions, the proposed method jointly optimizes a learnable policy network and encoder with smoothness regularization to maintain temporal coherence of abstract states. Long experiments in benchmark environments demonstrate that the dynamic abstraction approach significantly outperforms common baselines, such as DQN, Static Abstraction RL, and Latent Space PPO in terms of task accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(89.3 \%)$</tex>, generalization <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(83.1 \%)$</tex>, and sample efficiency (81.9%). The results of the experiment testify to the merits of adaptive abstractions for faster convergence and stability. The contribution shows the promise of dynamic representation learning towards developing more efficient and generalizable RL agents.

title: Space Motion Modeling and Control System Optimization Design of Unmanned Underwater Vehicles Based on Comprehensive Simulation
authors: Jia-Lin Niu, Jia-Hao Fang, Meng Wen, Ya-Nan Niu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Based on the physical and motion characteristics of typical Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (UUVs), mathematical models for kinematics and dynamics were established. The spatial motion equations were then derived through mathematical methods, which effectively characterize the multidimensional motion parameters of the UUV. Subsequently, linearized models and their corresponding transfer functions for the pitch, yaw, and roll channels of the vehicle were obtained via mathematical approaches. PID control laws were designed for each channel, resulting in a stable PID control system for the UUV. Finally, simulations were conducted to verify the feasibility of the design method and the stability of the control system.

title: Designing Adaptive User Interfaces for Elderly Users in Smart Homes: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Zainab Almahafdha, Shaikha Hasan, Wael Elmedany
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: As the elderly population increases, the adoption of smart home technologies to enhance elderly lives has gained significant attention. However, traditional user interfaces (UIs) in smart home environments do not fit with the needs of older users having poor vision and decreased cognition. This emphasizes the need for an adaptive user interface (AUI) that provides a solution for elderly problems by fitting the design to their needs and requirements. The aim of this study is to examine the current research trends and design principles for designing adaptive user interfaces specifically for elderly users in smart home environments. A systematic literature review (SLR) following PRISMA guidelines will be conducted to identify existing user interface design principles, and propose future design best practices for elderly users. The study synthesizes the findings from peer-reviewed articles which were published from 2014 to 2024. The results of this SLR identify key design considerations, challenges, and suggestions for enhancing elderly user experience and accessibility in smart homes.

title: Design and Launch of Solid Propellant Reusable Rocket
authors: Reel Mohamed Ahmed, Yousef Salman, Karan Anoop Whabi, Sharul Sham Dol, Mohammed Alavi, Ahmad Al Ramahi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: $A B S T R A C T$ -The purpose of this project is to design and build a solid propellant-powered reusable rocket using repurposed and easy to obtain materials, with a safe landing mechanism, emphasizing reusability, recyclable materials and cost effectiveness. Several factors are the success rate of the rocket, along with the efficiency of the propulsion system, which was a key driver in the success of this project. The available propulsion motor was an Estes B6-4 motor. Hence, the propulsion system influenced the design and choice of materials for the rocket. The rocket model was simulated using the OpenRocket simulator, a software which gave rapid estimates of the expected flight phases and characteristics allowing quick rectifications of the rocket design through testing for different configurations to achieve the optimum results. The simulation results closely matched results from the launch where an apogee altitude of 59 meters, a maximum velocity of $31.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and a safe landing in the span of 16.6 s was achieved. These results marked the success of the rocket launch and imply the concept of reuseable model rocketry supported by easily adaptable softwares like OpenRocket

title: FarmSense Pro: A Data-Driven Approach to Crop Yield and Price Estimation
authors: Anupriya K, Satheesh Kumar D, Bala Murugan K, Bala Nithin RK, Gunal Christ A, Praveen A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The economic development and food security of every nation require farmers and policymakers to forecast future crop yields and market prices through systematic analysis of multiple data sets. The research project presents Farm Sense Pro as an advanced web-based platform which scientists can use to predict crop yields and market prices through the application of machine learning methods. The platform uses agricultural data and environmental data and market data to help farmers make better decisions about their crop production and sales activities. For example, when predicting crop production, several variables are considered, including soil type, amount and timing of rainfall, temperature and weather conditions, amount of fertilizer applied, irrigation methods, area of the country are farming, and time left until the crop is harvested. For predicting the selling price per crop, the platform will consider a demand index, supply index, seasonal variations in price, previous price at the market, temperature and amount of rain in a particular geographic area, and month. Recent machine learning algorithms, including Linear Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest and XGBoost are used in developing predictive models in order to improve the accuracy of predicting crop production and selling price. The platform was developed using Python and Stream lit to create an interactive, user-friendly web-based dashboard that allows users to enter data, generate predictions and visualize predictions through charts and analytics. Additionally, the platform contains support materials such as insights about the market and seasonality outlooks on prices to help users understand the seasonal price swings of their crops.

title: Preserving What, Removing Where: Dual-Stream Beneficial Feature Pre-Debiasing for HOI Detection
authors: Fang Li, Lihua Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Human-object interaction detection is a crucial task in scene understanding, aiming to localize both people and objects and infer their interactive relationships in images. However, existing visual models often rely excessively on background information, leading to biased interaction predictions. To address this issue, this paper proposes a causal intervention framework, Dual-Stream Benefit Feature Pre-bias Removal (DSB-HOI), for the dual-stream HOI architecture, integrating a detector and a context mining module. This method requires no retraining and is plug-and-play, eliminating bias solely through feature adjustments during inference. In the factual scenario, the model makes predictions based on the visual features of people and objects, as well as semantic labels and background information. In the intervention scenario, we retain only the background information while setting the visual features and semantic labels of people and objects as unobservable. By comparing the two scenarios, we effectively isolate background interference, preserve the primary contribution of human and object visual features to the interaction, eliminate background bias, and simultaneously enhance the interpretability of the model’s decisions. Experimental results on the HICO-DET benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of the DSB-HOI method, which substantially enhances human-object interaction detection performance and quantifies the dual-stream architecture’s dependence on background information.

title: Leveraging Generative AI to Advance Technology-Based Startup Incubation and Societal Progress
authors: Mohd Tabrej Alam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The rapid development of Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) based on foundation models (GPT-4, DALL E, and Claude) is capable of altering entrepreneurial ecosystems with evolving content creation, predictive analytics, and dynamic mentoring. Although the Gen AI application is rapidly expanding in all sectors, its practical absorption in the context of technology-orientated business incubators (TBIs) is an insufficiently developed area. In this study, this gap is covered by using empirical research to explore the impact of Gen AI on improving one in the quality of startup selection, improvement of innovation capability, and shortening of time-to-market in incubation settings. On the basis of the Technology–Organisation–Environment (TOE) framework and Entrepreneurial Ecosystem Theory, a quantitative research design is used by referencing data provided by the incubator managers and the founders of the emerging enterprises, which operate in various industries. With the partial least square structural equation modelling (PLS-SEM) and then robustness checks done using the two-stage least squares (2SLS) regression, the results support the substantial beneficial impacts of Gen AI adoption on startup performance outcomes. The most significant shift was AI-assisted mentoring, suggesting that large-scale skill development may be feasible. Findings provide both theoretical implications for the application of Gen AI capabilities in relation to stages of the incubation lifecycle and practical implications for incubators, entrepreneurs, and policymakers. This study establishes Gen AI as a business strength in terms of promoting inclusive, sustainable and innovation-based entrepreneurial ecosystems.

title: A Big Data Architecture for Smart Bus System
authors: Mohd Azri Rosli, Nor Azuana Ramli, Nor Izzati Jaini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: As urban transportation systems advance toward intelligent and data-driven operations, integrating big data technologies is vital for enhancing efficiency and decisionmaking. This paper proposes a comprehensive big data architecture for smart bus systems, focusing on the methodological aspects of data processing and analytics integration. The proposed framework covers the complete data pipeline, including data assessment, exploration, ingestion, storage using data lakes and data warehouses, preparation, computation, and analytics. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based model is incorporated within the analytics layer to support predictive insights for operational optimization. The study also evaluates cloud implementation in terms of cost and scalability, emphasizing the benefits of distributed and elastic computing for large-scale transportation data. A comparative analysis with existing architecture highlights the proposed framework’s modularity and adaptability. Although no empirical validation is presented, architecture provides a robust foundation for future implementation and evaluation in developing intelligent transportation systems.

title: Enhanced Smart Retail Decision Through Sentiment Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Yoganand Sharma, Ankita Dadhich, Minal Sharma, D. S. Rao, Reema Ajmera, Sangeeta Soni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Retail business is quick to digitalize with the adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and big data technologies, which increase the amount of customer-generated data the number of online reviews, ratings, posts on social media, feedback replies exponentially. This huge unstructured textual information contains opinions, feelings, degree of satisfaction, and buying preferences of the customers and it is a valuable asset to be used in intelligent retail decision-making, but the problem is how to extract important information out of such data is also a complicated issue. Sentiment analysis also refers to opinion mining has been a lucrative remedy to the challenge as it works towards recognizing sentiment polarity, affects, and sentiments in customer-feedback text automatically. The paper is a review on the topic of sentiment analysis methods, and how they can help improve the process of smart retail decisions and includes a discussion of how the traditional machine learning models (Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machines, and Logistic Regression) have evolved over the years to more advanced deep learning methods (Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and transformer-based architectures like BERT and RoBERTa). It talks about data sources, preprocessing approaches, feature extraction approaches, evaluation approaches applied in retail sentiment analytics and mentions key uses of such analytics, including demand forecasting, product development, dynamic pricing, recommendation systems, brand monitoring, and customer retention. Other major issues such as the detection of sarcasm, multi-language data processing, detection of fake reviews, data imbalance, privacy issues, and the issue of realtime deployment are also discussed. Lastly, the paper identifies future research topics, which include multimodal sentiment analysis, explainable AI, emotion-aware analytics, and realtime intelligent retail systems, concluding that sentiment analysis based on ML is a core element in proactive, customercentric, and data-driven smart retail systems.

title: Skin Lesion Classification for Skin Cancer Using MobileNetV2
authors: E. Loganathan, K.S. Chowmiya, N. Prakash, J.K. Kanimozhi, P. Anitha, R. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This study focuses on building a dependable skin lesion classification model to support early skin cancer diagnosis. The work compares the performance of a standard CNN architecture the MobileNetV2 model when applied to dermoscopic images. Two independent groups were designed for the evaluation. Group 1 involved a CNN trained with traditional convolutional operations, whereas Group 2 used MobileNetV2 incorporating depthwise-separable filters and transfer learning to enhance feature extraction of lesion patterns. In the Kaggle dataset, 2,500 dermoscopic images were split for training, validation and testing. Group 1 achieved accuracy of 89.27% using MobileNetV2 outperforming Group 2 using CNN model that was inferior in performance with significant margin. When researchers measured the performances of the two techniques they found out that the difference was statistically significant. In other words, it was found that MobileNetV2 was more efficient in classifying skin lesion images. The MobileNetV2 architecture was found to be more efficient, reliable, and stable than the conventional CNN model for skin lesion classification. MobileNetV2 can be utilized in early skin cancer screening which can also be used for realtime dermatology-assisted systems.

title: Location-Based Adaptive Grouping Algorithm for Federated Learning
authors: Jiachen Wang, Donghang Duan, Qi Zhou, Xu Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: In wireless federated learning networks, high communication overhead remains a critical bottleneck. While existing location-based clustering methods mitigate long-distance transmission, they typically employ fixed grouping strategies that overlook the heterogeneity of data distributions among local clients, leading to suboptimal training accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a Location-Based Adaptive Grouping Algorithm (ADBR) to jointly optimize communication energy and model accuracy. Specifically, we design a sliding window mechanism on clients to detect real-time performance fluctuations and trigger regrouping requests. On the server side, a batched re-grouping strategy is introduced to process requests in parallel, thereby reducing system coordination overhead. Theoretical analysis and experimental results demonstrate that ADBR significantly improves model accuracy and reduces communication costs while ensuring convergence and security.

title: IEEE Copyright and Consent Form
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)

title: Study of Dynamic behaviour of the Linear Susceptibility on Pulse Propagation in Fiber Optics Communication
authors: Aayushi Mehta, A.A. Koser, Bhavana Kshirsagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This paper examines the impact of vibrant behaviour of linear susceptibility on the propagation of pulse in fiber optics communication. Our method to construct the adaptive response of the medium is mathematical, computational, and then we consider temporal variations in the linear susceptibility may influence the pulse modulation, group velocity dispersion (GVD) and pulse broadening. Some of the results are that even minor changes in time-dependence susceptibility affect the refractive index profile and broadening of the pulse.

title: Technological Analysis on Exploring Emotional Intelligence Through Cultural Lenses
authors: Emery Bokey, Aparna Vajpayee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The researches have shown vital importance of emotional intelligence in various hemisphere of life. The proposed research has an emphasis to compare the emotional intelligence levels of people from India and Africa as well as between athletes and non-athletes. It will be interesting to study the level of emotional intelligence in athlete and non-athlete among two cultural group to understand the impact of culture in shaping emotional intelligence. Study comprises 50-50 participant who are athlete and non-athletes from India and Africa for a comparative research approach. Individuals between the ages of 18 and 28 completed tests of emotional intelligence using established procedures. Two-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was used to analyses the data in order to determine how nationality and athletic status interacted with emotional intelligence scores. The results of the study have shown impact of culture in shaping the emotional intelligence. The cultural impact of Africa has more influence in shaping the emotional intelligence as compare to Indian athlete and non-athlete. Furthermore, across both nationalities, athletes scored higher on emotional intelligence tests than non-athletes. Nevertheless, there were no noteworthy interactions between athletic status and nationality.

title: Patchwork-Based Audio-Visual Watermarking Using ADPCM and Adaptive Embedding
authors: Chandani Sharma, Muhammad Khalid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This research work proposes a novel approach to audio and visual watermarking leveraging adaptive techniques. These are inspired by current artificial intelligence frameworks with a comparative illustration of existing audio watermarking to a robust and transparent embedding strategy built upon Adaptive Differential Pulse Code Modulation (ADPCM). In the proposed method, watermark embedding is done using a dynamic audio patch, rather than fixed or low-frequency carrier that is generally used. The selected audio segment is divided into frames, and thereafter a pseudo-random key is used to determine the frame positions for watermark insertion. The watermark, is embedded by modifying the Least Significant Bit (LSB) of selected audio samples using post-ADPCM compression. This adaptive watermarking process ensures minimal perceptual distortion and high resilience to compression and tampering. Furthermore, the same methodology can be extended for image and video watermarking by treating image or video frames as analogous data blocks. The proposed system thus offers a unified framework for multi-modal watermarking applicable across audio, image, and video domains.

title: FedAADC: Model Similarity-based Base Station Clustering for Federated Wireless Traffic Prediction
authors: Dongyang Li, Shuo Wang, Xinkun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Wireless traffic prediction plays a central role in enabling efficient resource allocation and performance optimization in next-generation mobile networks by characterizing the spatio-temporal dynamics of future network demands. However, achieving high prediction accuracy while maintaining efficient distributed training remains a significant challenge in large-scale cellular networks, due to pronounced data heterogeneity and limited communication resources. To address this challenge, this paper proposes FedAADC, a model similarity–based clustered federated learning framework for wireless traffic prediction. First, this framework exploits intermediate-layer activation features extracted from local base-station models to characterize model similarity via activation-based kernel alignment. Based on the resulting similarity metrics, base stations are dynamically grouped, enabling delayed and adaptive clustering during the federated training process. Moreover, an attention mechanism is further incorporated to achieve effective intra- and inter-cluster model aggregation. Experimental results demonstrate that FedAADC significantly improves prediction accuracy, reducing the mean squared error (MSE) by up to 23.66% and the mean absolute error (MAE) by up to 11.36% compared with state-of-the-art baselines, while exhibiting high scalability for large-scale distributed base-station networks and meeting practical deployment requirements.

title: AI Based Engineering Framework Addressing Total Quality Management Based on Workplace Management
authors: K.S.Sivakumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the evolving landscape of organizational excellence, Total Quality Management (TQM) emphasizes systematic improvement and performance optimization. However, its effectiveness can be significantly enhanced by incorporating human-centric, values-based approaches. This study explores the integration of Bhagavad Gita’s spiritual teachings—such as Nishkama Karma (selfless action), Dharma (righteous duty), Samatvam (equanimity), and Seva (selfless service)—into the workplace, and their impact on TQM outcomes. Using a mixed-method approach involving employee surveys, organizational performance data, and ANOVA statistical analysis, the research finds that Gita-inspired workplace spirituality positively correlates with improvements in productivity, customer satisfaction, process efficiency, and employee retention. Additionally, it enhances ethical behavior, stress resilience, and emotional intelligence among employees. The findings demonstrate that spiritual engagement is not merely a personal attribute but a strategic asset that aligns inner transformation with external excellence. The study positions workplace spirituality as an essential dimension of sustainable quality management in the 21st century.

title: Web-Based Platform for Efficient User-Vendor Interaction and Marketplace Integration
authors: Vaibhav Dabhade, Kunal Ahire, Namrata Ghuse, Trupti M. Gunjal, Aishwarya S. Ghumare, Sakshi K. Pingle
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This Platform is a comprehensive web-based solution that is intended to facilitate the connection between users and vendors who provide a diverse range of services, including plumbing and electrical work. The platform resolves inefficiencies in the conventional service request process by allowing users to submit tasks and complaints, which vendors can view and approve in real-time. The system also includes a marketplace where users and vendors can list, purchase, sell, rent, donate, or exchange products. The platform provides a secure, scalable, and user-friendly experience by simplifying the handling of service requests through its comprehensive complaint management, task assignment, and product listing modules. The platform incorporates features such as user and vendor registration, complaint listing and management, product listing, and authentication mechanisms. Designed with Flask, SQL Alchemy, and SQLite, this system ensures data integrity, scalability, and usability.

title: Cross-Modality Anatomy Knowledge Distillation for Retinal OCT Layer Segmentation with Limited Labels
authors: Xiang Li, Huihui Fang, Yuning Wang, Cen Chen, Qi Liu, Jinke Wang, Yi Shi, Lixin Duan, Weihua Yang, Yue Liu, Yanwu Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Automatic segmentation of retinal Optical Coher ence Tomography (OCT) images plays a vital role in the early diagnosis and treatment of ocular diseases. However, the scarcity of labeled OCT data poses a significant challenge for existing supervised learning methods. In contrast, Color Fundus Photography (CFP) provides clearer visualization of key anatomical regions, such as the optic disc and cup, making it easier to acquire high-quality annotations. To address the issue of insufficient OCT annotations, we propose a Cross-Modality Anatomy Knowledge Distillation (CMAKD) framework. This method leverages a large amount of annotated CFP data to learn cross-modality structural priors related to retinal layer organization and vessel-associated landmarks, which are then used to regularize the OCT model for retinal layer segmentation. In other words, CFP serves as a source of weak structural priors, providing spatial and contextual guidance for OCT feature learning rather than depth-resolved layer supervision or a direct supervisory signal. To achieve this goal, we design a Domain-invariant Contrastive Learning (DoCL) module to reduce the modality gap between CFP and OCT by aligning their feature distributions. Furthermore, we introduce a Layer-aware Self-ensembling Mean-Teacher (LA-MT) framework to fully exploit unlabeled OCT data and enhance the model’s ability to recognize layered boundary structures. Experimental results on three public datasets — Duke DME, MS, and GOALS — demonstrate that CMAKD significantly improves segmentation performance while reducing the reliance on OCT annotations. Under semi-supervised domain adaptation settings, CMAKD achieves relative performance gains of 17.8% and 8.2% on the Duke DME and MS datasets, respectively, and approaches the performance of fully supervised models on the GOALS dataset. Our code is available at: https://github.com/lixiangcog/CMAKD.

title: Post-Quantum Security for 6G–Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Thomas Zasowski
abstract: Quantum computers have made huge progress in recent years. In this tutorial paper, we explain why they pose a threat to encryption methods that are used in today’s communication networks. As there is the unique opportunity of 6G becoming the first cellular standard where post-quantum security could be considered from the very beginning, we present approaches to achieve post-quantum secure communications. Benefits and drawbacks of quantum key distribution (QKD) and post-quantum cryptography (PQC) are compared. Suggestions are made on how these approaches can be considered resulting in a post-quantum-safe 6G standard.

title: SpecEStop: Self-Supervised Hyperspectral Mixed Noise Removal via Deep Spectral Prior
authors: Ruobing Zhang, Michael K. Ng, Lianru Gao, Marina Ljubenovic, Lina Zhuang
abstract: Hyperspectral remote sensing images often suffer from mixed noise—Gaussian, stripe, and impulse—due to atmospheric interference, solar variability, and sensor imperfections. These noises are typically band-dependent and diverse in distribution, making unified denoising particularly challenging. Existing deep denoising methods rely on clean/noisy pairs, which are unavailable in real-world remote sensing, while traditional approaches require manual tuning and lack adaptability. We propose SpecEStop, a fully self-supervised spectral vector denoising framework that requires only a single noisy HSI for training. Leveraging a novel deep spectral prior, SpecEStop exploits the spectral bias of neural networks, which tend to learn low-frequency (clean) signal components with Gaussian noise before overfitting to non-Gaussian noise ones. An adaptive early stopping strategy halts training before non-Gaussian noise is learned, enabling effective suppression of complex noise patterns. To address remaining Gaussian noise, we purposely design the network architecture to preserve its statistical properties in the latent space, allowing the use of off-the-shelf Gaussian denoisers during inference. Without any clean supervision, SpecEStop achieves effective, stage-wise removal of mixed noise, as validated across diverse real-world scenarios. Code will be released at https://github.com/ruobing-Zhang<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: A Frequency-Domain Vision Accelerator for High-Resolution Object Detection on RAW Data
authors: Haoyan Li, Kangjie Long, Wei Zhou, Xiangyu Zhang, Xin Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: While high-resolution inputs improve recognition performance, they impose significant hardware overhead due to the computational intensity and memory bandwidth demands of conventional image signal processing (ISP). This inefficiency primarily stems from the structural mismatch between pixel-level ISP operations and the semantic information required by back-end neural networks. To address this, we propose an end-to-end RAW-based frequency-domain vision pipeline designed for efficient high-resolution detection. Under normal imaging conditions, the proposed pipeline bypasses the full human-perception-oriented ISP and directly processes RAW sensor data through lightweight channel mapping and a hardware-friendly approximate discrete cosine transform (DCT). To minimize front-end data movement, we reformulate the color-space transformation within the frequency domain and retain only a compact set of task-relevant low-frequency coefficients. Furthermore, we employ a multiplier-less, shift-and-add-based arithmetic architecture to simplify control logic and enhance energy efficiency. The pipeline is fully compatible with standard convolutional neural networks, requiring no architectural changes to the back-end detector. We validated the design using a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based prototype on the Xilinx ZCU102 platform integrated with a YOLOv3-Tiny detector. The system achieves real-time processing of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6144\times 4096$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RAW inputs at 33.18 frames per second (FPS). Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed frequency-domain approach maintains detection accuracy comparable to RGB-based pipelines while significantly reducing hardware resource utilization and power consumption.

title: FreMotion: A Frequency-Motion Synergy Framework for Imitation Learning in Robust Robotic Fine Manipulation
authors: Jie Mei, Xinkai Wu, Yue Zhang, Tao Song, Zhongxia Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Imitation learning has emerged as a promising paradigm for automating robotic fine manipulation, yet maintaining temporal coherence and execution precision in long-horizon tasks remains a formidable challenge. This requires addressing exponential error accumulation in long-horizon tasks, as well as the inherent trade-off between millimeter-level positioning precision and motion smoothness, which is critical for the stability and accuracy of robotic automation systems. To this end, we propose FreMotion, a task-driven asymmetric encoder-decoder framework designed to enhance the robustness and efficiency of robotic manipulation. Our approach introduces the Temporal Dynamic Encoder (TDE-Net), which employs linear attention with dynamic gating to capture long-range dependencies and mitigate error accumulation in continuous control. Simultaneously, the Frequency-Aware Decoder (FAD-Net) leverages a dual-path attention mechanism to explicitly disentangle and fuse high- and low-frequency information for precise visuo-motor coordination. Extensive experiments on bimanual robotic tasks demonstrate that FreMotion, with orders of magnitude fewer parameters (approx. 100M vs. Billions), not only outperforms state-of-the-art Vision-Language-Action (VLA) methods in success rate but also achieves faster inference time, thus ensuring high-frequency continuous control required for industrial automation.

title: Wireless Electromagnetic Tracking Using Ultra-Low Frequency Fields for Hardware-Constrained Medical Devices_supp2-3694425.mp4
authors: Chengzhi Hu
abstract: Quasi-static magnetic localization offers advantages in eliminating geomagnetic interference and compatibility with magnetic actuation. However, existing systems that rely on high-frequency magnetic fields (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq$</tex-math></inline-formula>1000 Hz) often require high sampling rates, which increase the communication bandwidth demands and power consumption in wireless applications. This article presents a wireless electromagnetic tracking system (WEMTS) that operates at ultra-low frequencies (210 Hz), specifically designed for hardware-constrained medical devices like capsule robots. The WEMTS hardware consists of two main components: three external coil groups and a wireless sensor module (WSM) that integrates a three-axis magnetic sensor and an RF transmitter. A multilateration-based sequential pose estimation method is implemented for the 6-DoF localization of the WSM, improving convergence stability and computational efficiency. Experimental results show that WEMTS achieves static localization accuracy of 3.09 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.99 mm in position and 2.21 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.62<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> in orientation, and dynamic tracking errors of 3.23 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 1.05 mm and 2.67 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.89<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> at 5 mm/s within a 400 mm × 200 mm × 300 mm workspace. To validate practical applicability, the WSM was integrated into a vibrating capsule and successfully tracked in real time inside an ex vivo porcine colon. The proposed WEMTS allows precise and robust tracking, effectively addressing the challenges posed by hardware-constrained medical devices.

title: Enhanced meta-transfer learning for few-shot recognition of communication interference signals
authors: Shaogang Dai, Jingtong Wu, Yinzhi Wang, Huaji Zhou
abstract: The ongoing challenge of large amounts of labeled data requirements in existing communication jamming signal recognition methods motivates this work. We develop a few-shot recognition framework integrating Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) and transfer learning, denoted MTL-RS+. A tri-phase progressive architecture is proposed, which includes pre-training, meta-training and meta-testing, pre-training initializes the network using known jamming classes; meta-training optimizes meta-parameters via N-way-K-shot episodic tasks, and meta-testing completes jamming signal recognition. An attention-enhanced residual module based on SENet is designed to use as the feature extractor, utilizing multi-scale convolutional kernels and channel attention mechanisms to enhance discriminative feature representation. An enhanced meta-transfer strategy is proposed that migrates the pre-trained feature extractor to the meta-training network, augmented by dynamic weight interpolation to bolster cross-domain generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that MTL-RS+ achieves average recognition accuracies of 90.89% (known classes) and 79.96% (unknown classes) with only 5 fine-tuning samples across 20 known and 10 unknown jamming types. This represents a > 6.4% accuracy gain over MAML-based baselines, significantly reducing annotation demands and enhancing anti-jamming robustness in dynamic electromagnetic environments.

title: Reliable Power Management for DCMG Clusters Based on Adaptive Droop Curve and DESO Under Line Impedance and Cyber Attacks
authors: Dat Thanh Tran, Kyeong-Hwa Kim, Jih-Sheng Lai
abstract: The proposed scheme employs a multilayered approach to address the critical issue of ensuring the stability and reliability in DC microgrid (DCMG) clusters facing both physical constraints such as line impedance and cyber-security threats. First, a distributed tertiary control based on the partially decentralized secondary approach is employed with only a unidirectional communication structure to cut down the system cost and enhance the scalable capability of DCMG clusters. Second, to eliminate the effect of line impedance among clusters, the adaptive V*-P droop curves are utilized in the primary control to ensure the local power sharing in the intra-cluster and global power management in the inter-cluster under system uncertainty conditions. By using the adaptive V*-P droop curves, the utility grid (UG) agent automatically adjusts its droop curve to optimize the electricity expenditure under the electricity price change. Third, for the purpose of constituting a resilient control against false data injection (FDI) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks both in the tertiary and secondary controllers, a dual-extended state observer (DESO) is presented to precisely estimate the attack signals. By using these estimated attack signals, a reliable power management scheme is constructed to achieve system stabilization without DC bus voltage (DCV) deviations as compared to existing schemes.

title: LlymX: Multimodal LLM-Augmented XR for Context-Aware Information Access
authors: Luca Cordioli, Matteo Valoriani, Maristella Matera
abstract: Extended Reality (XR) and Large Language Models (LLMs) can support context-aware, hands-free information access in industrial environments. This paper presents LlymX, a prototype combining a Unity-based 3D environment with an LLM-driven backend leveraging retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and vector databases. LlymX introduces an interaction-oriented XR–LLM pipeline in which spatial user actions, such as gestures and object selection, are grounded in semantic object representations and linked to retrieval from a structured knowledge base. Through speech, gestures, and text, users can identify objects and access contextually relevant information grounded in domain documentation. A user study comparing LlymX against menu-based and infographic-based XR interfaces shows that the LLM-based modality is significantly more usable (SUS) than static infographics and comparable to traditional menus. Task completion time and cognitive workload showed no statistically significant differences, though participants appreciated the flexibility of conversational interaction.

title: DGPDT: Detection-Guided and Prompt-Driven Transformers for Automated and Generalizable Cobb Angle Estimation
authors: Chih-Yi Lu, Chen-Hung Tu, Chun-Yi Hsieh, Chi-Kuang Feng, I-Yun Lisa Hsieh
abstract: Accurate Cobb angle measurement is essential for scoliosis assessment but remains labor-intensive and observer-dependent. We introduce DGPDT (Detection-Guided Prompt-driven Transformer), a unified transformer-based framework that integrates vertebra detection and foundation-model segmentation for generalizable spinal analysis. A Roboflow Detection Transformer (RF-DETR) with a DINOv2 backbone localizes vertebrae, followed by post-processing to ensure anatomical continuity. The resulting bounding boxes serve as automatic prompts for a fine-tuned Segment Anything Model 2.1 (SAM 2.1), which generates high-resolution vertebral masks. Cobb angles are then computed from vertebral masks, enabling estimation of both main and compensatory curves. Evaluations on the in-house (TVGH-SpineXR) and external (SpineWeb-16) datasets demonstrate encouraging performance on both internal and external datasets, achieving mean Dice coefficients of 0.944 and 0.781, respectively, and mean absolute Cobb angle errors of approximately 2–3° in-domain and 4.93° under cross-domain evaluation. Despite being trained solely on TVGH-SpineXR, DGPDT maintains accuracy comparable to models trained directly on the benchmark dataset. By coupling detection-guided prompting with transformer-based segmentation, DGPDT achieves a clinically acceptable mean absolute error (<5°), suggesting good reproducibility and potential applicability beyond the training dataset.

title: CaUCD: Causal Unsupervised Change Detection
authors: Dong Ren, Yang Zhang, Wenbin Wang, Gui-Song Xia, Xiang Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Current unsupervised change detection (UCD) methods face two key limitations: inaccurate pseudo-label generation and failure to account for land cover influence, resulting in unreliable change area predictions. Specifically, existing methods neglect the influence of context during the generation of pseudo-labels and the probability of different land cover changes when training change detection networks. To address these issues, we designed a causal unsupervised change detection framework (CaUCD), consisting of two networks: casual unsupervised semantic segmentation network (CUSeg) for generating pseudo-labels and causal change detection network (C2D) for detecting change area. CUSeg removes contextual interference, thereby obtaining accurate pseudo-labels for training C2D. C2D eliminates different land cover influence, thus accurately detecting change areas. On four datasets (SECOND, SYSU, CNAM-CD and DSIFN), CaUCD surpasses state-of-the-art UCD methods and demonstrates considerable segmentation results. Our code will be made publicly available at: https://www.github.com/zhangshao249/CaUCD.

title: Fast and Scalable Newton-Raphson Power Flow Analysis via Hierarchical Parallelism and Graph Node-based Hash Storage
authors: Tingdong Wang, Yinglong Ma, Yihu Liu, Siyan Liu, Yufei Zhang
abstract: The continuous expansion of smart grids has led to increasingly frequent power flow variations, thereby posing new demands for fast and scalable parallel implementation of Newton-Raphson power flow (N-R PF) calculations. However, existing parallel N-R PF methods often suffer from redundant floating-point operations and centralized data processing of matrix reconstruction for N-R PF calculation, which significantly increases the computational burden and impairs the parallel performance of N-R PF. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a fast and scalable hierarchical parallel strategy and graph node-based hash storage (HPGNH) method to accelerate N-R PF analysis. HPGNH integrates intra-node and inter-node parallel strategies for effectively eliminating redundant floating-point operations in Jacobian matrix updates and equation residual updates. Moreover, we present a graph node-based hash storage structure to efficiently construct and update the nonzero entries of sparse matrices in a fully parallel manner. Particularly for Q-limit N-R PF calculations, HPGNH only updates a subset of entries within the constructed storage structure instead of performing full-scale reconstruction, thereby significantly improving computational efficiency. Extensive experiments on twelve benchmark power systems demonstrate that HPGNH outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods, with computational performance improvements of 63.1% for N-R PF and 81.8% for Q-limit N-R PF.

title: A Cooperative Scheme for Voltage Ripple Extraction and Suppression in DC Distribution Networks Based on TTAO-VMD and Improved DC-APF
authors: Yi Zhang, Menghan Wang, Baojiang Wu, Sian Chen
abstract: Voltage ripple is a significant power-quality concern in DC distribution networks, and effective suppression requires precise extraction of the ripple components. Traditional methods, such as low-pass filtering, often suffer from inaccurate band division and limited dynamic adaptability, which constrain the performance of DC active power filters. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a collaborative governance scheme with two main innovations. First, a triangular topology aggregation optimizer (TTAO) is introduced to adaptively adjust the penalty factor and modal number in variational mode decomposition (VMD), minimizing envelope entropy for high-precision, real-time ripple extraction. Second, an improved parallel active DC filter (DC-APF) based on a buck-boost topology is developed, utilizing a dual-loop control strategy that integrates feedforward and feedback for rapid, accurate compensation current tracking. Simulation results show that the TTAO-VMD algorithm effectively prevents modal aliasing and achieves a maximum absolute ripple extraction error of less than 0.02 V. The DC-APF, using this accurate signal, reduces the DC bus voltage ripple peak-to-peak value from 32 V to 0.3 V (99.06% suppression) and lowers total harmonic distortion from 1.85% to 0.17%. These results demonstrate the scheme’s effectiveness for ripple control in DC distribution networks.

title: Back-to-Back Multiport Converter for Wind and Battery Integrated System With Reduced Power Conversion Stage
authors: Yu Huang, Dehong Zhou, Tiecheng Lan, Jianxiao Zou, Zewei Shen
abstract: The wind and battery integrated system is an attractive solution for improving power quality and stability in regions with high wind penetration. However, conventional configurations typically realize battery integration by employing high-voltage batteries or intermediate dc-dc converters, which present difficulties in balancing system cost, dc-link regulation capability, and power conversion stages. To this end, this paper proposes a back-to-back multiport converter (BTBMC) for battery integration, maintaining dc-link regulation while reducing costs and power conversion stages. In addition, a coordinated zero-sequence injection (CZSI)-based power flow control scheme is proposed by analyzing the decoupling characteristics of the BTBMC, ensuring machine/grid-side current control while facilitating the power flow among the wind turbine, dc link, battery, and grid. In the proposed scheme, a virtual two-level converter model is constructed to ensure the current control even under asymmetric dc-port voltages. Moreover, the zero-sequence component injection is coordinated on both the machine and grid sides to separately control the dc-link voltage and the battery power, thereby enabling flexible power flow in the wind and battery integrated system. Besides, the power flow control capability of the proposed scheme is analytically discussed. Experimental results on a downscaled prototype validate the effectiveness of the proposed topology and scheme.
